She could still feel the adrenaline of standing in front of the board, her voice steady as she delivered her plan, and the way the chairman’s words had hit her like a lightning bolt. *Exceptional. Forward-thinking. Practical.* The words played on repeat in her mind, each one a validation of the months of hard work she’d poured into that project.  

The memory of that triumph made her heart swell again, and she glanced at Jeff, who caught her eye and smiled. He’d been her first text after the meeting, his immediate pride and excitement for her shining through even in a few simple words. She remembered their playful back-and-forth, how he’d made her laugh when she told him about the video, and how he never failed to make her feel like she could take on the world.  

And now, here she was, ending her day with a perfect meal he’d prepared—steak cooked to perfection, a crisp salad, her favorite wine chilled and waiting. Even his lemonade had a few ice cubes floating in it, like he’d taken the time to think about every little detail.  

Jodi looked down at her plate, the knife and fork in her hands, and realized just how complete the day felt. It wasn’t just about her professional success, though that was huge. It was about *all* of it—the laughter, the teasing, the little triumphs, the love she felt so deeply from the man sitting across from her.  

“Penny for your thoughts?” Jeff asked, his voice pulling her back to the present.  

She smiled, setting down her fork as she leaned her elbows on the table. “I was just thinking about today,” she said softly.  

“Oh yeah?” he asked, tilting his head. “What about it?”  

“Just… all of it,” she replied, her eyes meeting his. “From this morning to the office to this dinner… it’s been such a good day. One of those days that feels like everything just falls into place, you know?”  

Jeff smiled, reaching across the table to take her hand. “You deserve good days like this, Jodi. Every single one of them.”  

Her smile widened, and she squeezed his hand. “Thanks, Jeff. For all of it—for believing in me, for celebrating with me, for making me laugh when I needed it most.”  

“Always,” he said, his voice warm.  

As the night stretched on, Jodi felt a sense of peace settle over her. The day had been perfect, yes—but more than that, it had been *hers.* From the chaos of the morning to the triumph of her presentation, to this quiet, beautiful moment with Jeff, it was a day she’d remember for a long time.  

She raised her glass, grinning. “To days like today,” she said.  

Jeff clinked his lemonade glass against hers, his eyes twinkling. “And to more of them,” he said.    

---

As they finished their dinner, Jodi leaned back in her chair, twirling the last sip of her wine in her glass. She glanced at Jeff, who was finishing his lemonade, and grinned mischievously.  

“So,” she began, her tone light but curious, “how soon were you thinking about having sex tonight?”  

Jeff blinked, caught off guard by the directness of her question. He set his glass down, smirking as he leaned forward, resting his elbows on the table. “Immediately,” he said without hesitation, “but why do you ask?”  

Jodi laughed, shaking her head. “Because I need to propose a slight delay,” she said, leaning her chin on her hand.  

Jeff raised an eyebrow. “A delay? What could possibly be more important than having sex with me right now?”  

“Well,” Jodi began, drawing the word out as she looked at him sheepishly, “I’ve got this… *situation* in the garage.”  

Jeff’s smirk widened. “Oh? What kind of situation are we talking about?”  

“It’s the ‘very large batch of recyclables that’s been taking over half the garage’ kind of situation,” Jodi admitted, sighing dramatically. “I’ve been meaning to take all that cardboard to the county recycling facility for weeks, but today just got away from me. And now…” She gestured toward him, her smile turning sweet. “Now I’m wondering if you’d be willing to help me load it up and drop it off before seven.”  

Jeff leaned back in his chair, crossing his arms as he studied her. “Let me get this straight,” he said, his tone teasing. “You want me to delay having sex with you so we can haul cardboard across town?”  

Jodi shrugged, her grin widening. “Well, if we do it now, you can park your car in the garage tonight. Doesn’t that sound nice?”  

Jeff laughed, shaking his head. “You really know how to sell a guy on a chore, don’t you?”  

“I try,” she quipped, standing up and grabbing her plate to take it to the sink. “But seriously, Jeff, if we load everything up now, we can get it out of the way before it gets too late. I’d have planned better, but today kind of… took off in unexpected ways.”  

Jeff stood up as well, following her to the sink with his own plate. “Yeah, I wonder why,” he said, nudging her playfully. “Something about a certain video and a career-defining presentation, maybe?”  

“Exactly,” Jodi said, smirking as she rinsed her plate. “It’s been a *day.* And now I’m asking for your help to end it on a high note—clean garage, empty recyclables, and then…” She trailed off, looking at him with a raised eyebrow.  

“And then?” Jeff prompted, leaning closer.  

“And then,” Jodi said, her voice dropping slightly, “we can have all the uninterrupted sex you want, and I won’t be stressing about cardboard the whole time.”  

Jeff groaned, though he was grinning. “Fine. But only because you’re adorable—and because parking in the garage *does* sound pretty nice.”  

“Thank you,” Jodi said, standing on her tiptoes to kiss him on the cheek. “I promise it won’t take long. We’ll be back before you know it.”  

“Better be,” Jeff said, grabbing the keys from the counter. “Because I’ve been thinking about you all day, and I don’t want cardboard ruining my plans.”  

Jodi laughed as they headed for the garage. “Trust me, once it’s out of the way, I’ll make it worth your while.”  

“Deal,” Jeff said, opening the garage door.  

As they began loading the cardboard into the car, the mundane task was filled with laughter and teasing, the perfect prelude to an evening that promised to end just as wonderfully as it had started.    

---

As Jeff navigated the familiar streets, the car filled with the faint crinkle of cardboard shifting in the backseat and the warm hum of the radio. The late evening light bathed the neighborhood in a soft glow as they passed Jodi’s mom’s house, the neatly trimmed hedges and cheerful flowerbeds unmistakable.

Jeff glanced over at Jodi, who was scrolling through her phone with a relaxed smile. “You wanna stop by your mom’s on the way back?” he asked casually, his hands resting comfortably on the wheel.  

Jodi snorted, glancing up at him with raised eyebrows. “Why would we do that?”  

“I don’t know,” Jeff said, smirking. “Thought maybe she’d want to see her daughter. Check in. Talk about how the presentation went today.”  

Jodi shook her head, laughing. “Yeah, no. All she’d do is tease me about getting laid. That’s her default setting these days.”  

Jeff’s eyebrows shot up, and he turned to her briefly at a stop sign. “Wait—she actually *teases* you about that?”  

Jodi rolled her eyes, though a grin tugged at her lips. “Not directly, but you know how she is. She’ll drop some vague comment about my ‘healthy glow’ or how I’m walking lighter on my feet. And then she’ll just *stare* at me until I crack. She’s relentless.”  

Jeff let out a laugh, shaking his head as he turned the car onto the next street. “Okay, but if that’s what she’d do, is it really teasing? Or just… you know, telling the truth?”  

Jodi gave him a mock glare, crossing her arms. “I *do not* need my mom making comments about my sex life, truthful or not.”  

Jeff grinned, his tone playful. “Maybe, but let’s be real—if I weren’t here, what would you be doing right now?”  

Jodi raised an eyebrow. “Unloading cardboard and relaxing at home?”  

Jeff shook his head, chuckling. “Nah. If I weren’t here, you’d be at her house, spilling all the details about your day and probably hinting at the part where you got laid. Then she’d tease *you* about it, and you’d laugh and swear you didn’t say anything.”  

Jodi laughed, nudging his arm. “You think you know me so well, huh?”  

Jeff smirked, glancing at her briefly. “I *do* know you. You and your mom are like an open book. It’s one of the things I love about you two.”  

Jodi sighed, shaking her head, though her smile lingered. “Alright, fine, you’re probably not wrong. But since you’re *here* and I’m *not* at her house, I’d rather not subject myself to that kind of teasing tonight. One of us teasing me about getting laid is more than enough.”  

Jeff chuckled, squeezing her hand. “Fair enough. But just so you know, next time we drive by, I’m stopping, and I’m bringing popcorn for the show.”  

Jodi laughed, leaning her head back against the seat. “You’re ridiculous.”  

“And yet, you love me,” Jeff said, grinning.  

She rolled her eyes, though her smile widened. “Yeah, yeah, I do.”  

As they pulled into the county recycling facility, Jodi found herself grateful—not just for Jeff’s help tonight, but for the way he always made even the most mundane tasks feel like an adventure.    

---

The unloading went quicker than Jodi expected, the two of them working in sync as they stacked and tossed flattened boxes into the designated bins at the county recycling facility. Jeff, ever the efficient one, made a game out of tossing the cardboard, throwing each piece with a dramatic flourish while Jodi laughed and rolled her eyes.

As Jeff shut the trunk and wiped his hands on his jeans, he leaned against the car with a grin, watching Jodi stretch her arms overhead. The golden light of the setting sun made her hair glow, and for a moment, he just admired her, his grin softening.

“Alright,” he said, his tone casual but teasing. “Last chance to stop by your mom’s before we head back. Speak now, or forever hold your peace.”

Jodi paused, narrowing her eyes at him. “You’re really pushing this, huh?”

Jeff shrugged, smirking. “Just making sure. Because once we get home, we’re showering, and then…” He straightened up, stepping closer to her, his voice dropping slightly. “Then I’m not letting you out of that bed for hours.”

Jodi blinked, her lips parting slightly as her breath hitched. “Oh,” she murmured, her heart suddenly racing.

Jeff leaned in, brushing a strand of hair away from her face. “First move,” he said, his voice low and deliberate, “is pinning you against the wall as soon as we get out of the shower. No towel, no hesitation. Just me and you.”

Jodi swallowed hard, her cheeks flushing as she stared up at him. Her playful confidence from earlier felt like it had evaporated in an instant, replaced by a heat she couldn’t ignore. “You’ve really thought this through, haven’t you?” she said, her voice shakier than she intended.

“Of course,” Jeff said, his smirk widening. “I’ve been thinking about it all day. And after that video you sent me earlier? Let’s just say I’ve got… a lot of plans.”

Jodi exhaled slowly, running a hand through her hair as she tried to steady herself. “You’re dangerous, you know that?”

“And yet, you can’t get enough of me,” Jeff teased, stepping back and opening the car door for her. “So, what’s it gonna be? Your mom’s house, or home for… what’s the word? Oh, right—hours.”

Jodi groaned, covering her face with one hand as she climbed into the passenger seat. “We’re going home. But you’re driving too fast if we’re getting there *that* soon.”

Jeff laughed as he slid into the driver’s seat, starting the car. “Fair warning, babe: once we’re home, I’m not slowing down.”

Jodi shook her head, though her body was already humming with anticipation. “You’re ridiculous,” she said, glancing out the window as the recycling facility faded into the distance.

“And you’re turned on,” Jeff shot back, glancing at her with a knowing grin.

Jodi didn’t respond, but the way she bit her lip gave her away. As Jeff turned onto the main road, the tension between them hung in the air, thick and electric, promising a very long—and very satisfying—night ahead.  
  
---

The warm water of the shower cascaded over their bodies, steam filling the bathroom and fogging the mirrors. Jeff’s hands roamed over Jodi’s soapy skin, his touch both tender and teasing as they rinsed off the day. Their laughter echoed against the tiles as Jeff leaned in to steal kisses, and Jodi playfully swatted his chest when his hands wandered a little *too* much.  

“Patience,” she teased, arching an eyebrow.  

“Patience isn’t really my thing,” Jeff replied, his grin mischievous.  

When the water turned off, Jeff grabbed a towel and handed it to Jodi, his eyes lingering as she wrapped it around herself. He didn’t bother with one for himself, letting the water drip from his hair and shoulders as he stepped out of the shower.  

As soon as she stepped out behind him, towel still tucked snugly around her, Jeff turned, and his eyes darkened with intent. “Told you,” he murmured, his voice low, “first move, no hesitation.”  

Before Jodi could respond, he closed the distance between them in two steps, backing her against the wall. Her damp towel slipped from her grasp, pooling at her feet, and Jeff’s hands framed her face as he kissed her deeply. The coolness of the bathroom wall against her back contrasted with the heat of his body pressed against hers.  

“You’re a man of your word,” Jodi breathed as his lips trailed down her neck.  

“Always,” Jeff murmured, his hands slipping to her hips as he lifted her slightly, her legs wrapping instinctively around his waist.  

When he entered her, it was slow, deliberate, his forehead resting against hers as they both exhaled in unison. Jodi’s fingers threaded through his damp hair, and she let out a soft laugh, her breath warm against his lips.  

“I really, really like that about you,” she whispered, her voice tinged with both affection and heat.  

Jeff chuckled, nipping at her jaw. “You’re not too bad yourself,” he said, his movements slow and steady as he took his time savoring her.

She glanced at the clock, her brow furrowing slightly as she took a deep breath. *It’s fine,* she told herself. *You’re in the zone now. You’ll decompress later.*  

And, for a while, she did. With her focus sharpened, she plowed through her emails, tackling tasks one after another, her productivity a testament to her ability to compartmentalize.  

Until the sound of heels clicking against the floor broke her concentration.  

“Jodi!” Tara’s voice rang out as she appeared in the doorway, leaning against the frame with her ever-present smirk.  

Jodi looked up, her fingers pausing over the keyboard. “Tara,” she said, her tone flat. “What do you want?”  

Tara grinned, crossing her arms as she stepped inside. “Oh, nothing much. Just checking in on our resident romantic to see if you’ve finally caught your breath after whatever *that* was this morning.”  

Jodi rolled her eyes, though a faint smile tugged at her lips. “I’m fine, Tara. Busy, as you can see.”  

“Sure, sure,” Tara said, waving a hand dismissively. “But come on—you’re telling me you didn’t feel even a *little* rushed out the door today? No time for… I don’t know, *post-game analysis?*”  

Jodi arched an eyebrow at her. “Do you mean aftercare?”  

“Yeah, that,” Tara said, grinning. “You’re always big on that, right? So I’m guessing it got cut from the schedule.”  

Jodi sighed, leaning back in her chair. “Thanks for reminding me,” she said dryly. “I was just starting to get over it.”  

Tara laughed, plopping down in the chair across from Jodi’s desk. “Hey, don’t shoot the messenger. But seriously, Jodi—you look like you need a *second* aftercare session just to recover from missing the first one.”  

Jodi shook her head, though she couldn’t help but smile. “I’m fine,” she said again, her tone softening. “It was still worth it.”  

“Oh, I’m sure it was,” Tara replied, her grin widening. “But you know, if you ever want to talk about it, I’m here. Totally not to gossip, of course. Purely supportive reasons.”  

“Uh-huh,” Jodi said, smirking as she reached for her mouse. “Thanks, Tara. Now, if you’ll excuse me, I’ve got work to do.”  

“Fine, fine,” Tara said, standing and smoothing out her skirt. “But don’t think I’m letting you off the hook at lunch.”  

“Looking forward to it,” Jodi called after her, shaking her head as Tara disappeared down the hall.  

As the office quieted again, Jodi let out a slow breath, her fingers returning to the keyboard. Her smile lingered as she dove back into her work, the morning’s memories still flickering warmly in the back of her mind.    

---

As Jodi clicked through the last of her emails, her stomach gave a low, insistent growl. She glanced at the clock and sighed. *Lunch.* The one thing she hadn’t had the presence of mind to plan for during her frantic morning dash.  

Her gaze shifted to the office door, where Tara had disappeared just moments ago. A thought crept into her mind, making her smirk. *Maybe I can convince her to hit the Mexican cantina with me,* she mused. *Nachos always make her talkative—and nosy.*  

Jodi leaned back in her chair, tapping her pen against her desk as she considered the pros and cons. On the one hand, she really did need a decent meal if she was going to survive the rest of the day. On the other, she knew full well that any meal with Tara would come with a price: namely, Tara’s relentless curiosity.  

*How graphic is she going to want me to be this time?* Jodi wondered, her cheeks warming slightly. Tara always seemed to walk the fine line between genuine interest and pure entertainment when it came to Jodi’s love life.  

It wasn’t just the teasing—it was the way Tara hung on her every word, nodding and laughing as though she were living vicariously through her stories. Jodi could hardly blame her; Tara had once confided that her own love life was “about as exciting as watching paint dry.” But still, there were moments when Jodi felt like she was putting on a show, like she was the main character in some spicy romance novel that Tara couldn’t put down.  

*And if she presses me for details this time…* Jodi thought, her smirk returning as her mind wandered to the scene she’d been imagining. She pictured Tara leaning over the plate of nachos, her eyes sparkling with curiosity, one hand poised to scoop up salsa-covered chips while the other gestured animatedly. *If she presses me too much, I’m throwing it right back at her.*  

Jodi grinned at the thought of challenging Tara on how much she seemed to live vicariously through her. *You’re always asking for the play-by-play,* she’d say. *Is it because you’re curious, or because you want ideas for your next date?*  

The idea made her laugh softly to herself. *Tara probably wouldn’t even deny it. She’d just smirk and call me out for being smug.*  

Her stomach growled again, louder this time, and Jodi sighed. *Alright, decision made. Tara’s buying nachos if I’m spilling tea. That’s the rule.*  

She grabbed her phone and shot Tara a quick text:  

**Jodi:** *Feeling generous enough to treat me to nachos at the cantina? Promise I’ll make it worth your while.*  

Within seconds, her phone buzzed with a reply.  

**Tara:** *Generous? No. But nosy? Absolutely. Meet you at noon.*  

Jodi shook her head, smiling as she set her phone down. *This should be interesting,* she thought, already preparing herself for the inevitable onslaught of questions—and for the moment she’d throw a little challenge Tara’s way, just to keep things fun.    

---  
As Jodi tidied up her desk, she couldn’t help but smirk at the mental script forming in her head. If Tara wanted details, Jodi would give them—*all* the details. Every spicy, raunchy, blush-inducing tidbit.  

*Well, up to a point,* she thought, her grin widening. She was more than willing to dish about the sixty-nine this morning, to watch Tara’s jaw drop as she casually described how maddeningly slow and deliberate it had been, how their tongues and hands had explored each other so completely.  

But right before she got to the real kicker—the “breakfast of champions” moment—Jodi planned to stop. Abruptly. Right at the juiciest moment.  

*If she wants to pry, she’s going to have to earn it,* Jodi thought, twirling her pen in her fingers.  

She pictured Tara, leaning over the table, her nachos forgotten as her curiosity burned a hole through the air between them. “Wait, wait,” Tara would say, waving her hands. “You can’t just *stop* there. What happened next?”  

And that’s when Jodi would strike.  

“Oh, I *can* stop there,” she’d say, her tone playful but sharp. “Because now I need to know something, Tara—why do you *need* to pry so deeply into my sex life? What’s the real reason?”  

The thought made Jodi laugh softly to herself, leaning back in her chair. She knew Tara well enough to predict the scene perfectly: Tara would try to brush it off at first, laughing and making some snarky comment. But Jodi would hold her ground, forcing her friend to come clean.  

*Is it because your love life is boring?* Jodi imagined herself saying. *Or is it because you’re living vicariously through me?*  

She grinned, already anticipating the way Tara would squirm under the pressure. *Come on, spill it,* Jodi thought, imagining her own voice in the moment. *You’ve got to admit you’re a little jealous. And until you do, I’m not saying a word about the last forty minutes or my… unconventional breakfast.*  

The mental image of Tara finally caving—rolling her eyes and throwing her hands up in defeat—made Jodi laugh again. *She’ll totally fold,* Jodi thought, her confidence growing. *And once she does, then maybe I’ll tell her about how Jeff had me pressed against the shower wall at one point last night, too.*  

Her phone buzzed on her desk, snapping her out of her thoughts.  

**Tara:** *Nachos at noon. Bring your best gossip. Don’t hold back.*  

Jodi smirked as she typed out her reply.  

**Jodi:** *Oh, don’t worry. I plan on being *very* detailed. Hope you’re ready to come clean about a few things yourself.*  

She set her phone down, already counting down the minutes until lunch. Whatever Tara thought she was getting into, Jodi knew she was about to flip the script—and she couldn’t wait to see her friend’s reaction.    

---  
As Jodi and Tara stepped out of the bank and into the crisp midday air, Jodi adjusted her purse over her shoulder and shot Tara a playful grin. “So, nachos and spicy girl-talk,” she said, her tone full of mischief. “Ready to get scandalized, Tara?”  

Tara snorted, waving her hand. “Please. You couldn’t scandalize me if you tried,” she said, though the glint in her eye suggested she was eager to be proven wrong.  

They’d barely made it halfway to the cantina when Naomi poked her head out of the doorway behind them. “Hey, are you guys going to lunch?” she called, jogging to catch up with them, her high ponytail bouncing.  

Tara glanced over her shoulder, raising an eyebrow. “Yeah, just down to the cantina,” she said.  

Naomi grinned, falling into step beside them. “Mind if I join you?”  

Jodi and Tara exchanged a quick look. Naomi was fresh out of college and still navigating her way through adult life—and, by all accounts, doing so with an almost *adorable* level of naïveté. She was sweet, eager to please, and hopelessly clueless about certain things, especially when it came to men.  

Jodi’s lips twitched into a massive, shit-eating grin as she turned to Tara, her eyes practically gleaming with mischief. *This could be hilarious,* her expression screamed.  

Tara, catching the look, gave Jodi a subtle shake of her head but sighed in resignation. She turned to Naomi with a raised eyebrow. “You can come,” she said, her tone even, “but just so you know, we’re going to have some *very* frank girl-talk. Stuff that might be, uh, a little much.”  

Naomi tilted her head, her curious smile unwavering. “That’s fine,” she chirped. “I love girl-talk!”  

Jodi bit her lip to stifle a laugh, while Tara pinched the bridge of her nose. “Alright,” Tara muttered. “Your call, Naomi. But don’t say we didn’t warn you.”  

“Warn me about what?” Naomi asked, her ponytail swishing as she glanced between them.  

“Oh, you’ll see,” Jodi said sweetly, her voice practically dripping with amusement as she opened the door to the cantina.  

The three women stepped inside, the warm scent of spices and sizzling meat greeting them as they found a booth near the back. Tara slid into one side of the booth while Jodi and Naomi took the other.  

As they settled in and looked over the menu, Jodi leaned closer to Tara, her grin widening. “This is going to be *fun,*” she whispered, her voice low enough that Naomi couldn’t hear.  

Tara gave her a pointed look, though her lips twitched into a reluctant smile. “You’re evil,” she muttered, shaking her head.  

“Evil, but entertaining,” Jodi shot back, winking as she turned her attention to Naomi, who was already studying the menu like it was her final exam.  

As the waiter approached to take their orders, Jodi and Tara silently braced themselves. This lunch was about to take a turn, and neither of them could wait to see just how hilariously awkward things might get.  

---

The three women had just gotten their drinks—margaritas for Tara and Jodi, a sparkling water for Naomi—when Tara leaned back in her seat, swirling her glass with a sly grin.  

“So, Jodi,” she began, her voice dripping with mischief. “Why don’t you explain to Naomi why you were *this close* to being late for work again today?”  

Jodi nearly choked on her sip of margarita, setting the glass down with an exaggerated cough. “Oh, you really want me to go there, huh?” she said, raising an eyebrow at Tara.  

“Absolutely,” Tara said, her grin widening. She glanced over at Naomi, who was sitting with her hands folded neatly in her lap, her bright-eyed expression radiating curiosity. “Naomi’s fresh out of college. She should learn a few things about life, don’t you think?”  

Jodi snorted, shaking her head as she leaned back in the booth. “Alright,” she said, her voice full of playful warning. “But don’t say I didn’t warn you, Naomi. This is going to be… frank.”  

Naomi tilted her head, looking between them with a wide-eyed smile. “That’s fine!” she said brightly. “I’m a grown-up, you know. I can handle it.”  

Tara bit back a laugh, gesturing for Jodi to continue. “Go ahead,” she said, smirking.  

Jodi exhaled, steeling herself before diving in. “Okay, so this morning, I woke up about seven minutes before my alarm, right? And I was having this *really* hot dream about my boyfriend, Jeff.”  

Naomi blinked, nodding politely as Jodi continued.  

“In the dream, he was on top of me, and, well… you can imagine the rest,” Jodi said, pausing just long enough to give Tara a knowing look.  

“Oh, I *can* imagine,” Tara said, smirking as she took another sip of her drink.  

“Anyway,” Jodi went on, “I woke up really frustrated, because, you know, the dream didn’t finish. So I look over at Jeff, and he’s got this *massive* hard-on—like, just lying there, completely oblivious.”  

Naomi’s eyes widened slightly, her cheeks already starting to flush, but she didn’t say anything.  

“So I decided,” Jodi continued, leaning forward a little, “that I wasn’t going to let him wake up *normally.* I straddled him, and right as the alarm went off, I turned it off and sank down onto him. First thing he woke up to was me riding him.”  

Tara burst out laughing, covering her mouth as she tried to contain herself. Naomi, meanwhile, looked like she was trying to process the sheer audacity of what she’d just heard.  

“Wait, so…” Naomi began, her voice hesitant. “You just… woke him up like that?”  

“Exactly like that,” Jodi said with a grin, swirling her margarita. “No foreplay. No buildup. Just straight to the point.”  

Naomi’s cheeks turned a deep shade of pink as she glanced between Jodi and Tara. “I—uh—wow, that’s…” She trailed off, clearly at a loss for words.  

“And that’s not even the best part,” Jodi added, smirking as she leaned back in her seat. “We spent another forty minutes doing… other things, including—”  

“Sixty-nine,” Tara interrupted, laughing as she raised her glass. “You didn’t think I was going to let you skip that detail, did you?”  

Naomi nearly spat out her water, her face now a deep shade of crimson. “Oh my God,” she muttered, covering her mouth with her hands. “I can’t believe you’re just *talking* about this!”  

Jodi grinned, raising an eyebrow at her. “Hey, you said you could handle it.”  

“I—I didn’t know you were going to be so *graphic!*” Naomi stammered, her voice squeaky with embarrassment.  

“Life lessons, Naomi,” Tara said, her voice full of mock wisdom. “It’s important to hear these things from experienced women like us.”  

Jodi laughed, shaking her head. “Alright, that’s enough for now,” she said, giving Naomi a reprieve as she reached for a nacho. “But let me just say this: don’t knock it until you try it.”  

Naomi groaned, covering her face with her hands as Tara cackled beside her.    

---  
Jodi popped a nacho into her mouth, savoring the burst of melted cheese and jalapeños as she leaned back in the booth. Naomi was still blushing furiously, picking at her sparkling water with the straw as though it might offer her an escape. Tara was, predictably, enjoying every second of Naomi’s awkwardness, her smirk practically permanent by now.

“Alright, Naomi,” Jodi said suddenly, setting her margarita down and fixing the younger woman with a curious look. “Honest question for you.”

Naomi glanced up, her wide eyes already wary. “Uh… okay?” she said hesitantly.

Jodi’s lips curved into a mischievous grin. “Are you still a virgin?”

Tara nearly spit out her drink, laughing so hard she had to grab a napkin to keep from choking. “Jodi!” she wheezed, shaking her head. “You can’t just *ask* her that!”

“Why not?” Jodi said, shrugging innocently. “It’s a normal question. And she *did* agree to join us for girl-talk, so…”

Naomi’s cheeks turned an even deeper shade of red as she stared down at her drink. “I—I don’t think that’s really relevant,” she mumbled, fidgeting with her straw.

“Oh, it’s *completely* relevant,” Jodi said, leaning forward, her grin widening. “You’re here, listening to all my raunchy stories, so it’s only fair that we know what kind of baseline you’re coming from. So… are you?”

Naomi squirmed in her seat, her gaze darting nervously between Jodi and Tara. “Well… no,” she admitted softly, her voice barely audible over the background noise of the cantina.

“Ha!” Jodi said triumphantly, slapping her hand against the table. “See? That wasn’t so hard.”

“Poor choice of words,” Tara muttered under her breath, earning another burst of laughter from Jodi.

Jodi ignored her, tilting her head as she studied Naomi. “Alright, next question: how many men have you slept with?”

Naomi’s jaw dropped, her eyes wide with disbelief. “You can’t *ask* that!” she squeaked, her voice high-pitched and flustered.

“I just did,” Jodi said, her grin turning wicked. “So? Spill.”

Tara folded her arms, clearly enjoying the show. “I’m with Jodi on this one,” she said. “If you’re sitting at this table, you’re in the circle of trust. So let’s hear it, Naomi.”

Naomi groaned, burying her face in her hands. “I hate you both,” she muttered, though her tone was more embarrassed than angry. After a moment, she peeked out from behind her fingers, her face still crimson. “Fine. Two, okay? Just two.”

Jodi raised an eyebrow, her grin softening slightly. “Hey, that’s nothing to be embarrassed about,” she said, her tone surprisingly kind. “Two’s a respectable number. Quality over quantity, right?”

“Exactly,” Tara added, nodding sagely. “Unless they were both terrible. Then you just have bad luck.”

Naomi let out a groan of exasperation, finally dropping her hands. “Why did I agree to this?” she muttered, shaking her head as she reached for her water.

“Because you love us,” Jodi said, grabbing another nacho. “And because deep down, you’re curious about all the stuff we’ve been through.”

Naomi rolled her eyes, though a small, reluctant smile tugged at her lips. “You two are the worst.”

“And you’re lucky to have us,” Tara quipped, raising her margarita in mock toast.  

Naomi sighed, but she clinked her water glass against Tara’s anyway, knowing full well she wasn’t going to win this round.    

---  
Jodi sat back with a smirk, licking a bit of cheese off her thumb as Naomi tried to recover from her interrogation. “Alright,” Jodi said, turning her attention to Tara, her eyes gleaming with mischief. “Your turn, Tara. What’s your number?”  

Tara, who had been casually sipping her margarita, froze mid-drink. She set the glass down slowly, her expression suddenly cautious. “What do you mean, my number?”  

“Oh, come on,” Jodi said, rolling her eyes. “You know exactly what I mean. How many men have you slept with? We’ve heard from Naomi, you’ve been grilling me about my adventures—so now it’s your turn.”  

Tara’s smirk faltered, and for the first time that day, she looked genuinely uncomfortable. “I don’t think that’s relevant,” she said quickly, waving her hand. “Besides, you go first.”  

“Nice dodge,” Jodi said, laughing as she leaned forward. “Alright, fine, I’ll go first. My number’s sixteen.”  

Naomi’s eyes widened. “Sixteen?!” she blurted out, her voice so loud that the table next to them turned to look. She clapped a hand over her mouth, lowering her voice. “That’s a lot!”  

Jodi shrugged, grinning. “Hey, I’ve been busy. It’s not like they were all at once.”  

Tara, however, had gone very quiet, her cheeks flushing slightly as she fiddled with the straw in her drink. Jodi’s grin widened as she caught Tara’s reaction. “Your move, Tara,” she said, her tone dripping with challenge.  

Tara hesitated, her lips pressing into a thin line as she avoided Jodi’s gaze. “You’re not going to let this go, are you?” she asked finally, sighing.  

“Not a chance,” Jodi replied, popping another nacho into her mouth.  

Tara shifted uncomfortably in her seat, her fingers tightening around her glass. “Alright, fine,” she muttered. “It’s… five.”  

Jodi blinked, surprised, before narrowing her eyes. “Five?” she repeated, her tone skeptical.  

“Yes, five!” Tara snapped, her cheeks turning an even deeper shade of pink. “Not everyone has the time—or the energy—to rack up numbers like you, Jodi.”  

Jodi tilted her head, studying Tara for a moment. “Is that why you’re so obsessed with my sex life?” she asked, her tone turning playful but curious. “Because you’re living vicariously through me?”  

Tara groaned, covering her face with her hands. “I am *not* obsessed,” she muttered, though her voice lacked conviction.  

“Oh, really?” Jodi said, raising an eyebrow. “Because you’re always the first one to press me for details. Every. Single. Time.”  

Naomi, who had been quietly sipping her water, chimed in, “She kind of has a point, Tara.”  

Tara dropped her hands with a sigh, glaring at both of them. “Fine,” she said, exasperated. “Maybe I do enjoy hearing your stories. Okay? Happy now?”  

“Very,” Jodi said, grinning triumphantly. “But why, though? Is it because you’re curious? Or…” She paused, her grin turning wicked. “Is it because you’re secretly jealous?”  

Tara groaned again, throwing her hands up. “I swear to God, Jodi, you’re impossible.”  

“And yet, here you are, eating nachos with me,” Jodi said, laughing.  

Tara couldn’t help but crack a smile, shaking her head. “You know what? Maybe I *am* living vicariously through you,” she admitted, her tone light but genuine. “You’ve got all these wild stories, and meanwhile, my love life is basically a desert. I mean, I’m happy for you, but… yeah. Maybe I like hearing about it because it’s entertaining. And because it reminds me that life doesn’t always have to be boring.”  

Jodi’s grin softened into something warmer, and she reached across the table to pat Tara’s hand. “Well, I’m glad I can provide some entertainment,” she said, her tone sincere. “But hey, Tara—five’s a respectable number. You’re not missing out as much as you think you are.”  

Tara rolled her eyes, though her smile lingered. “Thanks, Jodi. I think.”  

“Anytime,” Jodi said with a wink, grabbing another nacho. “And if you ever need more stories to live vicariously through, you know where to find me.”    

---  
Naomi tilted her head, curiosity shining in her wide eyes. “Why do you say your love life is a desert, Tara?” she asked gently, her voice softer than usual.  

Tara sighed, her fingers tracing the rim of her margarita glass as she glanced between the two women. For a moment, she hesitated, her usual smirk replaced by something more vulnerable. “It’s not that complicated,” she said finally. “It’s just… life didn’t work out the way I thought it would.”  

Jodi and Naomi exchanged a look, both of them leaning in slightly.  

“When I was in my twenties, I thought I’d have it all figured out by now,” Tara continued, her gaze fixed on the table. “I thought I’d meet someone, fall in love, get married, maybe have kids. You know—the whole happily-ever-after thing.” She let out a short, humorless laugh. “But instead, I focused on work. My career took off, and I just… put everything else on hold.”  

Jodi frowned, her chest tightening as she listened. “Tara, I didn’t know,” she said softly.  

Tara shrugged, looking up at her with a faint smile. “Why would you? It’s not like I talk about it much. But now I’m in my forties, and… I guess I feel like I missed my window, you know?” She paused, her fingers tightening around her glass. “I go on dates sometimes, but it’s hard. Everyone’s got baggage. Or they want someone younger. Or they just aren’t looking for what I’m looking for.”  

Naomi bit her lip, her expression full of sympathy. “That sounds tough,” she said quietly. “But… you’re not *too late,* Tara. You’ve still got time.”  

Tara smiled, though it didn’t quite reach her eyes. “Maybe,” she said. “But it feels like the longer I wait, the harder it gets. And honestly… I don’t even know if I’m good at the whole relationship thing anymore. I’ve been alone for so long that the idea of opening up to someone again kind of scares me.”  

Jodi reached across the table, resting her hand over Tara’s. “Hey,” she said, her voice soft. “I’m sorry I’ve been so oblivious. I didn’t know you were feeling this way.”  

Tara glanced at her, her smile softening. “It’s not your fault,” she said. “You’ve got your own life, and it’s… a pretty exciting one, clearly.”  

Jodi chuckled softly, though her expression remained serious. “Still. I feel like I’ve been rubbing my stories in your face, and I never stopped to think about how they might make you feel.”  

“It’s not like that,” Tara said, shaking her head. “I *like* hearing your stories, Jodi. They remind me that life can still be fun, you know? That it’s not all spreadsheets and meetings and… being alone.”  

Naomi smiled, her tone soft but optimistic. “You’re not alone, Tara. You’ve got us. And I know that’s not the same as, like, romance, but… we’re here for you.”  

Jodi nodded, squeezing Tara’s hand. “Naomi’s right. And if you ever want to talk about this stuff—or, I don’t know, get some terrible advice about dating from me—I’m all ears.”  

Tara laughed, her shoulders relaxing as some of the tension eased. “Thanks,” she said, her voice warmer now. “I appreciate it. Both of you.”  

Jodi grinned, picking up a nacho. “Anytime. And just for the record, Tara… you’re amazing. You’ve got so much to offer. If someone can’t see that, that’s their loss—not yours.”  

Tara smiled, her eyes glistening slightly as she raised her glass. “Alright,” she said, her tone lighter. “Here’s to not giving up. And to good friends who ask too many questions.”  

Jodi and Naomi laughed, clinking their glasses with hers.    

---

Jodi leaned back in the booth, swirling the last of her margarita as she studied Tara. “So, let me get this straight,” she said, a playful but curious tone in her voice. “If you’re living vicariously through me, does that mean you’re frustrated when I skip over some of the, uh, juiciest parts of my stories?”  

Tara smirked, tilting her head. “Frustrated might be a strong word,” she said, her tone teasing but honest. “But, yeah. I mean, let’s be real—when you start a story like, ‘So, I straddled Jeff right when the alarm went off,’ and then stop right before the *really* toe-curling parts? It’s like cutting off a good movie right before the climax.”  

Naomi gasped, leaning forward with wide eyes. “Wait, so there’s *more* to that story?” she asked, her voice full of unrestrained curiosity. “I *knew* you weren’t telling us everything!”  

Jodi groaned, rubbing her temples with a laugh. “You two are ridiculous,” she muttered, shaking her head. “Look, I’m trying to walk this fine line here. On one hand, I want to respect my own dignity and boundaries—you know, not spill *everything.* But on the other hand, it’s *really* fun to gloat about having the kind of toe-curling sex that makes you both sit there all jealous and wide-eyed.”  

Tara laughed, nodding knowingly. “It’s a tough balance, I’ll give you that. But I’ll say this—I *do* appreciate that you have boundaries, Jodi. I respect it.” She paused, her grin turning cheeky. “But when you’ve got zero filter? Oh, that’s when your stories are at their absolute best.”  

Jodi raised an eyebrow, her lips curving into a smirk. “So you’re saying you want me to completely abandon my sense of shame?”  

“Yes,” Tara said immediately, not even trying to hide her enthusiasm.  

Naomi clapped her hands together, her face lighting up. “Oh my God, *same!* This conversation is infinitely interesting, Jodi. Like, I’m dying to hear more. You have *no idea* how boring my life is compared to yours.”  

Jodi laughed, shaking her head as she glanced between them. “You two are terrible,” she said, though her grin betrayed her amusement. “So what—you want me to go into every little detail? Every moan, every position, every—”  

“Yes!” Naomi interrupted, leaning forward so far that her elbows nearly hit her nachos.  

Tara chuckled, raising her glass in agreement. “Look, I get that it’s your life, Jodi, and you get to decide what you share. But if you ever feel like throwing dignity out the window for an afternoon? Just know we’re here for it.”  

Jodi sighed, taking a long sip of her drink before setting the glass down. “Alright, fine,” she said, her grin widening. “You want zero filter? I’ll give you zero filter. But don’t come crying to me when it gets too spicy to handle.”  

“Deal,” Tara said, grinning as she reached for another chip.  

Naomi practically bounced in her seat, her excitement palpable. “I can *totally* handle it,” she said, her eyes shining.  

Jodi laughed, shaking her head as she leaned forward, ready to dive in. “Alright, buckle up, ladies,” she said, her voice dripping with mischief. “Because this morning? It wasn’t just toe-curling—it was *mind-blowing.*”    

---  
Jodi leaned forward, propping her elbows on the table as she smirked at Tara and Naomi. “Alright, you asked for it. No filter. But don’t say I didn’t warn you.”  

Tara raised her margarita like a toast. “Warn away. I live for this.”  

Naomi nodded so eagerly her ponytail bobbed. “Same! Please don’t leave anything out.”  

Jodi took a deep breath, her grin widening. “Okay, so it actually started *last night.* Jeff was making dinner—steaks on the stove, my favorite Chilean wine chilling, the works. But here’s the kicker: while he’s finishing up the steaks, he’s got a video playing on the living room TV. A *very specific* kind of video.”  

Tara arched an eyebrow, already grinning. “Go on…”  

Jodi leaned closer, lowering her voice to draw them in. “It was *me,*” she said, a mischievous glint in her eye. “A little 106-second clip of me—let’s just say—taking care of myself. Playing on a loop.”  

Naomi’s jaw dropped, her eyes as wide as saucers. “Wait, wait, wait,” she stammered. “You *sent him a video* of you—”  

“Exactly,” Jodi interrupted, clearly enjoying Naomi’s reaction. “I sent it to him while I was at work yesterday, right before my big presentation. Kind of a ‘thanks for being amazing’ gift. And let me tell you—his reaction when I walked in the door last night?” She let out a low laugh, shaking her head. “It was *electric.*”  

Tara laughed, shaking her head. “Of course it was. You’re literally the queen of turning up the heat.”  

“So dinner was *incredible,*” Jodi continued, her voice warming as she recalled the details. “We talked, we laughed, and then, after dinner, he poured me another glass of wine and said, ‘I want to show you just how much I loved that video.’”  

Naomi’s face was practically glowing with curiosity. “What happened next?” she asked, her voice barely above a whisper.  

Jodi smirked, taking a sip of her margarita. “Well, let’s just say things got *very* steamy. Jeff loves to take his time, and last night was no exception. He shaved me—like, really took his time with it—and then he went *down.* Relentlessly.”  

Tara let out a low whistle, her grin widening. “That man deserves an award,” she said, reaching for another nacho.  

“He does,” Jodi agreed, laughing. “And here’s the best part—after we showered this morning, he *still* had energy left. I mean, who does that? So, naturally, we sixty-nined for a good forty minutes before I realized I was running insanely late.”  

Naomi’s cheeks were bright red, but her expression was a mix of shock and fascination. “I can’t believe you’re *so comfortable* talking about this!” she said, though there was no judgment in her tone—just awe.  

Jodi laughed, shrugging. “You said you wanted details, right? I’m just giving you what you asked for.”  

Tara leaned forward, her eyes narrowing slightly. “Wait, wait, wait—go back to the video. Is this the same one you sent him yesterday?”  

Jodi grinned. “Yup.”  

“And it was playing on the TV when you walked in?” Tara pressed, clearly intrigued.  

“Yup,” Jodi said again, her smirk widening. “And speaking of that video…” She pulled her phone out of her bag, holding it up. “If you’re curious, I *might* still have it saved.”  

Tara and Naomi both froze, their eyes darting to the phone in Jodi’s hand.  

“No way,” Tara said, her tone full of disbelief.  

“Are you serious?” Naomi squeaked, leaning closer.  

Jodi’s grin turned wicked as she waved the phone slightly. “What? You said you wanted no filter. So… do you want to see it or not?”  

Tara burst out laughing, shaking her head. “You’re *too much,* Jodi.”  

Naomi, on the other hand, looked torn between fascination and embarrassment. “I—I don’t know if I *should,*” she stammered, though her eyes lingered on the phone.  

Jodi chuckled, slipping the phone back into her bag. “Relax, ladies. I’m not actually going to show it to you… unless you *really* beg.”  

Tara rolled her eyes, laughing as she reached for her margarita. “You’re evil. Pure evil.”  

“And you love me for it,” Jodi shot back, grinning.  

Naomi shook her head, still looking dazed. “I feel like I need a cold shower after just *hearing* about your life,” she muttered, making Tara laugh even harder.    

---  
Jodi leaned back with a self-satisfied smirk, sliding her phone back into her bag. “Alright, I think that’s enough heat for one lunch,” she said, grabbing another nacho.  

But Naomi, sitting across the table, wasn’t done. She stared at Jodi, her cheeks still flushed but her eyes sparkling with something new—curiosity, excitement, or maybe just the sheer audacity of the moment.  

“Wait,” Naomi said, holding up a hand to stop Jodi.  

Jodi froze, her nacho halfway to her mouth. “Wait what?”  

Naomi took a deep breath, her voice trembling slightly but her determination clear. “I… I want to see the video.”  

The table went silent.  

Jodi blinked, lowering the nacho as she stared at Naomi in utter disbelief. “You’re joking.”  
Naomi shook her head, her ponytail swishing. “I’m not joking. You said we could beg, and I—I’m not begging, exactly, but… I really want to see it. Just out of curiosity!”  

Tara choked on her margarita, laughing so hard she had to grab a napkin. “Oh my God, *Naomi!*” she sputtered, her voice cracking as she tried to catch her breath. “You *did not* just ask to watch Jodi’s sex tape!”  

“It’s not a sex tape!” Naomi protested, her cheeks turning an even deeper shade of red. “It’s just… you know, her doing stuff. And she *offered*! I’m just calling her bluff.”  

Jodi’s jaw dropped as she stared at Naomi, her mouth opening and closing like a fish out of water. “I can’t believe this,” she muttered, half to herself. “Of all people to call me out—Naomi! Sweet, naive Naomi!”  

Naomi fidgeted with her straw, her face bright red but her eyes steady. “I mean… it’s not like it’s *weird,*” she said, her voice barely above a whisper. “We’re all women here, right? And I’ve never seen anything like that before, so… I guess I’m just curious.”  

Tara, wiping tears from her eyes, leaned back in the booth, her grin stretching ear to ear. “Jodi, this is *your* fault,” she said, pointing at her. “You set this whole thing up with your big, no-filter, gloating attitude. And now look where we are!”  

Jodi groaned, covering her face with her hands. “I was joking!” she said, her voice muffled. “I didn’t actually think anyone would *take me seriously!*”  

“Well, I did,” Naomi said, her voice small but firm.  

Jodi peeked at her through her fingers, still incredulous. “Naomi, you blush when someone says the word ‘butt.’ And now you’re asking to watch a video of *me* getting off?”  
Naomi shrugged helplessly, looking more embarrassed by the second. “I mean… yeah?” 

Tara burst out laughing again, shaking her head as she reached for another nacho. “I’m sorry, but this is the best thing I’ve seen all week,” she said, her voice still cracking with laughter.  

Jodi groaned again, finally letting her hands drop as she looked at Naomi with a mix of exasperation and amusement. “You’re unbelievable,” she said, shaking her head.  

Naomi squirmed in her seat, her hands gripping the edge of the table. “So… does that mean you’ll show it to me?” she asked, her voice barely above a whisper.  

Jodi stared at her for a long moment, her expression unreadable. Then, finally, she let out a long sigh, grabbing her phone.  

“Alright,” she said, unlocking it. “But you’re *never* going to live this down, Naomi.”  

Naomi gasped, her eyes lighting up with a mix of excitement and nerves. “Wait, really? You’ll show me?”  

Tara leaned forward, her grin devilish. “Oh, this is *happening.*”  

Jodi rolled her eyes, setting the phone on the table. “I can’t believe I’m doing this,” she muttered. “But fine. You wanted no filter? Here’s no filter.”  

Naomi leaned in, her hands clasped tightly in her lap as Jodi opened the video. “Oh my God,” she whispered, her voice trembling. “I can’t believe this is real.”  

“Neither can I,” Jodi muttered, hitting play.  

Tara laughed so hard she almost fell out of the booth.    
 
---  
Naomi leaned over the table, her hands clasped tightly in her lap as Jodi hit play. The video began, filling the small booth with soft, rhythmic sounds that were unmistakable, even on low. Naomi’s eyes widened, her gaze fixed on the screen like it held the secrets of the universe.  

Tara, initially biting back laughter, clapped a hand over her mouth, her shoulders shaking. But before she could make a sound, Jodi turned and shot her a sharp look—a silent warning that said, *Don’t you dare ruin this.*  

Tara froze, her eyebrows shooting up in surprise. She leaned back in her seat, raising her hands in surrender, though the glint of mischief in her eye didn’t fade.  

Jodi turned her attention back to Naomi, who was still watching the video with rapt attention. Her expression wasn’t scandalized or amused—just… curious. Intense. Like she was absorbing every detail.  

And then it hit Jodi. *Oh my God,* she thought, her lips twitching into a faint smile despite herself. *She’s watching this like it’s a documentary.*  

Naomi leaned forward slightly, her brow furrowing as if she were mentally cataloging every movement, every angle. Her eyes darted between the screen and Jodi, her curiosity practically radiating off her in waves.  

Jodi sat back, her amusement growing as she realized what was happening. For Naomi, this wasn’t some salacious moment to gossip about later—it was *educational.*  

Tara, however, wasn’t catching on. She opened her mouth to crack a joke, but Jodi gently raised a finger to her lips. “Shh,” she murmured softly, her voice barely audible.  

Tara’s jaw dropped. Her eyes darted between Jodi and Naomi, and for once, she stayed quiet, though her expression clearly screamed, *Are you kidding me?!*  

As the video came to an end, Naomi sat back slightly, her cheeks pink but her gaze steady. She looked thoughtful, as though she were trying to process what she’d just seen.  

“Well?” Jodi asked, her tone gentle. “What’d you think?”  

Naomi hesitated, biting her lip. “I… I think I need to watch it again,” she said finally, her voice quiet but earnest.  

Tara made a strangled noise, her hand flying to her forehead as though she were having an out-of-body experience. Jodi ignored her, leaning forward to reset the video.  

“Go ahead,” Jodi said, sliding the phone back toward Naomi. “Take your time.”  

Naomi nodded, her eyes glued to the screen as the video played for a second time. Tara’s eyes bulged, and she looked like she was about to burst, but one glance from Jodi kept her in check.  

When the video ended again, Naomi let out a small breath, her fingers brushing nervously against her lap. “That was… really interesting,” she said softly, her cheeks still bright red.  

Jodi smiled warmly, reaching for her phone. “Want me to send you a copy?” she asked casually.  

Tara’s head whipped toward Jodi so fast she nearly spilled her drink. “You’re *kidding*,” she hissed under her breath, her eyes as wide as saucers.  

Naomi’s jaw dropped slightly. “You’d *do* that?” she asked, her voice full of disbelief.  

“Sure,” Jodi said with a shrug, already pulling up the file transfer app. “You’re curious, right? It might help to have a reference for, you know, future research.”  

Naomi nodded slowly, her expression both grateful and overwhelmed. “I—yeah. That would be… really helpful, actually.”  

Tara stared at them both like they’d lost their minds but said nothing, her mouth opening and closing as Jodi sent the file.  

“There,” Jodi said, sliding her phone back into her bag as the notification popped up on Naomi’s phone. “Now it’s yours.”  

Naomi looked down at her phone, then back up at Jodi. “Thank you,” she said sincerely, her tone full of admiration.  

“You’re welcome,” Jodi said with a smile, grabbing her wallet. “Now, let’s pay and get back to work before someone starts asking why we’re taking two-hour lunches.”  

Tara finally found her voice as she tossed a few bills on the table. “I don’t even know who you are anymore,” she muttered, shaking her head as she slid out of the booth.  

Jodi laughed, slinging her bag over her shoulder. “Oh, come on, Tara. You know exactly who I am.”  

The three of them walked back to the office, Naomi clutching her phone like it held the answers to all of life’s mysteries, Tara muttering under her breath about how she was going to need therapy, and Jodi grinning to herself, knowing this would be a lunch none of them would ever forget.    

---  
About an hour after their eventful lunch, Jodi was deep into her email inbox when her phone buzzed on her desk. She glanced at the screen, already smirking when she saw Tara’s name pop up.  

**Tara:** *WTF was that all about???*  

Jodi bit back a laugh, her fingers flying across the keyboard of her phone.  

**Jodi:** *What part, exactly? The nachos, the video, or the part where I might have just changed Naomi’s life?*  

Tara’s reply came almost instantly.  

**Tara:** *The VIDEO. Jodi. You gave her a *copy*. What were you thinking??*  

Jodi rolled her eyes, leaning back in her chair as she typed her reply.  

**Jodi:** *I was thinking she needed it more than I did. Did you SEE the way she was watching? Like it was a TED Talk.*  

A few moments passed before Tara replied.  

**Tara:** *Yeah, no kidding. But WHY? Why was she so into it?? She looked like she was trying to solve a mystery.*  

Jodi paused, her smirk fading slightly as she thought back to Naomi’s wide-eyed, rapt expression and the quiet intensity in her gaze. She drummed her fingers on her desk, piecing together the puzzle in her mind. Then, she typed:  

**Jodi:** *Here’s my theory: Naomi’s had sex with two guys, and neither one of them had the faintest clue what to do with her.*  

Tara’s reply came in record time.  

**Tara:** *Omg. Brutal. Go on.*  

**Jodi:** *Think about it. She didn’t just watch that video, Tara. She studied it. Like she was trying to figure out what sex is actually supposed to *feel* like.*  

Jodi paused, chewing on her lip as she added another thought.  

**Jodi:** *And honestly? I don’t think she has a clue about her *own* body either. I’d bet good money she’s never explored enough to even know what works for her.*  

The little typing bubble appeared immediately, followed by Tara’s response:  

**Tara:** *You think? She’s so sweet and bubbly, though. How does she not know??*  

Jodi sighed, glancing at Naomi’s desk out of the corner of her eye. Naomi was focused on her monitor, her ponytail swishing slightly as she typed, oblivious to the conversation.  

**Jodi:** *That’s the thing—she’s sweet, but sweet doesn’t mean experienced. And if the guys she’s been with weren’t any good… how would she know what she’s missing?*  

Tara sent a row of shocked-face emojis before replying:  

**Tara:** *Wow. Okay. So basically, Naomi’s been having the sex equivalent of unseasoned chicken.*  

Jodi snorted, quickly covering her mouth to stifle her laugh.  

**Jodi:** *Exactly. And she doesn’t even realize it’s unseasoned, because no one’s ever shown her the good stuff.*  

Tara didn’t respond for a minute, but when she did, her text was unusually serious:  

**Tara:** *So, what you did today… that was you seasoning the chicken?*  

Jodi leaned back, a thoughtful look crossing her face. She hadn’t thought of it like that at first—it had been funny, a little awkward, and mostly an attempt to humor Naomi’s curiosity. But now, thinking back to the way Naomi had looked at her with genuine gratitude, like she’d just unlocked a door she didn’t know existed, Jodi realized Tara wasn’t entirely wrong.  

**Jodi:** *I guess so. Weird way to put it, but yeah. Maybe she needed that.*  

Tara’s next text was back to her usual snark:  

**Tara:** *Can’t believe you just casually handed her a roadmap to Pleasure Town. You’re either the best coworker or the worst one. Not sure which.*  

**Jodi:** *Best. Definitely best. Don’t forget it.*  

Tara sent back a laughing emoji, followed by:  

**Tara:** *Fine. But if she starts asking for homework assignments, I’m blaming you.*  

Jodi laughed quietly, shaking her head as she put her phone down. She glanced over at Naomi’s desk again, a faint smile tugging at her lips.  

Maybe she *had* just handed Naomi a roadmap. But if it helped her learn something about herself, if it helped her figure out what she deserved and what she wanted? Well, Jodi could live with that.    
---  
---  
Jodi leaned against her desk, tapping out a quick text to Tara while eyeing Naomi across the office, her ponytail bobbing as she worked.  

**Jodi:** *What do you think about inviting Naomi out for beers after work?*  

Tara’s response was almost immediate:  

**Tara:** *Naomi? Beers? Do we even know if she drinks?*  

**Jodi:** *Nope.*  

**Tara:** *So your big plan is to invite Little Miss Sparkling Water out for alcohol? Bold move, Jodi.*  

**Jodi:** *I don’t know! I thought it might be a good idea. She’s still kind of new here, and after lunch… well, maybe it’s a chance to talk more and loosen her up a bit.*  

**Tara:** *Loosen her up how, exactly? Because between the nachos and the video, I think we already loosened her brain today.*  

Jodi stifled a laugh, shaking her head as she typed back.  

**Jodi:** *Not like that, smartass. I mean socially. She’s sweet, but she’s so green. I feel like there’s a whole world she doesn’t even know about yet.*  

**Tara:** *You mean the world of beer and raunchy girl-talk?*  

**Jodi:** *Exactly.*  

Tara’s typing bubble popped up, paused, then disappeared for a moment before reappearing. Finally, her response came through:  

**Tara:** *Okay, fine, but do you think she’d even say yes? It’s kind of last minute. And you know she’s probably the type who needs three days’ notice to plan anything.*  

Jodi glanced at the clock, biting her lip. It was already mid-afternoon, and she wasn’t sure if Naomi was the spontaneous type. Still, it was worth a shot.  

**Jodi:** *She might say no, but if she does, no harm done. It’s just an invitation.*  

Tara sent back a shrug emoji.  

**Tara:** *Fair point. But why the sudden interest in doing this tonight? You usually have other plans—or Jeff.*  

Jodi sighed, glancing at her phone before typing:  

**Jodi:** *Jeff’s flying to London tonight for work. He’s probably already at the airport. So I figured it’s a good night to focus on other things, you know?*  

Tara’s response was quick.  

**Tara:** *Ahhh, I see. Jeff’s away, and suddenly you have time to mentor the baby bird.*  

**Jodi:** *Stop calling her that!*  

**Tara:** *What? She is! A little baby bird who just watched her first naughty “nature documentary.”*  

Jodi snorted, shaking her head as she typed back.  

**Jodi:** *You’re impossible. But seriously, what do you think? Should I ask her?*  

Tara’s typing bubble popped up again, lingering longer this time. Finally:  

**Tara:** *Do it. But if she says yes, I’m coming too. No way I’m missing out on whatever chaos this turns into.*  

Jodi grinned, already drafting a message to Naomi.  

**Jodi:** *Deal. But if you scare her off with your “baby bird” comments, I’m disowning you.*  

**Tara:** *Fair enough. Let me know what she says.*  

Jodi glanced over at Naomi, her fingers hovering over her keyboard. She had no idea how Naomi would react to the invitation—if she’d be flattered, nervous, or just plain confused—but there was only one way to find out.    

---  
Jodi glanced up from her desk, eyeing Naomi across the office. She looked her usual self—cheerful, focused, typing away—but Jodi couldn’t shake the image of her at lunch, so wide-eyed and eager. Deciding there was no harm in asking, Jodi picked up her phone and typed out a quick message.  

**Jodi:** *Hey, Naomi. Are you free after work for a beer? Just something casual.*  

She hit send, then casually glanced over at Naomi’s desk. The younger woman’s phone buzzed, and she picked it up, reading the message. Her reaction was instant: her face lit up like a Christmas tree.  

Jodi’s phone buzzed back almost immediately.  

**Naomi:** *OMG YES! I’d love to! I’ve got SO many questions for you. Thank you so much for inviting me!!!*  

Jodi blinked, laughing softly to herself as she typed back:  

**Jodi:** *Uh, sure! Glad you’re excited. Any place you want to go?*  

Naomi’s reply took a little longer this time, but when it came through, it left Jodi genuinely surprised.  

**Naomi:** *There’s this downtown brewpub I LOVE! They make their own beer, and their food menu is amazing. They have these loaded pretzel bites that are soooo good. Want to go there?*  

Jodi arched an eyebrow, leaning back in her chair. *Naomi has a favorite downtown brewpub?* That was… unexpected. She quickly typed out a reply.  

**Jodi:** *Didn’t peg you for a craft beer fan, Naomi. This is a little shocking. But yeah, sounds great!*  

Naomi’s response came in almost instantly.  

**Naomi:** *I know, right?! I don’t drink often, but when I do, I love places like this. Thank you SO much again for inviting me! I’m so excited!!*  

Jodi chuckled, shaking her head. She wasn’t sure why she’d assumed Naomi’s idea of beer would be something like cheap college lager, but clearly, there was more to her than met the eye.  

Before she could put her phone down, another thought struck her. She quickly fired off one more message.  

**Jodi:** *By the way, Tara wants to come, too. That cool with you?*  

The pause was brief before her phone buzzed again.  

**Naomi:** *Cool?! That’s AMAZING! The more, the merrier! I can’t wait!!!*  

Jodi looked up to see Naomi bouncing slightly in her chair, her enthusiasm palpable even from across the office. *Her cup really does runneth over,* Jodi thought with a laugh.    
  
Later, as they wrapped up their workday, Jodi and Tara walked to the door together, waiting for Naomi to finish up.  

“So,” Tara said, glancing at Jodi with a smirk. “How’d she take it?”  

“She nearly cried,” Jodi replied, shaking her head with a chuckle. “She’s so excited she might actually explode when we get there. Oh, and get this—she suggested this downtown brewpub. Apparently, she’s a fan of craft beer and loaded pretzel bites.”  

Tara stopped in her tracks, staring at Jodi. “*Naomi*? Craft beer? Since when?”  

“I know, right?” Jodi said, laughing. “Every time I think I have her figured out, she surprises me.”  

Naomi hurried over to them, her purse slung over her shoulder, practically vibrating with excitement. “Ready to go?” she asked, her smile so wide it practically stretched off her face.  

“Lead the way, Naomi,” Jodi said, motioning for her to take the lead.  

As they headed downtown, Naomi couldn’t stop talking—about the brewpub’s beer flights, the appetizers, how it had such a cool vibe. Tara gave Jodi a pointed look, her expression screaming, *Who is this person?*  

Jodi shrugged with a grin. “She’s full of surprises,” she whispered.  

By the time they arrived, Naomi was practically bouncing as she held the door open for them. “You’re going to LOVE this place!” she said, her voice full of excitement.  

Jodi and Tara exchanged a glance before stepping inside. If the lunch earlier had been entertaining, tonight was shaping up to be just as memorable—if not more so.    

---  
Jodi looked down at the menu, her fingers lightly drumming on the table. The brewpub was bustling, the warm glow of Edison bulbs reflecting off shiny wooden tables, the sound of laughter and clinking glasses filling the air. People crowded the bar, some chatting with the bartender about hops and malts like they were sommeliers. Jodi glanced at Tara, who was pretending to study the menu but clearly people-watching with interest.  

“So… beer flight?” Jodi said finally, looking up.  

Tara raised an eyebrow, a small smirk tugging at the corner of her lips. “When in Rome, right?”  

They both waved over the server and ordered beer flights, choosing a mix of lagers, IPAs, stouts, and ales. Jodi tried not to think too hard about the beer snobs at the bar. She was just here to enjoy herself.  

Naomi, meanwhile, was absolutely glowing. She’d settled into the booth like she was right at home, already sipping on a bright golden lager and beaming at her companions.  

“This place really is great,” Jodi said, glancing around. “You come here often?”  

Naomi nodded eagerly. “Not as often as I’d like, but yeah, it’s my go-to when I’m in the mood for good beer. Their food’s amazing, too—wait until you try the loaded pretzel bites!”  

Tara leaned forward slightly, her curiosity piqued. “Okay, Naomi, spill. You’ve got to explain something to me. How are *you,* of all people, such a craft beer fan? I mean, no offense, but you’re not exactly the first person I’d picture rattling off beer stats.”  

Naomi laughed, her cheeks flushing slightly. “I get that a lot,” she admitted. “It’s kind of random, but my college roommate was a huge beer nerd. She dragged me to all these brewpubs and breweries, and I ended up loving it. Especially the way different beers can pair with food—it’s like this whole other world I didn’t know existed.”  

Jodi tilted her head, impressed. “Huh. That’s… actually really cool.”  

Naomi grinned, holding up her pint. “This one’s my favorite here—it’s a honey blonde ale. It’s got this perfect balance of sweetness and a little bitterness, and it goes with *everything.* You guys have to try it.”  

Tara looked at Jodi, her eyebrow raised. “Okay, when did she get so cool?”  

“I have no idea,” Jodi murmured, staring at Naomi like she was an alien who had just revealed a secret double life.  

Naomi laughed, taking another sip of her beer. “I’m not cool,” she said, shaking her head. “I just really like what I like, you know?”  

Their flights arrived, the server setting down wooden trays lined with tiny glasses of beer. Jodi and Tara exchanged a glance before lifting their first glasses, each taking tentative sips.  

“So?” Naomi asked, watching them with eager curiosity. “What do you think?”  

Jodi licked her lips, nodding slowly. “Not bad,” she said, swirling the amber liquid in her glass like she actually knew what she was doing. “This one’s smooth. Kind of malty.”  

Tara set her glass down, her eyes narrowing thoughtfully. “Mine’s got a little bite to it,” she said, leaning closer to inspect the menu. “Hoppy, I guess? Or is that what they call bitter?”  

“Exactly!” Naomi said, nodding enthusiastically. “That’s the hops—you’ve got a West Coast IPA. They’re known for being super hoppy and piney.”  

Jodi raised an eyebrow. “Piney?” she repeated, glancing at her own glass.  

Naomi grinned. “Yeah, like, kind of resinous, like the smell of pine trees. It’s weird, but once you taste it, you can’t un-taste it.”  

Jodi took another sip, narrowing her eyes slightly as she focused. “Huh. I think I get what you mean.”  

Tara shook her head, laughing softly. “I can’t believe we’re sitting here learning about beer from Naomi. This is… unexpected.”  

Naomi shrugged, her smile shy but proud. “Hey, there’s more to me than sparkling water,” she said with a playful wink.  

Jodi laughed, raising her tiny glass. “Well, here’s to that. Cheers, Naomi.”  

“Cheers!” Naomi said brightly, clinking her glass against theirs.  

As they sipped their beers and listened to Naomi rattle off her knowledge about malts, hops, and pairing suggestions, Jodi couldn’t help but feel a little in awe. Naomi might have started as their sweet, naive coworker, but tonight, she was holding her own in a way that was both surprising and kind of inspiring.  

“Alright, Naomi,” Tara said after a while, leaning back in her seat with a grin. “You win this round. I’m officially fascinated. You’ve got layers, and I’m here for it.”  

Naomi blushed, beaming as she sipped her beer. Jodi chuckled, shaking her head. “Tara’s right,” she said. “You’ve definitely got layers, Naomi. And tonight? I think we’re just scratching the surface.”  

---

Jodi leaned back in her seat, sipping one of the darker beers in her flight, and smirked at Naomi. “Speaking of scratching the surface,” she said, her tone dripping with mischief, “how come you were so fascinated watching me get off on that tiny phone video recording earlier?”  

Naomi froze mid-sip of her honey blonde ale, her eyes going wide. Her cheeks flushed bright red—so red, in fact, that Jodi wondered if it was possible for someone to blush harder than Naomi was right now.  

“I—uh…” Naomi stammered, her voice barely above a whisper. “I’ve just… never seen anything like it.”  

Tara, already grinning, leaned forward, her eyes gleaming with curiosity. “Wait,” she said, tilting her head, “do you mean literally or figuratively?”  

Naomi bit her lip, her blush deepening as she looked between Jodi and Tara. “Literally,” she admitted finally, her voice so quiet they had to strain to hear it.  

Tara’s jaw dropped, and she let out a short laugh, shaking her head in disbelief. “Wait, wait, wait—you’ve *never* seen anything like that before? Like, not even on your own phone? No… ‘personal research,’ as they say?”  

Naomi shook her head quickly, her hands gripping her pint glass like it was a lifeline. “No! I mean, I’ve seen, like, *scenes* in movies or whatever, but… not like that. Not… real, and not… someone I actually know.”  

Jodi’s lips curved into a knowing smile, and she rested her chin on her hand, watching Naomi with amusement. “That’s what I thought,” she said, nodding. “That’s why I sent you a copy.”  

Naomi’s head snapped up, her wide eyes locking onto Jodi’s. “You did?”

Jodi took a long sip of her drink, staring at him over the rim of her glass. She still wasn’t entirely sure if he was bluffing or not. But at this point? She wasn’t about to call him on it again.  

Mike, meanwhile, was *thriving.*  

He leaned back in his seat, completely at ease, knowing he was getting away with murder—and that there was absolutely *nothing* she could do about it.  

And for the first time in a long time, Jodi had no comeback.    

---  
Jodi leaned forward, narrowing her eyes as she studied him carefully. “Alright,” she said slowly, her tone measured. “Let’s just assume for a second that you’re serious.”  

Mike didn’t flinch. He just grinned at her, his smug confidence radiating across the table.  

“What does that *give* you?” Jodi asked, gesturing vaguely toward him. “What’s the *benefit* here?”  

Mike didn’t answer immediately. Instead, he waggled the fingers of his right hand in her direction, a playful little wave that made her want to both laugh and throw her drink at him.  

“Revenge,” he said, his voice calm, but with an undeniable edge of amusement. “That’s my primary motive.”  

Jodi blinked. “Revenge? For *what?*”  

Mike raised an eyebrow. “For every time you’ve embarrassed me. For every time you’ve teased me, tormented me, or made me the butt of the joke.” He smirked, leaning back in his seat. “And second? I’m having the *time of my life.*”  

Jodi groaned, rubbing her temples. “Jesus Christ.”  

“Oh, don’t act so shocked,” Mike said, his grin widening. “You’re the one who started this whole thing. I *never* imagined the lengths you and your little girlfriends would go to just to figure out what my cock looked like.”  

Jodi’s mouth opened to respond, but no words came out.  

Mike shrugged, still smirking. “And now here we are. You’ve been obsessing over it, *researching* it, and now? Now, I’m sitting here, on full display.”  

Jodi’s jaw tightened, her eyes narrowing further.  

“Oh, and fully erect, too,” Mike added casually, his grin turning wicked.  

Jodi let out an exasperated breath. “For *fuck’s* sake, Mike—”  

But Mike wasn’t done.  

“And the best part?” he continued, his voice dropping slightly as he leaned forward, resting his elbows on the table. “The best part is that all you’d have to do to confirm the truth of your little fantasy is get down under the table and have a look-see.”  

Jodi froze, her heart pounding.  

Mike’s grin widened as he studied her reaction. “It must be *mortifying* for you,” he said, his tone dripping with amusement. “To be so close to confirming your mental image… and yet, so far.”  

Jodi stared at him, completely at a loss for words. For once, she had no quip, no clever retort, no way to flip the script back in her favor.  

And Mike? Mike *knew* it.  

He sat back, his arms crossed, looking like a man who had just won the world’s most ridiculous chess match.  

“Well?” he said, raising an eyebrow. “What’s it gonna be, Jodi?”  

Jodi exhaled sharply, her mind racing. She *hated* that he had her cornered like this. But damn it, she couldn’t deny it—he’d played her perfectly.  

For now, she had no choice but to sit there and take it.  

And Mike? Mike was going to savor every second.    

---

Mike leaned in, his smug grin growing wider as he moved his right hand below the table, out of Jodi’s sight. His fingers flexed, his wrist shifted slightly—and the subtle movement was enough to send Jodi’s already racing mind spiraling further.  

“Alright,” Mike said smoothly, his voice low and teasing. “Let’s play a game. Guess what I’m gripping right now.”  

Jodi folded her arms, giving him a skeptical look. “You’re bluffing.”  

Mike tilted his head, his grin unwavering. “Am I?”  

“Yes,” Jodi said firmly, though the slight waver in her voice betrayed her uncertainty. “There’s no way you’re actually doing this.”  

Mike shrugged, the picture of nonchalance. “What would persuade you?”  

Jodi leaned forward slightly, narrowing her eyes. She wasn’t about to let him get away with this without a challenge. “Precum,” she said matter-of-factly.  

Mike blinked, his grin faltering for just a split second before he recovered, the glint in his eye turning downright wicked.  

“Precum?” he echoed, his tone playful. “You want proof that I’m leaking?”  

Jodi smirked, leaning back in her seat. “That’s right. If you’re really hard, and if you’re really doing what you’re claiming to do… there should be *evidence.*”  

Mike raised an eyebrow, his hand still hidden below the table. “Evidence, huh?” He leaned in slightly, lowering his voice. “What’s the matter, Jodi? Can’t smell it?”  

Jodi snorted, rolling her eyes. “No, Mike, I can’t smell your *dick juice.*”  

Mike chuckled, shaking his head. “That’s a shame. It’s probably dripping right now.”  

Jodi gave him an unimpressed look. “You’re so full of shit.”  

“Am I?” Mike said, his grin turning devious. He drummed his fingers on the table with his left hand, as if he had all the time in the world. “Tell you what, Jodi—if you want to see my *leakage,* all you have to do is ask.”  

Jodi groaned, dragging a hand down her face. “Mike—”  

“What?” Mike interrupted, still grinning. “You’re the one who wanted proof.”  

Jodi stared at him, her mind torn between calling his bluff and walking away from this ridiculous conversation entirely. She didn’t know what was worse: the fact that he was *this* committed to the bit, or the fact that she was actually starting to wonder if he was telling the truth.  

“Nope,” she finally said, shaking her head. “Not playing this game.”  

Mike smirked, his eyes glinting with amusement. “That’s what I thought.”  

For the rest of the meal, Mike kept his hand under the table, his smug grin firmly in place. And Jodi? She had to sit there, pretending she wasn’t absolutely dying of curiosity.    

---  
The server dropped the check on the table with a polite smile, and Mike casually reached for his wallet. His right hand emerged from under the table, and Jodi’s heart *leapt*.  

She tried *very hard* to keep her face neutral, but her eyes darted to his hand like a magnet. She *had* to know.  

Mike, ever the showman, moved slowly, deliberately. His fingers slid over the leather wallet, then pulled out his credit card with a flick of his wrist. The entire time, his hand was in plain view.  

And there it was.  

A faint sheen, barely visible but unmistakable.  

Jodi’s breath caught. Her mind raced. *Is that—*  

She squinted, her lips pressing into a thin line as she tried not to make it obvious that she was studying his hand. The shine was subtle, but it caught the light in just the right way.  

*Oh my God. That’s not sweat.*  

Mike must have noticed her scrutiny, because he smirked, sliding the credit card into the little black folder. “Something on your mind, Jodi?”  

Her eyes snapped up to meet his, and she quickly straightened in her seat, forcing a casual expression. “Nope,” she said, though her voice was tighter than usual.  

Mike leaned back, folding his arms, his grin widening. “Uh-huh.”
