Jodi clenched her jaw, her gaze flicking to his hand one more time before she forced herself to look away.  

It was *true.* He hadn’t been bluffing.  

Mike had been sitting across from her, fully erect, *leaking*, and having the time of his life—and she’d *let it happen.*  

She didn’t know whether to be horrified, impressed, or both.  

Mike, meanwhile, was *thriving*.  

As the server came to collect the check, Mike casually wiped his right hand on his napkin, the motion so nonchalant it was almost maddening.  

Jodi’s stomach flipped. *Was that deliberate? Did he just wipe off—*  

She groaned inwardly, hating how much he’d gotten into her head.  

When the server walked away, Mike leaned forward slightly, resting his chin on his hand. “So,” he said, his tone light but teasing, “you seem a little distracted.”  

Jodi gave him a pointed look. “You’re *insufferable.*”  

“And yet,” Mike said, grinning, “here we are.”  

Jodi rolled her eyes, grabbing her bag. “I hope you’re proud of yourself.”  

“Oh, I am,” Mike said, standing up and adjusting his slacks with *obnoxious* confidence. “I’m very proud.”  

Jodi shook her head, following him toward the door. She didn’t say it out loud, but in the back of her mind, one thought lingered.  

*Incredible.*    

---  
Jodi was still processing. Still trying to wrap her head around the *fact* that Mike had been telling the truth, that she had just spent the last several minutes sitting across from him while he… *enjoyed himself*.  

And worse? That she had been *entranced* by it.  

Mike, of course, *knew it.*  

He slid his right hand back under the table, slow and deliberate. Jodi’s breath hitched slightly, her body tensing.  

Then—just the *barest* movement.  

A shift in his right shoulder. A subtle, rhythmic motion. Nothing dramatic. Nothing too obvious. Just enough.  

Jodi’s mouth went *dry.*  

She felt it first in her throat, then in her stomach, then *lower.*  

Mike leaned in slightly, his eyes locked onto hers, his voice smooth as silk.  

“You are *completely* enthralled, aren’t you?”  

Jodi’s lips parted. She tried to think of something—*anything*—to say that would put her back in control, that would turn the tables back in her favor.  

But her mind was *blank.*  

She swallowed once. Then again.  

Then, in the quietest, most *honest* voice she had ever used around him, she admitted:  

“Yes.”  

Mike’s grin was *devastating*.  

He didn’t need to say a word.  

Jodi had *lost.*  

And she had *never* been more aware of it.    

---  
Jodi stared at Mike, her throat still dry, her heart pounding in her chest. She was *completely* caught off guard by how far he’d taken this little game—and by how much she was enjoying it.  

Mike, leaning in close, his right hand still under the table, let his grin widen just enough to make her stomach flip. “So,” he said, his voice low, teasing, “how far am I allowed to go?”  

Jodi blinked, her lips parting slightly. For a moment, she wasn’t sure if she should answer—or if she *could*.  

“Excuse me?” she managed, her voice just a little breathless.  

“You heard me,” Mike said smoothly, tilting his head. “You’ve been teasing me for *years*, Jodi. But now? I’m the one in control. So I’m asking…”  

He shifted his shoulder slightly, and her eyes flicked to the movement before snapping back to his face.  

“How far am I allowed to go?” he repeated, his tone playful but laced with something deeper.  

Jodi bit her lip, her mind racing. *How far was she going to let this go?*  

Her instincts told her to push back, to fire off a sarcastic comment, to do *anything* to regain the upper hand. But she didn’t. Instead, she leaned forward, resting her elbows on the table, and smirked.  

“Why don’t you show me?” she said, her voice calm but daring.  

Mike raised an eyebrow, his grin turning even more wicked. “Oh, you’d *love* that, wouldn’t you?”  

Jodi’s smirk widened. “Maybe.”  

Mike chuckled, shaking his head slightly. “You know, you’re *really* enjoying this more than I expected.”  

“Am I?” Jodi asked, tilting her head innocently.  

“Yeah,” Mike said, leaning closer, his voice dropping. “I can see it in your eyes. You’re *thrilled* that I’m finally playing your game. That I’m not just sitting here, letting you run circles around me.”  

Jodi’s smirk faltered—just a little. Because, damn it, he wasn’t wrong.  

Mike caught it immediately, his grin widening. “See? You love it.”  

Jodi rolled her eyes, trying to play it cool. “I wouldn’t go that far.”  

“Oh, I think you would,” Mike said, his tone dripping with confidence. He gestured toward himself with his free hand. “Look at me, Jodi. You can’t stop. You’re *enthralled.*”  

Jodi narrowed her eyes, leaning in closer. “You’re so full of yourself.”  

“And you’re so *curious,*” Mike countered, not missing a beat.  

Jodi opened her mouth to retort, but the words died on her lips. Because, once again, he wasn’t wrong.  

Mike leaned back, studying her with a smug, knowing look. “So,” he said, his voice lighter now, “what’s your next move?”  

Jodi blinked. “My *next move?*”  

“Yeah,” Mike said, shrugging casually. “You’re the one who started this. Don’t tell me you’re out of ideas now.”  

Jodi’s jaw tightened, her competitive streak flaring to life. “Oh, I’m *far* from out of ideas,” she said, her tone cool.  

Mike smirked. “Good. Because I’m *just getting started.*”  

The tension crackled between them, sharp and electric, as they stared each other down.  

Jodi didn’t know how this game was going to end, but one thing was certain—she was *loving* every second of it.    
 
---  
Jodi held Mike’s gaze, her pulse thrumming in her ears. She was *supposed* to be the one in control. She was *supposed* to be the one making *him* squirm. And yet, here she was—utterly *riveted* by his audacity, barely holding onto her composure.  

Mike, of course, *knew it.*  

He leaned in just slightly, his voice smooth, teasing, and completely self-assured.  

“You can look now,” he said, tilting his head, “or you can wait thirty seconds for some *proof.*”  

Jodi blinked, her breath catching.  

Mike smirked. “Your call.”  

Jodi scoffed, crossing her arms. “Oh, *proof?*” she echoed, arching an eyebrow. “What, exactly, am I supposed to be waiting for?”  

Mike’s smirk deepened. “A handful,” he said simply. “For you to *examine.*”  

Jodi’s stomach flipped.  

Her fingers gripped the edge of the table.  

Her brain told her to *shut this down*, to fire back with something cutting, something that would knock him down a peg. But her body? Her body betrayed her—because she hesitated.  

Mike *saw* the hesitation. And that was *everything.*  

His eyes gleamed, dark with amusement. “Thirty seconds,” he reminded her, his voice low. “Unless you want to check now.”  

Jodi’s lips parted, but no words came out.  

Mike tilted his head, *mocking* her silence. “No comeback?” he asked, grinning. “That’s a first.”  

Jodi clenched her jaw, her pride warring with something far more dangerous—curiosity.  

Because, damn it, what if he *wasn’t* bluffing?  

Mike chuckled, his voice pure satisfaction. “You’re dying to know, aren’t you?”  

Jodi exhaled sharply, shaking her head. “You’re insufferable.”  

Mike just grinned. “And *you love it.*”  

Jodi hated that he was right.  

But she *hated* even more that she was so, so close to knowing the truth…  

And yet, still *so far.*    

---

Mike leaned back in the booth, his shoulders dropping slightly, his body settling into an almost lazy posture. But his eyes—those *damn* eyes—were locked onto Jodi’s with a quiet intensity that made her pulse quicken.  

His breathing hitched, just barely, and then it grew ragged—subtle, but impossible to ignore in the charged silence between them.  

For a single, searing second, his gaze pierced hers.  

“Last chance,” he whispered, his voice low, rough, and laced with both a challenge and a promise.  

Jodi’s breath caught in her throat.  

Her mind screamed at her to stay in control, to laugh this off, to *not give him the satisfaction*. But her body? Her body had other ideas.  

Her fingers fidgeted against the table, her heart pounding. She swallowed hard, trying to steady herself, but the temptation was *all-consuming*.  

For years—*years*—she had teased Mike, toyed with him, built him up in her mind as this quiet, unassuming guy she could always outmaneuver. And yet here he was, utterly unbothered, flipping the tables on her so completely she didn’t know whether to be impressed or infuriated.  

But the *curiosity*—oh, the *curiosity*—was eating her alive.  

Her brain went to strange places, grasping for analogies to make sense of what she was feeling.  

*What am I even about to see?* she wondered, her breath coming shallow now. *Is this going to be like visiting the Vatican for the first time and laying eyes on Michelangelo’s work?*  

The thought made her stomach flip. She’d spent so much time building this up—turning Mike’s private parts into some kind of mythical, untouchable masterpiece.  

And now, she was *this close* to seeing it.  

*What if it doesn’t live up to the hype?*  

*What if it does?*  

Mike must have caught the flicker of indecision in her eyes, because his smirk softened into something almost… knowing.  

“Come on, Jodi,” he said softly, his voice a quiet taunt. “You’ve wanted this for so long. Don’t stop now.”  

Her jaw tightened, her pride screaming at her to resist. But her hands twitched, her eyes darting downward before snapping back up to his face.  

Mike tilted his head slightly, his grin widening. “What’s it gonna be?”  

Jodi exhaled sharply, her chest tight with the weight of her own anticipation.  

She didn’t know what she was about to do.  

But she *did* know one thing:  

She was either about to win *big*…  

Or lose everything.  

---

Jodi’s fingers tightened against the edge of the table, her heart pounding as she *almost*—*almost*—let herself go through with it.  

But at the last second, something shifted inside her.  

She exhaled sharply and leaned back in her seat, crossing her arms, shaking her head with a small, knowing smile.  

Mike smirked. “Backing down?”  

Jodi studied him, her amusement tinged with something softer—something almost *wistful.*  

“You know,” she said, her voice quieter now, “I probably should have been more honest about this *years* ago.”  

Mike blinked, thrown just slightly off balance. “About what?”  

Jodi gestured vaguely between them. “This. *Us.* The fact that instead of playing mind games, instead of going behind your back trying to figure you out, I could’ve just…” She sighed, trailing her fingers along the rim of her glass. “I don’t know. *Asked.*”  

Mike tilted his head, studying her. “And yet, here we are.”  

Jodi let out a short laugh. “Yeah. Here we are.”  

A pause stretched between them—not tense, not awkward, but heavy with something unspoken.  

Mike leaned forward slightly, resting his elbows on the table. “So?”  

Jodi raised an eyebrow. “So, what?”  

Mike smirked. “Are you gonna ask now?”  

Jodi exhaled, her lips pressing together, considering. Then, she met his gaze with a small, confident smile. “No.”  

Mike’s eyebrows lifted slightly. “No?”  

Jodi shook her head. “Because I don’t *need* to.” She leaned in just enough to make him tilt toward her, her voice dropping to a low murmur. “I already know everything I need to.”  
Mike let out a quiet chuckle, shaking his head. “Oh, do you?”  

Jodi smirked, picking up her drink. “I do.” She took a slow sip, savoring the weight of the moment. “And for the record?” She set her glass down, fixing him with a look that was equal parts teasing and *completely* sure of herself.  

“I *won.*”  

Mike let out a *soft huff* of laughter, sitting back, shaking his head. “God, I *hate* you.”  

Jodi grinned. “No, you don’t.”  

Mike smirked, grabbing his drink. “No. I don’t.”  

And just like that, the game reset.  

Neither of them had lost.  

Neither had *really* won.  

But Jodi knew, without a doubt, that the next round?  

Was going to be *even better.*    

---  
Much later that night, Jodi lay in bed, her phone resting on her stomach, her thoughts still tangled from the last few days.  

She exhaled sharply, biting her lip. She wasn’t *guilty*, exactly—Jeff had always known she was a *shameless* flirt, and they both had a pretty open and trusting relationship. But still…  

She owed him *something.*  

After a moment of hesitation, she grabbed her phone and typed:  

**Jodi:** *Hey. So… I may have gotten into some mischief the past few days.*  

She hit send before she could overthink it.  

The three dots appeared almost immediately.  

Then—  

**Jeff:** *Shocker. What kind of mischief?*  

Jodi huffed a soft laugh, shaking her head. *Of course* he wasn’t going to let her off that easy.  

**Jodi:** *The kind where I maybe spent a little too much time making Mike’s life a nightmare. And then he turned the tables on me.*  

**Jeff:** *…I’m listening.*  

Jodi sighed, staring at the ceiling. *No turning back now.*  

**Jodi:** *Okay, full disclosure—I may have been messing with him about his size, but then it kind of spiraled. He played me back. Hard. And I may have gotten in *way* over my head.*  

The dots reappeared.  

**Jeff:** *Did you have fun?*  

Jodi blinked, caught slightly off guard.  

She thought about it. About every ridiculous moment, every push and pull, every time she’d almost *lost* but somehow came out grinning.  

**Jodi:** *…Yeah. I did.*  

A beat.  

Then—  

**Jeff:** *Did you stay out of trouble?*  

Jodi smirked.  

**Jodi:** *Define "trouble."*  

**Jeff:** *Jodi.*  

Jodi laughed, rolling onto her side.  

**Jodi:** *Fine. Nothing serious. Just an absurd amount of mind games and tension and a whole lot of power struggles.*  

**Jeff:** *Sounds like you had the time of your life.*  

Jodi smiled, staring at the screen.  

**Jodi:** *You know me too well.*  

**Jeff:** *That’s why I love you.*  

Jodi felt a soft warmth bloom in her chest.  

**Jodi:** *I love you too. Get home safe, okay?*  

**Jeff:** *Always.*  

She locked her phone, exhaling as she stared up at the ceiling.  

Yeah. She’d had *fun.*  

And she had a *feeling* that this wasn’t over.  

Not by a long shot.    

---

A full week had passed since Jeff had been gone, and the moment he walked through the door, Jodi practically *radiated* energy.  

“Welcome home,” she said, wrapping her arms around his neck and pulling him in for a deep kiss before he even got a chance to set his bag down.  

Jeff chuckled against her lips, holding her close. “Miss me?”  

Jodi pulled back just enough to give him a look. “You have *no idea.*”  

Jeff smirked, brushing a strand of hair from her face. “Oh, I think I do.”  

Jodi sighed dramatically, stepping back and stretching. “Okay, so, here’s my plan for tonight: I want food. I want a drink. I want sex. And *maybe* we should go get some cookies for my work potluck.”  

Jeff nodded, unbothered. “Sounds reasonable.” He kissed her forehead. “What order are we doing this in?”  

Jodi tapped a finger against her chin, considering. “Well… if we have sex *first*, then we’ll be all relaxed and lazy and probably just order takeout.”  

Jeff nodded. “That checks out.”  

“But if we eat *first*, then we might have *too* much food and not have the *energy* for sex.”  

Jeff smirked. “That would be a disaster.”  

“Exactly,” Jodi said, sighing dramatically. “And if we *drink* first…”  

Jeff raised an eyebrow. “That’s just asking for chaos.”  

Jodi grinned. “You love chaos.”  

Jeff gave her a knowing look. “*Controlled* chaos.”  

Jodi laughed, grabbing his hand and tugging him toward the kitchen. “Alright, here’s the plan: We eat *light*, grab a drink *to go*, and pick up cookies while *heavily* implying to the store cashier that we’re about to go home and have sex.”  

Jeff chuckled. “So you *want* to traumatize some poor bakery worker?”  

Jodi smirked. “It’s my way of giving back to the community.”  

Jeff shook his head, setting his bag down. “Fine. Light food, drinks, cookies, and then sex. But if we get *too* into the drinks and end up forgetting the cookies, I take no responsibility.”  

Jodi gasped dramatically. “As if I’d ever forget *dessert.*”  

Jeff just grinned, grabbing his keys. “Let’s go before you change your mind and rearrange the entire night.”  

Jodi looped her arm through his. “I *promise* I won’t change my mind.”  

(They were absolutely going to change their minds.)  

But that was half the fun.    
 
---  
The car smelled of sesame oil and roasted seaweed as Jeff and Jodi split their second roll of gimbap, the perfect light dinner on the go. Jeff balanced his steering with one hand while the other held a piece of the tightly rolled rice, veggies, and meat combo.  

“God, I missed this,” Jodi sighed, popping a bite into her mouth as she watched the city lights blur by.  

“Korean food or me?” Jeff teased, glancing over at her.  

“Both,” Jodi said with a cheeky grin, licking a bit of soy sauce from her thumb. “But right now? This gimbap is winning.”  

Jeff chuckled. “Fair enough.”  

For a moment, they drove in companionable silence, the kind that only came from being completely at ease with each other. But as they got closer to the bar, Jodi fidgeted in her seat, her fingers tapping lightly against the to-go box in her lap.  

Jeff noticed immediately. “Alright, spill,” he said, glancing at her again. “What’s rattling around in that head of yours?”  

Jodi sighed, setting the box down and twisting slightly to face him. “Okay, so… there’s something I need to tell you. Something I *did* while you were gone.”  

Jeff raised an eyebrow but kept his tone light. “You didn’t burn the house down, did you?”  

“No, but it might’ve been close,” Jodi quipped, then shook her head. “No, seriously. I want to be transparent with you about this.”  

“Alright,” Jeff said, his voice calm. “I’m listening.”  

Jodi took a deep breath, her fingers fidgeting again. “So… I may have spent a lot of time messing with Mike while you were gone.”  

Jeff’s lips twitched, trying to suppress a smile. “Define ‘messing with.’”  

Jodi groaned, leaning back against the seat. “You *know* how I am. It started as harmless teasing—just me giving him crap about his… you know, *size.*”  

Jeff raised an eyebrow. “Uh-huh.”  

“And then,” Jodi continued, her words picking up speed, “it kind of spiraled. He started firing back, and it turned into this whole ridiculous game of one-upmanship, and—”  

“Let me guess,” Jeff interrupted, smirking. “He got the better of you?”  

Jodi’s mouth opened to protest, but then she sighed, crossing her arms. “Okay, *yes.* He turned the tables on me. And I *let* him. And it was—” She hesitated, biting her lip. “It was fun, Jeff. Stupid, ridiculous fun. But… I should’ve been honest with you about it from the start.”  

Jeff was quiet for a moment, his face unreadable. Then, he reached over and gently patted her knee. “You didn’t run off to Vegas with him, right?”  

Jodi snorted. “No.”  

“Then we’re good,” Jeff said simply, his tone light.  

Jodi blinked, caught off guard. “Wait, that’s it? You’re not… mad?”  

Jeff chuckled, shaking his head. “Jodi, you’ve been teasing Mike for years. You’re not exactly hiding your playfulness. And I trust you.”  

Jodi’s shoulders relaxed, but her face was still hesitant. “Even when I get a little… carried away?”  

Jeff glanced at her, his eyes warm. “Especially when you get carried away. It’s part of what makes you *you.*”  

Jodi let out a breath she didn’t realize she’d been holding, her lips curving into a small smile. “Thanks. I just… I didn’t want you to think I was keeping secrets.”  

Jeff smiled, turning into the parking lot of the bar. “You’re good, babe. But I have one question.”  

“Yeah?”  

Jeff parked the car, unbuckling his seatbelt as he turned to her with a mischievous glint in his eye. “Did you win?”  

Jodi laughed, her head tilting back against the seat. “I think it’s safe to say we called it a draw.”  

Jeff smirked. “Sounds about right. Come on, let’s grab that growler. You can tell me more about how you *almost* outsmarted Mike.”  

Jodi grinned, following him out of the car. “Oh, I *definitely* outsmarted him—just not the way you think.”    
---  
---

Jeff leaned against the car, arms crossed, watching Jodi with that calm, assessing look he always had when she was confessing something *particularly* ridiculous.  

“So,” he said, voice even, “were either one of you naked?”  

Jodi pressed her lips together for a second, then sighed. “Yeah.”  

Jeff arched an eyebrow. “And I’m guessing he… *finished?*”  

Jodi ran a hand through her hair, exhaling sharply. “Probably.”  

Jeff let out a quiet chuckle, shaking his head. “That was his power play, wasn’t it?”  

Jodi looked at him, a little startled by how easily he’d pinpointed it. “Yeah,” she admitted. “I mean, I *thought* I was winning. But every time I tried to push things further, he just… let it happen. Until, well… he popped.”  

Jeff nodded, his smirk deepening. “So you played chicken, and *he* was the one who never swerved.”  

Jodi groaned. “God, that’s exactly what happened.”  

Jeff reached over and tapped her chin with his knuckle. “You love getting into these little games, but every once in a while, you meet someone just as stubborn as you.”  

Jodi scoffed, nudging his arm. “Shut up and buy me a drink.”  

They headed inside the bar, the cozy space buzzing with low conversation and the scent of hops and wood polish. Jodi beelined for the counter and, without hesitation, ordered a honey-infused lager.  

Jeff, still deciding on his own drink, raised an eyebrow at her choice. “*Honey*? That’s new.”  

Jodi shrugged, glancing at the taps. “I dunno, it sounded good.”  

Jeff gave her a look. “Where did *that* come from?”  

Jodi hesitated just a second too long before responding, which made Jeff smirk. “Oh,” he said, catching on immediately. “*That* pause means there’s a story. Spill.”  

Jodi sighed dramatically, turning to him. “I went drinking with a *very* naive coworker.”  

Jeff’s eyes gleamed with amusement. “Oh, this *has* to be good.”  

Jodi smirked, grabbing her beer as it was slid across the counter. “Oh, it *was*.”  

She took a sip, licking a bit of foam from her lip, before glancing at him. “You’re gonna love this one.”  

Jeff picked up his own beer, nodding for her to continue. “I’ve got all night.”    
---  
---  
Jodi took a sip of her honey lager, pressing her lips together, already feeling the giggles threatening to bubble up. *Okay, keep it together,* she told herself.  

Jeff, meanwhile, leaned against the bar, his beer in hand, watching her like a man who *knew* he was about to be entertained. “Alright,” he said, smirking, “start from the top.”  

Jodi exhaled sharply, schooling her expression into something *semi*-serious. “Okay. So. It all started with my video.”  

Jeff raised an eyebrow. “That’s never a boring sentence.”  

Jodi shook her head. “Nope. And this one *really* wasn’t.”  

She took another sip of beer, steadying herself before continuing. “So, my coworker—Naomi—she’s, uh… let’s just say *incredibly* naive.”  

“How naive?” Jeff asked, amused.  

Jodi set her beer down and held up a hand. “Like, *church camp* naive.”  

Jeff whistled. “Damn.”  

“Right?” Jodi laughed, already struggling to keep a straight face. “So we’re out drinking, and she’s asking *questions.* Like, *a lot* of questions. And, you know me, I’m all about education.”  

Jeff smirked. “Oh, I *know* you are.”  

Jodi grinned, then cleared her throat. “So I may have… *gifted her a video.*”  

Jeff tilted his head. “Define ‘gifted.’”  

Jodi bit her lip, trying not to laugh. “I *sent* her a video. Of *me*.”  

Jeff blinked. “Of *you*?”  

Jodi nodded, struggling to contain herself. “Yep.”  

Jeff let out a short laugh, shaking his head. “Hold on. You sent your *naive* coworker a *personal* demonstration?”  

Jodi covered her face with one hand, laughing. “I *thought* it would be *educational!*”  

Jeff groaned, rubbing his temples. “Jesus, Jodi.”  

Jodi peeked at him through her fingers, her shoulders shaking. “Oh, it gets *so much worse.*”  

Jeff took a slow sip of his beer. “I don’t even know how that’s possible, but okay. Continue.”  

Jodi inhaled deeply, trying to calm herself. “So she watches it. And she’s just sitting there, like, *analyzing* it. *Silently.* Like she’s watching a goddamn *documentary.*”  

Jeff nearly choked on his drink. “No.”  

Jodi *lost it*, slapping the bar as she laughed. “YES. She’s all serious, like she’s *studying technique*, and I’m sitting there thinking, *oh my God, what have I done?!*”  

Jeff wiped a hand down his face, shaking his head. “And *then* what?”  

Jodi, still breathless from laughing, grinned at him. “Then she asked for *homework.*”  

Jeff *stared* at her. “I—what? What does that *even mean?*”  

Jodi smirked, picking up her beer. “It means I told her to go home and *practice* until she got it right.”  

Jeff groaned, dragging his hand down his face. “You *coached* her?”  

Jodi shrugged, still grinning. “Look, she *needed* the guidance.”  

Jeff took another long sip of his beer, shaking his head. “I leave for *one week,* and you turn into a goddamn *sex ed professor.*”  

Jodi smirked, clinking her glass against his. “What can I say? I’m *very* passionate about education.”  

Jeff sighed, still chuckling. “I don’t know whether to be proud or *deeply* concerned.”  

Jodi winked. “Probably both.”  

Jeff exhaled, setting his beer down. “Okay, so, serious question.”  

Jodi raised an eyebrow. “Shoot.”  

Jeff smirked. “Did she pass?”  

Jodi *howled* with laughter, throwing her head back. “Oh, *she passed with flying colors.*”  

Jeff shook his head, amused. “Of course she did.”  

Jodi grinned, sipping her beer. “Best. *Lesson.* Ever.”    
---  
---  
Jeff took another slow sip of his beer, eyes locked on Jodi, studying her the way he always did when he *knew* she wasn’t telling the *whole* story.  

Jodi, still grinning, reached for her drink, but he caught her wrist before she could take another sip. “Hold on,” he said smoothly, tilting his head. “You’re leaving something out.”  

Jodi blinked innocently. “Who, *me*?”  

Jeff smirked. “Yes, *you.*” He leaned in slightly, his voice teasing. “I *know* you, Jodi. There’s always *more.*”  

Jodi bit her lip, trying to suppress a laugh. “I *may* have skipped a few… *minor* details.”  

Jeff raised an eyebrow. “*Minor?*”  

Jodi hesitated.  

Jeff grinned, leaning back. “Alright,” he said, taking another sip of his beer. “I challenge you to *fill in the blanks.*”  

Jodi groaned, covering her face with one hand. “God, I *hate* when you do this.”  

“No, you don’t,” Jeff said, smirking. “Now spill.”  

Jodi peeked at him through her fingers. “Promise you won’t *judge?*”  

Jeff chuckled. “Oh, *I’m absolutely judging,* but I *still* want to hear it.”  

Jodi sighed dramatically, tapping her fingers against her glass. “Fine. So, you know how I said Naomi *passed* her little… *homework assignment?*”  

Jeff nodded. “Yeah?”  

Jodi exhaled, trying not to laugh. “She, uh… *reported back.*”  

Jeff tilted his head. “Define ‘reported back.’”  

Jodi winced, shaking her head. “She *texted me.* At *11:25 PM.*”  

Jeff let out a *low whistle.* “That’s *specific.*”  

“Oh, it gets worse,” Jodi said, pressing her fingers against her forehead.  

Jeff smirked. “Please tell me she didn’t send—”  

“She sent a *photo,*” Jodi blurted, groaning.  

Jeff *choked* on his drink. “*WHAT?!*”  

Jodi was *howling* now. “NOT LIKE THAT! *Relax!* It was just her *face!* But she was *so proud*—grinning like she *won a goddamn trophy*—holding up ten fingers like she just finished a *marathon!*”  

Jeff *wheezed* with laughter, slamming his hand on the bar. “No. NO. Are you telling me she *posed* like she just won an *award?!*”  

Jodi nodded *wildly*, barely able to breathe. “And then she said—” She gasped for air. “She said *‘Mission Accomplished.’*”  

Jeff *lost it.* He practically doubled over, gripping the bar, laughing so hard he almost spilled his beer.  

Jodi wiped tears from her eyes. “And you *wonder* why I needed a drink that night.”  

Jeff shook his head, grinning. “Jesus Christ, Jodi.”  

Jodi smirked. “You *challenged* me to fill in the blanks.”  

Jeff exhaled sharply, shaking his head. “I *really* should’ve known better.”  

Jodi clinked her glass against his. “*And yet,* here we are.”  

Jeff smirked. “You know, I think I *have* to meet Naomi now.”  

Jodi laughed. “Oh, trust me. You *really* don’t.”  

Jeff took another sip of his beer, still grinning. “No, I do. I need to see the face of the girl who turned her *first orgasm* into a goddamn *victory lap.*”  

Jodi shook her head, giggling. “Just promise me you won’t ask her about her ‘studies.’”  

Jeff grinned. “No promises.”    
---  
---  
Jeff wiped the last of his laughter from his eyes, still shaking his head. “Alright, now I *have* to see this.”  

Jodi blinked. “See *what?*”  

Jeff smirked. “The *photo.* Naomi’s ‘Mission Accomplished’ moment.”  

Jodi snorted. “Oh my *God,* Jeff.”  

“What?” he said, grinning. “You painted a *vivid* picture, but I feel like I need to witness the *actual* moment for myself.”  

Jodi exhaled dramatically, pulling out her phone. “Fine. But don’t say I didn’t warn you.”  

She scrolled through her messages and found *the* picture—the one of Naomi, her face *completely flushed*, her hair slightly mussed, grinning like she *just won the Olympics*, holding up ten fingers like she was taking *credit* for a historic achievement.  

Jodi tilted the phone toward Jeff. “There. Behold.”  

Jeff took one look at the photo and *cracked up* all over again. “Jesus *Christ,* she really *does* look like she just won a trophy.”  

Jodi grinned. “I *told* you.”  

Jeff shook his head, but then his expression changed slightly as he *actually* looked at the screen. His smirk faded just a bit, his eyes narrowing.  

Jodi noticed immediately. “What?”  

Jeff pointed at the top of the message thread. “This is a *group chat.*”  

Jodi’s stomach *dropped*.  

Jeff looked at her, his amusement shifting into something far more *interested*. “Who were the *other participants?*”  

Jodi pressed her lips together, debating *very* hard whether she could dodge this.  

Jeff *grinned.* “Jodi.”  

She exhaled sharply, shaking her head. “Okay, okay—*Tara was in it too.*”  

Jeff blinked. “Wait. *Tara?*”  

Jodi groaned, rubbing her temples. “Yes.”  

Jeff chuckled, piecing things together. “So *Tara* also got to witness *Naomi’s* big achievement?”  

Jodi sighed. “She *did.*”  

Jeff smirked, shaking his head. “So… *two* people now have a *shared* history of guiding your naive coworker into sexual enlightenment.”  

Jodi threw up her hands. “Look, it just *happened,* okay? I wasn’t planning on starting some *weird mentoring program.*”  

Jeff let out a *long*, amused sigh. “And yet… *you did.*”  

Jodi groaned. “Oh my *God*, do *not* make this worse than it is.”  

Jeff chuckled, looking back at the screen. “So, what did *Tara* say when Naomi sent this?”  

Jodi sighed, scrolling up slightly and showing him.  

Jeff read the texts, his smirk deepening. “Holy *shit.* Tara was *not* prepared.”  

“No *one* was prepared, Jeff,” Jodi said dramatically.  

Jeff shook his head. “I leave for one week, and you and Tara *accidentally* turn some poor girl’s sex life into a *group project.*”  

Jodi groaned, hiding her face in her hands. “*God,* when you put it like that—”  

Jeff laughed, setting his drink down. “Alright, new rule. You are *not* allowed to take on any more students while I’m gone.”  

Jodi smirked. “No promises.”  

Jeff sighed, shaking his head. “Yeah. That’s what I thought.”    
---  
---  
Jeff scrolled through the chat, his smirk growing wider by the second. But then, something caught his eye.  

He tapped the screen, frowning slightly. “Hold on.”  

Jodi immediately tensed. “Uh, hold on *what?*”  

Jeff glanced at her, amused. “Tara said ‘OMG’ like… *eight times in a row.*”  

Jodi pressed her lips together. “Yeah, so?”  

Jeff arched an eyebrow. “So… was that just her being dramatic, or…” He scrolled back up slightly, his tone turning *far* too knowing. “Are there *missing texts*?”  

Jodi swallowed.  

Jeff’s smirk deepened. “Or.” He tilted his head, enjoying this *way* too much. “Are there… *missing images*?”  

Jodi sucked in a breath, *visibly* struggling to keep her face neutral. “Jeff.”  

Jeff laughed, shaking his head. “Jodi.”  

She sighed, dragging a hand down her face. “Look, before you freak out—”  

“Oh, I’m *not* freaking out,” Jeff interrupted, still grinning. “I just want to know what, exactly, was *so shocking* that Tara temporarily lost the ability to type actual sentences.”  

Jodi groaned. “God, I *knew* you were going to ask about that.”  

Jeff shrugged. “I mean, how could I *not?*” He leaned in slightly. “So? What *aren’t* you showing me?”  

Jodi fidgeted. “Technically… *nothing* is missing.”  

Jeff raised an eyebrow. “That doesn’t sound convincing at *all.*”  

Jodi sighed dramatically, picking up her beer and taking a *long* sip before answering. “Okay, fine. After Naomi sent her ‘Mission Accomplished’ text… she, uh… *added another one.*”  

Jeff waited, eyes glinting. “And?”  

Jodi sighed. “She asked what it was like when *I* got shaved.”  

Jeff blinked. “Wait. *What?*”  

Jodi winced, nodding. “Yeah. She wanted to know how it *felt* to be completely smooth.”  

Jeff stared at her, stunned. Then, *slowly*, his smirk returned. “Jodi.”  

Jodi groaned. “Oh, *God.*”  

Jeff set his beer down. “Tell me *you* didn’t.”  

Jodi covered her face. “It was *educational!*”  

Jeff *laughed*. “You *sent* her a picture?!”  

Jodi peeked through her fingers. “A *tasteful* one!”  

Jeff shook his head, grinning. “Oh, *so* that’s why Tara lost her *entire* mind.”  

Jodi sighed. “Tara was *not* ready.”  

Jeff exhaled, still grinning as he scrolled back to the string of *eight* "OMGs" from Tara. “Honestly? I don’t blame her.”  

Jodi groaned. “You’re having *way* too much fun with this.”  

Jeff took another sip of his beer, still smirking. “You *love* that I am.”  

Jodi rolled her eyes but smiled. “Shut up and drink your beer.”  

Jeff clinked his glass against hers. “Gladly.”    
---  
---  
Jeff leaned back in his chair, shaking his head as he scrolled through the ridiculous group chat one last time before locking his phone. His grin hadn’t faded once since Jodi started confessing *exactly* how much chaos she had caused while he was gone.  

“Well,” he said, stretching his arms over his head, “that was *deeply* enlightening.”  

Jodi groaned. “I *hate* that you’re enjoying this so much.”  

Jeff smirked. “Oh, I’m *having a blast.* And I *hope* you are, too.”  

Jodi huffed, grabbing her drink. “I’d be having a *better* time if you weren’t grinning like the damn Cheshire Cat.”  

Jeff chuckled, tapping the side of his glass. “Come on, let’s go to the grocery store and buy some *good* stuff.”  

Jodi raised an eyebrow. “Good stuff? Define ‘good.’”  

Jeff shrugged. “Something fancy. Something indulgent. We’ve been living off gas station gimbap and bar beer for the past few hours. Let’s go pick up some ridiculous cheeses, overpriced chocolate, and *probably* an irresponsible amount of cookies for your work potluck.”  

Jodi tilted her head. “Are you bribing me with fancy snacks?”  

“Yes,” Jeff said immediately.  

Jodi exhaled, shaking her head. “Fine. But I get to pick the cookies.”  

Jeff smirked. “Obviously. It’s your *potluck*, after all.”  

Jodi grabbed her bag, smirking as she stood. “You *do* realize that if we go shopping together, people are going to assume we’re *domestic and adorable*?”  

Jeff grinned, standing up beside her. “Jodi, we literally *are* domestic and adorable.”  

Jodi rolled her eyes, linking her arm through his. “Ugh. Gross.”  

Jeff kissed the top of her head. “Come on, let’s go buy out the bakery section.”  

Jodi laughed as they headed for the door, still buzzing from their *absolutely absurd* conversation.  

And somehow, she had a feeling the night wasn’t over yet.    
---  
---  
Jodi stood by the bakery section, watching Jeff pile cookie boxes into their cart like a man on a mission. Chocolate chip, oatmeal raisin, snickerdoodles, sugar cookies with colorful sprinkles—he was throwing in *everything*.  

“Four dozen?” Jodi asked, raising an eyebrow.  

Jeff looked up, completely unfazed. “You said it was a potluck, right?”  

“Yeah, but I don’t need to bring *enough for an army.*”  

Jeff smirked. “Why not? You know they’re going to talk about you anyway. May as well make sure the cookies soften the blow.”  

Jodi rolled her eyes but couldn’t hide her grin. “Fine. But I’m keeping the snickerdoodles for *us.*”  

“Deal,” Jeff said, steering the cart toward the wine aisle.  

Jodi followed, leaning against the handle of the cart as Jeff scanned the shelves. Finally, he picked out a bottle of Chilean white wine and held it up for her approval.  

“For you,” he said with a smile.  

Jodi’s grin widened. “Oh, look at you, being all thoughtful.”  

Jeff chuckled, dropping the bottle into the cart. Then, with zero warning, he pantomimed her giving him a blow job—leaning forward slightly, miming her hands and head motion, and finishing it off with a cheeky wipe of his mouth.  

Jodi’s jaw *dropped.*  

“JEFF!” she hissed, looking around to see if anyone had noticed.  

Jeff straightened up, his grin smug. “Just making sure you get the message.”  

Jodi groaned, her face heating, but she couldn’t help laughing. “Oh, I *got* the message. Loud and clear.”  

Jeff grabbed the cart, pushing it down the aisle with an air of satisfaction. “Good. Just wanted to be sure.”  

As they moved toward the checkout, Jeff glanced over at her. “So, before we finish up, is there anything else you need on an emergency basis? Now’s the time to ask.”  

Jodi shook her head, still grinning. “I think we’re good.”  

“Perfect,” Jeff said. Then, as casually as if he were commenting on the weather, he added, “By the way, how’d Tara take the whole clean-shaved bit?”  

Jodi nearly tripped over her own feet.  

“*Excuse me?*” she said, spinning to face him.  

Jeff smirked, stopping the cart. “You heard me.”  

Jodi’s face turned red, though whether it was from laughter or embarrassment, even she wasn’t sure. “Oh my *God,* Jeff.”  

“What?” Jeff said, grinning. “You’ve been *very* open about your adventures this week. I figured this was fair game.”  

Jodi crossed her arms, trying to look annoyed but failing miserably. “Okay, fine. She was… *surprised.*”  

Jeff raised an eyebrow. “Surprised how?”  

Jodi sighed, picking at the edge of a cookie box in the cart. “She said it was, and I quote, ‘next-level maintenance.’ And then she *asked questions.*”  

Jeff laughed, shaking his head. “Of course she did.”  

Jodi glared at him half-heartedly. “Don’t *start.*”  

Jeff leaned over, kissing her temple. “You make my life so interesting.”  

Jodi laughed, swatting him away. “Come on, let’s check out before you embarrass me any further.”  

“Not a chance,” Jeff said, smirking as he pushed the cart toward the register.    
---  
---  
Jeff loaded the last of the cookies onto the conveyor belt, still smirking as he thought back to Jodi’s *deeply flustered* reaction to his question.  

“So, hold on,” he said, shaking his head. “My *act* of shaving you caused all this *commotion?*”  

Jodi sighed, pulling a divider between their items and the next person’s groceries. “Yeah, apparently.”  

Jeff frowned slightly. “I mean, I wasn’t making some *grand statement.* I was just having fun.”  

Jodi snorted. “Oh, I *know* that, Jeff. *You* know that. But apparently, to others, it seemed a *wee bit over the top.*”  

Jeff grabbed the bottle of Chilean wine and placed it on the belt, giving her a skeptical look. “Over the top? Really?”  

Jodi gave him a *look*. “Jeff. You *shaved my entire crotch.* With the *focus* of a man sculpting a damn *marble statue.*”  

Jeff shrugged. “Yeah, and?”  

Jodi threw up her hands. “*And?!*”  

Jeff smirked. “I mean, it was kinda fun.”  

Jodi groaned. “Jeff, I *know* it was fun. That’s *not* the point. The point is that apparently, to people on the *outside*, it seemed… *weirdly intimate.*”  

Jeff blinked, genuinely surprised. “Huh.”  

Jodi folded her arms. “Yeah. *Huh.*”  

Jeff considered this for a second, rubbing his chin. “So… if it was such a *big deal* to everyone else, then I gotta ask…”  

He turned his head, fixing her with a curious, *slightly* mischievous look.  

“How did *you* think about it?”  

Jodi opened her mouth, then immediately closed it.  

Jeff *grinned.* “Ohhhh, see, now I *need* to know.”  

Jodi sighed, rolling her shoulders, trying to play it cool. “Okay, fine. *At first*, I thought it was just silly and playful. You being… well, *you.*”  

Jeff nodded. “That tracks.”  

“But then,” Jodi continued, tapping her fingers against the checkout counter, “somewhere around the time you started going *painstakingly slow* and, like, *studying your progress* like you were a damn surgeon, I had this moment of like… *oh*. This is actually kind of… *a thing.*”  

Jeff tilted his head. “A thing?”  

Jodi waved a hand. “You *know*. It stopped being just *silly fun* and started feeling… I dunno, *intense?* Like it wasn’t just about the *shaving* part, it was…”  

She trailed off, her fingers still tapping.  

Jeff waited, clearly *enjoying* watching her wrestle with her own words. “It was…?”  

Jodi exhaled. “It was *one of the most intimate things we’ve ever done,* okay?”  

Jeff blinked. “Wait, seriously?”  

Jodi groaned, rubbing her temples. “YES, *seriously!* I don’t know why, but *yes!*”  

Jeff looked genuinely surprised for a second, then smirked. “So what I’m hearing is… I gave you a next-level bonding experience with a *cheap drugstore razor.*”  

Jodi pointed at him. “*Don’t ruin it.*”  

Jeff *laughed*, grabbing the bag of cookies off the counter. “Alright, alright. I’ll be respectful of the fact that I *accidentally* gave you an emotional revelation while shaving your crotch.”  

Jodi grabbed her wine, shaking her head. “You’re *so* lucky you’re cute.”  

Jeff smirked, kissing her cheek. “You *love* that I’m cute.”  

Jodi sighed dramatically. “Unfortunately.”  

Jeff laughed as they walked toward the exit, still grinning.  

Jodi knew *one thing* for sure—she was *never* telling Tara *any* of this.    
---  
---  
As they walked up to the counter to pay, Jeff leaned over to Jodi and said, his voice low and teasing, “You know, I wouldn’t mind repeating that one day soon.”  

Jodi’s lips curved into a soft smile, and she glanced up at him. “I hoped you’d say that.”  

Jeff grinned, setting the cookies and wine on the conveyor belt.  

But Jodi, ever the wildcard, turned to the cashier—a young woman who was just trying to survive her shift—and said, completely deadpan, “Hey, have you ever had your cootch shaved?”  

The cashier *froze*.  

Her hand hovered over the scanner, her face turning beet red as her eyes darted between Jodi and Jeff, *utterly unsure* how to respond.  

Jeff’s face went from mildly amused to full-on *horrified* in less than a second.  

“Oh my God,” he muttered under his breath, pulling out his wallet.  

Jodi, meanwhile, leaned against the counter, completely unfazed. “I’m serious,” she said, as if this were a perfectly normal question. “It’s way more intimate than you’d think. Like, I’m not sure I even realized how—”  

“*Jodi,*” Jeff hissed, sliding two twenties across the counter like he was trying to bribe the poor cashier into forgetting this entire interaction. “*Stop.*”  

The cashier, still blushing furiously, managed a shaky laugh. “Um… no, I haven’t,” she stammered, clearly unsure if she should even be answering this question.  

Jodi nodded thoughtfully. “You should try it sometime. It’s life-changing.”  

Jeff groaned, pinching the bridge of his nose. “I am *so sorry,*” he said to the cashier, who waved a hand awkwardly as she handed him his change.  

“It’s, uh… fine,” the cashier said, her voice a little too high-pitched to be convincing.  

Jeff grabbed the bags and nudged Jodi toward the door. “Okay, we’re leaving *right now,*” he muttered, his ears burning.  

Jodi laughed, walking alongside him as he all but dragged her out of the store. “What? She seemed curious!”  

Jeff stopped just outside the door, staring at her in disbelief. “You *cannot* just ask random people if they’ve had their *cootch shaved.*”  

Jodi smirked, tilting her head. “Why not? It’s a valid question.”  

Jeff exhaled sharply, shaking his head as he unlocked the car. “You’re *impossible.*”  

“And yet,” Jodi said, sliding into the passenger seat, “you love me anyway.”  

Jeff climbed into the driver’s seat, setting the bags down and glancing at her. “Unfortunately,” he said with a smirk.  

Jodi laughed, leaning over to kiss his cheek. “You’re welcome for keeping your life interesting.”  

Jeff chuckled, starting the car. “You know, I’m never bringing you back to that grocery store again.”  

Jodi grinned, buckling her seatbelt. “Oh, please. They’ll miss me.”  

Jeff shook his head, still smiling as he backed out of the parking spot. With Jodi, life was *never* boring.    
---  
---  
The soft hum of the car filled the silence as Jeff and Jodi drove home, the grocery bags of cookies and wine rustling gently in the backseat. The city lights faded behind them as the road stretched into quieter, tree-lined neighborhoods.  

Jeff glanced over at her, his hand casually resting on the gearshift. “You know,” he said, his voice light, “we’re about to drive past your mom’s place. Do we need to stop by?”  

Jodi didn’t hesitate. “Nope.”  

Jeff raised an eyebrow, smirking. “That was fast. Are you sure? I mean, she might have cookies of her own.”  

Jodi rolled her eyes, leaning her elbow against the window. “Jeff, what I *need* right now isn’t cookies.”  

Jeff glanced at her, his smirk deepening. “Oh? What do you *need,* then?”  

Jodi turned to him, her lips curving into a wicked grin. “Sex.”  

Jeff chuckled. “Oh, is *that* all?”  

“I’m serious,” Jodi said, her tone playful but tinged with urgency. She gestured out the window toward the road ahead. “We could drive straight home. Or—” She paused, tilting her head, her grin widening. “We could take a right down that old one-lane farm road…”  

Jeff’s eyebrows lifted slightly, his curiosity piqued. “And what happens if we take the farm road?”  

Jodi leaned closer to him, her voice dropping to a low murmur. “I’ll blow you. Right here. Right now.”  

Jeff let out a sharp laugh, gripping the steering wheel a little tighter. “Jesus, Jodi.”  

“What?” Jodi said, shrugging innocently. “I’m just trying to make your evening a little more… *interesting.*”  

Jeff exhaled, shaking his head as he slowed down slightly, glancing at the narrow dirt road she was pointing to. “You’re *ridiculous,* you know that?”  

“And you love me for it,” Jodi shot back, grinning.  

Jeff hesitated for just a moment, then smirked as he flipped on his turn signal and steered the car onto the old farm road.  

The headlights cut through the darkness, the gravel crunching under the tires as they drove deeper into the quiet, secluded stretch of road. Jeff glanced over at Jodi, his smirk widening.  

“Alright,” he said, parking the car and cutting the engine. “Let’s see if you’re all talk.”  

Jodi grinned, unbuckling her seatbelt. “Oh, you’re about to find out I’m *definitely* not.”    
---  
---  
Jeff exhaled slowly, leaning back as far as the seat would allow, letting his hands rest behind his head. He watched Jodi move with *complete* confidence, her eyes gleaming with mischief in the dim glow of the dashboard lights.  

“You know,” he murmured, shaking his head slightly, “I *really* don’t know what I did in a past life to deserve you.”  

Jodi smirked, resting a hand on his thigh. “Maybe you saved a bus full of orphans. Or rescued a puppy from a burning building.”  

Jeff chuckled. “If I did, I sure don’t remember it.”  

Jodi leaned in closer, her grin softening into something *genuinely* affectionate. “Well,” she said, tilting her head, “you *do* deserve it. Every bit of it.”  

Jeff felt a warmth spread through his chest—not just from her words, but from the way she *said* them. With complete certainty, like she *truly* believed it.  

His fingers brushed her cheek, his voice quieter now. “You *love* making me happy, don’t you?”  

Jodi’s expression turned *radiant*, her smile widening. “Oh, *so much*.”  

Jeff exhaled, his head pressing back against the seat as he *really* took in the moment. The secluded road, the quiet hum of the night, the fact that this *incredible* woman was *so deeply* invested in his happiness.  

“Jesus,” he muttered, shaking his head. “I’m *so* damn lucky.”  

Jodi laughed, light and teasing. “Oh, I *know*.”  

Jeff smirked. “No, I don’t think you *do*.”  

Jodi raised an eyebrow. “Oh?”  

Jeff reached for her hand, intertwining their fingers. “I don’t think you *fully* realize how much joy you bring me. How much I *love* the way you light up when you’re scheming. How I *look forward* to whatever ridiculous idea you’re about to throw at me next.”  

Jodi’s grin softened again, her fingers tightening around his. “You know,” she murmured, “you could *just* say you love me.”  

Jeff smiled. “I *do* love you.”  

Jodi leaned in, pressing her forehead against his. “Good. Because I *really* love you too.”  

For a moment, they just stayed there, breathing each other in, wrapped in their own little world.  

Then—Jodi pulled back, her smirk returning. “Now. Are you *done* being sentimental, or do you want me to keep talking until sunrise?”  

Jeff laughed, shaking his head. “Jodi?”  

“Yes, Jeff?”  

He grinned, squeezing her hand. “Be my guest.”  

Jodi beamed. “Oh, I *fully* intend to.”  

And with that, the night carried on, filled with warmth, laughter, and the kind of *perfect, ridiculous intimacy* only they could create together.    
---  
---  
Jodi was *positively glowing*—smug, confident, and absolutely thriving in the moment.  

Jeff, still leaning back, watching her with that *infuriatingly amused* expression, tilted his head slightly. “You know,” he said casually, “I wonder if you’d be less distracted if you took your blouse off.”  

Jodi raised an eyebrow, smirking. “Oh? And what *exactly* makes you think that?”  

Jeff shrugged, keeping his tone completely innocent. “For science, of course.”  

Jodi laughed, tilting her head. “Ah, I see. Purely an *academic inquiry*.”  

“Obviously,” Jeff said smoothly. “I just think it would be… *interesting* to test the hypothesis.”  

Jodi pursed her lips, pretending to consider. “And what is the hypothesis, exactly?”  

Jeff smirked. “That *you* would enjoy this even more if you weren’t wearing so many *pesky* layers.”  

Jodi chuckled, reaching up to toy with the top button of her blouse. “Mmm. That *does* sound like an experiment worth conducting.”  

Jeff grinned, his fingers tapping lazily on the armrest. “You *do* love a good science project.”  

Jodi nodded solemnly. “I *do.*”  

A pause.  

Then, with a wicked glint in her eye, she leaned forward, lowering her voice to a whisper. “But you know what the first rule of science is?”  

Jeff smirked. “What’s that?”  

Jodi grinned. “A *proper* experiment requires a *control group*.”  

Jeff blinked. “Uh-huh…”  

Jodi reached out, *deliberately* tugging at the hem of *his* shirt. “So, if I take mine off…” She tilted her head. “You *first.*”  

Jeff exhaled sharply, chuckling as he shook his head. “I *should’ve* seen that coming.”  

Jodi smirked. “You *really* should’ve.”  

Jeff let out a long, amused sigh. “Fine.” He reached for the back of his collar, pulling his shirt over his head in one smooth motion. “Satisfied?”  

Jodi grinned. “Very.”  

Jeff leaned back again, watching her expectantly. “Your turn.”  

Jodi exhaled, giving him one last playful smirk. Then, slowly—*very* slowly—she unbuttoned her blouse.  

The air between them *crackled* with anticipation.  

Jeff, arms still resting behind his head, let out a soft chuckle. “You know…” He met her eyes, his voice low and teasing. “I *love* science.”  

Jodi laughed, tossing her blouse onto the passenger seat. “I *know* you do.”  

And with that, the experiment *began.*    
---  
---  
Jeff leaned in, pressing soft kisses along Jodi’s collarbone, then down to the swell of her breasts, taking his time as if he had all the hours in the night. Jodi sighed, her fingers threading through his hair, pulling him closer.  

But then she couldn’t help but chuckle, the memory popping into her mind unbidden.  

Jeff paused, glancing up with a smirk. “Something funny?”  

Jodi grinned, shaking her head. “I was just thinking about that morning when we shared a 69, and I had to rush like a *maniac* to get to work afterward.”  

Jeff chuckled, trailing his fingers lightly over her sides. “Oh yeah? What happened?”  

Jodi sighed dramatically, rolling her eyes. “Well, you had me *so* blissed out that when I finally checked the time, I realized I had, like, twenty minutes to get out the door.”  

Jeff laughed softly against her skin. “You’re usually more on top of things.”  

“*Usually,*” Jodi agreed, grinning. “But that morning? I was scrambling around, half-dressed, couldn’t find my shoes, and then I realized I hadn’t even packed my lunch.”  

Jeff raised an eyebrow, pressing another kiss to her chest. “So what did you do?”  

Jodi huffed, biting her lip. “I threw on the first thing I found—which I didn’t realize until later had a dryer sheet stuck in it—and just *ran* out the door. Didn’t even grab my coffee. I was combing my hair at every red light, praying I wouldn’t hit traffic.”  

Jeff chuckled, his hands gently tracing patterns along her back. “Sounds like quite the adventure.”  

“Oh, it was,” Jodi said, laughing. “Got to work with about thirty seconds to spare, and the whole team just *knew.* They were teasing me all day about my ‘glow.’”  

Jeff grinned, kissing the corner of her mouth. “You *did* have that glow. You still do.”  

Jodi rolled her eyes playfully. “Well, thank you for being the reason I nearly lost my sanity that morning.”  

Jeff’s smile softened, his fingers brushing a stray hair from her face. “If it means I get to be the reason you’re glowing… I’ll take it.”  

Jodi’s breath hitched slightly, her teasing demeanor fading as she looked into his eyes. “You’re impossible, you know that?”  

Jeff smiled, pressing his forehead against hers. “And yet, you wouldn’t have it any other way.”  

Jodi grinned, wrapping her arms around his neck. “Yeah. I guess I wouldn’t.”  

In the quiet intimacy of the moment, the two of them melted into each other, laughter and love intertwining effortlessly.    
---  
---  
Jodi shifted slightly, her fingers trailing teasingly along Jeff’s stomach as she continued her story. Her voice was light, casual—like she *wasn’t* slowly working him up, like this was just another one of their late-night chats.  

“So,” she said, her nails grazing over his skin, “there I was—running on *zero* caffeine, still feeling… *very* well-used from the morning, and absolutely *starving*.”  

Jeff exhaled sharply, both from her touch and the memory. “Mmm. That’s a shame,” he murmured, lips brushing against her shoulder. “I like keeping you well-fed.”  

Jodi smirked, tilting her head. “Oh, don’t worry. I *improvised.*”  

Jeff pulled back slightly, giving her a knowing look. “Oh, I *know* that tone. What did you do?”  

Jodi grinned. “I raided the break room. We had, like, *nothing* good. But then I spotted Tara’s loaf of bread.”  

Jeff huffed a laugh. “You *stole* Tara’s bread?”  

“I *borrowed* it,” Jodi corrected, pressing her palm flat against his chest. “And then, I found grape jam from Mike’s stash. So, I made toast.”  

Jeff blinked. “Wait. You skipped breakfast, showed up late, *stole* half of your coworkers’ groceries, and made yourself *toast*?”  

Jodi nodded, looking *far* too pleased with herself.  

Jeff smirked, brushing his thumb along her waist. “And *nobody* said anything?”  

“Oh, *they did.*” Jodi rolled her eyes. “Tara *knew* immediately that I had the ‘Breakfast of Champions’ again.”  

Jeff let out a sharp laugh, gripping her hip slightly. “She *called* it that?”  

Jodi grinned. “She did. And then I *very helpfully* pointed out that a load of cum is, like, *ten calories max*, so I *needed* something more substantial.”  

Jeff groaned, dropping his head back. “Jodi.”  

Jodi *beamed.* “What?”  

Jeff ran a hand down his face. “You *told* them that?”  

“Of course I did!” Jodi laughed, trailing her fingers lower along his stomach. “You think I’m gonna let them *shame* me for having a *great* morning?”  

Jeff shook his head, chuckling. “You are *too much.*”  

“And you *love it,*” Jodi teased, her grin widening as she leaned in.  

Jeff smirked, exhaling as he felt her touch wandering again. “Mmm. I *really* do.”  

Jodi kissed his jaw, her voice dropping to a whisper. “Good. Because I *fully* intend to keep making your life interesting.”  

Jeff laughed softly, pulling her closer. “Jodi?”  

“Yeah?”  

He met her gaze, smirking. “Be my guest.”  

Jodi *beamed.* “Oh, I *planned on it.*”    
---  
---  
Jeff exhaled deeply, his head pressing back against the seat, his fingers lazily threading through Jodi’s hair. “God, it’s good to be back in the USA,” he muttered, half-laughing, half-groaning.  

Jodi smirked, not pausing in her work. “Mmm. Welcome home.”  

Jeff chuckled, shaking his head. “You *really* missed me, huh?”  

Jodi hummed in response, her fingers gripping his thighs just slightly.  

Jeff let out another sharp breath, tilting his head to look down at her. “You know,” he murmured, his voice light but teasing, “if I’d known you were this enthusiastic about *American hospitality,* I’d have flown back sooner.”  

Jodi paused just long enough to shoot him a smug look. “Oh? And miss your big BBC contract negotiations?”  

Jeff groaned. “Don’t *remind* me. The whole trip was basically contract riders and polite lectures about *not* swearing too much on live television. Apparently, last winter’s incident was a bit much.”  

Jodi snorted, grinning. “Oh, *Ofcom* got involved? That’s when you know it’s bad.”  

Jeff laughed breathlessly, shaking his head. “Yeah, yeah. But honestly?” He ran his fingers through her hair, his smirk deepening. “What I *really* needed was for you to be in my hotel room… doing exactly this.”  

Jodi arched an eyebrow. “Mm. Sounds like a compliment.”  

Jeff groaned again, his head dropping back. “Oh, it *is.*”  

Jodi, delighted, picked up her pace just slightly, making Jeff curse under his breath.  

After a few moments, Jeff let out another low chuckle. “You know… for someone who made such a big deal about needing a *proper* breakfast the other day, you sure seem happy to have *dessert* right now.”  

Jodi smirked, pausing just long enough to murmur, “I *did* say I love variety.”  

Jeff exhaled sharply, shaking his head. “Unbelievable.”  

Jodi grinned. “And yet, you *love* me.”  

Jeff chuckled, reaching over to turn the car’s defroster on. “Yeah, yeah. I *love* you.” He smirked, watching the windshield begin to fog slightly. “And *I* love that we’re about to need this.”  

Jodi hummed, clearly pleased.  

Jeff sighed, still smirking. “Now the *real* question is—how the *hell* am I supposed to return the favor?”  

Jodi, pulling back just enough to meet his gaze, grinned. “Oh, I *fully* trust your creativity.”  

Jeff smirked, rolling his shoulders as he settled deeper into the seat. “Good. Because now I *really* have something to prove.”  

Jodi beamed. “That’s what I *love* to hear.”  

And with that, the night only got *better.*    
---  
---  
Jeff exhaled, still catching his breath, when a thought hit him. “You know,” he murmured, running his fingers through Jodi’s hair, “we *might’ve* finally solved the band’s issue—”  

Jodi *immediately* sat up, stuck both fingers in her ears, and started chanting *loudly*—  

“LALALALALALA! *CAN’T HEAR YOU!*”  

Jeff *laughed*, his head thudding back against the seat. “Jesus, Jodi.”  

She *grinned*, wiggling her fingers deeper into her ears. “Nope! No work talk during *this*—it ruins the *vibe!*”  

Jeff smirked, shaking his head. “Fine, fine, I’ll shut up about it. But only if you *restart.*”  

Jodi arched an eyebrow. “That’s a *big* request, Jeff.”  

Jeff grinned. “Yeah, well. I’m a *big* deal.”  

Jodi snorted but relented, leaning back in closer.  

Just before she resumed, Jeff casually added, “By the way, you should come to Oslo with me next month.”  

Jodi *paused*, tilting her head slightly. “Oslo, *Norway*?”  

Jeff smirked. “Unless you know a different one.”  

Jodi rolled her eyes and gave his ribs a light tickle, making him *jerk* slightly. “You *know* I hate long flights.”  

Jeff chuckled, still twitching from her fingers. “Yeah, but you *love* adventure.”  

Jodi sighed, her fingers resting against his stomach as she *actually* considered it for a second. “Mmm. Oslo, huh?”  

Jeff smirked, brushing his knuckles along her cheek. “I’ll even let you steal half my hotel breakfast.”  

Jodi’s lips twitched. “Do they have waffles?”  

“*Norwegian* waffles,” Jeff said, raising an eyebrow. “With *brown cheese.*”  

Jodi *squinted*. “Cheese… on a waffle?”  

Jeff grinned. “See? You *have* to go now.”  

Jodi let out a thoughtful hum, then shrugged, smirking as she resumed exactly where she left off. “I’ll think about it.”  

Jeff exhaled sharply, laughing. “I *knew* you were gonna say that.”  

Jodi winked up at him. “You *love* the suspense.”  

Jeff groaned, tilting his head back. “Unbelievable.”  

Jodi grinned. “And *yet,* here we are.”  

And just like that, Oslo *might* have a new traveler next month.    
---  
---

Jeff *groaned*, his head lolling back against the seat, his fingers gripping the edge of the door like he needed to hold onto something solid. “Jesus, Jodi…”  

Jodi grinned at the praise, casting a smug glance up at him before letting out a small, exaggerated *slurp*—just to mess with him.  

Jeff let out a *breathless laugh*, shaking his head. “Unreal.”  

Jodi smirked. “You *love* it.”  

Jeff exhaled, still catching his breath. “Oh, I *do.*” He reached for the dashboard, flipping on the defroster again, watching as the windshield fogged up even more.  

Then, still sounding *slightly dazed*, he murmured, “When you’re done, maybe you should drive.”  

Jodi blinked, *amused*. “Oh? And *why’s* that?”  

Jeff smirked, tilting his head down to meet her gaze. “Because being with you makes me *dizzy.*”  

Jodi *paused*, genuinely caught off guard for a second.  

She *knew* him—every inch of him. After over a decade together, she could *predict* him in most ways. His body, his expressions, his reactions—she’d mapped them all like a scientist studying a long-running experiment.  

And yet, *every once in a while*, he still *surprised* her.  

Like now.  

She tilted her head slightly, eyes glinting with curiosity. “Really?”  

Jeff chuckled, his hand drifting to her cheek, brushing his thumb over her skin. “You *still* don’t get it, do you?”  

Jodi arched an eyebrow. “Get *what?*”  

Jeff exhaled, shaking his head fondly. “That you *do* this to me. That I *lose myself* with you.”  

Jodi’s breath hitched slightly.  

Jeff smirked. “Oh, you like that, huh?”  

Jodi *didn’t* answer right away—not verbally, at least. But Jeff caught the slight shift in her expression, the way her eyes darkened just a little, the way her fingers flexed slightly where they rested on his thigh.  

He *definitely* had her attention now.  

Jeff’s smirk widened, his fingers sliding into her hair, his voice dropping into something *just* between teasing and commanding.  

“Can I hold your head?” he murmured. “Control the cadence?”  

Jodi inhaled slowly, her heartbeat *thrumming*.  

She lifted her head just slightly, enough to meet his gaze with a playful, challenging smirk. “You *think* you can control me?”  

Jeff chuckled, his grip in her hair tightening just slightly. “You *love* when I try.”  

Jodi *beamed.* “Maybe.”  

Jeff exhaled, tilting his head back again, his grip steady and firm. “Then let’s *see*.”  

Jodi *grinned.* “Oh, Jeff.” She winked. “Be my *guest*.”  

Jeff laughed *deeply*, shaking his head. “God, I *love* you.”  

And with that, the *real* fun began.    
---  
---  
Jeff sighed deeply, his fingers tracing slow circles against Jodi’s skin. The night was quiet except for their breathing and the faint hum of the car’s engine. He reached up and tapped the control for the moonroof, sliding it open to reveal the night sky above them.  

“Look up,” he murmured.  

Jodi tilted her head, glancing through the glass, her lips curving into a small, surprised smile. The stars were faint but steady, and the sliver of the moon cast a soft, silver glow inside the car.  

Jeff exhaled, shaking his head slightly as his eyes trailed over her. “You look… unbelievable right now.”  

Jodi blinked, caught slightly off guard. “What?”  

Jeff’s voice was low, warm. “The moonlight.” He traced a lazy fingertip along her shoulder. “The way it catches your skin. You’re all alabaster and ruddy red. It’s…” He let out a breath. “*Damn.*”  

Jodi, for all her bravado, felt a rare *aw shucks* moment creep up on her.  

She wasn’t used to *blushing*. Not with Jeff. Not after all these years. But something about the way he said it—so effortless, so *genuine*—made warmth creep up her neck.  

She huffed, trying to play it off. “You just like me half-naked in a car.”  

Jeff chuckled, brushing a strand of hair behind her ear. “I mean… yeah. Obviously.”  

Jodi laughed, finally shaking off her moment of fluster. Then, because she could *never* let a quiet moment be *too* quiet, she smirked.  

“You know,” she said, resting her chin against his chest, “this reminds me…”  

Jeff arched an eyebrow. “Of what?”  

Jodi sighed dramatically. “Of how *much effort* some people put into grooming.”  

Jeff tilted his head. “Oh?”  

Jodi grinned. “*Tara’s* maintenance routine, for example.”  

Jeff blinked. “Oh, *God.*”  

Jodi *beamed*, delighted to have caught him off guard. “I mean, I *thought* I kept things tidy, but Tara? *Next-level.*”  

Jeff groaned, rubbing his hands down his face. “Jodi, are we *really* talking about your friend’s grooming habits right now?”  

Jodi grinned. “Would you *prefer* I stop talking?”  

Jeff *laughed*, shaking his head. “Jesus. Fine. Tell me.”  

Jodi smirked. “Oh, *you* asked for this.”  

And with that, she launched into a *thoroughly* detailed account of Tara’s routine, enjoying every second of Jeff’s *half-mortified, half-amused* reactions.  

It was, in its own way, *perfectly* them.    
---  
---  
Jodi stretched luxuriously, sighing contentedly as she propped herself up on Jeff’s chest, her fingers idly tracing patterns along his skin.  

“So,” she said, her voice light and *far too pleased with herself*, “Tara and I? *Completely* different approaches.”  

Jeff smirked, still recovering from her *very unexpected* topic shift. “Oh, *do* tell.”  

Jodi nodded sagely. “I mean, *I* go for practicality. A good trim, neat, efficient. Keeps things fun without feeling like I’ve committed to an *upkeep contract*.”  

Jeff chuckled. “Fair.”  

Jodi grinned. “But *Tara*?” She let out a soft whistle. “That woman treats her routine like an *art form*.”  

Jeff tilted his head, amused. “How so?”  

Jodi propped herself up on her elbows. “Okay, *first of all*—she *waxes*. Like, *regularly*.”  

Jeff winced instinctively. “Oof.”  

Jodi nodded. “*Right?* And apparently, she has a *standing appointment*. Like it’s some kind of *board meeting*.”  

Jeff huffed a laugh. “I admire the commitment.”  

“Oh, we’re *just getting started*,” Jodi said, her eyes glinting. “*Second*, she *shapes* it. Like, *strategically*.”  

Jeff raised an eyebrow. “Shapes?”  

Jodi nodded. “You know. *Landing strip, French cut, bare with a little flourish—*”  

Jeff shook his head. “Jodi, I don’t think I need *visuals*.”  

Jodi *laughed*. “Oh, *you do*, but I’ll be respectful.”  

Jeff smirked. “Okay, and *how exactly* do you know all this?”  

Jodi blinked innocently. “Oh, I *dug* for this information.”  

Jeff laughed, shaking his head. “You *interrogated* her?”  

Jodi grinned. “Obviously. You think she just *offered up* this level of detail?”  

Jeff exhaled, rubbing his temples. “Jesus, Jodi.”  

Jodi *beamed*. “Oh, come on, you *love* that I’m thorough.”  

Jeff smirked. “I do. But I’m almost afraid to ask *how* you got her to spill.”  

Jodi tilted her head, pursing her lips like she was remembering the moment. “A little wine. Some casual curiosity. A *well-timed* comment about how much maintenance *I* do.” She shrugged. “Next thing I knew, she was giving me a *full* run-down on her process, her aesthetic choices, and—get this—her *esthetician’s name*.”  

Jeff’s eyes widened. “Wait. She *outsourced* it?”  

Jodi grinned. “Yep. And you know what? I *respect* it.”  

Jeff sighed, rubbing his hands down his face. “I *love* how this is what you do when I’m out of town.”  

Jodi *smirked*. “What, you *think* I just sit around, twiddling my thumbs, waiting for you to come home?”  

Jeff grinned, pulling her closer. “No. I *know* you’re out there making some poor, unsuspecting friend *deeply* regret a casual conversation.”  

Jodi *cackled*, wrapping her arms around his neck. “And *yet*, she still texts me first *every time* she gets waxed.”  

Jeff shook his head, laughing. “You are *too much*.”  

Jodi grinned, pressing her forehead against his. “And you *love* it.”  

Jeff sighed dramatically. “Unfortunately, *yes*.”  

Jodi *beamed*. “That’s what I *love* to hear.”  

And just like that, *another* utterly ridiculous conversation had been permanently seared into their relationship history.    
---  
---  
Jeff exhaled, his head pressing back against the seat, fingers threading through Jodi’s hair as she moved in perfect rhythm with him.  

The night was quiet except for their breathing and the occasional hum of the car’s engine. Above them, the stars blinked faintly through the moonroof, casting just enough light to make Jodi’s skin look almost unreal—pale where the moon hit, warm where the shadows fell.  

She looked up at him then, her expression playful but thoughtful. “How hard is it?” she murmured.  

Jeff blinked, barely able to think straight. “Huh?”  

Jodi’s fingers traced absent patterns against his thigh. “For a guy like *you* to stay faithful to a woman like *me* back in Ohio.”  

Jeff’s entire body *tensed*.  

For the first time all night, his mind snapped *completely* into focus, like a cold wind had just blown through the car.  

Jodi didn’t *ask* questions like that lightly.  

He swallowed hard, his fingers stilling in her hair. “Jodi…” He exhaled sharply. “It’s… surprisingly hard.”  

Jodi paused for just a second, her eyes flickering over his face, reading everything in an instant. But instead of retreating, she simply murmured, “I always want to hear what’s on your mind.”  

Jeff let out a slow, shaky breath, staring up at the stars.  

She *meant* that.  

She always *meant* that.  

And that was the thing that made it *worse*.  

He shut his eyes, running a hand down his face. “You know how long it’s been.”  

Jodi nodded against him. “More than five years.”  

Jeff exhaled again, fingers tightening slightly in her hair. “Five years isn’t forever.”  

Jodi considered that for a moment before speaking. “No. But it’s long enough to prove a *pattern shift*.”  

Jeff let out a humorless chuckle. “Is it?”  

Jodi’s head lifted slightly, her lips quirking just a little. “Well, *statistically* speaking—”  

“Jodi,” Jeff groaned.  

Jodi smirked but softened, her fingers brushing against his stomach. “Okay. So what are you *really* trying to say?”  

Jeff let his hand drop into her hair again, staring up at the dark expanse above them. His voice was quieter now, almost raw.  

“I don’t trust myself, Jodi.”  

She didn’t speak right away.  

She didn’t interrupt.  

She just *listened.*  

“I tell myself I’m different now. That I know what I have. That I *wouldn’t* screw it up again. But I remember who I was. How I *let* myself justify things that should’ve never been justified. I remember the rush. The excuses. And I just…” He let out another long breath. “I don’t *believe* in my own goodness the way you do.”  

Jodi was quiet for a moment, then, very gently, she said, “You don’t have to be a *good* man, Jeff.”  

Jeff frowned slightly, glancing down at her. “No?”  

Jodi tilted her head, her expression thoughtful. “No. You just have to be *my* man.”  

Jeff exhaled sharply, something in his chest *cracking* open. “Jodi…”  

She smirked a little, as if sensing his reaction, but her voice stayed soft. “Look, I know who I fell in love with. I know *all* of you—past mistakes included. I’m not asking you to be a saint, Jeff. I just need you to *choose* me. Every day. Over and over again.”  

Jeff swallowed hard, his fingers brushing against her cheek. “And if I ever slip?”  

Jodi’s eyes darkened just slightly. “Then you *tell me*—before I have to find out another way.”  

Jeff let out a breathless laugh, shaking his head. “God, you’re impossible.”  

Jodi grinned. “And yet, you *love* me.”  

Jeff sighed, pulling her closer. “Unfortunately, yes.”  

Jodi *beamed*, settling against him again. “That’s what I *love* to hear.”  

And with that, Jeff *finally* let himself relax.  

Because he wasn’t perfect.  

But with Jodi?  

He didn’t *have* to be.    
---  
---  
Jeff exhaled, running a hand through Jodi’s hair as she settled back into rhythm, his breathing steady but his mind still *lingering* on what he’d just confessed.  

“I just…” He sighed. “I’m *constantly* surrounded by temptation, Jodi.”  

Jodi didn’t pause, but her brow *did* lift slightly.  

Jeff let out another slow breath, his fingers idly stroking her temple. “That’s part of why I asked you to come to Oslo with me. I just… feel better when you’re around. Less challenged.”  

Jodi *still* didn’t pause, but this time, she lifted a single hand—her index finger popping up in the air, waggling side to side like a metronome.  

Jeff *chuckled*, shaking his head. “Oh, come *on*—”  

Jodi waggled her finger again.  

Jeff sighed, pressing his head back against the seat. “This will pass.” His voice was firmer now, more certain. “I’m feeling really *good* about life right now. My work. *Us.*”  

Jodi smacked her lips *loudly* in response.  

Jeff let out a short laugh, glancing down at her. “You’re in *awe* of how I’m still talking, aren’t you?”  

Jodi didn’t stop what she was doing, but her eyes *absolutely* twinkled with mirth.  

Jeff sighed, shaking his head. “Twenty-five minutes, huh?”  

Jodi *hummed* in confirmation.  

Jeff chuckled, rubbing his temple. “Yeah, yeah. *Noted.*”  

Jodi *beamed*, victorious.  

Jeff exhaled, letting his body relax completely against the seat, his fingers idly tracing over her back. “God, I *love* you.”  

Jodi tapped his thigh twice—her silent way of saying *I know.*  

And with that, Jeff finally *shut up* and just *let himself enjoy the moment.*    
---  
---  
Jeff exhaled *deeply*, his entire body relaxing against the seat, his fingers slipping from Jodi’s hair to rest limply at his side. His breaths were slow, measured, and she *knew*—she *knew*—that was him *surrendering.*  

Jodi *loved* that feeling. That moment when he let go, when he wasn’t overthinking or calculating, just *existing* with her. She *savored* it, lingering in the moment, watching him ride the waves of something deeper than just pleasure.  

And yet… something was *different* this time.  

She studied his face, the way his breathing changed, the way his fingers *twitched* slightly against the car seat like his mind was *already moving*.  

Jeff was *thinking*.  

*Deeply.*  

It had been a *long time* since he’d been this contemplative.  

Jodi exhaled softly, resting her chin against his stomach, studying him.  

She could *see* the shift happening in real time—the gears *turning* in his head, the way his gaze went a little distant, the way his jaw tensed *just slightly* before relaxing again.  

People always thought Jeff wasn’t *very* demonstrative.  

They were *wrong.*  

Jodi *saw* him.  

She saw *every little signal he gave off*. The quiet ways he pulled inward, the subtle shifts in his expression when he was wrestling with something bigger than he knew how to say.  

And right now?  

She could *sense* that something had changed.  

Jodi frowned slightly, pressing her lips together as she considered her next move.  

Was he going to go home and *write* instead of having more sex with her?  

Because *she* was still *very much* in the mood for more.  

But she also knew Jeff.  

And Jeff—when his brain *flipped* into this particular mode—was damn near impossible to redirect.  

She sighed dramatically, pressing a soft kiss against his stomach before propping herself up slightly. “I *know* that look.”  

Jeff blinked, glancing down at her, still a little dazed. “Huh?”  

Jodi smirked, tapping his chest lightly. “You’re *thinking*.”  

Jeff exhaled, running a hand down his face. “Yeah… I guess I am.”  

Jodi tilted her head, watching him. “Are you going to go home and *write* now instead of having more sex with me?”  

Jeff *laughed*, shaking his head. “Jesus, Jodi.”  

Jodi crossed her arms, pouting playfully. “I *knew* it.”  

Jeff smirked, trailing his fingers lazily over her arm. “What can I say? You *inspire* me.”  

Jodi huffed. “Well, if I’m inspiring you, then why aren’t you dragging me into bed *immediately*?”  

Jeff chuckled, his voice softer now. “Because sometimes… I need to get things *out of my head* before I can get back in *my body*.”  

Jodi *exhaled*, rolling her eyes but smiling. “Ugh. Fine. I *guess* I can allow that.”  

Jeff grinned, pulling her closer. “You’re *so* magnanimous.”  

Jodi smirked, flicking his forehead lightly. “I *know*.”  

But as she curled into his arms, letting the night settle around them, she found herself watching him *closely*.  

Because *something* was brewing in that head of his.  

And she had a feeling it was going to be *big.*    
---  
---  
Jodi sat up, stretching her arms above her head before tilting her gaze toward Jeff, who still looked half-dazed, half-lost in thought.  

“Alright,” she said, tapping his thigh. “Put your cock up, get out of the driver’s seat, and we’re trading.”  

Jeff blinked like he was coming back into his body. “Huh?”  

Jodi smirked, reaching for the door handle. “You heard me. You’re in no shape to drive. I’ll take us home.”  

Jeff exhaled, dragging a hand down his face. “Yeah, okay.” He shifted slightly, reaching for something beside him. “Here—take your bra back.”  

Jodi glanced at the lacy bundle in his hand, then shook her head. “Nah, I don’t need it right now.”  

Jeff arched an eyebrow but didn’t argue, setting it aside as they both got out of the car and switched seats. Jodi slid into the driver’s side, adjusting her mirrors, while Jeff settled into the passenger seat, still looking *distracted*.  

Before she started the car, she glanced toward his center console. “Hey, can I get a swig of your water bottle?”  

Jeff smirked. “Thirsty?”  

Jodi shot him a dry look. “Your load was *really* salty, and I could use some *H2O*.”
