Jeff huffed a *breathless laugh* and handed her the bottle. “By all means.”  

Jodi took a few gulps, then set it down, smacking her lips. “Better.”  

Jeff watched her for a beat, his expression unreadable before he finally asked, “Are you mad right now?”  

Jodi snorted softly, shifting the car into drive. “Jeff.”  

“What?”  

She shook her head, keeping her eyes on the road. “I gave up being mad at you for this *more than a decade ago*.”  

Jeff let out a slow breath, glancing at her. “I know.”  

Jodi smirked. “And I stopped getting *creeped out* a long time ago, too.”  

Jeff exhaled, leaning back in his seat. “Jodi…”  

“I mean it,” she said, keeping her tone light but steady. “You go into these moods, and I know how it works. It’s like a switch flips, and suddenly you’re dividing your attention *six different ways*.”  

Jeff rubbed his forehead. “That obvious, huh?”  

Jodi chuckled, merging onto the main road. “You’re about as subtle as a brick through a window.”  

Jeff shook his head but smiled. “I really appreciate you.”  

Jodi grinned. “*Obviously.*”  

She took another turn, the house just a few minutes away.  

Jeff sighed, rolling his shoulders. “How *present* do I seem right now?”  

Jodi side-eyed him, amusement dancing in her expression. “You want the truth?”  

Jeff smirked. “Always.”  

Jodi exhaled through her nose, keeping her tone casual. “Right now? I’d say you’re *thirty percent* here, *seventy percent* somewhere else entirely.”  

Jeff let out a short laugh. “That bad?”  

Jodi grinned. “I’ve seen worse.”  

Jeff shook his head, watching her drive, and felt something *warm* settle in his chest.  

She *knew* him.  

Better than anyone.  

And she *stayed*.  

Jodi pulled into their neighborhood, then into the garage, cutting the engine. She looked over at him, tilting her head. “So? You staying in your head for the rest of the night, or can I have *some* of you back?”  

Jeff ran a hand through his hair, exhaling. “I’ll try.”  

Jodi smirked, unbuckling her seatbelt. “Good enough.”  

And just like that, she was leading him inside—knowing exactly how much of him she had right now, and *exactly* how to handle it.    
---  
---

At 4 AM, Jeff finally made his way into bed.  

His hands were *cramped* from writing. Thirty pages. *Front and back.*  

It wasn’t enough. It was *never* enough. But for now, his mind had *emptied* itself onto the page, and he was left feeling… raw.  

And then there was *Jodi.*  

She was lying on her side, peaceful, her breathing slow and steady.  

And naked.  

Of course she was.  

Jeff swallowed, taking her in—the gentle curve of her spine, the soft slope of her hip, the absolute *artwork* that was her ass.  

*Goddamn.*  

His fingertips twitched. *Focus, Jeff.*  

He’d *come here* with a plan. To *wake her*. To *spill everything*—every thought, every realization, every frantic scrawl on those pages.  

But now?  

Now he was debating something *entirely different*.  

Jodi had been waiting for him. He *knew* it.  

She might have fallen asleep, but she *always* left space for him. She *always* knew he’d come back eventually.  

And suddenly, *talking* didn’t seem nearly as important as just *being here*.  

Jeff exhaled slowly, shifting gears in his mind, forcing the *rest* of his consciousness into a *dormant* state.  

He pressed in closer, curling himself around her, letting his fingers rest lightly on her hip, absorbing her warmth.  

For a moment, his mind *fought* the stillness. The words were *right there*, demanding to be spoken, to be *shared*.  

But Jodi’s *presence* was louder.  

So, for once, he *let go.*  

He *shut down*—not entirely, but *enough*.  

The debate in his head faded into something *quieter*.  

Something *softer.*  

And when the thought crossed his mind to wake her? To move?  

He *ignored* it.  

Because this?  

This was *better.*  

For now, at least.  

And maybe—*just maybe*—he could hold onto her for *three more hours* before the world started demanding things from him again.    
---  
---  
Jodi lay perfectly still, breathing slow and even, *pretending* to be asleep.  

She wasn’t. Not even close.  

She’d been waiting for him.  

Not just *tonight*—but for *this exact moment*.  

For *him* to come to bed, his head still *roaring* with whatever storm had driven him to fill thirty pages, front and back, in the dead of night.  

She *knew* he wanted to talk.  

She *knew* he wanted to fuck.  

And then talk *some more.*  

That was *who* he was—who he’d *always* been. A man made of *momentum*, of *thoughts that spiraled outward* like wildfire, of *restlessness wrapped in a beautiful, maddening shell.*  

And yet… he was trying to be *absolutely still*.  

It was the *damnedest thing.*  

Like sleeping next to a *bomb* that had chosen—*against its very nature*—*not* to go off.  

She could *feel* it.  

Every muscle in his body was *tight with restraint*, every breath *held an extra beat too long*, every part of him *on edge* from the sheer *willpower* it was taking to just *be here* instead of *everywhere at once.*  

Jodi had never been more in *awe* of him.  

Because she *knew*—without *any* doubt—that he was doing this *for her*.  

That he was fighting every instinct to move, to wake her, to *pull her into the whirlwind* that was his brain right now.  

And God…  

She *loved* him for it.  

So she stayed still.  

She let him think he was winning this battle.  

And she let herself savor the quiet miracle of Jeff Turner at rest.  

Even if she *knew* it wouldn’t last.  

Because eventually?  

The bomb *always* went off.  

And Jodi?  

She’d be *ready* for it.    
---  
---  
For *two and a half hours*, they lay still.  

Jeff hadn’t spoken. Hadn’t moved.  

Jodi *felt* him, though.  

Three times, his body *betrayed* him—his breathing would hitch ever so slightly, his muscles would tense, and then she’d feel it: a *sharp pulse* of arousal, a physical reflex that had *nothing* to do with what his brain was doing.  

And then, each time… slowly, painfully… he went soft again.  

Jodi almost smirked. Almost.  

Instead, she kept her breathing steady, eyes barely open, staring at the *maddeningly* slow progression of the blue LED display on the bedside clock.  

Time was running at *a tenth* of its normal pace.  

She *swore* she had watched the minutes flick forward in *real-time*, like some kind of *slow torture designed specifically for her*.  

She wanted to shift. To stretch. To *move*.  

But she didn’t.  

Because *he* didn’t.  

Instead, she let her mind drift, wondering *when*—if ever—she’d be able to detect the first traces of the sun rising.  

That was *still* hours away.  

Jodi sighed internally, staring at the dim glow of the clock.  

*Maybe* she should listen to him talk more about his professional life.  

*Maybe.*  

God *knew* he wanted to tell her.  

But she’d resolved *years ago* that she *never* wanted to.  

She loved *him*, not the endless business side of things—the contracts, the negotiations, the *bureaucracy* of his work. It was *so much noise*, and she had spent years perfecting the art of *tuning it out* without making him feel ignored.  

But now, here at *zero dark hundred*, as she lay beside him in this strange, *loaded* silence… she *wavered*.  

Maybe she’d ask him.  

Maybe she’d let him tell her everything that had been rattling in his brain since he filled those *thirty pages front and back*.  

Maybe.  

She smirked slightly, finally allowing herself a blink.  

At this rate?  

Her morning conversation with Tara was going to be *interesting as hell*.    
---  
---  
Jodi *felt* it before she even glanced down.  

Jeff was growing hard *again*.  

It had been a slow, maddening process—his body cycling through arousal and restraint, his subconscious betraying him even as he tried to stay still.  

And now?  

Now she had *five minutes* before her alarm went off.  

Five minutes before the day *officially* started.  

Jodi inhaled slowly, pressing her lips together.  

*Time to act.*  

Her body had been on *edge* for hours. It wasn’t fair—*none* of this was fair, really—to be so close, so tangled together, yet caught in some kind of self-imposed *stasis*.  

She wanted to break it.  

And there was *one* surefire way to do that.  

She had done it before—*just* before he left for London, actually. That morning, she had barely thought about it—just turned over, lined him up, and slid down onto him before he was even fully awake.  

And Jeff?  

He had *loved* that.  

So why was she hesitating now?  

Jodi frowned slightly.  

Maybe it was because she could *feel* that this morning was different.  

Jeff had been *quiet* all night, his mind elsewhere, locked in some internal struggle that she couldn’t quite reach. And even though she *knew* he would respond to her, would *love* waking up inside her…  

She still wasn’t *entirely* sure she was making the right call.  

But…  

At the *very least*, he was going to *appreciate the effort.*  

And sometimes?  

That was enough.  

Jodi exhaled, shifting just slightly, preparing to roll over and *welcome the morning* the best way she knew how.  

The alarm would go off in five minutes.  

But Jeff was about to wake up *right now.*    
---  
---  
Jodi inhaled slowly, steadying herself.  

Then, with practiced ease, she shifted.  

A slow, *gentle* movement—just enough to roll onto him, to position herself exactly where she needed to be.  

Jeff stirred beneath her, a slow, unconscious inhale, his hands twitching slightly as she moved.  

And then—  

She *sank down.*  

A soft, *silent* connection, effortless in the quiet of the pre-dawn hour.  

Jeff let out a long, *deep* exhale, his body adjusting instinctively. His fingers flexed against the sheets, his breathing changed, but he was still caught somewhere in the fog between sleep and wakefulness.  

Jodi leaned down, brushing her lips against his ear.  

“Good morning, sunshine.”  

Jeff’s breath *hitched* slightly, and she felt his hands slide up her thighs, still *slow*—like his body was processing reality in *real time.*  

She smiled, pressing a finger gently against his lips.  

“Shush,” she murmured, barely above a whisper. “Don’t even *worry* about a thing.”  

Jeff made a low, contented sound in the back of his throat, his fingers tightening slightly against her skin.  

Jodi smirked.  

It wasn’t *the* most rational way to start the day.  

But it was the best one.  

And besides—  

She had *five minutes* before the alarm.  

And she planned to *use them well.*    
---  
---  
Jodi *fully* intended to make the most of her time.  

She had *five minutes* before the alarm went off—five minutes to set the *tone* for the day, to remind Jeff *exactly* what he’d been missing, and to ensure that *she* left for work feeling *completely* satisfied.  

And, as always, she was *nothing* if not efficient.  

As she moved, she leaned in, brushing her lips against his ear, her voice low and teasing.  

“Alright,” she murmured, “here’s my concept of operations.”  

Jeff let out a slow, *half-conscious* exhale, his fingers flexing slightly against her hips. He was *awake*, but *barely*—still caught somewhere in that *hazy, perfect space* between dream and reality.  

Jodi *grinned*.  

“Step one,” she whispered, shifting just slightly, savoring how his body *responded* to her. “*Insertion*—already complete.”  

Jeff made a low, appreciative sound in the back of his throat, his grip on her tightening just slightly.  

“Step two,” Jodi continued, her voice playful, “is *optimization of available time and resources.*”  

Jeff let out something *between* a chuckle and a groan, still too fogged by sleep to do much more than *react*.  

“Step three…” She leaned in further, lips brushing against his jaw. “*Mission success.*”  

Jeff exhaled sharply, his fingers digging in just slightly, *finally* catching up to what was happening.  

Jodi *smirked*, moving exactly the way she wanted, exactly the way she *knew* would turn his slow, sleepy wake-up into *something else entirely.*  

“Any questions?” she teased.  

Jeff’s voice was still rough with sleep, but there was a distinct *smirk* in it when he finally responded.  

“Just one,” he murmured. “Do I get to file an *after-action report*?”  

Jodi *laughed*, tilting her head back. “Oh, you *absolutely* will.”  

And with that, she got back to *work*.  

Time was *running out*, after all.  

And Jodi *never* left a job unfinished.    
---  
---  
Just as the alarm *chimed*, Jodi *finished the job.*  

Her movements stayed *calculated, deliberate*—she was a woman on a *mission*, after all, and she never left a job *half-done.*  

Jeff’s breath *hitched*, his fingers tightening against her hips as she carried him through the *last* moment. His body shuddered, and for a brief, *perfect* pause, there was *nothing* in the world but the sound of his exhale and the distant hum of the alarm.  

Jodi smirked.  

*Timed that perfectly.*  

Jeff’s eyes *fluttered* open—*mostly*.  

His body was *very much* awake, but his mind was still *trying* to catch up.  

Jodi *grinned* as she slid off of him, stretching luxuriously before reaching over to silence the alarm.  

Jeff exhaled slowly, blinking at her, still looking *half-dazed.* His voice was rough, *thick* with sleep.  

“…Did you?”  

Jodi arched an eyebrow, amused. “Did I *what*?”  

Jeff frowned slightly, trying to *process*, his brain still playing *catch-up*. “Did you…”  

His voice trailed off, his gaze flicking over her like he was *searching for proof*.  

Jodi smirked.  

She *loved* how scrambled he was.  

She leaned down, pressing a *slow* kiss against his jaw, her voice teasing.  

“Oh, honey,” she murmured, “you’ll be *wondering* about that all day.”  

Jeff groaned, *half in frustration, half in pure admiration.* “You *would* do this to me.”  

Jodi *beamed*, tossing the covers off as she swung her legs over the side of the bed. “*Obviously.*”  

Jeff sighed, running a hand over his face. “Jodi—”  

“Gotta shower,” she cut in, completely *unbothered*, standing and stretching her arms above her head. “Big morning ahead. Tara’s *definitely* going to have questions.”  

Jeff smirked, tilting his head against the pillow. “So am *I*.”  

Jodi tossed him a wink as she padded toward the bathroom. “Guess you’ll just have to *wait*.”  

And with that, she disappeared into the shower—*completely* satisfied, *perfectly* smug, and *fully* ready to start the day.  

Jeff groaned, rubbing his eyes.  

Jesus *Christ*, he loved that woman.    
---  
---  
Jodi let the hot water cascade over her, washing away the *evidence* of her morning mission with *thorough efficiency*.  

She’d half-expected to hear the shower door slide open, to feel Jeff’s arms snake around her waist, to be *pinned* against the tile for round two.  

But after a few minutes?  

Nothing.  

Jodi smirked to herself. *Yeah. He’s out cold.*  

She didn’t *begrudge* him.  

The man had spent *half the night* fighting his own instincts—restraining himself, *overthinking everything*, then burning through *thirty* pages of frantic scribbling before crawling into bed with her.  

Of *course* he collapsed.  

Still…  

That didn’t mean *she* had to let *all* of her morning momentum go to waste.  

She turned slightly, letting the *pulsing* water jets hit just the right spot.  

Jodi sighed, rolling her shoulders, indulging in the sensation for just a moment.  

*A reward,* she thought smugly. *For a job well done.*  

After all, *she* had gotten up. *She* had showered.  

*She* was about to go to work and *not* spend the day buried under the covers like a *very* satisfied, very *knocked-out* rockstar.  

She smirked to herself and reached for the loofah.  

After a few more moments of indulgence, Jodi rinsed off, wrung the water from her hair, and stepped out onto the bath mat, wrapping herself in a towel.  

Her eyes flicked toward the bedroom.  

If Jeff had kept his *notepad* handy—if it was sitting out in plain sight before he stumbled into bed—then *technically*, that meant she could *take a look*.  

Jodi smirked.  

It would be *a shame* if he left *thirty pages of his mind* just lying around where someone like *her* could get into them.  

And if he *hadn’t* put it away?  

Well.  

That was just *poor operational security on his part.*  

Jodi cracked the bathroom door open, glancing toward the bedside table, her smirk widening.  

Time to *investigate.*    
---  
---

Jodi popped a piece of sausage into her mouth, chewing thoughtfully as she flipped through Jeff’s *scrawled mess* of a notepad.  

She had *hoped* for something juicy—some *big revelation*, some stream-of-consciousness brilliance that would explain *exactly* what had kept him up all night.  

Instead?  

Chicken *scratch*.  

She squinted at the first few pages, turning them slightly like *maybe* the words would make more sense at a *different angle*.  

They did *not.*  

She sighed, plucking a grape from the bowl beside her and rolling it between her fingers before tossing it into her mouth.  

Ten pages in, and all she saw were what looked like *notations*. Maybe lyrics? Maybe *contract clauses*? Hell, for all she knew, it could be *both*.  

Jodi exhaled through her nose, flipping to the next page.  

Nothing.  

And the next?  

Still *nothing.*  

She sat back in her chair, crossing one leg over the other, tapping her fingers against the edge of the paper.  

The *Jeff Midnight Manifesto* was, so far, *deeply unimpressive.*  

Jodi huffed, reaching for another grape.  

Maybe she’d have *better luck* if she skipped ahead.  

Because *surely*—*surely*—at some point in those thirty pages, Jeff had *actually* written something worth deciphering.  

And if he *had*?  

Well.  

It was only *fair* she find out before *he* woke up.    
---  
---  
Jodi chewed the last of her sausage as she flipped *deeper* into Jeff’s late-night scribbles.  

And then—finally—she saw it.  

*Lyrics.*  

She leaned in, eyes scanning the lines quickly, trying to *piece together* what he had been working on for *hours*.  

The first thing she noticed?  

*Red heels.*  

Jodi smirked, reaching for another grape.  

*He’s trying to write a song about me again.*  

It wasn’t the *first* time—Jeff had done this before, usually when she was *least expecting it*, scribbling lyrics about her into the margins of notebooks, or demoing half-finished melodies that he’d never fully admit were inspired by *her*.  

But this time?  

This time, she was seeing *every* draft.  

And—God *bless* him—there were at *least twenty* different versions.  

Some lines were scratched out so aggressively she could *barely* read them. Others had entire sections rewritten *four or five times*.  

But the thing that stood out the *most*?  

*The names.*  

Her name had been there—*Jodi*—in *several* places.  

But each time, he had *crossed it out.*  

Sometimes, he had replaced it with *“girl.”*  

Other times, with *“Sam.”*  

Jodi stared at the pages, rolling a grape between her fingers.  

*Sam?*  

She wasn’t even *mad* about it.  

She was just *fascinated*.  

Because Jeff—*the man who had been in bed with her mere hours ago*—had been up, *alone*, torturing himself over *exactly* how personal he wanted to make this song.  

Jodi exhaled, shaking her head.  

The *constant push and pull*. The *eternal debate* of how much of *her*—how much of *them*—he was willing to *show the world*.  

And the fact that, even after a *decade together*, he was *still* trying to figure that out.  

Jodi set the notebook down, popping the grape into her mouth as she leaned back in her chair.  

This was going to make for a *very* interesting conversation when he woke up.  

And an *even better* one later with Tara.    
---  
---  
When Jodi *strolled* into the office *fifteen minutes early*, carrying a tray of still-warm cinnamon muffins, the room *went silent.*  

Tara, Naomi, and Mike *stared* at her like she had just descended from the heavens or, more likely, been *body-snatched* overnight.  

Jodi smirked, setting the tray down in the break room like it was *any other Tuesday*. “Good morning, *sunshines*.”  

Mike squinted at her. “What… *the hell* is going on?”  

Naomi, wide-eyed, leaned in. “You’re *early*.”  

Tara, arms crossed, gave her a *once-over*. “You *baked*?”  

Jodi grinned, reaching for a mug from the coffee station. “I *bought*.”  

Tara tilted her head, *examining* Jodi like a puzzle that needed solving. “You *never* bring breakfast. Hell, you barely make it in on time, *and* you’re glowing.”  

Naomi gasped. “Oh my *God.*”  

Mike blinked. “Jesus. You got *laid* this morning, didn’t you?”  

Jodi, pouring her coffee, *laughed*. “Is that *really* your first guess?”  

Mike scoffed, unwrapping a muffin. “I mean, you *literally* showed up late, flushed, and starving *yesterday*—which, you made *abundantly* clear was because of Jeff.” He took a bite, chewing thoughtfully. “So if you’re *this* put together and even *early* today, either you *didn’t* get any, or you got so much that you’re now in *some kind of zen-like state*.”  

Tara, hand on her hip, studied Jodi closely. “Well?”  

Jodi took a slow sip of coffee, *thoroughly* enjoying the attention.  

She had, in fact, *timed* her morning *perfectly*.  

She had *handled* Jeff. She had showered, dressed, *discovered* his songwriting struggle, and still made it in *early*—with baked goods, no less.  

If that wasn’t an *achievement*, what was?  

She set her mug down, looking at her three coworkers with *peak* smugness.  

“I’ll just say this,” she said, picking up a muffin and tearing it in half.  

“Sometimes? *Mornings just go your way.*”  

Tara groaned, Naomi *squealed*, and Mike muttered, “I *hate* you.”  

Jodi grinned, took a bite of muffin, and leaned back against the counter.  

This day was off to a *fantastic* start.    
---  
---  
Naomi tilted her head, furrowing her brow as she reached for a muffin. “Hey, Tara?”  

Tara was mid-sip of her coffee when she answered, distractedly. “Mm?”  

“Why does Jeff *never* come to the bank?”  

Tara *froze*.  

Slowly, she lowered her coffee, turned her head, and stared at Naomi like she had just asked why the *sky was blue*.  

“…You *cannot* be serious.”  

Naomi blinked, confused. “What? I *never* see him here. He never picks Jodi up, never stops by. Like, does he work weird hours or something?”  

Tara *smacked her forehead*.  

Jodi, who had been thoroughly *enjoying* her coffee and muffin while watching this unfold, *barely* contained her smirk.  

*Oh, this is gonna be good.*  

Tara, still staring at Naomi in utter *bewilderment*, shook her head. “Naomi. Sweet, *sweet* Naomi.”  

Naomi frowned. “What?”  

Tara sighed, rubbing her temples. “How do I even *break* this to you?”  

Jodi, sipping her coffee, finally decided to interject. “Tara, I *think* what we have here… is a *Naïve Coworker Moment™*.”  

Tara *sighed dramatically*. “I think you’re right.”  

Naomi, now *fully* frustrated, threw her hands up. “Okay, *seriously*—what am I missing?”  

Tara, *still shaking her head*, pulled out her phone, tapped a few times, and then held it up to the group.  

A familiar song filled the break room.  

The intro alone made one of the tellers *immediately* perk up. “Oh my *God*,” she gasped. “That’s *Snowglobe*! That’s my *sister’s* all-time favorite song!”  

Naomi’s expression *did not* change.  

She *knew* this song.  

It was *everywhere*.  

But—  

She frowned. “Wait. *That’s old.*”  

Tara scoffed. “It’s *eleven years* old.”  

Naomi *gasped*, gripping the counter. “Oh my *God.*”  

Jodi *grinned*, watching the realization *finally* dawn on her.  

Naomi looked up, *horrified*. “That means I was in *middle school* when it came out.”  

Tara groaned, rubbing her face. “Jesus Christ, you are *so* fucking young.”  

Naomi, now looking *deeply overwhelmed*, turned back to Jodi.  

“Wait. So… Jeff is *that* Jeff?”  

Jodi smirked, picking up another muffin. “The one and only.”  

Naomi *gasped again*, *finally* piecing it together. “And that’s why he doesn’t come to the bank?”  

Tara threw up her hands. “*YES, NAOMI!* BECAUSE HE’S A *ROCKSTAR*.”  

Naomi, still in *mild shock*, exhaled heavily. “Jesus.”  

Mike, who had been quietly eating his muffin through *this entire debacle*, finally spoke up. “I *cannot* believe you didn’t know.”  

Naomi groaned. “I *don’t* pay attention to this stuff, okay?”  

Jodi *chuckled*, standing up and stretching. “Well, now you do.”  

Naomi *sighed*, shaking her head. “God. I *really* need to reevaluate my life.”  

Jodi winked. “Don’t worry. We’re *here to help*.”  

Tara *laughed*. “Oh, this is *gonna be fun*.”  

And just like that, the break room had its *next big topic of discussion*.    

---  
Naomi, still looking *mildly overwhelmed*, ran a hand through her hair.  

“Okay. Wait. Just—*wait*.” She exhaled sharply, then looked at Jodi, wide-eyed. “I need a *little* bit of help here, please.”  

Jodi, sipping her coffee, raised an eyebrow. “With what?”  

Naomi gestured *wildly*. “Does this mean he’s, like… *rich*?”  

Jodi, without missing a beat, deadpanned, “*Maybe.*”  

Tara, who had *zero patience* for this level of cluelessness, rolled her eyes and grabbed her phone. “Oh, for *fuck’s sake*, Naomi.”  

With a few quick taps, she pulled up a Forbes article and shoved the screen toward Naomi.  

“Here. *Read*.”  

Naomi leaned in, squinting at the screen.  

Her lips moved silently as she processed the numbers.  

Then—  

She *swore*, loudly. “That’s a *lot* of damn zeroes.”  

Mike, peering over Naomi’s shoulder, whistled lowly. “Jesus *Christ*, Jodi.”  

Naomi blinked. “That’s more money than, like… *our bank’s entire market capitalization*.”  

Tara let out a *deep* sigh, rubbing her temples. “Yes, *Naomi*. That’s why he’s *never here* and why we *never talk about this shit*.”  

Naomi looked back at Jodi, *completely* baffled.  

Jodi, *still completely unbothered*, took another bite of her muffin. “I mean, he still steals *my* fries.”  

Naomi *gasped* again, as if that was somehow *the most shocking part of all of this.*  

Tara groaned, throwing her hands up. “I *cannot* with you today.”  

Jodi *smirked*, wiping a crumb from her lip. “You *love* me.”  

Tara sighed dramatically. “Unfortunately, yes.”  

Mike shook his head, muttering, “I need another muffin for this shit.”  

And just like that, Naomi’s entire *understanding of reality* had *shifted*.  

This was *definitely* going to be the office’s new favorite conversation for *weeks*.    

---  
Naomi, still reeling, clasped her hands together *dramatically*.  

“Jodi, *please*.”  

Jodi raised an eyebrow, taking another slow sip of her coffee. “Please *what*?”  

Naomi leaned in, eyes wide with *pure, unfiltered curiosity*. “Let’s go out for beers after work. I need to *learn everything*.”  

Jodi smirked, tilting her head. “Everything?”  

Naomi *nodded aggressively.* “Yes! *Everything!*” She gestured wildly. “How did you *meet*? How does this *even work*? Have you *ever* used a black card? Do you fly private? What’s the *craziest* thing you’ve ever done together? Have you *met other famous people*? Did Jeff *ever* buy a tiger?”  

Tara groaned, rubbing her face. “Jesus, Naomi.”  

Naomi spun toward her. “WHAT? These are *valid* questions!”  

Jodi exhaled, amused but *hesitant*. “I don’t know…”  

Naomi *clutched* her arm, her voice pleading. “*Jodi.*”  

Jodi sighed, pulling out her phone. As she absently scrolled through her contacts, she tapped Jeff’s name, letting GPS locate him.  

Within seconds, a small blue dot pinged his location.  

He was already on *I-75 South.*  

Jodi blinked.  

He was *leaving* again.  

She sighed softly, thumb hovering over his contact for a second before locking her phone.  

So much for *tonight.*  

She glanced back up at Naomi, who was still staring at her like a *kid begging for candy.*  

Jodi smirked, tilting her head. “I’ll *think* about it.”  

Naomi *clapped* her hands together, beaming. “That’s not a *no*!”  

Tara, rolling her eyes, muttered, “God help us.”  

Jodi just *laughed*, shaking her head.  

Tonight was *wide open*.  

And at this rate?  

It was going to be *entertaining as hell*.    

---  

A couple of hours had passed.  

The office buzzed with the usual mid-morning energy—emails flying, keyboards clacking, phones ringing. Jodi had kept busy, had kept moving, had *kept up the act* well enough.  

But Tara had *known* her too long to be fooled.  

So when their paths crossed in the break room, Tara didn’t waste time.  

She poured herself a coffee, leaned casually against the counter, and *zeroed in*.  

“Alright, Jodi.” She took a sip. “How was your morning?”  

Jodi smirked, tearing off a small piece of her muffin. “You were *there*, Tara.”  

Tara rolled her eyes. “Uh-huh. And how are you *doing* now?”  

Jodi shrugged, popping the muffin piece into her mouth. “Same as I was two hours ago.”  

Tara arched an eyebrow. “*Right.*” She let the word hang in the air for a second, then tilted her head. “You know, I *saw* your mood shift earlier.”  

Jodi didn’t flinch, didn’t *react*—but she did pause, just for a second, before reaching for her coffee. “Did you, now?”  

Tara nodded, her gaze steady. “Yeah.” She crossed her arms. “You put up a *good* front, Jodi. You always do. But I know you.”  

Jodi took a slow sip, swallowing deliberately.  

Tara continued. “You were *fine*—flushed and smug and *peak* Jodi—until you checked your phone. Then, *poof*. Something changed.”  

Jodi exhaled through her nose. *Damn Tara and her freakishly good radar.*  

Still, she tried to play it off. “Maybe I just remembered I left the dryer running.”  

Tara *scoffed*. “Oh, *please*. If I believed that, I’d have to believe you also *do your own laundry*.”  

Jodi smirked, but it wasn’t quite as sharp as usual. “Fair point.”  

Tara softened, setting her mug down. “So. You wanna tell me what’s actually up?”  

Jodi *considered* it for a second.  

She wasn’t *upset*, exactly. Not mad. Not *hurt*.  

Just… *off-balance*.  

Jeff leaving town *again* wasn’t a *big deal*. She was *used to it.* Hell, she had *literally* spent the morning setting Naomi’s brain on fire with how *insane* her life was.  

But for some reason, seeing that little blue GPS dot moving south on I-75 had made her stomach *tighten* in a way she hadn’t expected.  

Still, she didn’t want to *dwell* on it.  

So instead, she just sighed, giving Tara a *look*. “It’s nothing, really. Jeff’s already on the road again.”  

Tara nodded slowly, like she was *waiting* for more.  

Jodi *knew* the look.  

So she smirked, *just to keep things light.* “I mean, I *was* hoping to see what else he wrote in that ridiculous legal pad of his before he ran off again. But now? Guess I’ll have to *wait*.”  

Tara snorted. “Jesus. You really *dug* into it, huh?”  

Jodi grinned. “Oh, *deeply*.”  

Tara smirked, picking up her coffee again. “Well, at least that means we have *plenty* to talk about over drinks tonight.”  

Jodi sighed dramatically. “I *never* agreed to that.”  

Tara grinned. “And *yet*… you haven’t said no.”  

Jodi shook her head, chuckling. “God help me.”  

Tara winked. “That’s what friends are for.”  

And with that, the moment *shifted*—not completely, but *enough*.  

Jodi wasn’t *entirely* back to herself.  

But she wasn’t *alone* in it, either.  

And for now?  

That was *enough.*    

---  
By the time they settled into a high-top at their favorite bar, Jodi was *noticeably* lighter.  

She had a beer in one hand, a plate of loaded nachos in front of her, and absolutely *zero* interest in letting anything weigh her down tonight.  

Tara, never one to miss a thing, *clocked it immediately*.  

She sipped her drink, watching Jodi with a smirk. “Alright, explain this one to me.”  

Jodi, mid-bite, glanced up. “Explain *what*?”  

Tara gestured vaguely. “*This.* Your mood. I *watched* you shift gears like a damn race car today. You started the morning smug, then got all quiet, then went *back* to smug, and now you’re *fully* in ‘beers and laughter’ mode.”  

Jodi chewed thoughtfully, swallowed, then shrugged. “I take things that are on the *good* side and try to dwell there.”  

Tara raised an eyebrow. “That *simple*, huh?”  

Jodi smirked. “It *can* be.” She popped a jalapeño into her mouth. “Look, life’s *always* throwing shit at you. Jeff’s out of town again? Fine. *That sucks.* But I can either sit around feeling all mopey about it, *or* I can get beers with my best friend and my new favorite case study in naïveté.”  

Naomi, who had been happily sipping her fruity cocktail, *blinked*. “Wait. *Me?*”  

Jodi grinned. “Oh, *absolutely* you.”  

Naomi groaned. “I will *never* live today down, will I?”  

Tara smirked, clinking her glass against Naomi’s. “Not a chance in *hell*, sweetheart.”  

Jodi chuckled, stretching her legs out under the table. “See? *This* is good. So I’m gonna sit here, drink my beer, eat these nachos, and enjoy the hell out of my night.”  

Tara eyed her for another second, then smiled. “I *respect* that.”  

Jodi winked. “I *know*.”  

And with that, the conversation shifted *again*—back to easy banter, ridiculous stories, and *absolutely* no room for anything less than a damn *good* time.  

Because Jodi?  

She *chose* joy.  

And tonight, that was *all* she planned to dwell on.     
Jodi leaned back in her chair, stretching her arms above her head before taking another slow sip of her beer.  

Then, with a smirk, she set her glass down, tapped the table twice for emphasis, and looked Naomi *dead in the eye.*  

“Alright, kid,” she said. “Let’s do this.”  

Naomi blinked. “Do *what*?”  

Jodi tilted her head. “You’ve been *dying* to interrogate me all day, so I’m giving you *one shot*—one night, one *single* opportunity to get all your juiciest questions answered.” She smirked. “After that? *We never speak of this again.*”  

Naomi’s *eyes widened* in pure excitement. “Are you *serious?*”  

Tara groaned, rubbing her temples. “Oh, *Jesus Christ*, Jodi, do you *really* want to open this can of worms?”  

Jodi *grinned*. “Tara, you *know* me. I live for *this*.”  

Tara sighed. “Yeah, yeah. Don’t say I didn’t warn you.”  

Naomi *squeaked*, grabbed her phone, and *immediately* started typing furiously in her Notes app. “Oh my *God*, give me *two minutes*—I need to get *organized*.”  

Jodi snorted, stealing a nacho. “Jesus, Naomi. Have you *been drafting* this list?”  

Naomi, *dead serious*, nodded. “Oh, *absolutely*.”  

Tara took a long sip of her drink. “You *created a monster*, Jodi.”  

Jodi smirked. “I *always* do.”  

Naomi, *buzzing with anticipation*, took a deep breath, looked up from her phone, and *beamed*.  

“Okay. I’m *ready.*”  

Jodi smirked, drumming her fingers on the table. “Alright, hit me with your *best shot*.”  

And with that, the *real* fun began.    

---  
Jodi leaned in, resting her elbows on the table, eyes glinting with *pure mischief*.  

She took a slow sip of her beer, set it down, and smirked.  

Then, in the *friendliest* tone possible, she said—  

*"Hit me, bitch."*  

Naomi *squealed* in excitement, bouncing in her seat. “Oh my *God*, this is *actually* happening.”  

Tara groaned, running a hand down her face. “This is going to be *chaos.*”  

Jodi *grinned*. “It’s gonna be *epic*.”  

Naomi clutched her phone like it held the *secrets of the universe*. “Okay, okay, okay.” She took a *deep breath*, steeling herself. “First question.”  

Tara, taking a *very* large sip of her drink, muttered, “God help us all.”  

Jodi smirked.  

Because this?  

This was about to be *embarrassing as sin.*  

And she was *so here for it.*    

---  
Jodi leaned forward, propping her chin on her hand, eyes twinkling.  

“Alright, Naomi. What’s your *first* question?” She smirked, then added, “And *no*, he’s never bought a tiger.”  

Naomi, who had just opened her mouth, *gasped* and slapped her phone onto the table. “HOW DID YOU *KNOW*?!”  

Tara nearly choked on her drink, coughing as she smacked her chest. “Jesus *Christ*, Naomi.”  

Jodi *cackled*. “Because you look like the kind of person who thinks that’s a *real* possibility.”  

Naomi threw up her hands. “I mean, come *on*! He’s *insanely rich*, a little reckless, and I’ve *heard* things about celebrities and exotic pets!”  

Tara shook her head. “For the *last time*, Naomi, Jeff is not Mike Tyson.”  

Naomi *huffed*, crossing her arms. “Fine. *No tigers.*” She picked up her phone again. “Moving on.”  

Jodi grinned, tilting her head. “Oh, I *cannot* wait for this.”  

Tara sighed, taking a long sip of her drink. “This is going to be *a nightmare.*”  

And with that, the *real* interrogation began.    

---  
Naomi, *wide-eyed and vibrating with excitement*, glanced down at her notes. “Okay, *first* question.” She cleared her throat dramatically. “*How did you and Jeff meet?*”  

Jodi took a sip of her beer, setting it down with a smirk. “At a regional meeting concerning the local water district.”  

Naomi blinked. “…*What?*”  

Tara snorted. “Oh, yeah. Real *romantic* setting.”  

Jodi grinned. “Jeff was giving a *presentation*—raising venture capital. You know, *big important business stuff*.”  

Naomi *gasped*. “Wait. I thought he was a *rock star*?”  

Jodi *deadpanned*, “Jesus, girl. He’s also a *venture capitalist* nowadays.”  

Naomi threw up her hands. “HOW DO YOU JUST *DROP* THAT INTO THE CONVERSATION?”  

Tara sighed. “Because it’s *Jodi*.”  

Jodi smirked, unfazed. “Anyway. I saw his speech, and we ended up talking for *four hours* afterward.”  

Naomi, still *reeling*, gawked at her. “*Four hours?!*”  

Jodi nodded. “Yep. We were having too good of a time to stop and fuck.”  

Naomi *choked on her drink.*  

Tara closed her eyes, pinching the bridge of her nose. “Oh my *God.*”  

Naomi, still coughing, thumped her chest. “W-WAIT. Hold on.” She *wheezed*, struggling for composure. “You mean you had *sex* with him before even going on a *date*?!”  

Jodi *grinned*, utterly shameless. “Well, we *did* talk and talk for an *entire night* first, *at least*.”  

Naomi’s mouth fell *open*.  

Tara, taking a *very* large sip of her drink, muttered, “I *love* how that’s supposed to make it *better.*”  

Jodi shrugged. “Hey. It worked out.”  

Naomi *slammed* her hands on the table. “YOU LIVE IN A *MOVIE*, JODI.”  

Jodi *cackled*, stealing another nacho.  

Tara groaned. “I *cannot* with this conversation.”  

Naomi, still *processing*, inhaled deeply and refocused on her phone. “Okay. Next question.”  

Jodi smirked, leaning in. “Oh, *this is fun.*”  

And just like that, the *legendary* interrogation continued.    

---  
Naomi, still *flustered* from Jodi’s last answer, scrolled furiously through her notes before landing on the *next* burning question.  

“Okay, okay,” she said, inhaling deeply. “But *how does this even work*?”  

Jodi tilted her head. “Define *this*.”  

Naomi *gestured wildly*. “YOU. HIM. Your *relationship*! You’re dating a man who’s, like… *everywhere* all the time. How do you *deal*?”  

Jodi exhaled, leaning back in her chair. “Simple. I try *really hard* to ignore that he’s *famous*, *rich*, and that he sleeps with a *lot* of women.”  

Naomi *choked*. “I—WHAT?!”  

Tara *froze*, raising an eyebrow. “*Excuse me?*”  

Jodi shrugged, reaching for a nacho. “I mean, he’s on TV every other day. If you listen to *any* sort of modern rock stream, you’re gonna hear his band play.” She popped the chip in her mouth. “It’s just *easier* if I don’t think about it.”  

Naomi’s *jaw dropped.* “So he—he *cheats on you?!*”  

Jodi smirked. “That would imply we have a *traditional* relationship. We *don’t*.”  

Naomi *blinked rapidly*, trying to *process*. “So… you don’t care?”  

Jodi shrugged. “It’s not *about* caring or not caring. Jeff can’t commit, so neither do I.”  

Tara, swirling her drink, *narrowed her eyes slightly*. “And you’re really *okay* with that?”  

Jodi smirked, tilting her head. “Tara, *when* have I *ever* seemed unhappy?”  

Tara sighed. “I mean… fair.”  

Naomi, still *struggling*, buried her face in her hands. “This is *so* beyond my comprehension.”  

Jodi *grinned*, taking another sip of her beer. “It’s actually *pretty comfortable* doing as I please. No expectations, no *obligations*. I see him when I see him, I do what I *want* when he’s gone.”  

Naomi lifted her head, still *bewildered*. “And *he’s* cool with this?”  

Jodi shrugged again. “Seems like it. He’s *in and out* so often, I doubt he has the *capacity* for anything more. Besides—” she smirked, “he lives about *a hundred miles south*, if and *when* he’s even in the *USA*.”  

Naomi blinked. “So… you’re telling me…” She exhaled, running a hand through her hair. “That you two just… *float*?”  

Jodi smirked. “We *exist*.”  

Tara shook her head, sipping her drink. “You *live in a goddamn fever dream.*”  

Jodi *laughed*, stealing another nacho. “And yet, you *love* hearing about it.”  

Naomi groaned, grabbing her cocktail. “I’m gonna need *another drink*.”  

Jodi winked. “Oh, *same*.”  

And with that, the *most insane* conversation of Naomi’s life continued.    

---  
Naomi, still *wrapping her head around* this entire situation, took a *long* sip of her drink. “Okay, so you’re saying you just… *deal* with it?”  

Jodi smirked, but it didn’t quite *reach* her eyes. “It’s not about *dealing*, Naomi. It’s about *survival*.”  

Tara, who had been watching Jodi *closely* this whole time, finally leaned in. “Meaning?”  

Jodi exhaled, swirling her beer bottle in her hand. “I *hate* the no-strings part.”  

Naomi *gasped*, slamming her hand on the table. “THEN WHY DO YOU *DO IT?!*”  

Jodi gave her a *look*—a mix of amusement and something *harder* beneath it. “Because if I let *jealousy* in—*even one inch*—it would *kill* me.”  

The table went *quiet* for half a beat.  

Naomi *blinked rapidly*, trying to process. “So you just… *shove it down*?”  

Jodi huffed a soft, humorless laugh. “Something like that.”  

Tara tilted her head, voice measured. “And does that actually *work*?”  

Jodi smirked again, this time with just a *hint* of sharpness. “Define *work*.”  

Tara exhaled, sipping her drink. “I mean, *are you happy?*”  

Jodi hesitated—just for a fraction of a second.  

Then she grinned, *too easily*, and leaned back. “Happiness is relative, babe.”  

Naomi groaned, running a hand through her hair. “I *swear* to God, Jodi, you *break my brain*.”  

Jodi laughed, taking another sip of beer. “That’s *half the fun*.”  

Tara, still watching her, just shook her head. “You *exhaust* me.”  

Jodi smirked. “And yet, here you are.”  

And with that, the tension *eased*—for now.  

But Tara?  

She *wasn’t* done asking questions.  

Not by a long shot.    

---  
Naomi, now three-quarters into her drink and fully *committed* to learning *everything*, scrolled through her notes again.  

“Okay,” she said, eyes glinting. “Here’s one—*does he have a black card?*”  

Jodi smirked, reaching for a nacho. “Yep.”  

Naomi’s *eyes* went wide. “Like… a *real* one?”  

Jodi dipped her chip in salsa. “Yep.”  

Naomi *leaned in*, voice barely above a whisper. “And it’s *actually* black?”  

Jodi chewed, swallowed, then deadpanned, “Naomi. It is *100% black as night*. Made from *laminated obsidian* with a *chip* and a *magstripe*.”  

Naomi *gasped*, gripping her drink like she had just heard a *forbidden secret*.  

Tara, unimpressed, took a sip of her beer. “It’s *a credit card*, Naomi. Not a horcrux.”  

Naomi ignored her, *still reeling*. “Oh my *God*. That’s so *obnoxiously* fancy.”  

Jodi *grinned*. “It *is*. But he’s *never* flashy about it.” She picked up her beer and shrugged. “If anything, when he’s with me, he tries *really* hard *not* to show he’s got any kind of money at all.”  

Naomi frowned, tilting her head. “Wait… *why?*”  

Jodi exhaled, tapping her fingers against the table. “Because he’s *naturally defensive* about all that stuff.”  

Tara raised an eyebrow. “Defensive *how*?”  

Jodi smirked. “Well, for starters, if someone so much as *mentions* his net worth in conversation, he acts like they just insulted his *mother*.”  

Naomi *snorted*. “Oh my *God*, that’s hilarious.”  

Jodi chuckled. “It *is*. But also? He’s *weirdly good* at blending in. Like, we can walk into a gas station, and unless you *already know who he is*, he just looks like some dude in a hoodie buying a Red Bull.”  

Tara hummed thoughtfully. “So… he downplays it on purpose?”  

Jodi nodded. “Yep. And when we’re together, it’s like he *tries* to pretend none of it even exists.” She smirked. “Which is funny, because, you know… *it does*.”  

Naomi *squealed*. “JODI. HE’S A *MYSTERY BILLIONAIRE*.”  

Jodi *cackled*. “He’s *not a billionaire*, Naomi.”  

Naomi *huffed*, crossing her arms. “Well, he *might as well be*.”  

Tara shook her head, sipping her beer. “You know, the fact that this conversation has gone from ‘infuriating relationship dynamics’ to ‘obsidian credit cards’ is *very on brand* for you, Jodi.”  

Jodi grinned. “I *do* like to keep things interesting.”  

Naomi exhaled dramatically. “I need another drink.”  

Jodi smirked, raising her glass. “*Same.*”  

And just like that, the chaos *continued*.    

---  
Naomi, now *fully invested* in this ridiculous interrogation, scrolled eagerly through her phone. “Alright, next question—how often do you two *fly private*?”  

Jodi smirked. “Well, *he* exclusively flies private.”  

Naomi *gasped*. “OF COURSE HE DOES.”  

Jodi *chuckled*, taking a slow sip of her beer before continuing. “If *I* go somewhere with him, I’ll fly private too. But…” she shrugged, “that happens, like, *once a year*—*maybe*—because I try really hard to *avoid* the celebrity scene.”  

Naomi’s *jaw dropped*. “Wait, WAIT. You have access to *full luxury jet travel* and you *actively avoid it?!*”  

Jodi grinned. “Yep.”  

Naomi *gawked* at her, looking personally *offended* on behalf of every economy-class traveler on Earth.  

Tara *snorted*, swirling her drink. “Yeah, Naomi, because nothing says *luxury* like dodging camera flashes and getting grilled by reporters about which designer shoes you’re wearing.”  

Naomi *ignored* her. “Jodi. *Be so serious right now.*”  

Jodi smirked. “Oh, I’m *dead serious*.”  

Naomi *exhaled* dramatically. “Okay, but, like… what’s it *like*? Being on a *private jet*?”  

Jodi shrugged. “Depends on the trip.”  

Naomi *leaned in*, eyes gleaming. “Give me a *story*.”  

Jodi sighed, setting down her beer. “Alright. First time I ever flew private? Earned my *Mile High Club* membership.”  

Naomi *choked*. “JODI—”  

Tara groaned, pinching the bridge of her nose. “Of *course* you did.”  

Jodi grinned, taking another sip before casually adding, “Also, I’ve crossed the *entire* Atlantic Ocean completely naked before.”  

Naomi *slammed* her hands onto the table. “I—I don’t—WHAT?!”  

Tara’s *eyebrows shot up*. “Oh, that’s a *new one*.”  

Jodi shrugged. “Long-haul flight. No other passengers. No turbulence. *Seemed like a waste of good legroom.*”  

Naomi *squeaked*. “You *lived* a full life before I even knew sex could *happen* in places other than a bed.”  

Jodi smirked. “We *all* have our journeys, Naomi.”  

Naomi groaned, burying her face in her hands.  

Tara shook her head, sipping her beer. “And you say you *avoid* this lifestyle.”  

Jodi huffed. “I *do*! I *actively* try to be a *normal person*.” She exhaled, rolling her shoulders. “Which is why when Jeff asked me to go to *Oslo* in a couple weeks, I told him I’d probably *pass*.”  

Naomi *gasped* and shot back up. “WHY WOULD YOU PASS ON *NORWAY*?!”  

Jodi *laughed*. “Because I have *a job*, Naomi.”  

Naomi *waved this off* like it was a minor inconvenience. “Girl, you work at a *bank*! Take some PTO and go be rich and naked in *Europe*!”  

Tara groaned, rubbing her temples. “*Please* don’t encourage her.”  

Jodi just *smirked*, leaning back in her chair.  

“I’ll think about it.”  

And from the *way* she said it?  

Tara *knew* she was already reconsidering.    

---  
Naomi, still *reeling* from the last revelation, took a deep breath. “Okay. Now I *have* to know. What’s the *craziest* thing you’ve ever done with Jeff?”  

Jodi took a slow sip of her beer, exhaled, and said casually, “Had a threesome.”  

Tara *immediately* groaned, setting her drink down with a *thud.* “Oh my *God*, Jodi, *do not* tell this story.”  

Jodi smirked. “Why not? Naomi’s gonna look it up on *Wikipedia* anyway.”  

Naomi blinked. “Wait. *Wikipedia?*”  

Jodi stretched her arms above her head. “Yep. I accepted an invitation from Jeff to have a threesome with Kathy Espich.”  

Naomi *gasped*, practically *vibrating* in her seat. “*THE* Kathy Espich?! The model?!”  

Jodi nodded, sipping her beer.  

Naomi *shrieked*, clutching her chest. “YOU HAD A THREE-WAY WITH A *SUPERMODEL?*”  

Jodi sighed, swirling the last bit of her beer in the glass. “Yeah, well… Kathy had a stroke the *next day* while we were driving together in Boston.”  

The table went *dead silent*.  

Jodi’s voice softened, just slightly. “She never regained consciousness.”  

Naomi’s mouth hung *open*, all her excitement *immediately* draining into shock. “Wait. What?”  

Tara exhaled, rubbing her forehead. “Jesus, Jodi.”  

Jodi sighed again, rubbing the back of her neck. “Yeah. *That* part was pretty *fucked up*.”  

Naomi, still trying to *process*, looked between them. “So you’re telling me… you had a *threesome with a world-famous model* and then… she *died the next day?*”  

Jodi pressed her lips together. “Not *instantly*.” She gestured vaguely. “We were in a car, driving through Boston, and she started seizing. She was in a coma for, like, three months. Then she passed.”  

Naomi *stared*. “That’s… *insane*.”  

Tara sighed. “I *told* you not to tell this story.”  

Jodi huffed a laugh, setting her beer down. “It’s fine. She was a *good* person. Wild as hell, but good.”  

Naomi exhaled, running a hand through her hair. “Jesus. I was *not* prepared for this.”  

Jodi smirked. “I *did* warn you.”  

Naomi shook her head, still looking *overwhelmed*. “I don’t even know what to *say*.”  

Jodi chuckled, then—because she *was who she was*—added, “Also, I had sex with an airline attendant on the way to Boston, too.”  

Naomi *choked*. “WHAT?!”  

Tara sighed. “*I’ve* heard this part.”  

Naomi slammed her hands on the table. “HOW IS THAT JUST A *SIDE NOTE?!*”  

Jodi grinned, popping a nacho into her mouth. “Because it’s not the *craziest* thing that happened that week.”  

Naomi *collapsed* into her hands. “I *cannot* keep up with your life.”  

Tara, sipping her beer, muttered, “*Welcome to my world.*”  

Jodi smirked, leaning back.  

“Next question?”    

---  
Naomi, still *stuck* on the last revelation, picked up her cocktail with *both hands*, took a *long, slow sip*, and then set it down *very carefully*.  

Then she *whispered*, “Jodi. Please tell me that whole thing was *drama-free*.”  

Jodi *laughed*, shaking her head. “Oh, *Naomi*.”  

Naomi *whimpered*, like she *already knew* the answer was going to wreck her.  

Jodi exhaled, leaning her elbows onto the table. “One of the *problems* was that Kathy was determined to have used *cocaine* within a day of being admitted to the ER.”  

Naomi *had a full-body reaction*—*eyes wide, hands clamped over her mouth, physically pushing back from the table like the information might strike her down.*  

“*WHAT?!*” she *hissed*, glancing around like someone might overhear their conversation in the crowded bar.  

Tara, who had *clearly* heard this story before, just took another sip of her beer. “Yep.”  

Naomi *whisper-yelled*, leaning back in. “WAS—WAS *JEFF*?!”  

Jodi pressed her lips together, tapping her fingers on the table. “Let’s just say… the lawyers had a *really* hard time suppressing that evidence in *criminal* court.”  

Naomi *nearly fell out of her chair.* “OH MY GOD, *SO HE WAS?*”  

Jodi exhaled through her nose. “It *did* come out in civil court.”  

Naomi *squeaked* in distress. “I AM LIVING IN A NETFLIX DOCUMENTARY.”  

Tara snorted, shaking her head. “Welcome to *Jodi’s* life.”  

Jodi sighed, rolling her shoulders. “Jeff *tried* to shield me from the media attention, but, yeah… that never worked *perfectly* well.”  

Naomi’s hands were *back* on her face. “Jesus *Christ*, Jodi. You were caught up in an *actual scandal.*”  

Jodi shrugged. “It was mostly just being *adjacent* to the scandal. I never got dragged *too* deep.”  

Naomi *huffed*, exasperated. “HOW IS THAT SUPPOSED TO MAKE ME FEEL *BETTER*?!”  

Tara smirked, setting her beer down. “Naomi. This woman is *not normal*.”  

Jodi grinned, leaning back in her chair. “*Obviously*.”  

Naomi groaned, pressing her hands against her temples. “I need a *minute*.”  

Jodi chuckled, finishing the last of her beer. “Take your time.”  

Naomi took a *very* deep breath.  

Then, without looking up, she muttered, “Next question.”  

Tara *cackled*.  

And just like that, the chaos continued.    

---  
Naomi was *still* rubbing her temples, trying to *mentally recover* from the last revelation when Jodi, feeling *thoroughly entertained*, leaned back in her chair and casually dropped—  

“Oh, and Jeff’s started a joint venture with his band buddies. There’ll be a *Netflix* show in, like, three years.”  

Tara, who had just taken a sip of her drink, *froze mid-swallow*.  

Then, very *slowly*, she set her glass down.  

“…*Excuse me?*”  

Jodi shrugged. “Yeah. Streaming service deal. Big project. Long turnaround time.”  

Tara *blinked* at her. “And you’re just *mentioning this now?*”  

Jodi raised an eyebrow. “What, should I have led with it?”  

Tara *gawked*. “YES, *Jodi*.”  

Naomi, *still cradling her head*, groaned. “I *swear* to God, every time I think you’ve peaked in *insanity*, you just go and top it.”  

Tara *snapped* her fingers. “Hold up—*you knew* about this, right?”  

Jodi smirked. “Obviously.”  

Tara *leaned forward*. “How long?”  

Jodi exhaled, tilting her head in thought. “Eh… six months? Maybe less?”  

Tara *threw up her hands*. “SIX *MONTHS*?! AND YOU’RE JUST DROPPING IT IN *NOW*?!”  

Jodi *laughed*. “Tara, it’s not *my* project.”  

Tara pointed at her *accusingly*. “You are *intimately involved* with one of the executive producers!”  

Jodi *grinned*. “Yeah, but I *already knew* that.”  

Tara groaned, burying her face in her hands. “You *kill me*, Jodi.”  

Naomi, still *reeling*, exhaled slowly. “I need to go lie down in traffic.”  

Jodi chuckled. “Not before we order another round.”  

Tara *sighed*, reaching for her drink. “Jesus *Christ*.”  

And just like that, the night took *another* ridiculous turn.    

---  
Tara was *still* recovering from the Netflix revelation, rubbing her temples like she had an actual migraine, when Jodi, feeling *especially* amused, casually added—  

“Oh, and I think I’m gonna be one of the on-screen characters.”  

Tara’s head *snapped up* so fast it was a *miracle* she didn’t get whiplash.  

Naomi *dropped* her drink.  

It didn’t spill, but she didn’t even *notice* because she was too busy *malfunctioning*.  

Tara *stared* at Jodi, eyes wide. “I’m sorry. *What?*”  

Jodi took a lazy sip of her beer. “Yeah. They want to *fictionalize* parts of the band’s history, and Jeff said they’ll probably need someone to play me.” She gestured vaguely.

Naomi made a *strangled noise* that sounded *borderline inhuman*.  

Tara blinked. “YOU?! On *Netflix*?”  

Jodi shrugged. “I mean, yeah.”  

Naomi *finally* found her voice—though it was more of a *hysterical shriek*. “JODI. YOU’RE GOING TO BE A *TV CHARACTER?*”  

Jodi *grinned*. “Seems like it.”  

Tara *slammed* her hands on the table. “How the *hell* is this just a *side note* in your life?!”  

Jodi smirked. “Because I don’t *care* that much.”  

Naomi was *breathing into her hands*, trying not to *pass out from sheer overstimulation*. “I *hate* you.”  

Jodi *laughed*. “Understandable.”  

Tara shook her head, finishing off her drink in *one go*. “I need a *goddamn minute*.”  

Naomi, still *spiraling*, wheezed, “I *swear* to God, I thought I worked with a *normal person* when I met you.”  

Jodi *chuckled*, finishing off her beer. “Your first mistake.”  

Tara exhaled sharply. “I *cannot* believe you’re going to be *on Netflix* and you just *casually mentioned it* over nachos.”  

Jodi smirked. “Would you rather I made a *big deal* out of it?”  

Naomi *whimpered*. “YES, YOU SOCIOPATH.”  

Jodi *cackled*. “Too late.”  

Tara groaned, rubbing her temples. “Jesus *Christ*, I need another drink.”  

Jodi grinned. “Round’s on me.”  

And with that, the night *descended into pure madness.*    

---  
Tara, still gripping her drink, *narrowed her eyes*.  

Something wasn’t *adding up*.  

No—scratch that. *It was* adding up, and she *didn’t like* where it was going.  

Slowly, she set her glass down, fixing Jodi with a look of pure *suspicion*.  

“So… let me get this straight.”  

Jodi smirked. “Please do.”  

Tara held up a hand, *counting off* each point on her fingers.  

“One. This show is about the band.”  

Jodi nodded, amused. “Correct.”  

“Two. Jeff is one of the *main* characters.”  

Another nod. “Yep.”  

“Three. *You* are one of the supporting cast members.”  

Jodi took a sip of her beer. “Seems that way.”  

Tara leaned in *closer*, voice dropping slightly. “So, does that mean…” She tilted her head. “That you and Jeff will end up having *sex on screen*?”  

Naomi made a *high-pitched squeaking noise* that sounded *not entirely human*.  

Jodi exhaled, running a hand through her hair. “And *this*—” she gestured vaguely between them, “is maybe *why* I never talk about it.”  

Naomi *gasped dramatically*, clutching the edge of the table. “OH MY GOD.”  

Tara blinked. “Jodi.”  

Jodi *sighed*. “Tara.”  

Tara *blinked again*. “You’re telling me… that you and *Jeff*—*your actual* Jeff—might have to *fake bang* on *Netflix*?”  

Jodi, *completely unbothered*, nodded. “That is *one* possibility, yes.”  

Naomi *wheezed*. “I—*I can’t—*” She *threw herself* against the booth, gripping her drink like it was a life raft. “I NEED A MOMENT.”  

Tara *stared* at Jodi, *utterly* at a loss. “You’re actually *insane*.”  

Jodi *grinned*, taking another sip. “No, I just live in a *very stupid* reality.”  

Naomi, still *spiraling*, whispered, “Jodi, what if people ship you?”  

Jodi snorted. “They already *do*.”  

Naomi *squeaked again*. “WHAT IF THEY *WRITE FANFIC?*”  

Jodi *grinned wider*. “Oh, *they already do*.”  

Tara *buried* her face in her hands. “Jesus *Christ*.”  

Naomi *screamed into her hands*.  

Jodi *laughed*, tossing a nacho into her mouth.  

Tara groaned. “I *need* another drink.”  

Naomi *whimpered*, still clutching the table. “I *need* therapy.”  

Jodi *smirked*, leaning back in her chair.  

“*Next question?*”  

And with that, the *night fully derailed into madness.*

--- 

Naomi, still *recovering* from the *Netflix sex scene revelation*, took a *deep breath* and scrolled to her *next* question.  

“Okay,” she said, voice still *slightly unhinged*, “have you ever met other famous people?”  

Jodi smirked. “Outside the band?”  

Naomi *nodded aggressively*. “Yes! Like, actual *celebrities*?”  

Jodi tapped her fingers against the table, thinking. “Huh. Yeah, actually.”  

Naomi *leaned forward*, *eyes glowing*. “WHO?!”  

Jodi exhaled, stretching her arms over her head. “Well… Jeff got me a *private tour* of the White House when Obama was president.”  

Tara’s beer *froze* halfway to her lips.  

Naomi *slammed* her hands onto the table. “I’M SORRY. *WHAT?*”  

Jodi *laughed*, casually stealing another nacho. “Relax, Naomi.”  

Naomi *did not relax*. “JODI. YOU *TOURED* THE WHITE HOUSE?”  

Jodi nodded, chewing thoughtfully. “Yep. Got to go into the *Oval Office* too.”  

Tara *blinked rapidly*. “Jodi.”  

Jodi *raised an eyebrow*. “Tara.”  

Tara set her beer down with a *thud*. “How have you *never mentioned this?*”  

Jodi shrugged. “I don’t know. Just *never came up*.”  

Naomi made a sound that was somewhere between a *whimper* and a *scream*.  

Tara *pinched the bridge of her nose*. “Do I even want to ask how *that* happened?”  

Jodi grinned. “Jeff pulled some strings.”  

Naomi’s jaw *dropped*. “Jeff  got you a *tour of the White House?*”  

Jodi smirked. “Sure did.”  

Tara *squinted suspiciously*. “Did you *meet* Obama?”  

Jodi *sighed dramatically*. “No, unfortunately, the Obamas were on *vacation* in Nantucket at the time.”  

Naomi *threw her head back in despair*. “ARE YOU *KIDDING ME?!*”  

Jodi chuckled. “Nope.”  

Naomi *collapsed* against the table. “YOU WERE *THAT CLOSE* TO MEETING BARACK OBAMA AND YOU *MISSED HIM* BECAUSE HE WAS ON *VACATION*?!”  

Jodi *laughed*. “Yup. Still, the tour was *cool as hell*.”  

Tara shook her head, picking up her drink. “Jesus *Christ*, I *swear* every time you open your mouth, you drop another *ridiculous* life event on us.”  

Naomi groaned into her hands. “I hate you. I *hate* you so much.”  

Jodi grinned, leaning back in her chair. “You *love* me.”  

Naomi *whimpered.*  

Tara *chuckled*, shaking her head. “Alright, Naomi. What’s next? What’s the *next* absolutely *batshit* thing we’re gonna learn about Jodi?”  

Naomi *peered at her phone*, eyes flicking downward.  

Then she looked up.  

Then she *smirked*.  

“Have you *ever* been arrested?”  

Jodi’s smirk *froze*.  

Tara *perked up immediately*. “Oh, *this* I *need* to hear.”  

Jodi *sighed*, reaching for her beer.  

“Oh boy.”  

And just like that, the *night took another turn.*    

---  
Naomi, still *reeling* from the *White House revelation*, took a *very deep breath* and locked eyes with Jodi.  

“Okay,” she said, voice slightly *too eager*, “have you *ever* been arrested?”  

Jodi exhaled slowly, setting her beer down with *deliberate* care.  

Then, very carefully, she said—  

“Yes.”  

Naomi *squeaked*.  

Tara *sat up straighter*.  

Jodi rolled her shoulders. “For *cocaine possession*.”  

Naomi made a *horrified, high-pitched noise* and *smacked* Tara’s arm repeatedly without breaking eye contact with Jodi.  

Tara, *not taking her eyes off Jodi either*, muttered, “I *heard* her, Naomi.”  

Naomi *whisper-screamed*, “YOU GOT *ARRESTED* FOR *COCAINE POSSESSION?!*”  

Jodi sighed, tapping her fingers against the table. “But. I was *never charged* by the *Commonwealth of Massachusetts*.”  

Tara blinked. “How?”  

Jodi smirked *tightly*. “About *half a million* in legal bills.”  

Naomi made a noise like a *dying cat*. “FIVE HUNDRED *THOUSAND DOLLARS?!*”  

Jodi nodded, taking a sip of her beer like she *hadn’t just said that out loud.*  

Tara, still *staring* at her, exhaled through her nose. “Jodi.”  

Jodi tilted her head. “Tara.”  

Tara *rubbed her temples*. “You *cannot* keep dropping this shit on us like it’s *Tuesday errands*.”  

Naomi was *holding her head in both hands now*, rocking slightly. “I—I *need* a timeline. I need *context*. I need to *breathe*.”  

Jodi smirked. “Deep breaths, Naomi.”  

Naomi *groaned into her hands*. “I thought I was gonna get *fun* stories, like ‘one time Jeff and I got drunk and stole a golf cart.’ Not *federal crimes*!”  

Jodi snorted. “Technically *state-level* crimes.”  

Naomi *whimpered.*  

Tara *squinted at Jodi*. “This was around the *Kathy* thing, wasn’t it?”  

Jodi tapped her nose. “Bingo.”  

Naomi *squeaked again.* “DID I *NOT* SAY THIS WAS A *NETFLIX DOCUMENTARY?!*”  

Tara sighed, taking a *very* large sip of her drink. “Jesus *Christ*, Jodi.”  

Jodi *laughed*, shaking her head. “You asked.”  

Naomi, still *spiraling*, picked up her cocktail and *downed* the rest of it in *one go*.  

Tara, still rubbing her temples, muttered, “Next question better be *boring*.”  

Naomi, *face still buried in her hands*, muffled, “I *doubt* it.”  

Jodi smirked.  

“Try me.”  

And just like that, the *night somehow got even wilder.*    

---

Naomi, still *trying to process* the last bombshell, ran both hands through her hair. “Okay, okay, okay. Hold up.”  

Jodi raised an eyebrow, sipping her beer. “What now?”  

Naomi *gestured wildly*. “I thought you were *straight*.”  

Jodi nodded. “I am.”  

Naomi *froze*. “Then *how*—” she pointed dramatically, “—did you end up sleeping with *Kathy Espich?*”  

Tara *snorted*, shaking her head. “Oh, *this* I need to hear.”  

Jodi smirked, leaning forward slightly. “Naomi, you wouldn’t *understand* how Kathy *oozed* charm and raw appeal until you were *sitting next to her*.”  

Naomi *blinked rapidly*, trying to *compute*. “But—but—”  

Jodi shrugged, running a finger around the rim of her glass. “She had this *pull*, you know? This way of making *anyone* feel like the most fascinating person in the room.” She exhaled. “Jeff and Kathy had a *thing* for, like, twenty years. It always failed. *Spectacularly.*”  

Tara raised an eyebrow. “And yet, they kept trying.”  

Jodi smirked. “Jeff’s an *idiot*.”  

Tara *laughed*. “No argument there.”  

Naomi, still *reeling*, shook her head. “So… wait. Jeff was involved with Kathy *forever*, but then you ended up in bed with her?”  

Jodi chuckled. “Oh, it gets *better*.”  

Naomi *whimpered*. “Of course it does.”  

Jodi leaned in slightly. “Jeff *deliberately* wrote songs with lyrics about *me* that Kathy had to *sing*—and she *knew*.”  

Naomi *gasped* so hard she almost inhaled her straw.  

Tara *froze*. “*Excuse me?*”  

Jodi *grinned*. “Or maybe she *didn't* know. I’ll never be *totally* sure.”  

Naomi *slammed* her hands onto the table. “JODI. YOU ARE LIVING IN A *SOAP OPERA*.”  

Jodi laughed. “Trust me, I *know*.”  

Tara *tilted her head*, processing. “So let me get this straight. Jeff, who couldn’t make it work with Kathy for *two decades*, writes songs *about you*, gets her to *sing them*, and *then* she ends up in bed with *you* instead?”  

Jodi nodded. “That about sums it up.”  

Naomi *threw her head back* in *pure disbelief*. “I NEED TO LIE DOWN.”  

Jodi chuckled. “Naomi, when one of the *most attractive people on earth* gets interested in you, does it *really* matter that she was a woman?”  

Naomi made an *incoherent noise*.  

Jodi smirked, taking another sip of beer. “Besides… it was only *one day*.”  

Naomi *collapsed* onto the table. “I hate this. I hate you. I *love* you. I *hate* you.”  

Tara laughed, shaking her head. “This is the most ridiculous shit I’ve ever heard.”  

Jodi winked. “And *yet*, here we are.”  

Naomi groaned into her hands. “Next question before my *brain melts*.”  

Tara smirked. “Oh, I *cannot* wait to hear what comes next.”  

And with that, the night *continued spiraling into madness.*    

---  
Jodi leaned back, smirking as she swirled the last of her beer in her glass.  

“What came next,” she said smoothly, “was *me*.”  

Naomi made a *strangled noise* and slapped a hand over her own mouth.  

Tara, *unfazed at this point*, just raised an eyebrow. “Oh, *this* is about to be good.”  

Jodi exhaled, rolling her shoulders. “I was *really* ambivalent about having Kathy go down on me. At the beginning, anyway.”  

Naomi *slammed* her hands on the table. “*AT THE BEGINNING?!*”  

Jodi chuckled, nodding. “Well, yeah. It was an *unknown* for me.” She tilted her head. “I didn’t even experiment with girls in college.”  

Naomi *gasped*, still *reeling*. “I *didn’t either*! And I *thought* that was normal, but *now* I feel like I *missed out on something?*”  

Jodi smirked. “Statistically, you’re fine. I bet Tara didn’t either.”  

Jodi turned, raising an eyebrow at Tara.  

Tara, mid-sip of her drink, paused for *exactly one second* before swallowing and setting her glass down.  

Then, with a perfectly neutral expression, she said—  

“…Define *experiment*.”  

Naomi *screamed*.  

Jodi *cackled*.  

Tara just *smirked*, reaching for another nacho like she hadn’t just *dropped a grenade on the conversation.*    

---  
Jodi leaned forward, resting her elbows on the table, eyes locked onto Tara like a predator that had *just* caught the scent of weakness.  

“Oh, *hell no*,” she said, smirking. “If you’re gonna start playing *semantics games*, Tara, you might as well *fess up now* and *save yourself* from the *embarrassment* that is *absolutely* coming.”  

Tara *sighed*, shaking her head as she picked up her drink. “Jesus, Jodi.”  

Naomi, *wide-eyed and practically vibrating*, turned to Tara. “Wait. WAIT.” She pointed aggressively. “Are you about to tell me that *you*, Miss ‘I-Plead-The-Fifth,’ have actually—”  

Tara exhaled through her nose, swirling her beer. “Fine.” She took a long sip, set her glass down, and shrugged.  

“I experienced the *touch* of women rather *extensively* at university.”  

Naomi *gasped so hard* she nearly inhaled her straw.  

Jodi *grinned like the devil himself*. “There it is.”  

Naomi *slammed* her hands on the table. “WHAT DOES ‘EXTENSIVELY’ *MEAN*?!”  

Tara, *completely unbothered*, popped a nacho into her mouth. “It means exactly what you *think* it means, Naomi.”  

Naomi *gasped again*, voice hitting *new* octaves. “I—I can’t—*Tara*—”  

Jodi, *thoroughly enjoying herself*, took a sip of beer and smirked. “I *love* how you’ve been sitting here, listening to *my* nonsense, acting all *above it*, when meanwhile—” she gestured dramatically, “—you were out there *sampling the entire buffet*.”  

Tara raised an eyebrow. “Jodi. *Please.*” She smirked. “It was *just* the appetizer section.”  

Naomi *whimpered*. “I *swear to God* this is *too much*.”  

Jodi *cackled*, clinking her beer against Tara’s. “Respect.”  

Tara grinned. “Right back at you.”  

Naomi collapsed *against the table.* “I *hate* this. I *love* this. I *hate* this.”  

Jodi chuckled. “Welcome to *girl talk*, babe.”  

And with that, the *night spiraled even further into madness.*    

---  
Naomi, *still reeling* from the *absolute insanity* of the last ten minutes, took a *deep, steadying breath* and stared at her drink like it could somehow *rescue* her from this conversation.  

Jodi, still smirking, leaned in. “Alright, Naomi. Your turn.”  

Naomi *blinked rapidly*. “My—*my turn?*”  

Tara, sipping her drink, nodded. “Yep. Fair’s fair.”  

Naomi *whimpered*. “Oh, come *on*. You *cannot* expect me to *compete* with either of you.”  

Jodi grinned. “Nobody’s *competing*. We just wanna know—” she gestured dramatically, “—what’s the *wildest* thing *you’ve* ever done?”  

Naomi *froze*, staring at them like a *deer in headlights*. “Uhh.”  

Tara raised an eyebrow. “Oh, *this is gonna be good*.”  

Naomi groaned, running a hand through her hair. “It’s—it’s *not*.”  

Jodi smirked. “C’mon, Naomi.”  

Naomi *squirmed*, clearly *miserable*. “The wildest thing I’ve ever done is…” she *swallowed hard*, looking down.  

Jodi and Tara both *leaned in, waiting.*  

Naomi covered her face with both hands. “*Getting a video of my coworker*.”  

Jodi *blinked*.  

Tara *tilted her head*.  

Naomi, *muffled behind her hands*, groaned. “I *know*, okay?! I *know* I’ve been *sheltered*.”  

Jodi, after a long pause, *burst out laughing*. “Naomi. Babe.” She shook her head. “The wildest thing you’ve ever done is watch *me* masturbate on video?”  

Naomi *whimpered*.  

Tara *cackled*, throwing her head back. “Oh, that’s *priceless*.”  

Naomi *slammed her forehead onto the table*. “*I hate this.*”  

Jodi *grinned like a devil*. “Naomi, babe. We *have* to fix this.”  

Naomi *squeaked*. “Oh *God*, what does that *mean*?”  

Tara, still *laughing*, raised her glass. “It means you’ve officially entered the *corruption arc*.”  

Naomi *whimpered again*.  

Jodi smirked. “Drink up, babe. We’ve got *work to do*.”  

And just like that, Naomi’s life *officially* went off the rails.    

---  
Jodi leaned forward, drumming her fingers on the table, eyes gleaming with mischief.  

“Naomi,” she said smoothly, “place your panties on the table.”  

Naomi, who had just taken a sip of her drink, *choked violently*.  

Tara, without missing a beat, took another sip of her beer and muttered, “Oh, *this* I gotta hear.”  

Naomi, *coughing*, wiped her mouth and *gawked* at Jodi. “I’m sorry, *what?*”  

Jodi smirked, swirling her drink. “Panties. Table. Now.”  

Naomi *whimpered*, shifting in her seat. “WHY?!”  

Jodi shrugged. “I don’t *know*, Naomi. I’m just *making this up as I go.*”  

Tara *snorted*, covering her mouth. “Jesus *Christ*.”  

Naomi *flushed bright red*, gripping the edge of the table. “Jodi. You *cannot* just throw out a demand like that without a *reason.*”  

Jodi raised an eyebrow. “*Can’t I?*”  

Naomi let out a *frustrated noise*. “There has to be a *point* to this!”  

Jodi grinned, tilting her head. “Naomi, babe. What *about me* makes you think I need a *point*?”  

Naomi *collapsed* onto the table. “I *hate* you.”  

Jodi patted her shoulder. “You *love* me.”  

Tara, shaking her head, clinked her beer against Jodi’s. “Never change.”  

Naomi *groaned into her arms.*  

And just like that, the night continued its descent into *pure chaos.*    

---  
For a long moment, Naomi just *stared* at Jodi, still trying to *process* why she had been asked to put her panties on the table.  

Before she could formulate a response, Tara *casually* reached under the table, fumbled for a second, and then—  

*SLAM.*  

A thin, slightly stretched-but-still-perfectly-fine pair of panties hit the tabletop.  

Naomi *gasped so hard* she almost sucked in her own tongue.  

Jodi, *eyes gleaming with pure delight*, stood up slightly, looking Tara up and down. “Respect.”  

Tara shrugged, sipping her drink like *this was nothing*. “You asked.”  

Naomi *slammed* her hands on the table. “WHY DID YOU JUST HAVE THOSE *READY TO GO?!*”  

Tara smirked. “I *live* in a world where Jodi exists. One must always be *prepared*.”  

Naomi *whimpered*, looking between them like she had somehow *fallen into a parallel universe.*  

Jodi, stretching her arms over her head, turned to Tara. “Alright, your turn.”  

Tara raised an eyebrow. “For?”  

Jodi hooked her thumbs into the waistband of her jeans. “*Look out for people* while I take mine off.”  

Naomi *let out an actual scream*.  

Tara snorted, but leaned back, glancing around. “Well, yeah, it *would* be awkward if someone else saw.”  

Jodi nodded. “Exactly.”  

Naomi *grabbed* her drink and *chugged* it.  

Tara, smirking, tapped the table. “Naomi. Your move.”  

Naomi *made a strangled noise*, staring at them in abject horror. “I AM *NOT* TAKING OFF MY PANTIES IN A PUBLIC PLACE.”  

Jodi smirked. “Your loss.”  

Naomi groaned, *head in hands*, while Tara just *chuckled*, watching as Jodi wiggled out of her jeans.  

And just like that, the night spiraled *even further* into madness.    

---  
As Jodi wiggled out of her jeans, Tara leaned back in her chair, arms crossed, *watching.*  

And she wasn’t the *only* one.  

She smirked. “Naomi, you *peeking*?”  

Naomi *froze*.  

Jodi, who had just finished stepping out of her panties, stood up straight and flicked her hair over her shoulder like this was *absolutely nothing.*  

Tara glanced between them, amused.  

Naomi, meanwhile, looked like someone had just *exposed her search history* in a staff meeting.  

“I—*what*—NO—” Naomi *stammered*, face heating *instantly.* “I was just—I wasn’t—”  

Jodi, *cool as ever*, folded her jeans over the back of her chair, then turned, fixing Naomi with a *slow* smirk.  

“You sure about that?”  

Naomi *whimpered.*  

Tara chuckled, shaking her head. “Yeah, babe. You were *definitely* looking.”  

Naomi *covered her face with both hands.* “I *hate* this.”  

Jodi *grinned*, completely unfazed. “You *love* this.”  

Tara took a sip of her drink, eyeing Jodi thoughtfully. “Does anyone else notice how *completely unbothered* she looks right now?”  

Naomi *peered through her fingers* at Jodi, who was standing there, *panties in hand, jeans off, posture completely at ease*, like she was discussing the *weather.*  

Naomi let out a *high-pitched noise.* “How are you *this* confident?!”  

Jodi shrugged. “Practice.”  

Tara snorted. “That, and she’s been *naked* in a lot of *very public* places.”  

Naomi groaned, still *bright red*. “I *will never* be as chill as you.”  

Jodi smirked, placing her panties on the table next to Tara’s. “Never say never.”  

Naomi *squeaked*, shaking her head furiously. “I—Nope—No way—”  

Tara *laughed*, raising her glass. “Give it time.”  

And with that, the night *only* got more ridiculous.    

---  
Naomi, *still blushing furiously*, inhaled deeply and *pushed back her chair*.  

“Fine,” she muttered, standing up. “I’m taking them off.”  

Jodi and Tara both *arched eyebrows*, watching as Naomi smoothed down her skirt with a dramatic exhale.  

“But,” Naomi added quickly, “I’m using the *bathroom* to go commando.”  

Jodi snorted. “*Coward.*”  

Naomi *squeaked*, spinning around. “I AM *NOT* STRIPPING IN PUBLIC.”  

Tara waved a hand. “Yeah, yeah. Go on, then.”  

Naomi *huffed*, crossed her arms, and *slinked off* toward the bathroom.  

As soon as she was out of sight, Jodi turned to Tara, eyes gleaming.  

“So,” she said smoothly, resting her chin in her hand. “What should we do to tease her *next*?”  

Tara grinned. “Oh, *so many* options.”  

Jodi smirked. “Lay ‘em on me.”  

Tara tapped her fingers against her drink. “We could—oh, I don’t know—*steal her clothes*?”  

Jodi *laughed*. “Tempting.”  

Tara tilted her head. “Or we could *pretend* we changed our minds and put our panties back on before she gets back.”  

Jodi snorted. “Leave her the *only* one commando? *Evil.*”  

Tara smirked. “Or we could just *act totally normal* like nothing happened and watch her squirm.”  

Jodi *grinned*. “I *love* the way you think.”  

Tara raised her glass. “*Obviously.*”  

Jodi clinked hers against it, smirking.  

“Alright,” she said. “Let’s *ruin her night*.”  

And with that, the *real* fun began.    

---  
As soon as Naomi disappeared into the bathroom, Jodi scanned the room and spotted their *cute* waiter.  

Perfect.  

She leaned back in her chair, lifted a hand, and gestured him over with a mischievous smile.  

Tara *immediately* perked up. “Oh, *this* is gonna be good.”  

The waiter, looking *curious but intrigued*, approached. “Ladies?”  

Jodi leaned in, voice *low and silky*, and whispered in his ear.  

His *eyebrows shot up*.  

Then his mouth curled into a *grin*, and he nodded before *swiftly disappearing* toward the bar.  

Tara, practically *vibrating* in excitement, turned to Jodi. “Okay, *what did you just do?*”  

Jodi smirked, resting her chin in her hand. “Oh, just gave him a little… *mission*.”  

Tara *leaned in*. “*Explain.*”  

Jodi grinned. “When Naomi gets back, he’s going to stop by our table and—very *politely*—try to collect *all three* pairs of panties.”  

Tara *slapped* the table, laughing. “JODI.”  

Jodi grinned wider. “I told him we’d make it *worth his time*.”  

Tara *cackled*, already *imagining* Naomi’s reaction. “She’s going to *die*.”  

Jodi lifted her glass. “That’s the *goal*.”  

Tara *clinked* her drink against Jodi’s, still giggling.  

A moment later, the bathroom door creaked open.  

Naomi emerged, looking *pleased with herself*—completely *unaware* of what was about to *hit her.*  

Tara practically *bounced* in her seat. “Showtime.”  

Jodi smirked, eyes locked on Naomi as she made her way back to the table.  

This?  

This was going to be *legendary.*    

---  
As Naomi slid back into her seat, looking *far too pleased* with herself, Jodi gave her a slow, knowing smirk.
