Then, with the *slightest tilt of her head*, she *signaled* the waiter.  

Naomi, oblivious, reached for her drink. “What?”  

Jodi’s eyes *flicked downward*, scanning Naomi’s lap with exaggerated interest. “Hmm.”  

Naomi *froze mid-sip*. “…What?”  

Jodi smirked. “Purple, huh?”  

Naomi *choked*. “EXCUSE ME?!”  

Tara *burst out laughing*. “Oh, *this is gold*.”  

Naomi’s *entire face* turned red. “HOW DO YOU KNOW THAT?!”  

Jodi *grinned* like a cat who had just cornered a particularly *naïve mouse*. “Because, babe, I *see things*.”  

Naomi *whimpered*.  

Jodi leaned in. “Was it a *matched set*?”  

Naomi *gasped*, slamming a hand on the table. “I—JODI—”  

Before Naomi could fully *self-destruct*, their waiter arrived—grinning like a *man with a mission.*  

With a *graceful sweep*, he *scooped up* all three pairs of panties from the table.  

Naomi *screamed*.  

Tara *collapsed* into laughter.  

Jodi *toasted* the waiter with her beer as he *deftly* slid three *freshly poured* ones onto the table.  

“Ladies,” he said smoothly, “your drinks.”  

Naomi, *practically hyperventilating*, clutched her chest. “*I need to leave this establishment immediately.*”  

Jodi *cackled*, raising her glass. “Cheers, babe.”  

Tara, *wiping away tears*, clinked hers against Jodi’s. “To *never* wearing panties again.”  

Naomi *whimpered into her hands.*  

And just like that, the night *officially* spiraled into *complete insanity.*    

---  
Tara, still *buzzing* from the absolute *spectacle* that had just unfolded, grabbed her beer and grinned.  

“Alright, ladies,” she said, lifting her glass. “We *chug* these, and then we go *dancing* next door.”  

Naomi, still *recovering* from the *panty heist*, nearly *choked*. “*What?!*”  

Jodi raised an eyebrow, smirking. “Tara, babe, you *hate* dancing.”  

Tara shrugged. “Yeah, but *Naomi* is still processing her *moral collapse*, and you’re *clearly* in the mood to cause *more* chaos. So what better way to wrap up this *disaster of an evening* than with *bad decisions on a dance floor?*”  

Naomi *groaned*, rubbing her temples. “I am *not* in moral collapse.”  

Jodi grinned, tipping her beer toward her. “You’re literally *not wearing underwear* in public.”  

Naomi *whimpered*. “That’s not *moral collapse*. That’s *peer pressure*.”  

Tara *cackled*. “And what better way to *fully embrace your downfall* than by grinding on strangers to bad pop remixes?”  

Jodi *laughed*, taking a sip of beer. “Okay, I *support* this plan.”  

Naomi let out a *helpless squeak.* “You *would.*”  

Tara smirked. “Bottoms up, ladies.”  

With that, she tipped back her beer and started *chugging.*  

Jodi *grinned*, following suit.  

Naomi, *visibly panicked but with no escape route*, took a deep breath—  

And *chugged for dear life.*  

Because if she was going to *lose control* tonight—  

She was *going down swinging.*  

And just like that, the night *took another turn for the reckless.*    

---  
The three of them had been *drinking and dancing for hours.*  

The beat *pounded* through the floorboards, the air thick with heat and neon light. Jodi had *lost track* of how many songs they’d danced to. Tara had *zero shame* and was leading the charge, twirling Naomi *every chance she got*.  

And Naomi?  

Naomi had *officially let loose.*  

At first, she had been *awkward*, clutching her drink like a lifeline. But now? Now she was laughing, swaying, arms up, moving like she’d finally *stopped overthinking everything.*  

A few people in the crowd had *caught on* to their little game—the fact that none of them were wearing panties. A few men had even *tried* to dance closer, but Jodi and Tara had effortlessly *boxed them out*, keeping the night *theirs.*  

But Naomi?  

Naomi was *starting to notice something.*  

She leaned into Jodi’s ear, voice *slightly breathless* from dancing.  

“I feel way *overexposed* right now.”  

Jodi, still moving, smirked. “What, *finally* catching up to reality?”  

Naomi *whimpered*, tugging at the hem of her skirt, which was riding up a little *too much* with every twist and turn. “Jodi. I swear. If *one more person* figures this out—”  

Tara *cackled*, spinning in place. “Oh, babe, I *promise*—at least *four* people have *definitely* figured it out.”  

Naomi *squeaked*, *slamming* her thighs together. “*TARA!*”  

Jodi laughed, tilting her head. “You say that like you’re *mad about it*.”  

Naomi *whimpered*. “I *am* mad about it!”  

Jodi raised an eyebrow. “Are you?”  

Naomi opened her mouth.  

Paused.  

Closed it.  

Because, *if she was being honest*…  

She wasn’t *that* mad.  

Overwhelmed? Yes. Embarrassed? *A little.* But also?  

*Kind of electrified by the whole thing.*  

Jodi *grinned* as she saw the realization *dawn* in Naomi’s expression.  

“Oh, *babe*,” she teased. “You’re *starting to like this, aren’t you?*”  

Naomi *whimpered again*, burying her face in her hands.  

Tara *howled with laughter*, grabbing her by the wrist and *spinning her back onto the dance floor*.  

“C’mon, Naomi,” she *purred*. “Let’s see how much more *overexposed* we can get.”  

And with that, the night *only got wilder.*    

---  
About an hour later, the three of them were *still* on the dance floor.  

Naomi had *stopped fighting it*.  

She had *stopped overthinking*.  

She had *stopped* worrying about her skirt riding up, about the fact that some people *definitely* knew she wasn’t wearing anything underneath, and about the way Jodi and Tara were *watching her* with pure amusement.  

And yet—  

She still wasn’t sure *what* she was feeling.  

Tara, sensing an *opportunity*, leaned in close.  

“So,” she said smoothly, voice just loud enough over the music, “are you *turned on* right now?”  

Naomi *froze mid-step*.  

Jodi *raised an eyebrow*, intrigued.  

Naomi, *blinking rapidly*, looked *completely lost*. “I—what?”  

Tara smirked, twirling a lock of hair around her finger. “Are you turned *on*?”  

Naomi’s *face went bright red*. “I—*what does that even mean?*”  

Jodi *snorted*, taking a sip of her drink. “Oh, *babe*.”  

Tara *grinned like the devil himself*. “You *need help* understanding *the concept*?”  

Naomi, *clearly panicking*, waved her hands. “I mean—I get it *theoretically*—”  

Tara smirked. “But you don’t *know*?”  

Naomi *squeaked*, shifting awkwardly. “I—I don’t *think* so?”  

Jodi leaned in, voice *silky smooth*. “So you’re saying your heart’s racing, your skin’s all sensitive, and you *don’t* know?”  

Naomi *whimpered*, pressing a hand to her chest. “That’s not—”  

Tara tilted her head. “Babe, if you *have to ask*…”  

Naomi *covered her face with both hands.* “I *hate* this conversation.”  

Jodi grinned. “No, you *don’t.*”  

Tara *laughed*, grabbing Naomi’s wrist and spinning her under her arm.  

“C’mon,” she purred, “let’s keep testing your theory.”  

And with that, Naomi *realized she was in very, very deep.*    
  
---  
Tara, eyes gleaming with mischief, *gently* took Naomi’s wrist and guided her hand *inward*.

Naomi barely had a second to *process* before her fingers met the undeniable, *damp* heat beneath Tara’s dress.  

Her *entire body locked up*.  

Tara, *utterly unbothered*, leaned in close and murmured, “*That*—is what being turned on feels like.”  

Naomi *stared* at her, mouth *slightly open, brain short-circuiting*.  

Tara smirked. “It’s a *perfect buzz*—in my head, in *other places*—” she squeezed Naomi’s fingers *just* slightly before letting go, “—and it makes me feel *ridiculously confident*.”  

Naomi *whimpered*, her hand *jerking back* like she had touched a live wire.  

Tara chuckled, stepping back like this was all *very normal* for her. “You *really* are new to this, huh?”  

Naomi, *visibly struggling to breathe*, *nodded once.*  

Tara *grinned*, running a hand through her hair. “I’m getting three shots. Be right back.”  

And just like that, she *turned on her heel* and floated toward the bar, weaving through the growing crowd like a *hummingbird darting toward nectar.*  

Jodi, who had *watched everything* with a *deeply amused* smirk, turned back to Naomi, eyes twinkling.  

Naomi was *still standing there*, *still stunned*, *still processing*.  

Jodi tilted her head. “So,” she said smoothly, “I’ll ask again.”  

Naomi *gulped*.  

Jodi stepped in closer, lowering her voice.  

“Are you *actually* turned on right now?”  

Naomi *squeaked.*  

Jodi *grinned*.  

Because at this point?  

They *both* already knew the answer.    

---  

Jodi exhaled, stretching her arms overhead like she was *completely at ease*—which, *of course*, she was.  

“This,” she said, glancing at Naomi with a *knowing smirk*, “is what a *good time* is all about.”  

Naomi, still *visibly reeling* from Tara’s stunt, let out a *soft, unsure noise*. “I—I don’t know if I’d call this *normal fun*.”  

Jodi chuckled, swirling what was left of her drink. “Babe, *no one* said it was *normal*.” She leaned in slightly. “But it’s *fun*, right?”  

Naomi hesitated.  

Then, slowly—*reluctantly*—she nodded.  

Jodi grinned. “Exactly.”  

Naomi exhaled, brushing a loose strand of hair behind her ear. “I just… I *don’t understand* how it *escalated* like this.”  

Jodi raised an eyebrow, intrigued. “Oh?”  

Naomi bit her lip. “It’s like… every time I thought we’d reached *peak insanity*, something else happened. And then something *else* happened. It’s like a—” she gestured vaguely, “—spiral of chaos.”  

Jodi smirked. “That, babe, is called *escalation*.”  

Naomi blinked. “Escalation?”  

Jodi nodded. “One-upmanship. Daring. *Momentum.*” She tilted her head. “Someone pushes the boundary a *little* further, then someone else pushes it *again*—and before you know it, you’re somewhere you *never* thought you’d be.”  

Naomi *visibly swallowed*.  

Jodi smirked. “And the best part?”  

Naomi, still *flustered*, shook her head slightly. “W-what?”  

Jodi’s grin widened. “It builds *camaraderie*.”  

Naomi’s eyes *narrowed slightly*, like she wasn’t *entirely sure* she believed that.  

Jodi chuckled. “Think about it. The three of us just had an absolutely *deranged* night, and instead of running away screaming, you’re *still here*.”  

Naomi groaned, dropping her face into her hands. “Oh my *God*.”  

Jodi *laughed*. “Exactly.”  

At that *very moment*, the *cute waiter*—now *off the clock*—casually slid into the seat next to Jodi, beer in hand, like he had *always belonged there.*  

Naomi’s head *snapped up*—and she looked *startled*.  

No—more than startled.  

She looked… *envious.*  

Jodi, *of course*, *immediately* noticed.  

And it *delighted* her.  

She *tilted her head slightly*, meeting Naomi’s eyes, and—without a single word—asked:  

*Oh? Do you wish he sat next to you?*  

Naomi’s face *burned red.*  

And Jodi?  

Jodi *smirked*.  

Because this night?  

It wasn’t *over* yet.    

---  
Jodi, still *deeply entertained* by Naomi’s *not-so-subtle* envy, turned to the waiter with a smirk.  

“So,” she said smoothly, “what did you *do* with our panties?”  

Naomi *choked on air*.  

Tara, now *returning with three shots*, *froze* mid-step, then burst out laughing. “Oh, *I love this question.*”  

The waiter grinned, lifting his beer. “They’re in my glove box.”  

Naomi *gasped*. “I—I *beg your pardon?!*”  

He shrugged. “Trophies.”  

Jodi tilted her head, considering this. “Huh.”  

Then, after a beat, she *grinned wickedly* and asked,  

“*So what place did I come in?*”  

Tara *almost dropped the shots.*  

Naomi *squeaked*.  

The waiter leaned back in his chair, eyes gleaming with amusement. “Oh, *we’re ranking them now*?”  

Jodi shrugged, smirking. “Well, if you’re calling them *trophies*, I’d like to know if I’m bringing home *gold* or just an *honorable mention*.”  

Naomi *looked like she wanted to sink into the floor and vanish forever.*  

Tara, *recovering*, slid the shots onto the table and *grinned at the waiter*. “Well? *Answer the lady.*”  

The waiter took a *slow sip* of his beer, studying Jodi like he was *genuinely deliberating.*  

Finally, he set his drink down and said,  

“…Silver.”  

Jodi’s *eyebrows shot up*. “*Silver?*”  

Tara *cackled*. “Oh, *this is getting good*.”  

Naomi *leaned in, horrified and fascinated*. “Wait, *who got gold?!*”  

The waiter grinned. “*Tara.*”  

Tara *roared* with laughter, throwing up her hands. “*Hell yes!*”  

Jodi smirked, giving Tara a *mock begrudging nod*. “Fine. I’ll allow it.”  

Then, she turned back to the waiter, resting her chin in her hand.  

“But,” she said, voice *smooth as silk*, “that does mean *Naomi* came in third.”  

Naomi *physically convulsed*. “OH MY GOD *STOP TALKING*.”  

Jodi and Tara *clinked* their shot glasses together.  

“To *victory*,” Tara purred.  

Jodi grinned. “And to *Naomi’s corruption arc*.”  

Naomi *whimpered*.  

And the night?  

It still wasn’t *over* yet.    

---  
Naomi, still *reeling* from the entire *panty-ranking debacle*, took a deep breath, crossed her arms, and looked the waiter dead in the eye.  

“So,” she asked, tilting her head, “what were you *planning* to do before you changed your plans and sat with us?”  

The waiter smirked, leaning back slightly. “I was gonna hit the *sauna* downtown.”  

Jodi raised an eyebrow. “You mean like a *spa sauna*?”  

Tara, on the other hand, suddenly *perked up*. “Wait, *which* sauna?”  

The waiter took another sip of his beer. “One of the *traditional* ones.”  

Tara’s *eyes gleamed*. “Do they use *real birch*?”  

The waiter grinned. “Hell yeah.”  

Tara leaned in. “Do they *flagellate themselves* nude at *insanely high* temperatures?”  

Naomi *choked*. “*Excuse me?*”  

The waiter *laughed*, nodding. “Yep. The *old-school* ways are *bitchin’*.”  

Tara looked *ridiculously intrigued*. “You’re telling me there’s an actual *authentic* steam house *in this city* and I *didn’t know*?”  

The waiter shrugged. “Guess you weren’t *looking*.”  

Naomi, still trying to *process*, glanced between them and asked, “What’s *stopping* you from going, then?”  

The waiter raised an eyebrow. “Uh, well, I figured hanging with you guys was more interesting.”  

Naomi *huffed*, flipping her hair over her shoulder. “Well, if it’s *so fun*, maybe we should *all go*.”  

Jodi smirked, lifting a brow. “Oh?”  

Tara *beamed*, already *on board*. “I *love* this plan.”  

Naomi, now *realizing* what she had just said, blinked rapidly. “Wait—*I didn’t mean—*”  

Jodi leaned in, *grinning like the devil himself*. “Babe. Are you *suggesting* that we all go to a *high-heat, fully nude sauna experience* together?”  

Naomi *short-circuited on the spot*.  

Tara *cackled*. “Oh, I *adore* where this is going.”  

The waiter grinned. “Y’know… that *does* sound fun.”  

Naomi, *visibly panicking*, waved her hands. “I—*I was just saying*—”  

Jodi chuckled, patting her on the back. “No backsies, babe. Looks like we’re going.”  

Naomi *whimpered*.  

And just like that, the night *took yet another turn for the unhinged.*    

---  
Jodi, ever the *responsible* one—despite being the *ringleader* of the night’s madness—turned to the waiter and asked,  

“You good to drive?”  

The three of them weren’t *drunk*, but they’d had *enough* that she wasn’t about to risk it.  

The waiter grinned. “Yeah, I’ve only had one beer. I’m good.”  

Tara *clapped her hands together*. “Perfect. Let’s go get *steamed*.”  

Naomi *whimpered*.  

The drive wasn’t *long*, but Naomi was getting *quieter* by the minute.  

Jodi noticed, of course.  

When they pulled up, the place was *low-lit and unassuming*, with a subtle glow from frosted windows. The building exuded a sense of *calm*, a stark contrast to the chaos that had *led* them here.  

Inside, the air was *warm* even in the lobby, scented with eucalyptus and cedar.  

The girls were directed to the *women’s locker room*, while the waiter headed toward the *men’s side*.  

The moment they were *alone*, Naomi let out a *tiny, panicked squeak*.  

Tara, *already unbuttoning her top*, grinned. “You’re *adorably* nervous right now.”  

Naomi *hugged herself*, shifting awkwardly. “I—*I don’t know*—*this is a lot*—”  

Jodi, effortlessly cool, started pulling off her sweater. “Babe. It’s *just* a sauna.”  

Naomi *gestured wildly*. “But—*naked?!*”  

Tara *laughed*, kicking off her shoes. “Naomi, you *literally* weren’t wearing panties at the bar. What’s the difference?”  

Naomi *whimpered.* “That was *different*!”  

Jodi smirked, stepping out of her jeans. “How?”  

Naomi *struggled* for a response. “I—*I don’t know!*”  

Tara, now in *just* her bra, leaned against the lockers and grinned. “Listen, babe. I promise you *no one here cares*. You *do* this, and you *own* it. That’s the whole trick.”  

Naomi exhaled sharply. “I just… I don’t *want* to be the awkward one.”  

Jodi stepped closer, resting a hand on Naomi’s shoulder. “Then don’t be.” She gave her a teasing smirk. “Or, better yet… just *own* that, too.”  

Naomi bit her lip, eyes darting between them.  

Tara clapped her hands. “Alright. Time to *strip* and *sweat out our sins*.”  

Naomi let out a *helpless little whimper*.  

Jodi grinned.  

And just like that, the night took *yet another* unexpected turn.    

---  
The three of them stripped down, their clothes disappearing into the lockers, folded neatly on benches.  

Jodi stretched, completely *at ease*, rolling her shoulders like this was just another *Wednesday night* activity. Tara, naturally, was *grinning*—all excitement, no hesitation.  

Naomi, on the other hand, stood there with her arms *very strategically placed*, shifting from foot to foot like she was *reevaluating every life choice* that had led her to this moment.  

Jodi *grinned* at her. “Babe, you can’t be *half* confident.”  

Naomi let out a *tiny*, panicked *whimper*.  

Tara *laughed*, tossing an arm over her shoulder. “C’mon. If anyone so much as *looks* at you funny, we’ll just *sweat* on them aggressively until they leave.”  

Naomi *whispered*, “That’s not how that works.”  

Jodi chuckled. “Guess we’ll find out.”  

Then, *with zero fanfare*, she pushed open the door to the sauna—  

And they *walked straight into hell.*  

The *heat* hit them like a *wall*—a thick, *scorching* embrace that instantly wrapped around their skin. The air was *dense*, filled with the sharp scent of *birchwood and steam*, and the water vapor turned everything into a *hazy, glowing blur*.  

Naomi gasped, *instinctively* pressing a hand to her chest. “Oh my *God*—”  

Tara exhaled sharply. “*Holy shit.*”  

Jodi? Jodi just *grinned*. “Now *this* is heat.”  

Their ability to *see* was immediately *reduced*—the thick, swirling mist cloaking the room, making it impossible to make out more than a few *feet* in front of them.  

Somewhere in the haze, they heard a voice—  

“*Yo! Over here! Left side!*”  

The waiter.  

Or, as Jodi decided in that moment—  

*Doofus.*  

Jodi *rolled her eyes* but started heading left. “Of *course* he found a spot first.”  

Tara chuckled, following. “Maybe he’s secretly a *sauna expert*.”  

Naomi, *still trying to get used to breathing in an *oven**, hesitated before stumbling after them. “I can’t see *anything*.”  

Jodi smirked. “Just keep moving toward the *idiot noises*.”  

The heat *wrapped around them*, the mist swirling with each step, their bare skin already *gleaming* with moisture.  

Somewhere up ahead, the waiter called out again, his voice *muffled by the haze*.  

“Hurry up! You’re letting all the *good suffering* escape!”  

Naomi groaned. “I *hate* that sentence.”  

Tara *cackled*. “And yet, here we are.”  

Jodi just shook her head, smirking.  

This night?  

It *just kept getting better.*    

---  
Jodi spotted the waiter through the *thick haze*, his outline becoming clearer as they got closer.  

He was *already seated* on one of the wide wooden benches, leaned back *casually*—but there was a distinct, almost *comically forced* ease about him.  

Because, well—  

None of them were wearing *clothes*.  

And for *Americans*, this was *definitely* new territory.  

Jodi smirked, dropping onto the bench beside him, her movements as *effortless* as ever. “Hello again.”  

The waiter turned to her, giving an easy grin, though his fingers *tapped* slightly against his knee—just a small, unconscious tell. “Hey.”  

Yeah. He was feeling the *weirdness* of the moment, too.  

Jodi *chuckled*. “Bit *different* than hanging out at the bar, huh?”  

He *exhaled*, rolling his shoulders. “Yeah, well… welcome to *real sauna culture*.”  

Jodi gestured lazily toward the mist, the *intense* heat already making *everything* slick with moisture. “Gotta say, it’s a hell of a vibe.”  

He smirked. “Told you it’d be *bitchin’.*”  

Behind them, Tara *stretched out languidly*, arching her back like a *cat in a sunbeam*, completely *unbothered* by their collective nakedness.  

She *sighed contentedly*. “Oh, yeah. This? *This* is living.”  

Jodi chuckled and gestured between them. “Right. So, just to *formalize* things—Tara, Naomi, this is *Doofus*.”  

The waiter *snorted*. “That’s *not* my name.”  

Jodi grinned. “But it fits.”  

Naomi, meanwhile, was experiencing a *full-on existential crisis*.  

She had *no idea* how to *sit*.  

Cross-legged felt *weird*. Legs *together* felt *way too formal.* Spreading out like Tara? *Absolutely not.*  

She *adjusted herself* once. Then again. Then *again*.  

Jodi *smirked*, watching her fidget. “You good over there?”  

Naomi let out a *tiny, strangled noise*.  

Tara *cracked one eye open*, amusement flickering across her face. “Just pick a position, babe. Own it.”  

Naomi *whimpered*. “I *don’t know how*.”  

The waiter, watching this unfold, chuckled. “You *really* are new to this, huh?”  

Naomi *hid her face in her hands*. “This was a *terrible* idea.”  

Jodi grinned, leaning back. “Nah. You’re just *overthinking* it.”  

Tara sighed dramatically. “Babe, we *dragged you to a nude sauna*. The *worst* part is *already* over.”  

Naomi peeked through her fingers. “That is *not* helping.”  

Jodi laughed. “Just sit however you want. No one *cares*.”  

Naomi, still *very much* struggling, let out a *long, slow breath* and finally settled—legs *drawn up slightly*, arms resting over her knees, still *clearly hyper-aware* of her own skin.  

Jodi nodded approvingly. “There. Was that so hard?”  

Naomi *groaned*. “*Yes.*”  

The waiter chuckled, shaking his head. “You’ll get used to it.”  

Tara smirked, stretching again. “Or you’ll spontaneously combust from *overexposure*.”  

Naomi whimpered. “That is *not* funny.”  

Jodi grinned. “It’s *a little* funny.”  

Naomi groaned into her hands.  

And just like that, the *absurdity* of their night hit *a whole new level.*    

---  
Tara let out a long, *satisfied sigh*, stretching again like she was a *queen on a throne*.  

“This,” she declared, eyes closed, “is my *new favorite thing ever*.”  

Jodi smirked, wiping a bead of sweat from her forehead. “Yeah?”  

Tara nodded, utterly *blissed out*. “I feel *amazing*. Like, *so* much better than I did an hour ago.”  

Naomi, still sitting *awkwardly* and *definitely not relaxed*, *squinted* at her. “Are you sure you’re not just… I don’t know… *overexposed*?”  

Jodi *snorted*. “As opposed to *who*, exactly?”  

She gestured around the *hazy*, steam-filled room. “I count *four penises* and *three vaginas* that *belong to total strangers*—and *then* there’s *us*.”  

Naomi *whimpered* and buried her face in her hands. “Oh my *God*, do not put it like that.”  

Tara *cracked up laughing*. “She’s *not wrong*, babe.”  

Naomi *groaned*. “That doesn’t mean she had to *say* it.”  

Jodi smirked. “You’re in a *room full of naked people*, Naomi. Might as well *embrace the reality*.”  

Naomi made a *tiny, strangled noise*.  

Tara, still *basking* in the heat, waved a hand lazily. “Honestly? Getting in here *without planning to* was the *best* part. No time to *stress* about it.”  

Jodi considered that, nodding. “Yeah, no *buildup*, no *overthinking*—just, *boom*, you’re in.”  

Naomi *glared* at them both. “That’s great for *you*, but *I* need processing time for things like—like *nudity in public*!”  

Tara *grinned*, tilting her head. “Babe, *you’re here.*”  

Jodi chuckled, nudging Naomi’s foot with hers. “You *already did* the hard part.”  

Naomi let out a *frustrated whimper*. “I *hate* that you’re right.”  

Tara stretched again, closing her eyes with a *pleased sigh*. “Give it another twenty minutes. You’ll be saying this is your *new favorite thing ever*, too.”  

Naomi muttered under her breath. “*Doubt it.*”  

Jodi smirked. “We’ll see.”  

And just like that, the night’s absurdity *only deepened*.    
  
---  
The waiter—who, up until now, had been known exclusively as *Doofus*—leaned back against the wooden bench, letting out a content sigh.  

“By the way,” he said, stretching his arms out, “name’s *Kevin*.”  

Jodi smirked. “Kevin, huh?”  

He nodded. “Yeah. Y’know. *Not actually Doofus*.”  

Tara, still in a *state of pure relaxation*, cracked one eye open. “That remains to be seen.”  

Kevin laughed. “Fair.” He glanced at them, shaking his head slightly. “But honestly? I’m *shocked* you guys are here.”  

Jodi raised an eyebrow. “Why?”  

He grinned. “You don’t exactly *scream* ‘hardcore Finnish sauna enthusiasts.’”  

Tara waved a hand lazily. “Hey, don’t lump me in with them. I am *thriving*.”  

Jodi nodded. “Yeah, Tara’s about one minute away from demanding dual citizenship.”  

Naomi, *still trying to adjust*, let out a *tiny, suffering noise*.  

Kevin smirked, tilting his head at her. “Not a *Scandinavia girl*, huh?”  

Jodi *immediately pounced*.  

“Oh, *Naomi?*” she drawled, grinning. “No, no, no. She’s *all about* embracing new things. Just ask her how she spent her *afternoon*.”  

Naomi *gasped*. “JODI.”  

Tara cackled. “Oh, babe, you *walked into that one*.”  

Kevin chuckled, looking between them with interest. “What *did* you do this afternoon?”  

Naomi *slid down the bench*, burying her face in her hands. “*Nothing.*”  

Jodi smirked, *clearly* enjoying herself. “She’s just a little *slow* to embrace *Scandinavian culture*.”  

Kevin grinned. “Well, lucky for her, *this* is one of the best parts.”  

Jodi exhaled, finally taking in her surroundings again.  

The heat was *relentless*, thick and oppressive, and it had fully transformed *everyone* in the room.  

Skin *glistened*, slick with sweat and condensation, water beads rolling down arms, collarbones, thighs—  

She dragged a lazy hand down her own shin, watching the way the moisture *collected* under her fingertips.  

The *haze* in the room made *everything* look dreamlike, bodies shifting in and out of visibility like *ghosts in the steam*.  

Jodi smirked. “Y’know…” she mused, eyes flicking over at Naomi.  

Naomi, still flustered, peeked up warily. “What?”  

Jodi grinned. “For someone who’s *not* embracing Scandinavia… you’re looking *pretty damn Nordic* right now.”  

Naomi *whimpered*.  

Tara laughed. “She *is* looking pretty slick.”  

Kevin chuckled, leaning back. “Give her another ten minutes. She’ll be converted.”  

Jodi smirked, stretching. “I *love* how that’s always the promise. ‘Give it ten more minutes.’”  

Tara sighed dramatically. “Because it’s *true*.”  

Naomi *muttered something unintelligible* into her hands.  

Jodi grinned.  

And just like that, the night’s chaos *only deepened*.    

--- 
Naomi, *still slick with sweat, still flustered beyond belief*, suddenly *sat up straighter*.  

Jodi, mid-smirk, *narrowed her eyes slightly*.  

Because *that* was a shift.  

Naomi took a deep breath, then turned to her with *purpose*.  

“Alright,” she said, voice *steady*, “you keep teasing me about not embracing this whole thing. But you know what?”  

Jodi arched an eyebrow. “What?”  

Naomi squared her shoulders. “I did something *today* that was completely outside my comfort zone. *I* took a leap. So now? I’m *challenging you*.”  

Jodi’s smirk *deepened* instantly. “Oh?”  

Tara, watching this unfold like it was the *best show of her life*, leaned in. “Oh, *hell yes*.”  

Kevin, now *deeply intrigued*, glanced between them. “Should I be worried?”  

Jodi, still eyeing Naomi with interest, ignored him. “What *exactly* is the challenge?”  

Naomi hesitated *for only half a second*, then *grinned*, the first *genuine, non-flustered* grin she’d shown all night.  

“I want you to do what I did,” she said smoothly.  

Jodi’s eyes *glinted*.  

Tara *burst out laughing*. “Babe, *are you flipping the script right now?*”  

Naomi *tilted her head*. “What’s wrong? *Too much for you?*”  

Jodi *laughed*—a real, *delighted* laugh. “Oh, *you think you can bait me*?”  

Naomi smirked, settling back into her seat, *finally* looking relaxed. “I *know* I can.”  

Jodi chuckled, shaking her head. “Alright, babe. You *want* me to match you?”  

Naomi *nodded*. “That’s the deal.”  

Jodi stretched, cracking her neck. “Alright, then.”  

Then, with *zero hesitation*, she said—  

“Challenge *accepted*.”  

Tara *clapped her hands together*. “Oh, *this* is getting *so good*.”  

Kevin *blinked rapidly*. “I *definitely* should be worried.”  

Jodi smirked.  

Because at *this point*?  

The night was about to *truly* go off the rails.    
---  
---    
Jodi stretched, rolling her shoulders like she was *warming up for battle.*  

“Alright, babe,” she said, *grinning* at Naomi. “You threw the gauntlet. Now tell me *exactly* what I’m supposed to match.”  

Naomi’s confidence wavered for a *split second*, but then she squared her shoulders and smirked. “I want you to do *what I did*.”  

Tara gasped, hand *dramatically* over her heart. “Naomi, are you asking Jodi to have a *self-discovery moment* in a *public sauna*?”  

Naomi’s *smirk cracked instantly*. “*WHAT?! NO!*”  

Kevin *choked on absolutely nothing.* “I—I’m sorry—*what exactly are we talking about?*”  

Jodi *grinned*. “Don’t panic, Doofus. Naomi’s just trying to make me sweat.”  

Kevin gestured *around them wildly.* “Jodi. You’re in a *200-degree room*. You’re *already sweating*.”  

Tara *wheezed* with laughter. “He’s got a point.”  

Jodi waved a hand. “Technicalities.” She turned back to Naomi. “So what, then? You want me to—what? Overcome something? Embarrass myself? Because babe—” she stretched her arms out lazily, “—I have *zero* shame.”  

Naomi squinted. “You *say* that, but—”  

Jodi held up a finger. “Ah, ah. Challenge is *issued*. No take-backs.”  

Tara *kicked her feet happily against the bench.* “I love *everything* about this.”  

Kevin *looked increasingly nervous.* “I feel like I *should* leave, but I also feel like if I move, something *worse* will happen.”  

Jodi smirked, then turned to Naomi. “Alright, babe. Time to pay the piper. *What did you do?*”  

Naomi *hesitated*.  

Then, in a *very* small voice, she said,  

“…I sent a video.”  

Jodi’s eyebrows shot up. “*A video?*”  

Naomi nodded, *not quite meeting her eyes.*  

Tara *let out a delighted cackle.* “NAOMI. YOU MINX.”  

Kevin made a *distressed* noise. “Oh my *God*.”  

Jodi *grinned like a wolf*. “Wait, *you* sent a scandalous video and now you’re challenging *me* to match it?”  

Naomi, *visibly realizing she had made a terrible mistake*, swallowed. “…Yes?”  

Jodi smirked. “Oh, *babe*.”  

Naomi *whimpered*.  

Jodi tapped her chin thoughtfully. “Alright, let’s see… I *could* send a video. But that’s *boring*.”  

Naomi *blinked rapidly.* “*What do you mean boring?!*”  

Tara *snorted*. “She’s right. Jodi sending a *video* is like a *Thursday activity*. You gotta *elevate*.”  

Jodi nodded sagely. “Exactly. It needs *pizzazz*.”  

Kevin *put his face in his hands.* “This is *so much worse* than I thought it would be.”  

Jodi *grinned* and turned to Naomi.  

“So, babe,” she purred. “Should I *one-up you right now*? Or should I make you *wait in fear*?”  

Naomi *visibly short-circuited.*  

Tara *howled* with laughter.  

Kevin muttered, “I regret *everything*.”  

Jodi, *supremely entertained*, stretched out languidly on the bench, looking *completely unbothered.*  

“Take notes, babe,” she told Naomi. “This is how *escalation* is *done*.”  

And just like that, the night’s chaos *reached a whole new level.*    

---  
Naomi sat there, *trapped*, her own *challenge* looming over her like a *storm cloud made of regret.*  

Jodi, meanwhile, was *relaxed as hell*, stretching *lazily* like she had *all the time in the world* to decide how to *ruin Naomi’s life next.*  

Kevin, still looking like he *deeply* regretted his life choices, muttered, “I should’ve *gone to the sauna alone.*”  

Tara, barely containing her *glee*, leaned forward. “So, Jodi… what’s it gonna be?”  

Jodi exhaled, tapping her chin *thoughtfully*.  

“See, Naomi,” she mused, “you had to *work up the courage* to send your little scandalous video, yeah?”  

Naomi, *who had never regretted anything more in her life*, nodded. “*Yes.*”  

Jodi smirked. “So *I* have two options. *Match* you… or *escalate*.”  

Naomi’s face *drained of color.*  

Tara *perked up like a wolf scenting blood*. “Oh, babe, there’s *only one right answer here.*”  
Jodi *grinned* at Naomi.  

“I’m escalating.”  

Naomi *audibly whimpered.*  

Kevin *put his face in his hands.* “Why am I here.”  

Jodi stretched *lazily*, then *tilted her head at Naomi.*  

“Now, babe,” she purred, “I *need you to take notes*.”  

Naomi *blinked rapidly.* “*Notes?!*”  

Jodi nodded, completely *unbothered*. “Oh yeah. If you’re gonna issue *challenges*, you need to *understand escalation.*”  

Tara grinned. “Class is *in session*.”  

Kevin *groaned.* “I don’t like this *lesson plan*.”  

Jodi *completely ignored him* and turned to Naomi.  

“Rule one,” she began smoothly, “escalation *requires an audience*.”  

Naomi *made a strangled noise.*  

Kevin muttered, “This is *so much worse* than I thought.”  

Jodi *grinned.* “Rule two: the best escalation *is immediate*. No waiting, no buildup, just *boom*—higher stakes.”  

Naomi *visibly short-circuited.*  

Tara *cackled*. “I *adore* this.”  

Jodi *finally* sat up, *completely* in control. “And rule three?” She smirked, locking eyes with Naomi.  

“…*You never let your opponent breathe.*”  

Naomi *stopped functioning as a person.*  

Tara *howled* with laughter.  

Kevin whispered, “I need *new friends*.”  

Jodi, *supremely entertained*, leaned back again, stretching out in the *hot, hazy sauna*, and *grinned*.  

“Now, babe,” she teased, “watch and *learn*.”  

And just like that, the night took *its most insane turn yet.*    
---  
---  
Jodi, still *completely unbothered*, leaned back against the *scalding-hot* wooden bench, stretched her arms above her head, and smirked.  

“Well, babe,” she purred at Naomi, “since you *demanded* I match you…”  

Naomi, *already spiraling*, whimpered, “I *didn’t think you’d actually do it*.”  

Tara *cackled*. “Naomi, *babe*, you really don’t know who you’re dealing with.”  

Kevin, who had officially *given up on life*, just muttered, “I *really* should’ve gone to the sauna alone.”  

Jodi smirked, shifting slightly so the *moisture beading off her skin* glistened in the dim, *hazy light.*  

“Alright,” she said, voice lazy, “let’s make sure you *appreciate the effort*.”  

And then—  

She *showed them.*  

Naomi *stopped breathing.*  

Tara, after *two full seconds* of stunned silence, exhaled.  

“That is *hot*,” she said simply.  

Then, she shrugged. “But, y’know. That’s *redundant.*”  

Jodi *chuckled*, shaking her head. “You *always* know how to flatter me.”  

Tara leaned forward slightly, tilting her head as she examined Jodi *with genuine appreciation.*  

“But I *will* say,” she continued smoothly, “I’m *impressed* you decided to try out *madness* for once.”  

Jodi *arched an eyebrow*. “For *once*?”  

Tara smirked. “You usually keep a *toe* in the crazy. But this? This is *diving in headfirst.*”  

Jodi grinned. “I *do* like to commit.”  

Tara nodded, then sat back against the bench, watching her with a look that was *somewhere between admiration and amusement.*  

“I *appreciate* your look,” she said simply.  

Jodi smirked. “Yeah?”  

Tara gestured vaguely. “The *entire aesthetic*. The *‘fearless, glowing, post-debauchery goddess’* thing.”  

Kevin, who was *clearly questioning his entire existence*, groaned. “Why are we *talking* like this is normal?”  

Jodi turned to him, *grinning*. “Oh, babe.”  

Kevin *exhaled sharply.* “*Don’t call me babe.*”  

Jodi chuckled, tilting her head at Naomi, who still looked like she was experiencing *a full-on mental blue screen.*  

“So,” Jodi said smoothly, “*did that meet your challenge?*”  

Naomi, *voice barely above a whisper*, muttered, “I hate that I *respect* you right now.”  

Tara *howled* with laughter.  

Kevin looked at the *ceiling*, like he was praying for an *act of God.*  

And just like that, the night *completely derailed into chaos.*    
---  
---  
For a *solid ten seconds*, the sauna was *dead silent.*  

Steam *swirled* around them, condensation *dripped* lazily down skin, and the *weight* of the moment sat *thick and humid* in the air.  

Then, Naomi—still *spiraling* from everything she had just witnessed—*gasped*, eyes going *wide*.  

“Oh my *God*,” she *squealed*, practically vibrating.  

Jodi and Tara both *raised an eyebrow*.  

Kevin, who had been *mentally begging the universe to reset reality*, *blinked*. “What?”  

Naomi *pointed aggressively* at him, eyes *wild with delight.*  

“We *found a way to break you*!” she accused. “So much for playing the *cool, unaffected old man* game—*you’re human after all!*”  

Jodi *grinned like a wolf.* “Oh, *is that right?*”  

Tara *snorted*, covering her mouth. “Oh, *Kevin*.”  

Kevin, who had officially *lost any chance of plausible deniability*, ran a hand down his face and let out a *gravelly chuckle*.  

“Guilty,” he admitted, shaking his head.  

Naomi *cackled*, practically bouncing. “You *totally lost your chill*.”  

Jodi smirked. “So *tell us*, Kev—was it the *aesthetic*? The *fearless confidence*? The *highly educational demonstration*?”  

Kevin chuckled again, exhaling sharply. “I *plead the fifth*.”  

Tara *grinned wickedly*. “Yeah, that’s not how this works.”  

Kevin *dragged a hand through his damp hair*, shaking his head. “Y’know, I’m just *grateful* for the *complete lack of cameras* in here.”  

Naomi *gasped dramatically.* “OH MY GOD—*imagine if there were!*”  

Jodi *grinned*. “We’d be *Netflix-ready*.”  

Kevin groaned. “Jesus Christ, don’t even *joke* about that.”  

Tara stretched, smirking. “Oh, *babe*, what’s a little *infamy* between friends?”  

Naomi *whimpered* in laughter. “He’s *never* gonna recover from this.”  

Kevin sighed, rubbing his temples. “No. No, I am *not*.”  

Jodi leaned back, crossing her arms *triumphantly*. “*Lesson learned.*”  

Kevin exhaled. “Which is?”  

Jodi *grinned*.  

“You *can’t* out-cool *us*.”  

And just like that, Kevin *officially* lost the game.    
---  
---  
Kevin, still *recovering* from his complete loss of composure, exhaled and leaned back against the bench.  

“Y’know,” he said casually, rubbing condensation off his forehead, “the best thing about this whole situation?”  

Jodi raised an eyebrow. “Oh, *please* enlighten us.”  

Kevin smirked. “*Comparative grooming.*”  

Naomi *choked on absolutely nothing*. “EXCUSE ME?”  

Tara, *completely unbothered*, stretched her arms over her head, looking *supremely pleased* with herself. “Oh, *this* is about to be good.”  

Jodi *grinned*, intrigued. “Go on, Professor Doofus.”  

Kevin gestured vaguely between them. “I mean, I’ve *never* seen such *a complete contrast* in a group of people who are, otherwise, so… *tight-knit*.”  

Naomi, still *reeling*, squeaked, “YOU’RE ANALYZING US?!”  

Tara *chuckled*. “Naomi, babe, *you knew* he was looking.”  

Naomi *whimpered.* “I *hate* this conversation.”  

Kevin ignored her completely and *continued like he was leading a TED Talk*.  

“Tara,” he said, nodding approvingly, “is *mightily impressive looking*. A true *masterpiece* of planning, dedication, and precision.”  

Tara *beamed*. “Thank you, *Kevin*.”  

Kevin nodded. “You look *exquisite*.”  

Naomi made a *strangled noise*. “Are we REALLY DOING THIS?!”  

Kevin, completely undeterred, *gestured lazily at her.* “Meanwhile, the *old-school* style *you’re* rocking? *Back to the future.*”  

Naomi’s *soul left her body*. “I AM GOING TO DIE.”  

Jodi, *laughing so hard she nearly slid off the bench*, gasped, “Oh my *God*, Naomi, you just got *critically analyzed*.”  

Naomi, *whimpering*, *hid her face in her hands*. “I *hate* all of you.”  

Kevin shrugged. “Nothing *wrong* with vintage.”  

Naomi *let out an actual scream.*  

Tara, grinning, nudged her. “Babe, I think what he’s saying is, *it’s time to embrace change*.”  

Naomi *whimpered again.*  

Then, after a *long* beat, she muttered,  

“…I *am* currently considering a *change of pace*.”  

Jodi *gasped dramatically*. “OH SHIT—NAOMI’S HAVING A *GROOMING REVELATION*.”  

Tara *clapped her hands together*. “THIS IS A *BIG DAY*.”  

Kevin smirked. “Happy to inspire.”  

Naomi *whimpered into her hands*. “I *hate* you all.”  

Jodi grinned, nudging her with her foot. “Babe. This is just what *escalation* looks like.”  

And just like that, the night *took yet another ridiculous turn.*    

---  
Jodi, still *highly entertained* by Naomi’s *entire existence*, tilted her head slightly.  

“You know,” she mused, “I’m surprised *this* isn’t bothering you as much anymore.”  

Naomi blinked. “What?”  

Jodi smirked. “Your *boobs*.”  

Naomi *sputtered*. “JODI—”  

Jodi *grinned*. “I mean, you were *ready to die* walking in here, and now? You’re just sitting there, *fully exposed*, without the whole *arms-crossed panic defense*.”  

Naomi, realizing *Jodi had a point*, glanced down at herself like she was only *just now* remembering she was topless.  

Then, after a *moment*, she shrugged. “I dunno… I guess I’m just *not as uncomfortable* as I was when I *paraded myself in*.”  

Tara waved a hand dismissively. “Babe, don’t *worry* about it.”  

Jodi snorted. “It wasn’t a *parade*.”  

Kevin, *who was fighting for his goddamn life over here*, muttered, “*I don’t see how this subject change is supposed to help me get soft again.*”  

Jodi *howled with laughter.*  

Naomi *turned bright red*. “OH MY *GOD*.”  

Tara smirked. “Yeah, Kev, I don’t think this *conversation* is doing you any favors.”  

Kevin groaned, running a hand down his *very damp* face. “Yeah, thanks for the *observation*, Tara.”  

Jodi, *grinning wickedly*, leaned in. “Should we *help* you out?”  

Kevin immediately *threw up both hands.* “NO. *Absolutely not.*”  

Tara, snickering, nudged Naomi. “See? He’s human *after all*.”  

Naomi, *horrified but weirdly proud*, covered her face. “This is the *worst* night of my life.”  

Jodi smirked. “Oh, *babe*, this is the *best* night of your life.”  

Kevin groaned. “It is *not* the best night of *my* life.”  

Tara stretched luxuriously. “Could’ve been worse. We could’ve brought cameras.”  

Kevin let out an *actual strangled noise.*  

Naomi *screamed.*  

And just like that, the night’s *utter chaos* continued.    
---  
---  
Tara, still *lounging like a queen*, stretched her arms above her head and smirked at Jodi.  

“Alright, babe,” she said lazily. “Tell us what you did with *Jeff* last night.”  

Jodi, who had just taken a slow breath of the *thick*, eucalyptus-laced air, exhaled sharply.  

She *gave Tara a look.* “Why?”  

Tara grinned. “Because I *want to hear it*.”  

Naomi, who had been *very determinedly staring at the floor* ever since Kevin’s last comment, suddenly *looked up*.  

Kevin, who was *actively trying to reset his brain*, muttered, “Oh, *great*, now we’re doing *story time*.”  

Jodi *stretched her legs out*, acting like she was *completely* unbothered. “I don’t know if I feel like—”  

Tara cut her off. “Oh, *bullshit*. You *love* talking about him.”  

Jodi *huffed dramatically*, rolling her eyes. “That is *so* not true.”  

Tara raised an eyebrow. “Babe, every time his name comes up, you do that *thing*.”  

Jodi blinked. “What *thing*?”  

Naomi, apparently feeling *braver* now, smirked. “That *smug, satisfied* thing.”  

Jodi *snorted.* “Oh, *shut up.*”  

Kevin sighed. “Jesus Christ, just tell the damn story so we can *move on* before something *worse* happens.”  

Jodi, knowing full well that she was *losing the battle*, crossed her arms and sighed.  

“Fine.”  

Tara *grinned victoriously*. “That’s my girl.”  

Jodi *took her time*, pretending to think. “Let’s see… where to *begin*…”  

Naomi, who was now looking *suspiciously invested*, whispered, “Start with *why* you’re so smug.”  

Jodi shot her a *pointed* look. “I *am not* smug.”  

Tara smirked. “Babe, you *glowed* when you walked into work today.”  

Jodi *huffed* but finally relented. “Alright, fine. So last night, Jeff gets home after a *week away*—”  

Tara cut in. “*Internationally* away.”  

Jodi nodded. “Yes. And obviously, I *had plans*.”  

Naomi, *blinking rapidly*, mumbled, “Of *course* you did.”  

Kevin muttered, “I *really* should’ve gone to the sauna alone.”  

Jodi smirked. “Oh, Kev, this is *educational*.”  

Kevin groaned. “No, it *is not*.”  

Jodi *ignored him* and *continued*, getting *into it now*. “So I figure—*I’ve been waiting all week*, right? I’m not exactly trying to waste *time*.”  

Naomi, clearly *too curious for her own good*, asked, “So what did you do?”  

Jodi smirked. “I straddled him *the second the alarm went off*.”  

Tara *snorted.* “Oh, *classic*.”  

Naomi’s *face combusted.* “YOU—THE *SECOND* THE ALARM WENT OFF?!”  

Jodi *grinned.* “What? It’s efficient.”  

Kevin let out a *pained sigh*. “Oh my *God*.”  

Tara, wiping tears of laughter from her eyes, said, “So, babe, when *exactly* did he *regain consciousness*?”  

Jodi smirked. “Oh, *about three minutes in*.”  

Naomi *whimpered.* “I *cannot* handle this.”  

Kevin muttered, “I *told* you story time was a mistake.”  

Jodi stretched. “And *that’s* how you welcome a man home.”  

Tara *clapped her hands together*. “Beautiful. I *love* a solid opening strategy.”  

Naomi, *face still burning*, whimpered, “I need to *leave this sauna*.”  

Jodi grinned, looking *supremely pleased with herself.*  

Kevin muttered, “I need *therapy*.”  

And just like that, the *chaos* continued.    
---  
---  
A *sudden, loud commotion* broke through the humid haze, the wooden door *swinging open* with force as a *massive* group of men entered.  

The *heat* in the room hadn’t changed, but somehow, the *atmosphere* did.  

Kevin *immediately* sat up, clocked who just walked in, and smirked. “Oh. That would be the *men’s swimming team* from the university.”  

Naomi’s *soul physically left her body.*  

She *froze*, eyes going *wide*, mouth slightly open as she *visibly short-circuited* at the *density* of *pure male athleticism* that had just *infiltrated* the room.  

Jodi, watching *every single stage* of Naomi’s *mental collapse*, smirked.  

“Babe,” she purred, nudging her, “have you *ever* seen anything like this before?”  

Naomi *made a small, inhuman noise*.  

Then, in a whisper, she *breathed*, “No.”  

A long pause.  

Then—  

She *exhaled slowly, reverently.*  

“I think I’m *in love* with the sauna.”  

Kevin *snorted*.  

Jodi *howled with laughter.*  

Tara, *beaming*, *clapped her hands together*. “Oh, *Naomi*, babe, you’re *doing so well*.”  

Naomi, *still very much malfunctioning*, *did not respond*.  

Kevin, amused but also *fully aware* of what could happen next, *cleared his throat* and leaned toward Naomi.  

“Babe,” he said, voice *half-amused, half-serious*, “etiquette.”  

Naomi *blinked rapidly, attempting a system reboot*. “Etiquette?”  

Kevin nodded. “You can *look*, but don’t *stare*. You can *appreciate*, but don’t *gawk*.”  

Naomi *nodded rapidly*, looking like she was trying to *hold it together*. “Right. Yes. I *know* that.”  

Kevin smirked. “Do you?”  

Naomi *did not respond*.  

Jodi, *highly entertained*, smirked. “Y’know, babe, you’re *probably* the only woman in this room that’s their age.”  

Naomi, *brain still buffering*, whispered, “That’s *so much worse*.”  

Tara, now *visibly enjoying herself far too much*, *sighed dramatically*. “Honestly? I’m *getting a reaction* just watching *this many guys* walk in here.”  

Jodi smirked. “Babe, *same*.”  

Tara turned to Kevin. “So what’s the *most* you’ve ever seen in here at once?”  

Kevin, shifting comfortably like a *professor about to deliver a lecture*, exhaled. “The *most crowded* was a special event—over *150* people.”  

Naomi, *still spiraling*, *visibly gasped*.  

Kevin continued, gesturing lazily. “On a *regular* day? The biggest crowd is about *40*.”  

Tara *tilted her head*, smirking. “And now, here we are, in a *room full* of cocks.”  

Kevin sighed *wistfully*, shaking his head. “If only more ladies would *buy memberships*.”  

Jodi raised an eyebrow. “What, not enough *sauna-loving women* in this town?”  

Kevin sighed, clearly *disappointed in society as a whole.* “Not *nearly* enough.”  

Naomi *whimpered*.  

Jodi *grinned* and leaned into her. “Babe, looks like you’ve got a *civic duty* now.”  

Naomi *covered her face*. “I *hate* this conversation.”  

Tara *cackled.* “Oh, babe, no you *don’t*.”  

And just like that, Naomi’s *journey into madness* continued.    
---  
---  
Naomi, *visibly* trying to remain composed, suddenly felt about *six pairs of eyes* on her.  

She *froze*.  

Jodi, *always attuned to the energy shift*, smirked.  

“Oh, babe,” she said smoothly, “*even for me*, this is a *very unusual* situation.”  

Naomi *let out a strangled noise*. “GOOD TO KNOW.”  

Jodi *stretched lazily*, completely at ease. “I mean, I watch *a lot* of streaming sites. I enjoy *adult video* from time to time. But this?”  

She gestured vaguely at the *steam-filled room*, the *walls of muscle* now surrounding them.  

“This is a *hell of a show*.”  

Naomi, *still spiraling*, exhaled sharply. “I FEEL LIKE I’M *MELTING*.”  

Tara, who had been *comfortably stretched out*, *thought better of it* and smoothly sat up, resting her head on her knees.  

Jodi *chuckled.* “What, babe? Feeling a *little too visible*?”  

Tara smirked, *completely unbothered*. “Just doing a little *strategic repositioning*.”  

Naomi, *barely functioning*, suddenly asked, “HOW MUCH DOES THIS COST PER MONTH OR YEAR?”  

Kevin blinked at the *sudden topic shift.* “What?”  

Naomi *turned to him, eyes wild.* “THIS. THE MEMBERSHIP. HOW MUCH?”  

Kevin, still mildly *stunned* at her energy, replied, “Fourteen bucks a month… *if* you’re a YMCA member.”  

Naomi *gasped dramatically*.  

“Oh my *God*,” she *breathed*. “I *am* a YMCA member.”  

Jodi, *grinning*, crossed her arms. “Oh, *this is happening*.”  

Naomi *nodded frantically.* “WHEN WE GET DONE, I’M GONNA *BUY ONE*.”  

Kevin smirked. “Welcome to the club.”  

Naomi, *visibly sweating both from heat and sheer sensory overload*, exhaled. “I *swear* I’m *melting*.”  

Then, without thinking, she turned to Jodi.  

“Is *this* how you feel after sex with Jeff?”  

Jodi, momentarily caught off guard, blinked.  

“…Melting?”  

Naomi *nodded aggressively.*  

Jodi considered. Then, smirking, shrugged.  

“It *depends*.”  

Tara *howled* with laughter. “Oh, babe, you *knew* that was the answer.”  

Naomi *whimpered.*  

Jodi grinned. “But *now* I’m curious. What’s your *definition* of ‘melting’?”  

Naomi groaned, covering her face. “I DON’T KNOW, EVERYTHING IS *TOO MUCH*.”  

Kevin sighed. “Yeah, sauna *will* do that.”  

Jodi *grinned wickedly.* “Oh, babe. That is *not* the sauna.”  

Naomi *let out an actual scream.*  

Tara *cackled.*  

And just like that, Naomi’s *complete and utter unraveling* continued.    
---  
---  
Kevin *exhaled sharply* and sat forward, rubbing his face like a man who had *pushed his limits*.  

“Alright,” he said, stretching his arms, “I gotta get up. I’m hitting *my* limit.”  

Naomi, *who was still visibly melting into the bench*, blinked. “Wait. *There’s a limit?*”  

Kevin gave her a *look.* “Yes, babe. *There’s a limit.*”  

Jodi *snorted.* “You say that, but Naomi might just *live here* now.”  

Naomi, still *sweating like a sinner in church*, whispered, “I *could*.”  

Kevin *sighed* and stood up, shaking his head. “Look, you *ladies* might want to leave, too. You’ve been in here *maybe twenty minutes*.”  

Tara, still looking *ridiculously comfortable*, raised an eyebrow. “That’s bad?”  

Kevin smirked. “It’s *a lot* if you’re new to it.”  

Jodi *tilted her head.* “Okay, so what, we just… *walk out*?”  

Kevin chuckled. “No, you *cool down first*—and *don’t shower for at least twenty minutes* or you’ll *shock your system*.”  

Naomi *gasped dramatically*. “OUR SYSTEM?!”  

Kevin *grinned.* “Yeah, sauna heat *sticks*—if you hit cold water too soon, your body *will* freak out.”  

Jodi smirked. “So what *exactly* are we supposed to do?”  

Kevin shrugged. “Sit in the *locker room* and chill. Or…” he glanced at them, *grinning slightly*, “you could use one of the *hot tubs*.”  

Naomi perked up immediately. “*Oooooh*.”  

Tara, intrigued, smirked. “Alright, *now* we’re talking.”  

Kevin *held up a finger.* “BUT—”  

Jodi narrowed her eyes. “*But?*”  

Kevin *chuckled.* “The tubs are *gonna feel freezing cold* after this. *So watch out.*”  

Naomi, who had already mentally committed, shrugged. “I *like* cold water.”  

Kevin *snorted.* “Babe. You *say that now*.”  

Jodi smirked. “So basically, we *leave the sauna*… to go be *cold and confused*?”  

Kevin grinned. “Basically, yeah.”  

Tara sighed, standing up and *stretching luxuriously.* “Sounds *about right.*”  

Naomi, still *not fully understanding what she had signed up for*, *beamed.* “Let’s do it.”  

Jodi shook her head, *amused*. “Well, babe, this was *your revelation*.”  

Kevin *chuckled* as he led them out. “Alright, *ladies*, welcome to the *next phase* of your sauna initiation.”  

Naomi *whimpered in excitement.*  

Jodi smirked. “Oh, *this* is gonna be good.”  

And just like that, the chaos *continued.*    
---  
---  
The *moment* they stepped into the ladies’ locker room, Naomi made a *beeline* for the whirlpools.  

Jodi and Tara, *slightly more hesitant*, watched as she *confidently* dipped a foot into the water.  

She exhaled, then *nodded decisively.*  

“Oh, yeah,” she declared. “This is *fine.*”  

Then, without *any* hesitation—  

She *plopped* right in.  

Jodi and Tara, having *both* been warned that post-sauna water would feel *frigid*, *watched in slow motion* as Naomi’s *soul* left her body.  

A half-second passed.  

Then—  

“*HNNNGGHHH—*”  

Naomi *jerked violently*, *shaking* like a *cartoon character who had just licked a power line*.  

Jodi *howled* with laughter.  

Tara, already *doubling over*, gasped out, “*Oh my God, babe!*”  

Naomi, *visibly struggling to process the situation*, *clutched* the edge of the tub, eyes *wide in betrayal*.  

“The water’s a little… *chilly*,” she managed, voice *several octaves higher than normal*.  

Jodi, *wheezing*, *gasped for air* before correcting her.  

“Oh, no, babe. It’s a little bit… *nippy*.”  

Naomi, still *desperately clinging to life*, blinked rapidly.  

“…I don’t get it.”  

Jodi *stared at her*.  

Tara *cackled*. “Oh, babe. You *really* don’t?”  

Naomi, *visibly shaking*, frowned. “What?”  

Jodi *grinned* and gestured vaguely at Naomi’s chest. “Babe.”  

Naomi *looked down*.  

A *long pause.*  

Then—  

“OH MY *GOD*.”  

Tara *screamed* with laughter.  

Jodi *gasped for air*, leaning on the bench to *support herself*. “Babe, it took you *way too long* to clock that one.”  

Naomi *slapped her hands over her chest, horrified.* “I HATE YOU.”  

Jodi *wiped tears from her eyes.* “Babe, it’s *science.*”  

Tara, *still laughing*, exhaled. “Oh, this is *my favorite night ever*.”  

Naomi *whimpered* in sheer embarrassment. “I *really* thought it was *fine*.”  

Jodi *grinned wickedly.* “Oh, babe. That’s *what makes it so good*.”  

And just like that, Naomi *learned another valuable lesson.*    
---  
---  
Tara, still *wiping tears of laughter* from her eyes, exhaled and shook her head.  

“Babe,” she said, *grinning at Naomi*, “those *nipples* of yours are *something else*.”  

Naomi, still *recovering* from her *emotional support meltdown*, blinked rapidly. “WHAT?!”  

Tara *shrugged*, gesturing casually. “I just *never* would’ve figured you’d have, y’know… *prominent nipples*.”  

Naomi *covered her chest again, horrified.* “TARA.”  

Jodi *smirked* and leaned in. “No, no, babe. We *learned something today.*”  

Naomi *whimpered.* “I WANT A REFUND ON THIS EXPERIENCE.”  

Jodi, still *grinning*, decided to *finally* take a seat beside the whirlpool, opting to just *dip her feet* into the *foamy, swirling water* instead of fully committing to Naomi’s *frozen tundra disaster.*  

The *instant* she settled in, Naomi—still *very much processing her life choices*—suddenly perked up.  

Jodi *narrowed her eyes immediately.* “Babe. *What now?*”  

Naomi, *blinking innocently*, gestured vaguely in her direction.  

“Well, since you’re sitting *so close*…”  

Jodi *raised an eyebrow.* “Yeah?”  

Naomi, voice *far too casual*, continued, “Can I check out how your *shave job* holds up *up close*?”  

Tara *immediately* choked on nothing.  

Jodi, momentarily *caught off guard*, *snorted.* “I *beg your pardon?*”  

Naomi *shrugged*, now *fully committing* to her curiosity. “I mean, it’s *right there.*”  

Tara *wheezed.* “OH MY *GOD*, BABE.”  

Jodi *tilted her head, highly amused.* “So let me get this straight—you *absolutely lost your mind* over a *nip slip*, but *now* you’re requesting a *close-up inspection*?”  

Naomi *whimpered*. “I AM *NOT* THINKING CLEARLY.”  

Tara, now *fully unhinged*, *clapped her hands together in delight*. “NOPE. TOO LATE. THIS IS *OFFICIALLY HAPPENING*.”  

Jodi, still *grinning*, exhaled dramatically.  

“Well, babe,” she mused, “if you wanna *look*—I *suppose* I can allow it.”  

Naomi *squeaked in pure crisis.*  

Tara *cackled.* “OH, THIS IS *THE BEST NIGHT EVER*.”  

And just like that, the night *descended into complete madness.*    
---  
---  
Naomi, who had *already* been on a *journey of self-discovery* tonight, found herself *seriously considering* something *entirely new*.  

Laser hair removal.  

It had *crossed her mind before*, sure. But now, *sitting here*, with Jodi providing what could *only be described* as a *direct case study*—  

Well.  

This might be *the best opportunity of her life* to determine if this was a *desirable thing*.  

But—  

There was a *problem*.  

Because *how* exactly does one say *that* out loud?  

She had *no idea*.  

So, instead—  

She did *not* say it the *easy way*.  

Instead, she did what she *always* did.  

She *made it weird.*  

Naomi exhaled *sharply*, her *face an unreadable mix* of deep contemplation and *mild existential crisis*.  

Jodi, who had *been around Naomi long enough* to recognize *the exact moment she was about to say something utterly unhinged*, *narrowed her eyes slightly*.  

“Babe,” she asked, smirking, “*what’s* happening in that head of yours right now?”  

Naomi *blinked rapidly*, *visibly struggling* to form a sentence.  

Tara, already *highly entertained*, leaned in. “Oh, this is gonna be *good*.”  

Naomi *swallowed* and, after a *long pause*, finally said—  

“…*How committed* are you to this look?”  

Jodi *blinked once*.  

Then—  

She *grinned*.  

“Oh, babe,” she purred, *instantly realizing what was happening*. “Are you *considering a change*?”  

Naomi *panicked immediately*. “I—*NO*—I mean—*MAYBE*—I mean—”  

Tara, *cackling*, cut in. “Babe, that is *not* how you hide an impulse decision.”  

Naomi *whimpered.* “I’M JUST *THINKING*.”  

Jodi *tilted her head, highly amused*. “Thinking *about what*?”  

Naomi *covered her face with her hands*. “I DON’T KNOW HOW TO SAY IT.”  

Tara, *loving every second of this*, grinned. “Say *what*, babe?”  

Naomi, *visibly suffering*, finally blurted—  

“I’M CONSIDERING *LASER HAIR REMOVAL* AND I *NEEDED DATA*.”  

A beat of silence.  

Then—  

Jodi *snorted*.  

Tara *howled with laughter*.  

Naomi *collapsed into the water, mortified.*  

Jodi, wiping away a tear of amusement, smirked. “Babe, there were *a thousand better ways* to say that.”  

Naomi, voice *muffled*, groaned. “I KNOW.”  

Tara, *grinning like a wolf*, nudged her. “So? Did you get your *data*?”  

Naomi *whimpered.*  

Jodi stretched *lazily*, *fully basking in her victory*. “Babe, if you needed a *peer review*, you *could’ve just asked*.”  

Naomi *let out a strangled noise*.  

Tara, grinning wickedly, leaned in. “Babe, I *fully support your science journey*.”  

Naomi, still *desperately trying to sink into the whirlpool and disappear forever*, whispered, “I hate all of you.”  

Jodi smirked. “Babe, you *love us*.”  

And just like that, Naomi’s *education* continued.    
---  
---  
Naomi, still *half-sunk* in the whirlpool like she was trying to *dissolve into the water itself*, had just *loudly and unavoidably* admitted that she was considering *laser hair removal* and *needed data*.  

Jodi, smirking *like a cat who had just cornered a trapped mouse*, stretched *lazily*, reveling in the *absolute gift* she had just been handed.  

Tara, *positively glowing* with *secondhand chaos energy*, sighed dreamily.  

“Oh, babe,” she said, shaking her head. “This is the *best* night of my life.”  

Naomi, still *trying and failing* to disappear, *whimpered*. “I *knew* this was a mistake.”  

Jodi, grinning *far too wickedly*, tapped her chin *thoughtfully*. “So. *You needed data*, huh?”  

Naomi, voice *muffled by shame*, groaned. “*Shut up.*”  

Jodi *ignored her completely* and leaned in *slightly*. “Tell me, babe—did you *get enough* data? Or do you need to *take notes*?”  

Naomi *audibly whimpered*. “*I HATE YOU.*”  

Tara, *grinning like the devil*, *gasped dramatically*.  

“Oh my *God*—Naomi, do you need a *closer look*?”  

Naomi *let out an actual scream*.  

Jodi *howled with laughter*. “Oh, babe. You *walked right into this*.”  

Naomi, *gripping the side of the whirlpool like it was a life raft*, hissed, “I WAS *TRYING* TO BE *INQUISITIVE*.”  

Tara cackled. “Oh, babe, *you were trying to be scientific* and *instead*, you *wrote your own obituary*.”  

Jodi, *smirking triumphantly*, leaned back. “Alright, let’s *talk logistics*, babe.”  

Naomi *froze*. “…What?”  

Jodi shrugged *casually*. “If you’re gonna *do it*, you need to *know what you’re signing up for*.”  

Tara *perked up*. “Oh, *yes*! I love an *educational moment*.”  

Naomi, eyes *widening in horror*, shook her head. “No. NO. I AM *NOT* READY FOR THIS.”  

Jodi *ignored her completely* and started *listing things off on her fingers*.  

“First,” she said, “it’s gonna *hurt*, babe.”  

Naomi *visibly regretted everything*.  

Tara nodded *knowingly*. “Yeah, babe, they don’t *laser it away with love*.”  

Naomi *whimpered*. “WHY DID NO ONE TELL ME THIS?!”  

Jodi *grinned.* “Because *no one thinks about it* until they’re *half-naked in a sauna* conducting an *accidental field study*.”  

Tara *cackled*. “Babe, you’re *doing research in real-time*.”  

Naomi *hid her face in her hands.* “I *hate* both of you.”  

Jodi smirked. “Second—*you can’t shave or wax for weeks beforehand*.”  

Naomi *gasped*. “WHAT.”  

Tara *nodded sagely.* “Yeah, babe, you gotta *grow it out* so the lasers can *zap your soul away properly*.”  

Naomi *looked like she was having a *full-body spiritual crisis**.  

Jodi continued, *unfazed*. “Third—no sun exposure for *weeks*.”  

Naomi *blinked rapidly*. “*Weeks*?!”  

Jodi nodded. “Yep. Unless you *wanna burn* like a *marshmallow at a campfire*.”  

Tara *snorted.* “Babe, you are NOT emotionally prepared for this journey.”  

Naomi, now *fully spiraling*, *sank further into the water.* “I AM *RECONSIDERING EVERYTHING*.”  

Jodi *grinned wickedly*. “Oh, babe. *But think of the results.*”  

Naomi *covered her face again*.  

Tara leaned in, smirking. “Naomi. Babe. Do you *want* the clean aesthetic lines?”  

Naomi *whimpered.*  

Jodi *tilted her head*. “Do you want *to accentuate your femininity*?”  

Naomi *groaned*.  

Tara, *grinning ear to ear*, pressed further. “Or are you *gonna stay cloaked*?”  

Naomi, after *several seconds* of sheer existential crisis, *let out a defeated sigh*.  

“…I hate how much I’m *actually considering this*.”  

Jodi *laughed*. “Oh, babe. You’re *committed now*.”  

Tara *beamed.* “You’re *on a journey*.”  

Naomi, *fully resigned to her fate*, groaned, “I HAVE *SO MANY REGRETS*.”  

Jodi, *still basking in her victory*, stretched.  

“Babe, this is what we call *a teachable moment*.”  

And just like that, Naomi’s *life decisions* took a *very unexpected turn*.    
---  
---  
Naomi, *still deep in her existential grooming crisis*, exhaled sharply, *mentally committing* to her decision.  

Then, *with zero hesitation and absolutely no self-preservation instinct*, she tilted her head *thoughtfully* and mused—  

“Y’know… it’s kinda *cute* how your clitoris just *pops out* like a *Funko Pop*.”  

Silence.  

Pure.  

Absolute.  

**Silence.**  

Then—  

Jodi *HOWLED*—her *entire body convulsing* as she *completely lost it*, nearly *slipping straight into the whirlpool* in her *uncontrollable fit of laughter*.  

Tara, *mid-breath*, *SNORTED so loudly* she *startled herself*.  

Naomi, *who had just casually dropped the single most unhinged statement of her life*, *blinked rapidly*.  

“…What?”  

Tara *slammed a hand over her mouth, eyes wide in absolute horror*.  

“BABE,” she *gasped through laughter*, “YOU *DID NOT* JUST GO THERE.”  

Jodi, *still laughing so hard she was nearly breathless*, *clutched the edge of the bench for dear life*.  

“Oh my *GOD*, SHE *DID*,” she wheezed.  

Naomi, *suddenly realizing the depth of her mistake*, *whimpered in confusion*.  

“I—I was just—*making an observation*!”  

Tara *collapsed backward, absolutely wheezing*. “OH, BABE. *YOU DIDN’T JUST OBSERVE, YOU IMMORTALIZED IT.*”  

Jodi *wiped tears from her eyes, still struggling to breathe*.  

“*Funko Pops*, Naomi?! REALLY?!”  

Naomi *whimpered harder*. “*I DIDN’T THINK IT WAS THAT BAD*.”  

Tara, *laughing so hard she could barely function*, *waved a hand in the air like she was trying to process the sheer insanity of what had just happened.*  

“Oh my *God*, babe, that’s gonna *haunt you*.”  

Jodi, still *gasping* for air, finally *gained just enough control* to sit up properly.  

Then—  

She smirked.  

“Oh, *babe*,” she purred, still *high off laughter*, “this is *escaping the room*.”  

Naomi’s *soul left her body.* “*NO.*”  

Jodi *grinned wickedly*, leaning in. “This is getting *told to Jeff*.”  

Naomi *squeaked*. “*NO, NO, NO, NO, NO.*”  

Tara *HOWLED AGAIN*. “BABE, THIS IS GOING TO *LIVE FOREVER*.”  

Jodi, *cackling*, shook her head. “Oh, babe. This is *going global*.”  

Naomi *whimpered*, curling into a ball. “I *HATE EVERYTHING*.”  

Tara, still *cry-laughing*, *clapped her hands together*. “Babe. Your *legacy* is now *‘Funko Pop Clit’.*”  

Naomi *let out an actual, anguished scream*.  

Jodi, *completely triumphant*, stretched lazily.  

“Oh, babe. You *did this to yourself*.”  

And just like that, Naomi *officially* ruined her *entire life*.    
---  
---  
Tara, *finally regaining her breath after nearly laughing herself unconscious*, stretched lazily and *took back control of the conversation like the composed queen she was.*  

“Y’know,” she mused, “sometimes *having a full bush* can actually be the *more subtle and intriguing* choice.”  

Naomi, still *recovering from her catastrophic Funko Pop mistake*, *blinked rapidly*. “WHAT?”  

Tara smirked. “Oh yeah. The whole *bare-all aesthetic* is *great*, don’t get me wrong. But sometimes?” She shrugged. “A little *mystery* makes things *way more interesting*.”  

Jodi, *grinning*, tilted her head. “Oh, so you’re saying *the reveal* is half the fun?”  

Tara nodded *knowingly.* “Exactly. *Build-up, babe.*”  

Naomi, still *wanting to disappear into the water*, *whimpered*. “I would *love* to contribute to this conversation, but I have lost *all rights* after my last comment.”  

Jodi *snorted*. “Oh, babe, you didn’t just lose *rights*, you lost *dignity*.”  

Naomi *let out a soft, dying noise.*  

Tara smirked and *continued her original point*, now *thoroughly enjoying herself.*  

“Besides,” she said smoothly, “when I *am* aroused, my clit can be *very* prominent, but it’s going to be *shrouded in mystery* for the most part.”  

Jodi *grinned wickedly*. “So you’re saying you got a *VIP backstage experience*?”  

Tara *snorted*. “Oh, babe. *Exclusive access only.*”  

Naomi, *who had been *desperately* trying to recalibrate her entire existence*, exhaled *shakily* and *put her hands together in prayer*.  

“Okay. *New rule.*” She cleared her throat. “Can we all please agree to *never* use the phrases *Funko Pop* and *clitoris* in the same sentence ever again?”  

Jodi *grinned like a shark*.  

“Oh, babe.”  

Naomi *froze instantly.*  

Jodi leaned in.  

“*You don’t get to make rules anymore, Funko Girl.*”  

Tara *HOWLED WITH LAUGHTER.*  

Naomi *whimpered loudly*. “*NOOOOOOOO.*”  

Jodi *tilted her head, grinning ear to ear*. “But, hey, since we’re all *sharing*—what happens to *yours* when you’re raring to go?”  

Naomi *let out a strangled gasp.* “I AM *NOT* ANSWERING THAT.”  

Tara, *laughing so hard she was crying*, wiped a tear from her face. “Babe, you *cannot* just throw a grenade into this conversation and then *refuse to participate*.”  

Jodi *smirked*. “Oh, babe. *It’s only fair*.”  

Naomi, now *fully cornered*, *whimpered again*. “I *hate* both of you.”  

Jodi leaned back, stretching *luxuriously*. “Oh, babe. You *love us*.”  

Naomi *collapsed into the water.* “I NEED TO *RENOUNCE SPEECH*.”  

Tara *grinned.* “Babe, the *damage is done*.”  

And just like that, Naomi *officially lost control of her entire existence.*

Naomi, *still trying and failing to disappear into the water*, *whimpered* at the expectation *now bearing down on her like a freight train.*

Jodi smirked. “Come on, babe. *What’s the answer?*”  

Naomi *swallowed hard*. “I—um—*what was the question again?*”

Naomi *whimpered again.*

Naomi *visibly short-circuited.*  

“I—uh—*well*—I mean—um—”

Jodi *grinned wider.* “Oh, babe. *You can, and you will.*”

“OH MY *GOD*,” she *gasped*, “NAOMI, YOU ARE KILLING ME.”  

Naomi *collapsed into the water, hiding her face.* “I AM *DYING INSIDE*.”  

Tara *cackled*, shaking her head. “Babe, I *love* that your clit has *a personality type.*”  

Jodi, still *gasping for air*, managed, “Oh, babe. She’s got *introvert energy*.”  

Naomi *whimpered in pure agony.* “I NEED TO *LEAVE THIS EARTH*.”  

Jodi *snorted, wiping a tear from her eye.* “Oh, babe. Not yet.”  

Naomi *groaned loudly*. “WHY?”  

Jodi *tilted her head, smirking.* “Because *now* it’s been long enough for us to *finally* go shower and wash off all this *sweat and salt*.”  

Tara stretched. “Oh, *thank God*.”  

Naomi, *still reeling*, muttered, “Yeah, maybe if I stand under *boiling water* it’ll *purge this entire conversation* from existence.”  

Jodi *grinned wickedly.* “Oh, babe. That’s *not* how this works.”  

As they *stood up and prepared to exit*, Jodi *paused* and asked the *obvious* question.  

“Alright, who brought *quality* shampoo and conditioner?”  

A *long silence.*  

Tara sighed. “Shit. *Not me.*”  

Naomi, still *recovering*, muttered, “I… didn’t plan for this.”  

Jodi *exhaled dramatically*. “Okay, *so none of us thought this through*.”  

Tara shrugged. “Guess we’re stuck with the *cheap, sad* locker room soap.”  

Jodi *sighed*. “Well, *that’s unfortunate*.”  

Then, after a *beat*, she turned to Naomi, grinning *like a wolf.*  

“Well, babe, *since we’re all heading to the showers*…”  

Naomi *froze instantly.*  

Jodi smirked.  

“…maybe we should finally *see for ourselves* if *Funko Girl’s love button* is *as shy as she claims.*”  

Naomi *LET OUT AN ACTUAL SCREAM.*  

Tara *COMPLETELY LOST IT.*  

Jodi *grinned in absolute victory.*  

And just like that, Naomi *officially entered the ninth circle of hell.*

“Well,” she announced, *deadpan*, “it looks like we have a *stunning* selection of…”  

She squinted.  

“*Pert Plus.*”  

Tara *snorted*. “The *green* kind.”  

Jodi nodded *solemnly*. “Ah, yes. *The Classic.*”  

Naomi, still *emotionally recovering* from her last fifteen minutes of life, tilted her head. “What else is there?”  

Jodi sighed dramatically and *listed them off like a disappointment roll call.*

“Okay. *Lesson learned.*”

Naomi sighed dramatically. “I *really enjoyed my time*.”  

Jodi and Tara exchanged *knowing looks*.

She *stretched her arms overhead*, groaning slightly.  

“I laughed so hard my *belly muscles hurt*.”  

Jodi *nodded instantly*. “Oh, babe, *same*. Feels like we just did a *full-core workout*.”  

Tara *grinned wickedly*. “Oh, speaking of workouts—”  

She gestured toward the showers.  

“This is about to be *so much fun*.”  

Naomi narrowed her eyes. “Why?”  

Tara smirked. “Because *showering together* in a big open room? *Oh, babe.*”  

She waggled her eyebrows.  

“This is gonna feel *exactly* like a *bad women-in-prison movie* in just a second.”  

Naomi *froze*.  

Her *face did something complicated*.  

Then—  

“…I *understood* that reference.”  

Jodi *perked up instantly.* “Oh?”  

Naomi *cleared her throat awkwardly.* “My dad had some of those DVDs, but, uh—”  

She *visibly winced.*  

“I *never dared watch*.”  

Jodi *immediately* lost her *entire* mind.  

She *HOWLED*, clutching the vending machine for support.  

Tara *collapsed against the wall, gasping for air.* “OH MY *GOD*, BABE.”  

Naomi, *realizing what she had just admitted*, *covered her face in horror.*  

Jodi, *still cackling*, wiped a tear from her eye. “Oh, babe. You *never dared watch*, huh?”  

Naomi *whimpered*. “I *HATE* MYSELF.”  

Tara, *grinning ear to ear*, *leaned in close*.  

“Babe.”  

Naomi *groaned*. “*What?*”  

Tara smirked.  

“Are you *daring* to watch now?”  

Naomi *let out an actual scream*.  

Jodi *howled with laughter.*  

And just like that, the *shower scene* was about to become *a disaster of its own making*.

The locker room showers were *exactly* what you’d expect—*big, open, and completely unforgiving*.  

No stalls.  

No dividers.  

Just *wide open vulnerability* and a *terrible amount of echo*.  

Naomi, *visibly sweating* despite the fact that she *had already sweat out half her body weight in the sauna*, *froze* at the threshold.  

“OH,” she *breathed*, eyes going *wide in realization*.  

Jodi and Tara, already *completely at peace* with the situation, turned *in perfect sync* to look at her.  

Jodi smirked. “Oh, babe. You *just now* clocking this?”  

Naomi, *visibly experiencing ego death*, *nodded rapidly*.  

Tara *cackled*. “Babe, we *told* you. This is a full-on *women-in-prison* movie moment.”  

Naomi, *visibly vibrating*, whispered, “I AM *NOT READY*.”  

Jodi, *completely unsympathetic*, *grinned like the devil*.  

“Oh, babe,” she purred, “*but you dared to watch*.”  

Naomi *gasped dramatically*. “THAT WAS A MISTAKE.”  

Tara, *grinning ear to ear*, *clapped her hands together*. “Babe, it’s *fine*. Just act *natural*.”  

Naomi *gasped again.* “THERE IS *NO WAY* TO ACT NATURAL HERE.”

Naomi *visibly twitched.* “YOU WANT ME TO ROLEPLAY *WORLD WAR II*?!”  

Tara *snorted so hard she startled herself*.  

Jodi, *wheezing with laughter*, *waved a hand dismissively*. “Okay, okay—bad example.”  

She *gestured vaguely* at the row of *mockingly wide-open shower heads*.  

“Look, babe, we just gotta *get in, rinse off, and get out*.”  

Naomi *exhaled sharply*. “Right. Yes. *I can do that.*”  

Tara smirked. “Oh, babe, but can you?”  

Naomi *whimpered*. “I AM *TRYING*.”  

Jodi, *noting the continued hesitation*, smirked.  

“Well, Funko Girl, let’s not *forget*—we *did* say we were gonna take this moment to, y’know…”  

She *tilted her head dramatically.*  

“…check out *your love button*.”  

Naomi *SCREAMED.*  

Tara *FELL AGAINST THE WALL, WHEEZING.*  

Jodi *grinned in absolute triumph.*  

“Babe,” she teased, “is it *shy right now*? Or is it *prominent*?”  

Naomi *whimpered violently*. “I AM *NOT ANSWERING THAT*.”  

Tara, *barely able to breathe*, gasped, “BABE, YOU *STARTED THIS*.”  

Naomi, *vibrating on a molecular level*, turned *bright red*. “THAT WAS A MISTAKE.”  

Jodi smirked. “Oh, babe, but we’re *all here now*.”  

Naomi *collapsed into a ball of existential crisis*.  

Jodi, still *fully entertained*, finally *walked under the water*, letting the lukewarm spray *cascade down her body*.  

She *sighed dramatically*.  

“Well, *this is underwhelming*.”  

Tara, now *fully embracing the bit*, stepped in next to her.  

“Yeah, babe, this is *not the cinematic experience* we were promised.”  

Jodi *snorted*. “Right? No dramatic music? No *slow-mo shots*? *Disappointing*.”  

Naomi, *still not committed*, *hugged herself*.  

“I *hate* this.”  

Jodi grinned. “Oh, babe. You *love us*.”  

Naomi *whimpered*.  

Jodi smirked.  

“So? You *getting in*, or do we have to *drag you in*?”  

Naomi *let out a strangled noise*.  

And just like that, the *shower debacle officially commenced.*
