For the first *thirty seconds*, the water was *meh at best*.  

Lukewarm. *Not bad*, but *not great*.  

Then—  

The *hot water hit* all at once, and a collective *sigh of relief* passed through the group as the temperature *finally* became *properly perfect.*  

Jodi, Tara, and Naomi all got to work, *finally able* to scrub off the *layers of sweat, salt, and unhinged decisions* they had accumulated.  

For a moment, things were… surprisingly *peaceful*.  

No screaming.  

No chaos.  

Just the *soothing rhythm* of hot water cascading down their bodies, the soft sounds of hands working shampoo into hair, and the occasional sigh of relaxation.  

It was all going *so well.*  

And then—  

Tara *paused*.  

She blinked.  

Then—  

She *grinned wickedly.*  

“…Babe.”  

Jodi, mid-lather, *blinked lazily*. “Hmm?”  

Tara *tilted her head*, eyes locked on *one very specific thing.*  

Jodi’s positioning.  

More specifically—  

The fact that Jodi was *very much favoring* the heavy *direct spray* of her shower head.  

Right between her legs.  

Tara’s grin *widened.*  

“…Are you having *too much fun* right now?”  

Jodi *immediately* smirked.  

“What, babe? I *like* to dwell under *hot running water*.”  

Tara *snorted.* “Babe, that is *not* what you’re dwelling on.”  

Jodi, completely *unbothered*, grinned.  

“I mean… the water’s *hitting where it hits*.”  

Tara, *cackling*, shook her head.  

“Babe, you are *one step away* from making this *a solo date*.”  

Jodi *grinned wider*. “And?”  

Tara *howled with laughter*.  

Naomi, *still lathering her hair, completely oblivious*, *blinked at them in confusion*.  

“…Wait. What are you guys talking about?”  

Jodi *snorted*.  

Tara *immediately* turned to Naomi, *grinning like a devil*.  

“Oh, babe. You *don’t know?*”  

Naomi, *clueless as ever*, *shook her head*. “Know *what*?”  

Jodi, still *very much enjoying herself*, smirked.  

“Babe,” she said smoothly, “you *ever* just… *stand under the water* a little *too long*?”  

Naomi *blinked rapidly*. “What do you mean?”  

Jodi smirked.  

Then—  

She *tilted her hips slightly*, letting the hot water *pour* against her in a way that was *just slightly too intentional*.  

Naomi *stared*.  

For a long beat.  

Then—  

Her *entire face combusted.*  

“OH MY *GOD*.”  

Jodi *howled with laughter*. “Babe, *welcome* to the conversation.”  

Naomi *gasped dramatically*. “YOU *DO THAT?!*”  

Jodi *grinned unapologetically.* “Babe, I *love* to dwell.”  

Naomi *whimpered*. “THAT’S *ILLEGAL*.”  

Tara *lost her mind*. “Babe, you are *so fucking innocent*, it *kills me*.”  

Naomi, *visibly rethinking her entire life*, *shook her head violently*.  

“I—I—*NO*—I’VE NEVER—”  

Jodi, *grinning ear to ear*, tilted her head.  

“Oh, babe. You *should*.”  

Naomi *gasped again.* “I *CANNOT HANDLE THIS CONVERSATION*.”  

Tara smirked. “Babe, *you started it*.”  

Naomi *whimpered*.  

Jodi stretched luxuriously, *fully basking in her win*.  

“Oh, babe. *You’re learning so much tonight*.”  

And just like that, Naomi’s *education* continued.

Tara, who had *zero shame and a strong sense of adventure*, tilted her head *thoughtfully*.  

“Huh,” she mused, fingers working conditioner through her hair. “Might as well *try it* while I let this sit.”

“WAIT. WHAT?!”

“Oh, babe,” she purred, “she’s *experimenting*.”

Tara, already shifting to *angle herself properly*, smirked. “Babe, I *absolutely* can.”

Naomi *looked like she was about to implode.*  

“ARE YOU *SERIOUSLY*—YOU’RE *BOTH*—RIGHT *NOW*—”  

Tara, eyes *half-lidded*, *tilted her hips slightly into the water flow*.  

Then—

“Oh.”  

Jodi smirked. “Yeah, babe?”  

Tara *sighed lazily*. “*Pleasant.*”  

Naomi *let out an actual strangled noise.*  

Jodi *snorted with laughter.* “Babe, *you’re missing out*.”  

Naomi *frantically shook her head.* “I—NO—THIS IS *TOO MUCH*—”  

Tara, now *entirely comfortable*, peeked one eye open.  

“Babe.”  

Naomi *whimpered*. “WHAT.”  

Tara smirked. “Come on. Just *try it*.”  

Naomi *gasped like someone just suggested she commit an actual felony.*  

“*ABSOLUTELY NOT*.”  

Jodi, *still very relaxed*, smirked. “Oh, babe. You *want to*.”

Tara *grinned*. “Then *prove* it. *Stand under the spray.*”  

Naomi *froze completely.*  

Jodi, sensing *her hesitation*, *grinned wider*.  

“Ohhh, babe. You *do* want to.”  

Naomi *whimpered loudly*.  

Tara tilted her head. “Babe, *it’s just water*.”  

Jodi *snorted.* “Oh, yeah. *Just water*.”  

Naomi *visibly* fought an *internal war*.  

Then, *after a full-body crisis*, she *very hesitantly* shuffled half an inch forward.  

Jodi, *triumphant*, grinned.  

“Ohhh, babe. There’s a *good girl*.”  

Naomi *SQUEAKED IN HORROR.*  

Tara *HOWLED WITH LAUGHTER.*  

Jodi *cackled*, then gestured to her shower head.  

“Alright, babe. *Moment of truth*.”  

Naomi, *visibly wanting to die*, *took a breath*.  

Then—  

She *stepped under the spray*.  

A half-second passed.  

Then—  

She *visibly startled*.  

“Oh.”  

Jodi smirked. “Yeah, babe?”  

Naomi *blinked rapidly*. “…It’s, uh… *pleasant*.”  

Tara *grinned wickedly*. “Told you.”  

Jodi, *stretching luxuriously*, exhaled dramatically. “Oh, babe. And this one’s way more *powerful* than the standard *30 PSI* in a normal bath.”  

Naomi *blinked rapidly*. “Wait. You *know that?*”  

Jodi smirked. “Oh, babe. *I’ve done research.*”  

Naomi *let out a strangled noise.*  

Tara *snorted.* “Babe, Jodi is *literally a scientist now*.”  

Jodi *grinned triumphantly.* “Oh, babe. *A very dedicated one.*”  

Naomi *whimpered.* “I *hate* this.”  

Jodi *smirked*. “Babe. You *love it*.”  

And just like that, Naomi took her *first step* into *chaos.*

“Babe?! You good?”  

Then—  

She *clocked* Naomi’s face.  

And the fact that she was *flushed*.  

And the fact that she was *very much* still *standing directly under the spray*.

“Ohhh, babe.”

“Babe,” she purred. “You *sure* you’re good?”  

Naomi, *visibly fighting for her last shred of dignity*, *stammered violently.*  

“I—*this is nothing*—”  

Then—  

She *angled herself just a little more predictably than before.*  

A *beat of silence.*  

Then—  

Jodi *snorted so hard she nearly choked*.  

“Ohhhh, *babe*.”  

Naomi *whimpered*. “*I hate you.*”  

Tara *HOWLED with laughter.* “Babe, you *did this to yourself*.”  

Jodi, still *grinning ear to ear*, crossed her arms.  

“Well,” she mused, “we *don’t even need* to ask how you’re doing, babe.”  

She *gestured vaguely at Naomi’s entire body.*  

“Your reaction is *providing plenty of data*.”  

Naomi *covered her face with her hands.*  

“I AM *GOING TO DIE IN HERE*.”  

Tara, *wiping tears of laughter*, gasped, “Babe, this is your *villain origin story*.”  

Jodi *smirked*. “Oh, babe. You are *never going to be the same again*.”  

Naomi *whimpered loudly*.  

Jodi, still *completely victorious*, grinned.  

“So, babe. You *keeping that YMCA membership now?*”  

Naomi *SCREAMED.*  

And just like that, *Naomi was ruined forever.*

---  
Naomi, *still angled ever-so-slightly-too-perfectly*, was *visibly fighting a war on multiple fronts*.  

Her body was *giving her away*.  

Her brain was *malfunctioning at max capacity*.  

And Jodi?  

Jodi was *watching all of it unfold like an apex predator observing prey that had just tripped into its own damn trap.*  

Tara, *deeply entertained but also slightly concerned*, tilted her head.  

“Ohhhh, babe,” she mused. “You, uh… *doing okay over there?*”  

Naomi, *flushed like she’d been caught at the scene of a crime*, *whimpered violently*.  

“YES. *TOTALLY FINE.*”  

Then—  

She *ever-so-slightly* adjusted her stance again.  

Tara *snorted immediately*.  

Jodi *grinned like the devil himself.*  

“Ohhh, *babe*.”  

Naomi, still *desperately pretending she had control of the situation*, *sputtered violently*.  

“I—I—THIS IS *NORMAL*.”  

Tara *arched an eyebrow.* “Babe, for who?!”  

Jodi *tilted her head dramatically.* “Babe, *let’s review.*”  

She ticked off on her fingers.  

“One—you’re *red as hell*.”  

Naomi *let out a strangled noise*.  

“Two—you’re *not moving away*.”  

Naomi *whimpered*.  

Jodi smirked.  

“And three—you are *one bad decision away* from having to file this moment under ‘deeply personal life regrets.’”  

Naomi, *shaking slightly*, *covered her face*.  

“I *HATE THIS*.”  

Tara, now *cry-laughing*, *wiped a tear away*.  

“Oh my God, babe, you are *feral right now*.”  

Naomi, *completely wrecked*, *whimpered into her hands*. “I AM *NOT*.”  

Jodi *tilted her head thoughtfully.*  

“Well,” she mused, “that’s a bold claim, babe.”  

Then—  

She *stepped back, folded her arms, and stared at Naomi expectantly.*  

“Okay, then. *Move away from the spray.*”  

Naomi *froze instantly.*  

Tara *immediately perked up.* “OH *SHIT*.”  

Naomi *whimpered louder.* “I—I—”  

Jodi smirked.  

“*Go on, babe.*”  

Naomi *visibly twitched*.  

Tara *snorted*. “Babe, you are *stuck in place*.”  

Naomi, *panicking*, *whimpered again*.  

“I AM *NOT*.”  

Jodi *grinned wider.* “Prove it.”  

Naomi *did not move*.  

Tara *screamed with laughter.* “OH MY GOD, BABE.”  

Jodi, *triumphant*, tilted her head.  

“Babe,” she said smoothly, “you are about to *have a deeply complicated relationship* with showerheads for the *rest of your life*.”  

Naomi *let out a full-body whimper.*  

Jodi, *grinning ear to ear*, leaned in slightly.  

“So, babe. You *keeping that YMCA membership now?*”  

Naomi *SCREAMED*.  

And just like that, *Naomi was officially ruined beyond repair.*

---  
Naomi had officially *left the building.*  

Her mind? *Gone.*  

Her ability to pretend she had control of the situation? *Obliterated.*  

And now?  

Now she was *mewling*.  

*Moaning.*  

Making *noises* that had **no place** in a public shower.  

Jodi and Tara *exchanged a look*.  

Then—  

Jodi, *grinning like a fox in a henhouse*, subtly lifted a hand and gave Tara a *small, precise signal.*  

Hold.  

Let Funko Girl *be* for a minute.  

Tara, watching Naomi’s *full-body spiral into self-discovery*, raised an eyebrow.  

“…Huh.”  

Jodi smirked. “Oh, babe.”  

Tara, arms crossed, *tilted her head slightly*, studying Naomi like she was witnessing the *start of a scientific breakthrough*.  

“This is…” she mused, “…*oddly wonderful*.”  

Naomi, *who had fully lost the plot*, *whimpered violently*.  

Jodi, still *fully entertained*, casually *shifted under her own spray*, stretching luxuriously.  

“Oh, babe,” she purred, “this could be *pushed even further*.”  

Naomi *let out an actual, high-pitched whine.*  

Jodi *grinned in absolute triumph*.  

Tara, *completely entranced by the unraveling of Funko Girl*, leaned in slightly.  

“So, babe,” she murmured, “you *gonna let it happen*?”  

Naomi, *visibly vibrating*, *whimpered again*.  

Jodi, *relentless*, smirked.  

“Babe,” she said smoothly, “if you *really* want us to stop, just…”  

She gestured vaguely.  

“…*walk away from the spray*.”  

Naomi, *absolutely incapable of doing so*, *stayed rooted to the spot.*  

Jodi *grinned wider*.  

“Ohhh, babe.”  

Tara, *laughing softly*, shook her head.  

“She *really* can’t move.”  

Jodi, stretching *one last time*, sighed dramatically.  

“Babe,” she purred, “you’re gonna have to *start tipping your water bill extra*.”  

Naomi *SQUEAKED IN PURE MORTIFICATION.*  

Tara *HOWLED WITH LAUGHTER.*  

Jodi, *smirking like the devil*, crossed her arms.  

“So, babe. How do you *rate* this shower experience?”  

Naomi *SCREAMED.*  

And just like that, *Funko Girl was officially broken.*    
---  
---  
Naomi had just *screamed*.  

The question was: **why?**  

Jodi, *ruthless*, narrowed her eyes slightly.  

“Hmm,” she mused, tilting her head *just so*. “Now, babe… *was that mortification?*”  

Naomi, *already in a full-body meltdown*, *visibly twitched*.  

Jodi smirked.  

“Or…”  

She let the word *drag out*, watching as Naomi’s expression *crumpled* in real-time.  

“…was that *gratification?*”  

Tara *snorted immediately*. “BABE.”  

Naomi, *already broken*, gave Jodi a *miserable, desperate look*—like someone who had just *realized too late* that they were **not getting out of this alive.**  

And then—  

Right.  

Back.  

At.  

It.  

Jodi *howled with laughter.*  

Tara *clapped a hand over her mouth, eyes wide*. “OH MY GOD.”  

Naomi, *still barely functioning*, sputtered, “THIS ISN’T—*THIS ISN’T FAIR*—”  

Jodi, still *fully in command*, smirked.  

“Aww, babe,” she cooed, “can’t think straight?”  

Naomi *whimpered violently*. “NO, I CANNOT *THINK AT ALL*.”  

Tara, now *deeply fascinated*, crossed her arms and *studied Naomi like a scientist observing a new species in its natural habitat.*  

“You know,” she murmured, “there’s *something to this madness*.”  

Naomi, who had officially *lost all shame and self-respect*, *whimpered again*.  

Jodi, *basking in victory*, grinned.  

“Oh, babe.”  

Naomi *shuddered.*  

Jodi *tilted her head, grinning wider*.  

“*I think you like losing control.*”  

Naomi *SQUEAKED IN PURE DEVASTATION.*  

Tara *HOWLED WITH LAUGHTER.*  

Jodi, *smirking like an absolute menace*, crossed her arms.  

“So, babe.”  

Naomi, *breathing like she’d just run a marathon*, *whimpered again*.  

Jodi grinned.  

“How do you *rate* this shower experience?”  

Naomi **absolutely collapsed into a puddle of existential ruin.**  

And just like that, *Funko Girl was officially done for.*    
---  
---  
Naomi, still *breathing like she had just fought God and lost*, *whimpered violently*.  

Her face? **Flushed beyond reason.**  

Her body? **Wrecked.**  

Her **grip on reality?** Absolutely **obliterated**.  

And then—  

She *spoke.*  

“This was… *different* than the ten times I did it the other day.”  

Jodi, *perking up instantly*, smirked. “Ohhh, *babe.*”  

Tara *HOWLED WITH LAUGHTER.* “BABE, YOU JUST SAID THAT *OUT LOUD*.”  

Naomi, still *half-dazed*, *sputtered*, “I—THIS WAS *SHARPER*, *MORE POWERFUL*, *RELENTLESS*—”  

She exhaled *shakily*, blinking rapidly.  

“…Like dealing with a *hurricane*.”  

A **beat of silence.**  

Then—  

Jodi *GRINNED LIKE A MONSTER.*  

“Ohhh, babe,” she purred.  

She crossed her arms and *tilted her head just so*.  

“Well,” she mused, “were the *winds* exceeding **74 miles per hour**?”  

Naomi *visibly twitched*. “…WHAT?”  

Jodi smirked.  

“Otherwise, babe, it was just a *tropical storm*.”  

Tara *screamed laughing.*  

Jodi, *not letting up for even a second*, shrugged.  

“I mean, *tropical storms* are *pleasant*,” she continued, “but no one’s gonna get *worked up* about those.”  

Naomi **FULL-BODY WHIMPERED.**  

Tara, *doubled over*, *wheezed*. “OH MY GOD, BABE.”  

Jodi, *reveling in the absolute destruction she had wrought*, smirked wider.  

“So, babe,” she purred, “which was it?”  

Naomi, *trembling slightly*, *whispered*, “I *refuse* to answer.”  

Jodi *grinned*. “Oh, babe. **That means it was a Category 5.**”  

Naomi **ABSOLUTELY COLLAPSED INTO A PILE OF EXISTENTIAL RUIN.**  

Tara, *laughing so hard she had to hold onto the wall*, wheezed, “BABE, YOU JUST LIVED THROUGH **HURRICANE FUNKO GIRL.**”  

Naomi **LET OUT AN ACTUAL, DEVASTATED SQUEAK.**  

And just like that, **Hurricane Naomi officially made landfall.**    
---  
---  
Naomi, still *reeling* from the absolute **Category 5 disaster** that had just hit her, *whimpered softly*.  

Jodi, *grinning like an apex predator*, *was not done yet.*  

“Ohhh, babe,” she *purred*, “I gotta know—”  

She *tilted her head dramatically.*  

“What *exactly* were you **thinking**?”  

Naomi, *who had absolutely lost all ability to function*, *let out a strangled noise.*  

Tara, still *laughing*, *wiped a tear from her eye* and *answered for her*.  

“Oh, babe,” she smirked. “The *same thing* I’m thinking right now.”  

Jodi *arched an eyebrow, intrigued.* “Oh?”  

Tara *grinned*. “That this is a **lot of fun** and also *completely absurd*.”  

Jodi *cackled.* “Ohhh, babe, that’s a *given*.”  

Then—  

She *paused*.  

Something was different.  

Naomi was *wrecked beyond words*, sure—  

But **Tara?**  

**Tara was squirming.**  

Jodi’s eyes *narrowed slightly*, her smirk *widening*.  

“…Babe.”  

Tara, *still looking smug but now subtly shifting*, *arched an eyebrow*. “Hmm?”  

Jodi’s smirk *grew even more dangerous.*  

“Oh, babe,” she purred.  

She gestured *vaguely at Tara’s stance*.  

“…You *good* over there?”  

Tara, still *playing it cool*, shrugged. “Why *wouldn’t* I be?”  

Naomi, *still in the middle of her breakdown, but suddenly intrigued*, *blinked rapidly*.  

Jodi, *seeing an opening*, smirked.  

“Oh, babe,” she murmured. “You’re *fidgeting*.”  

Tara, *still refusing to give ground*, tilted her head. “*Am I?*”  

Jodi *snorted immediately*. “Ohhh, **babe.**”  

Then—  

She *studied* her.  

**And then she figured it out.**  

“…It’s different for you, huh?”  

Tara *stiffened slightly.* “What?”  

Jodi *grinned triumphantly*.  

“Ohhh, babe,” she purred, “the **water cascades differently for you, doesn’t it?**”  

Naomi, *suddenly forgetting her own suffering*, *perked up instantly.*  

Jodi *narrowed her eyes slightly, amused.*  

“…Because of the **thickness**.”  

Naomi *gasped dramatically.* “OH MY GOD.”  

Tara, still *holding her ground, but now visibly fighting a losing battle*, smirked slightly.  

“Maybe.”  

Jodi *HOWLED WITH LAUGHTER.*  

Naomi, *pointing wildly*, *shrieked*, “SHE’S **STRUGGLING.**”  

Jodi, *grinning like a menace*, tilted her head.  

“Ohhh, babe,” she murmured, “**are you getting left behind?**”  

Tara *paused for exactly half a second.*  

Then—  

She smirked.  

And said, smoothly—  

**“Bingo.”**  

Jodi and Naomi *screamed laughing.*  

And just like that, **Tara entered the storm.**    
---  
---  
Naomi, *still absolutely wrecked but now determined*, exhaled sharply.  

“Okay,” she said, *flushed to hell and back*, “I’m *turning into a prune*—”  

Tara, *who was now deep in her own personal disaster zone*, smirked.  

“Oh, babe, we’re all gonna need a full-body moisturizer after this.”  

Naomi *snorted but quickly sobered up*. “*But*,” she added, “since I *stuck through my hurricane without evacuating*…”  

She narrowed her eyes at Tara.  

“…the *least* I can do is **stick through yours.**”  

A beat of silence.  

Then—  

Tara *winked at Jodi*.  

“Ohhh, babe,” she purred, “then **Jodi better play catch-up,** because I am **fully unhinged** right about now.”  

She tilted her head *thoughtfully*, letting the hot water *pour exactly where it needed to* before adding, offhandedly—  

“Give or take **fifteen seconds.**”  

Naomi *SCREAMED WITH LAUGHTER.*  

Jodi, *mouth open in delighted shock*, *grinned like a demon.*  

“Ohhh, **babe.**”  

She cracked her knuckles.  

“Well, shit.”  

Then—  

She casually turned *directly* into the spray.  

Tara *smirked through her unraveling*.  

Naomi *let out an actual, horrified squeak*.  

Jodi *grinned wider*.  

“Alright, Funko Girl. We’re **all in the hurricane now.**”  

And just like that, **Tara’s disaster was officially joined by another Category 5.**    
---  
---  
Naomi, who had *barely* put herself back together after her **Category 5 personal crisis**, suddenly realized—  

**She was getting a sequel.**  

From **Jodi.**  

Right in front of her.  

In *real-time.*  

Naomi, *already at the limits of human comprehension*, **SQUEAKED IN HORROR.**  

“YOU’RE **DOING IT AGAIN**?!”  

Jodi, *grinning like an absolute menace*, *cracked her neck* like she was about to enter **The Final Boss Fight of Unhinged Decisions.**  

“Ohhh, babe,” she purred, “**you inspired me.**”  

Naomi, *visibly vibrating*, *clutched her head like she had just received world-ending news.*  

“WHAT DO I **DO WITH THIS INFORMATION?!**”  

Tara, **who was also currently experiencing an absolute system overload**, suddenly made a *strangled gasping sound*.  

Jodi *whipped her head toward her*.  

“Oh, babe—are **you good?**”  

Tara, *eyes blown wide, breathing like a malfunctioning engine*, **wobbled**.  

“I—”  

She *tilted slightly to the left.*  

“I THINK—”  

She *wobbled again.*  

“**I’M NOT GOOD.**”  

Naomi **SCREAMED.**  

Jodi, **who still had enough presence of mind to be a menace**, *grinned in victory.*  

“Ohhh, babe. **You’re FOLDING.**”  

Tara, **fully red in the face, visibly losing higher brain function**, *wheezed.*  

“I—”  

She *gasped dramatically.*  

“I **DON’T KNOW WHERE I AM.**”  

Naomi **LET OUT A FULL-BODY SQUEAL.**  

Jodi, *cackling, but also knowing better than to be cruel at a moment like this,* **bent her knees immediately**.  

Because **she knew.**  

She **knew** what happened when you locked your legs up in a moment like this.  

And she was a **menace**, sure—  

But she wasn’t **a monster.**  

“Babe,” Jodi said, *bracing Tara gently*, “don’t you **DARE lock your knees right now.**”  

Tara, **hyperventilating, gripping Jodi like she was the last lifeboat on the Titanic**, *wheezed violently.*  

“I THINK I **FORGOT HOW TO STAND.**”  

Naomi, *still in absolute disbelief, still unsure how she was even witnessing any of this*, *whimpered*.  

“I CAN’T **PROCESS THIS**.”  

Jodi, *grinning like a devil but also stabilizing Tara like a responsible menace*, tilted her head.  

“Ohhh, babe. **Are you officially in the hurricane?**”  

Tara, **still gripping onto Jodi for dear life**, *let out a strangled gasp.*  

“I **THINK I’M DROWNING.**”  

Naomi, *clutching her chest, eyes wide with sheer existential terror*, **COLLAPSED AGAINST THE WALL.**  

Jodi, **positively glowing with victory**, grinned ear to ear.  

“Ohhh, babe. **Welcome to the storm.**”  

Tara **let out a single, helpless, breathless sound.**  

Then—  

**She folded.**  

**COMPLETELY.**  

Naomi **SQUEALED IN PURE HORROR.**  

Jodi **HOWLED WITH LAUGHTER.**  

And just like that—  

The shower **collapsed into total, inescapable chaos.**    
---  
---  
The girls **finally** left the shower.  

*Barely.*  

Naomi, **still reeling from witnessing the absolute collapse of reality**, **would not shut up.**  

Not even a little.  

Not even for a **second.**  

“Oh my **GOD**,” she gasped, hair still dripping as she grabbed a towel. “I—I don’t even—I can’t—**WHAT THE HELL JUST HAPPENED?**”  

Jodi, **glowing like she had just returned from a month-long luxury spa retreat**, grinned as she **wrung out her hair** with a towel.  

“Oh, babe,” she purred, “you just witnessed **something magical.**”  

Tara, **who was still recovering from her full-body shutdown, but was also grinning**, smirked.  

“Babe, you just watched two hurricanes make landfall **at the same time.**”  

Naomi **sputtered violently.**  

“I CAN’T—I JUST—I MEAN—YOU GUYS—IN FRONT OF ME—RIGHT THERE—”  

Jodi *snorted immediately.* “Oh, babe, you were **a firsthand witness.**”  

Naomi, **still gasping like she was trying to process the entirety of human history**, **waved her hands dramatically.**  

“I HAVE **SO MUCH MENTAL IMAGERY NOW.**”  

Tara, **smirking**, grabbed her towel and started drying off.  

“Oh, babe,” she teased, “you got **weeks** of material now.”  

Naomi **whimpered violently.**  

Jodi, still **glowing, fully basking in her victory**, **tied her towel around her waist like an absolute queen.**  

“Well, babe,” she purred, “you did say you wanted to **learn some things.**”  

Naomi, **still struggling to form words**, **let out a strangled noise.**  

“**I DIDN’T KNOW THIS IS WHAT I WAS SIGNING UP FOR.**”  

Tara, **laughing**, pulled her shirt over her head. “Oh, babe, **you knew the risk.**”  

Naomi, **grabbing her clothes and frantically dressing**, **groaned dramatically.**  

“I JUST—UGH—”  

Then—  

She **paused.**  

Her expression **froze.**  

Jodi, *mid-shirt pull*, arched an eyebrow. “Hmm?”  

Naomi, **visibly experiencing an existential crisis all over again**, **clutched her jeans like they had personally betrayed her.**  

“…I wish I had my panties.”  

**A long beat of silence.**  

Then—  

Tara, **immediately smirking**, raised an eyebrow.  

“Oh, babe,” she purred.  

Naomi, **already realizing her mistake**, **whimpered.**  

Tara **grinned wider.**  

“You **mean the ones in Kevin’s glove compartment?**”  

Naomi **let out an actual scream.**  

Jodi **burst into hysterical laughter.**  

“Ohhh, babe,” she wheezed, pulling on her pants. “That was a **FAIR TRADE.**”  

Naomi, **visibly suffering**, **collapsed onto the locker bench.**  

“I **NEED TO REEVALUATE MY LIFE.**”  

Jodi, *still amused, but always the responsible one*, *paused for just a second*.  

She *tilted her head slightly*, studying Naomi’s expression.  

“…You *are* having fun, right, babe?”  

Naomi, **still whimpering, still internally combusting, still completely unhinged**, *blinked rapidly*.  

Then—  

“…We’re doing this **again next week, right?**”  

Jodi **HOWLED WITH LAUGHTER.**  

Tara **DOUBLED OVER, WHEEZING.**  

And just like that, **Naomi was officially part of the chaos.**    
---  
---  
Naomi, **finally dressed but still visibly processing the absolute catastrophe that had just unfolded**, let out a deep breath.  

Then—  

“…This wasn’t *weird* for anyone, right?”  

Jodi, **mid-hair fluff, grinning ear to ear**, **snorted immediately.**  

“Oh, babe,” she teased, “define *weird*.”  

Naomi **whimpered.** “I mean—I just—I don’t want this to be **a thing** that everyone regrets.”  

Tara, **smirking as she adjusted her top**, *shrugged*. “Babe, I regret a *lot* of things.”  

She glanced at Jodi.  

“This? **Not one of them.**”  

Jodi, **still fully basking in victory**, *nodded*.  

“Oh, babe, this is just a **Tuesday.**”  

Naomi **let out a full-body exhale.**  

“…Okay. Good.”  

Then—  

She **perked up suddenly.**  

“Okay, before we leave, I *have* to make sure I get a membership.”  

Jodi **arched an eyebrow immediately.**  

“Ohhh, babe.”  

Naomi, **fully determined now**, **crossed her arms**.  

“I’m **addicted.**”  

Tara **HOWLED WITH LAUGHTER.**  

Jodi, **grinning like a menace**, *tilted her head*.  

“Babe, are you *addicted to the sauna?*”  

Tara, **cackling**, added, “Or are you *addicted to the disaster?*”  

Naomi **whimpered violently.** “**DO NOT ASK ME THAT QUESTION.**”  

Jodi, **delighted beyond words**, *leaned in slightly.*  

“Oh, babe. We **already know the answer.**”  

Naomi **LET OUT AN ACTUAL SQUEAK.**  

Tara, **cry-laughing, wiping a tear away**, wheezed, “BABE, YOU ARE TOO **FAR GONE.**”  

Then—  

Jodi, *suddenly remembering another layer of Naomi’s situation*, smirked.  

“Oh, hey, babe.”  

Naomi, *still suffering*, *blinked rapidly*. “WHAT.”  

Jodi **grinned wider.**  

“So,” she purred, “how’d it feel being in such *tight quarters* with so many **naked college men** **ogling you?**”  

Naomi, **fully startled by the change in topic**, *snapped her head up so fast she nearly sprained something.*  

“**THEY WERE NOT OGGLING ME.**”  

Jodi, *delighted*, smirked. “Oh?”  

Naomi **shook her head violently.**  

“No *way*,” she insisted. “Because **I** was checking *them* out, and they seemed **self-absorbed.**”  

Tara **snorted immediately.** “Babe, that’s a **bold** assumption.”  

Naomi, *still firm in her stance*, crossed her arms.  

“No, seriously,” she insisted. “They were **so in their own world**. **No way** they were looking at me.”  

Then—  

A *beat of silence.*  

Naomi’s expression **shifted slightly.**  

She was **thinking.**  

Calculating.  

Running **numbers.**  

Jodi, **seeing the gears turn, grinned.** “Ohhh, babe.”  

Tara **perked up immediately.** “Babe, what are you plotting?”  

Naomi, **suddenly looking very, very thoughtful**, tapped her chin.  

“Well,” she mused, “if I **show up exactly one week from now…**”  

Jodi **tilted her head dramatically.** “Oh?”  

Naomi **nodded slowly.**  

“…Odds are good that *all of them* will be back **after practice.**”  

**A long beat of silence.**  

Then—  

Jodi and Tara **SCREAMED LAUGHING.**  

Jodi, **howling, clutching her stomach**, gasped, “**OH MY GOD, SHE’S RUNNING THE STATS.**”  

Tara, **crying from laughter**, wheezed, “BABE, YOU ARE **EVIL.**”  

Naomi, **still thinking like she was analyzing game theory**, *shrugged slightly.*  

“I *just think* it would be **scientifically interesting.**”  

Jodi, **doubling over laughing**, *shrieked*, “BABE, YOU WANT A **CONTROL GROUP.**”  

Tara, *barely breathing from laughing too hard*, *collapsed onto the locker bench.* “**OH MY GOD.**”  

Naomi, **still pretending she was doing this for research purposes**, shrugged again.  

“I *just* think it would be **useful information.**”  

Jodi, **absolutely sobbing from laughter**, wheezed, “OH, BABE, YOU ARE **OFFICIALLY ONE OF US.**”  

And just like that—  

Naomi **locked in her next field experiment.**    
---  
---  
Jodi, **casually stretching like she’d just completed a very productive gym session instead of total chaos**, pulled out her phone.  

“Alright, babe,” she murmured, thumbing through her ride-share apps. “I’ll order an Uber while Funko Girl locks in her **scientific obsession.**”  

Tara, **who had been staring off into space for a solid thirty seconds**, **blinked hard** and let out a breath.  

Then—  

“…I cannot believe **what we just did.**”  

Jodi **snorted immediately.** “Babe, *be specific.*”  

Tara *gestured vaguely at the entire locker room.*  

“This.”  

She exhaled again, shaking her head in disbelief.  

“This was the **wildest thing** I have ever done with you.”  

Then—  

A slow, almost dreamy smile spread across her face.  

“And I *loved* almost all of it.”  

Jodi, **grinning**, *arched an eyebrow.*  

“Almost?”  

Tara, **still lost in thought**, hummed slightly.  

“…I just wish Naomi had looked at me more.”  

Jodi, **immediately intrigued**, tilted her head.  

“Ohhh, babe.”  

She *paused the Uber request.*  

“Just **how jealous are you?**”  

Tara, **visibly weighing her words**, held up her fingers.  

“Just a **pinch.**”  

Jodi, **smirking**, *tilted her head further.* “Oh?”  

Tara **exhaled deeply**, a slow, thoughtful expression crossing her face.  

“I really… *really* think I have a **beautiful bush.**”  

Jodi **bit her lip to keep from laughing.**  

“Oh, babe,” she purred, “you **do.**”  

Tara, **grinning slightly, but also visibly processing something deeper**, nodded.  

“I was **extremely gratified** to get some great admiration for it tonight.”  

She sighed dramatically, stretching her arms behind her head.  

“But I was also kinda hoping…”  

She hesitated.  

Then—  

“…That Naomi would be **more like me.**”  

Jodi, **who was taking none of this too seriously but also respected a rare Tara moment of vulnerability,** considered that.  

Then—  

She shrugged.  

“Oh, babe,” she said smoothly, “that is probably gonna have to **slide.**”  

Tara, **smirking, but also nodding slightly in understanding**, sighed.  

“Yeah.”  

Jodi, *glancing at the locker room door where Naomi had disappeared*, hummed thoughtfully.  

“She gets to make those kinds of **choices.**”  

Tara, **rolling her shoulders slightly, stretching again**, nodded.  

“Yeah.”  

Then—  

She exhaled sharply, shaking her head with a small, *wry* smile.  

“It’s just **amazing** how much she’s changed in such a short period of time.”  

Jodi **grinned slightly, but there was something genuinely thoughtful behind it.**  

“It is.”  

Tara, *raising an eyebrow*, glanced at her.  

“…You *really* think it’s good having her in our social circle, huh?”  

Jodi **nodded immediately.**  

“Oh, babe. **Absolutely.**”  

Tara, **tilting her head**, squinted slightly.  

“…You don’t think it’s *weird*, though?”  

Jodi, **without even hesitating,** shook her head.  

“Nope.”  

Tara, **visibly thinking about it**, let that sit for a moment.  

Then—  

“…Yeah.”  

She smiled slightly.  

“Me neither.”  

Jodi, **grinning again,** nudged her playfully.  

“Oh, babe. Just **wait** until she starts making *us* uncomfortable.”  

Tara **SNORTED IMMEDIATELY.**  

And just like that—  

**Naomi officially belonged to the chaos.**    
---  
---  
The next morning, the **three women arrived at work in an orderly, professional fashion.**  

They walked in.  

They looked **completely normal.**  

They acted **completely normal.**  

Everything was **normal.**    
…    
Except for the fact that it **absolutely was not.**  

## **THE BREAK ROOM INCIDENT.**  

Jodi, Tara, and Naomi had **barely made it through the first hour of work** before they all ended up in the break room at the same time.  

And that’s when **things got weird.**  

It started *small*.  

A little *too much eye contact.*  

A slightly *too-long pause* before answering simple questions.  

A *tense, unspoken awareness* that **everything was different now** but also **they were NOT allowed to acknowledge it.**  

Mike, **who had been minding his own business stirring sugar into his coffee, suddenly looked up at them and squinted.**  

“…What the hell is going on with you three?”  

Jodi, **too quickly**, said, “Nothing.”  

Naomi, **who absolutely did not know how to act natural**, blurted, “COFFEE.”  

Tara, **who was trying so hard but failing even harder**, **immediately choked on her sip of tea.**  

Mike **narrowed his eyes.**  

“Y’all are **acting weird.**”  

Jodi, *not missing a beat*, **tilted her head, grinning smoothly.**  

“Oh, babe,” she purred. “We are **always** weird.”  

Naomi, **who was still recovering from her outburst,** tried to play it cool and **immediately failed.**  

“Well—*I mean*—we **did have a sauna night!**”  

Tara **FROZE.**  

Jodi **SQUEEZED HER EYES SHUT.**  

Mike **blinked slowly.**  

“…What?”  

Naomi, **now realizing her mistake, PANICKED.**  

“I MEAN—**A NORMAL ONE.**”  

Jodi, **visibly clenching her jaw, took a long, slow sip of coffee.**  

Tara, **staring at the floor like she wanted to disappear**, **choked again.**  

Mike, **eyebrows raised, glanced between them.**  

“…Right.”  

Then—  

He pointed his coffee spoon at Jodi.  

“You. Explain.”  

Jodi, **completely unfazed**, grinned.  

“Oh, babe,” she said smoothly, “we had a **very relaxing** time.”  

Tara **whimpered softly.**  

Mike **squinted.**  

“…Okay, that **did not** sound innocent.”  

Naomi, **still in full-blown nervous breakdown mode**, **started laughing.**  

Which was **the worst thing she could have done.**  

Mike’s eyes **narrowed further.**  

“…Alright. **What did you three DO?**”  

Jodi, **casually stirring her coffee**, smiled serenely.  

“Mike,” she said lightly, “some things **stay in the steam room.**”  

Tara, **visibly trying to keep it together, bit her lip.**  

Naomi, **who could not hold it together to save her life, let out an actual squeak.**  

Mike, **slowly lowering his mug, exhaled.**  

“**I hate all of you.**”  

And just like that, **the break room descended into absolute, unbearable tension.**    
---  
---  
Mike **was skeptical.**  

And honestly? **That was fair.**  

Because the **three women in front of him** were **radiating** something.  

Something **off**.  

Something that **screamed**:  

**“We have committed crimes of pleasure, and you will never know the full details.”**  

So, yeah.  

Mike, who **knew these women well enough to know bullshit when he heard it**, **leaned against the counter and crossed his arms.**  

“Alright,” he said slowly, looking directly at Tara.  

“Give me the **highlights and lowlights.**”  

Jodi, **immediately smirking**, took a casual sip of coffee.  

Naomi, **still visibly traumatized, whimpered softly.**  

Tara, **who had just finished swallowing a mouthful of tea**, exhaled sharply.  

Then—  

“Well,” she said smoothly, “highlight? **We lost control.**”  

Mike **narrowed his eyes.** “That sounds fake.”  

Naomi **whimpered again.**  

Jodi **snorted.** “Oh, babe. **It was very real.**”  

Mike **tilted his head.** “Alright. Lowlight?”  

Tara **sighed dramatically.**  

“…We lost control.”  

Mike **squinted.**  

Naomi, **staring into the middle distance like she had seen the face of God and lived to tell the tale, muttered,** “*So much control lost…*”  

Mike, **now deeply suspicious, crossed his arms tighter.**  

“…Okay, but what does that **mean?**”  

Tara **grinned, giving Jodi a sidelong glance.**  

“It means…”  

She took a long, slow sip of her tea.  

“…That **you** will never understand.”  

Mike **immediately groaned.**  

“Oh, for **fuck’s sake.**”  

Jodi, **grinning, stretched lazily.**  

“Ohhh, babe. **You’re thinking too hard.**”  

Mike **pointed at her.**  

“No, I’m **thinking just hard enough.**”  

Naomi, **who had absolutely lost the ability to function, collapsed onto the counter.**  

Mike **watched this.**  

Watched **all of it.**  

Then—  

He exhaled sharply, rubbing a hand down his face.  

“…Judging by your reactions,” he muttered, “maybe you **did** have fun losing control.”  

Jodi **smirked into her coffee.**  

Tara **winked at him.**  

Naomi **whimpered again.**  

And just like that, **Mike absolutely hated all three of them.**    
---  
---  
Jodi, **still enjoying herself far too much**, leaned casually against the counter and took another slow sip of coffee.  

Then—  

She sighed dramatically.  

“You know,” she mused, “high-pressure water flow can really **do wonders** for **morale.**”  

Mike, **already regretting asking anything**, **squinted immediately.**  

“…Whose morale?”  

Before Jodi could answer—  

Naomi, **who absolutely should not have spoken**, blurted:  

“**Mine went up twice.**”  

A **beat of silence.**  

Then—  

She blinked.  

Realized **what she just said.**  

Then—  

“…Once **massively.**”  

Jodi **HOWLED WITH LAUGHTER.**  

Tara **SNORTED HARD.**  

Mike, **who was *one second* away from walking into traffic**, **slowly turned to look at Naomi.**  

Naomi, **now realizing she had just outed herself in the worst way possible, immediately turned red.**  

Mike **blinked twice.**  

Then—  

“…**Do you know what morale means?**”  

Naomi, **visibly short-circuiting**, made a small, strangled noise.  

Jodi, **grinning like a cat who had just knocked a glass off the counter**, **tilted her head.**  

“Ohhh, babe.”  

She gestured toward Tara.  

“You wanna **explain it to her?**”  

Tara, **who was barely holding it together, wiped a tear from her eye and turned to Naomi.**  

“Babe.”  

Naomi, **visibly twitching.** “Y-yeah?”  

Tara **grinned wickedly.**  

“**You just told Mike you doubled your workplace enthusiasm via external plumbing.**”  

Naomi **SQUEAKED LOUDLY.**  

Mike, **mouth open in absolute horror, dropped his coffee stirrer.**  

Jodi **WHEEZED.**  

Tara **started crying from laughter.**  

Naomi, **clutching her face in existential agony, whimpered,** “**I AM GOING TO QUIT THIS JOB.**”  

Mike, **shell-shocked, let out a slow exhale.**  

Then—  

He shook his head.  

“You know what?”  

He grabbed his coffee mug and **started walking away.**  

“I **don’t want to know anything else.**”  

Jodi, **still laughing, called after him.**  

“Ohhh, babe. **We haven’t even started!**”  

Mike **immediately picked up his pace.**  

And just like that, **the break room was officially a crime scene.**    
---  
---  
Jodi had been **mentally preparing for this meeting** all morning.  

She had an entire **list of possible reasons** why she might be getting pulled aside.  

1. **Tardiness.** (Okay, fair. It had been… a *week*.)    
2. **Bawdy behavior.** (*Also* fair. But in her defense, she had *tried* to be subtle.)    
3. **Joint masturbation session in the shower room.** (…Yeah, okay, **that one** might’ve been *a little* inappropriate.)  

So when her **supervisor closed the door**, sat down, and folded their hands on the desk **seriously**—  

Jodi was already **mentally drafting a fake excuse.**  

Then—  

“…What the hell got into Naomi this week?”  

**A beat of silence.**  

Jodi **blinked.**  

“…I’m sorry. **What?**”  

The supervisor, looking **genuinely bewildered**, shook their head.  

“Jodi, I’m **serious.**”  

They leaned forward slightly, voice **lowering.**  

“**Every single project** she’s turned in this week has been **early.**”  

Jodi, **now completely thrown off-course, blinked again.**  

“…Okay?”  

The supervisor, **waving their hands slightly**, kept going.  

“Not just *early*, either. Her **quality control** on all of her spreadsheets is **unparalleled.**”  

Jodi, **now fighting the urge to laugh hysterically**, **kept a completely neutral face.**  

“…I see.”  

The supervisor, **still baffled**, leaned back.  

“Jodi.”  

They exhaled sharply.  

“**What the hell happened?**”  

Jodi, **who was currently flashing through every single moment of absolute insanity that had led to this moment,** had to bite her tongue.  

Because **there was no professional way** to say:  

*"Well, boss, funny thing—Naomi accidentally unlocked the mysteries of high-pressure water flow and now she's basically on performance-enhancing drugs via personal discovery."*  

Instead—  

Jodi, **keeping her composure with the **grace of a trained assassin**, smiled politely.  

“Well.”  

She folded her hands neatly in her lap.  

“I think she’s just… **found some new motivation.**”  

The supervisor, **squinting slightly**, sat back.  

“…Right.”  

Jodi **nodded solemnly.**  

“**A sudden surge of morale, if you will.**”  

**A pause.**  

Then—  

The supervisor narrowed their eyes.  

“…You’re messing with me, aren’t you?”  

Jodi, **grinning just slightly**, shrugged.  

“Oh, babe.”  

She stood up smoothly.  

“I would **never** do such a thing.”    
---  
---  
Jodi had **mentally prepared for disaster.**  

She was **fully ready** to be called out for:    
✅ **Tardiness.** (*Okay, fair.*)    
✅ **Bawdy behavior.** (*Again, fair, but also deeply hypocritical coming from a boss who once got drunk at the company retreat and overshared about their divorce.*)    
✅ **Massively inappropriate activities in the shower room.** (*Oops?*)  

So when her **supervisor folded their hands, leaned in, and deadass asked—**  

*"What the hell got into Naomi this week?"*  

Jodi’s brain **blue-screened.**  

## **ERROR. DOES NOT COMPUTE.**  

“…I’m sorry. **What?**”  

The supervisor, looking **genuinely alarmed**, exhaled sharply.  

"Jodi, I'm **serious.** Naomi has been on some kind of **warpath.**"  

They pulled up a **printed spreadsheet.**  

(Yes, a **printed** spreadsheet. For emphasis.)  

"Her **efficiency rate** has doubled."  

Jodi, **internally vibrating**, nodded slowly. "…Wow."  

"Her **error percentage** has dropped to *zero*."  

Jodi, **nodding again, carefully**: "…Incredible."  

The supervisor **slammed the spreadsheet onto the desk.**  

"She **optimized our entire project workflow.**"  

Jodi: "…Did she?"  

"**DID SHE?**" The supervisor waved their hands. "**She just saved the company roughly $32,000 in cost avoidance. IN A WEEK.**"  

Jodi, **now realizing Naomi had become an **accidental office prodigy***, slowly sat back.  

“…Well, damn.”  

The supervisor **leaned forward, lowering their voice.**  

"Jodi."  

Jodi, **bracing herself, trying not to laugh**: "Yes?"  

The supervisor:  

"**What the hell happened?**"  

Jodi:  

"……"  

Because, like.  

She **could not** say:  

*"Well, boss, turns out Naomi had a religious experience via high-pressure water and it unlocked the latent potential of her frontal lobe."*  

So instead—  

Jodi smiled sweetly.  

“Well.”  

She folded her hands neatly in her lap.  

“I think she’s just… **found some new motivation.**”  

The supervisor **squinted hard.**  

“Jodi.”  

Jodi: **"Yes?"**  

The supervisor, **narrowing their eyes**:  

“…Is this about **morale?**”  

Jodi, **internally screaming**, **kept her face neutral.**  

“Ohhh, babe. **It’s always about morale.**”  

The supervisor:  

**"WHAT DOES THAT MEAN."**  

Jodi:  

“Have a great weekend.”  

Then—  

**She left before she could be investigated for workplace crimes.**    
---    
## **Friday Night Reckoning: Naomi, The Corporate Prodigy**  

Jodi **power-walked** into the bar and spotted Tara and Naomi at a booth.  

Naomi, **who had just finished signing her **brand-new** gym membership**, was **glowing.**  

Tara, **sipping a beer,** raised an eyebrow. “Well?”  

Jodi **slammed her hands onto the table.**  

“**EXPLAIN YOURSELF.**”  

Naomi, **mid-sip, blinked.** “Huh?”  

Jodi, **wild-eyed, pointing dramatically**:  

“YOU JUST **SPEEDRAN YOUR WAY INTO MANAGEMENT MATERIAL.**”  

Tara, **choking on her drink.** “Babe, **WHAT.**”  

Naomi, **confused but also looking **really, really proud**, beamed.  

“Oh!”  

She **took another sip of beer.**  

“Yeah, apparently I’m *really good* at spreadsheets.”  

Jodi **threw up her hands.**  

“**YOU REINVENTED THE OFFICE.**”  

Naomi, **smiling innocently**: “I just… was *feeling good* this week.”  

Tara, **now squinting**, leaned forward. “Babe. Define *good*.”  

Naomi, **cheerfully sipping her beer**:  

"Like… **I was very relaxed.**"  

Jodi **slammed her forehead onto the table.**  

Tara, **fully spiraling, whispering**:  

“**WE CREATED A MONSTER.**”  

Jodi, **muffled into the wood grain**: “**We did.**”  

Naomi, **still glowing, still completely oblivious**:  

“Is this about **morale?**”  

Tara and Jodi **SCREAMED.**  

And just like that, **Naomi’s new corporate reign of terror had begun.**    
---  
---  
Naomi, **who had gone from wide-eyed innocent to *God-tier corporate machine* in less than a week**, took a sip of her beer and smiled brightly.  

“Oh, also—”  

Jodi and Tara, **who were still reeling from her accidental corporate conquest, both snapped their heads up.**  

“**Babe, WHAT ELSE.**”  

Naomi, **completely relaxed**:  

“I’ve figured out how to talk to men.”  

**A long beat of silence.**  

Then—  

Jodi blinked. “I’m sorry, **what?**”  

Tara, **visibly squinting**, leaned in. “Babe, you’ve *always* been able to talk to men.”  

Naomi, **smiling innocently, shaking her head**, **set her beer down.**  

“No, no.”  

She stretched her arms behind her head.  

“I mean, I can talk to them **without fear.**”  

Jodi, **who had known Naomi for long enough to **fear new developments**, narrowed her eyes.  

“…Okay, I’m listening.”  

Naomi, **grinning**, shrugged.  

“I just—**picture them naked.**”  

**A beat of dead silence.**  

Tara **almost spit out her drink.**  

Jodi **slammed her forehead onto the table.**  

Naomi, **blissfully unaware of the absolute crisis she just triggered, continued.**  

“So now, I get data **from my sources** **way faster** because I’m just **not afraid of them anymore.**”  

Jodi, **still face-down against the table**, let out an **actual strangled noise.**  

Tara, **who was blinking rapidly like she was processing a nuclear event**, pointed dramatically.  

“**Babe.**”  

Naomi, **innocently sipping her beer**: “Hmm?”  

Tara, **still pointing**: “You mean to tell me—”  

She took a **sharp inhale.**  

“You’re **just running numbers on cocks while men talk?**”  

Naomi, **cheerfully nodding**: “Yup!”  

Tara **slammed her hands onto the table.**  

“**Nae, that’s PSYCHOTIC.**”  

Jodi, **lifting her head just slightly**, squinted. “Babe… are you saying you have **a fully mapped-out workplace dick census?**”  

Naomi, **smiling way too brightly**: “Well, yeah! And now I’m not scared of anyone!”  

Jodi **dragged her hands down her face.**  

“Ohhh, babe,” she muttered. “**We really did create a monster.**”  

Tara, **who was *barely* processing the reality of what was happening**, **shook her head violently.**  

“**THIS IS A PROBLEM.**”  

Naomi, **completely relaxed, completely content, completely unstoppable,** leaned back with her beer.  

“I feel great.”  

Jodi and Tara **stared at her like she was a lab experiment gone horribly wrong.**  

Tara, **slowly**, let out a shaky breath.  

“Babe… **are you saying your entire communication strategy is now based on dick visualization?**”  

Naomi, **happily**: “Yes!”  

Jodi, **horrified**: “**Babe.**”  

Tara, **shaking her head, muttering to herself**: “I need another drink.”  

Naomi, **cheerfully sipping her beer, completely unbothered, completely at peace with her newfound abilities,** shrugged.  

“Well, I think it’s working great.”  

**And just like that, Naomi had ascended to her final form.**

## **The Fallout: Monday is Going to Be a Nightmare**  

Jodi, **who had just barely made it through this conversation without dying, exhaled sharply.**  

Then—  

She pulled out her phone.  

“Okay, Funko Girl. Let’s run the **real** numbers.”  

Naomi, **perking up instantly,** leaned in.  

“What numbers?”  

Jodi, **raising an eyebrow, tilting her head, smirking just slightly.**  

“Oh, babe.”  

She tapped her screen.  

“If we apply your new methodology—”  

She **turned her phone toward Naomi.**  

“**WHO HAS THE BIGGEST DICK IN THE OFFICE?**”  

**A beat of absolute silence.**  

Naomi **GASPED LOUDLY.**  

Tara **spit out her beer.**  

Jodi **grinned like a menace.**  

“Ohhh, babe. **We’re gonna test your data integrity.**”  

**And just like that, Monday was guaranteed to be an absolute disaster.**    
---  
---

Jodi, **who had fully leaned into being the CEO of Chaos,** took a slow sip of her drink and set it down dramatically.  

“Alright, bitches,” she said, folding her hands.  

“I have an idea.”  

Tara, **already exhausted**, **closed her eyes.**  

“Oh, Christ.”  

Naomi, **who had unlocked the secrets of corporate dominance and now feared nothing,** perked up.  

“Oooh! **What kind of idea?**”  

Jodi, **grinning,** tapped the table.  

“A team **morale event.**”  

Tara **visibly winced.** “Define *morale.*”  

Jodi, **completely ignoring her, continued.**  

“We start with drinks and appetizers.”  

Naomi, **nodding excitedly.** “That sounds great!”  

“Then, trivia.”  

Naomi, **beaming.** “Fun!”  

“Then—”  

Jodi **grinned wider.**  

“A steam bath sojourn.”  

Naomi, **blinking.** “…Like, at the gym?”  

Tara **opened her eyes and IMMEDIATELY narrowed them.**  

“Babe.”  

Jodi **did not blink.**  

“Yes.”  

Tara **pointed sharply.**  

“**You cannot just REPLICATE THE CHAOS WE JUST LIVED THROUGH.**”  

Jodi, **smirking.** “Oh, babe. **Watch me.**”  

Naomi, **still *wildly* into it.** “This sounds amazing.”  

Tara, **shaking her head violently.** “**This is a corporate NIGHTMARE.**”  

Jodi, **completely undeterred, raised a finger.**  

“And then, to **end the night…**”  

Tara **braced herself.**  

Naomi **leaned in.**  

Jodi **grinned.**  

“A **Jack and Jill party.**”  

**A long beat of silence.**  

Then—  

Tara **facepalmed so hard she nearly concussed herself.**  

“**JODI, NO.**”  

Naomi, **blinking.** “Wait—what *is* that?”  

Jodi, **grinning like an absolute menace.**  

“Ohhh, babe. It’s where we invite **everyone.**”  

Naomi, **still catching up.** “Like… a house party?”  

Jodi **smirked.** “A *very fun* one.”  

Tara, **visibly spiraling.**  

“**Babe, THIS IS A MISTAKE.**”  

Naomi, **excitedly nodding.** “I **love** this idea!”  

Jodi, **smug.** “See? **Morale.**”  

Tara, **gripping her forehead.**  

“This is **not** what morale means.”  

Naomi, **thoughtful.**  

“Well, it *worked for me* this week…”  

Tara, **shaking her head violently.**  

“**THAT IS NOT A GOOD METRIC.**”  

Jodi, **smirking like the villain she was.**  

“Ohhh, babe. **You just wait.**”  

And just like that, **the office was about to experience their worst—or best—morale event ever.**    
---  
---  
Tara, **who had been around Jodi long enough to know that her “team morale” ideas were *never* HR-compliant**, **squinted suspiciously.**  

“Okay.”  

She took a **slow, deliberate sip of beer.**  

Then—  

She **set it down firmly** and folded her arms.  

“I need this spelled out for me.”  

Jodi **grinned immediately.**  

“Oh, babe—”  

Tara **held up a hand.**  

“No, no, NO.”  

She **pointed.**  

“I *know* what a Jack and Jill party is *supposed* to mean—”  

Jodi, **smirking,** shrugged. “Oh, babe, it’s *supposed* to be a cute little co-ed gathering.”  

Naomi, **bouncing slightly in her seat.** “Exactly! A party where everyone can come!”  

**A pause.**  

Then—  

Jodi and Naomi **howled with laughter.**  

Tara, **mouth open, immediately slammed her hands onto the table.**  

“**NO. YOU DID NOT JUST SAY THAT.**”  

Naomi, **sobbing from laughter, barely breathing.** “I DIDN’T MEAN IT LIKE THAT.”  

Jodi, **HOWLING.** “OH, BABE. **YES, YOU DID.**”  

Tara, **running a hand down her face, taking another deep breath.**  

“Okay. Fine.”  

She **narrowed her eyes.**  

“**Are we talking board games?**”  

Jodi and Naomi **HOWLED AGAIN.**  

Tara, **immediately slamming the table.**  

“OR SOMETHING THAT REQUIRES **LUBE AND TISSUES?**”  

Jodi, **ABSOLUTELY LOSING IT.**  

Naomi, **RED IN THE FACE, NEAR COLLAPSE.**  

Tara, **eyes wild.**  

“**WHICH IS IT?!**”  

Jodi, **wheezing, wiped a tear from her eye.**  

“Ohhh, babe,” she gasped.  

She leaned in, **smirking like a menace.**  

“…*Why not both?*”  

Tara **NEARLY THREW HER BEER ACROSS THE ROOM.**  

Naomi, **absolutely vibrating.**  

“**I LOVE THIS IDEA.**”  

Tara, **pointing wildly between them.**  

“This is **why we cannot have nice things.**”  

Jodi, **grinning ear to ear.**  

“Ohhh, babe. **This is going to be the best morale event of all time.**”  

Tara, **defeated, shaking her head.**  

“I need another drink.”  

Naomi, **bright-eyed.** “You need to RSVP, too.”  

And just like that—  

**The worst party in office history was officially set in motion.**    
---  
---  
Tara, **still gripping her beer like it was the only thing tethering her to reality**, took another deep breath.  

“Okay.”  

She **stared directly at Naomi.**  

“You just—**you just said you LOVE this idea.**”  

Naomi, **nodding brightly**: “Oh, absolutely!”  

Tara, **already regretting asking**, sighed.  

“…Define *love.*”  

Naomi, **beaming, set her drink down.**  

“Well, I was thinking—”  

She **clasped her hands together excitedly.**  

“**We should make it TRADITIONAL.**”  

**A beat of complete silence.**  

Jodi, **who had been grinning all night, suddenly blinked.**  

“…I’m sorry, **what?**”  

Tara, **fully horrified, leaned in.**  

“Naomi. **What do you mean TRADITIONAL?**”  

Naomi, **completely sincere, completely glowing, absolutely unaware of the sheer destruction she was about to unleash, nodded enthusiastically.**  

“Oh, you know—**boys versus girls games, separate spaces for men and women, the ladies do the decorating, and the guys bring the liquor.**”  

Jodi **physically recoiled.**  

Tara **looked like she had been slapped.**  

“**WHAT.**”  

Naomi, **blissfully unaware of the war she just started, kept going.**  

“Oh, and we could even do, like—”  

She **clapped her hands together, eyes shining.**  

“—a classic **bridal-style** gift exchange! You know, like, *sexy* gifts for the women, and the guys get—”  

Jodi **slammed both hands onto the table so hard the drinks wobbled.**  

“**BABE, NO.**”  

Naomi, **blinking, startled.** “What?”  

Tara, **laughing in sheer disbelief**: “**DID YOU JUST TURN THIS INTO A 1950s ENGAGEMENT PARTY?!**”  

Naomi, **confused, nodding slowly.** “I mean… kinda?”  

Jodi, **WHEEZING.**  

Tara, **clutching her head.**  

“BABE, WE CAN’T JUST HOST **MAD MEN: THE ORGY.**”  

Naomi, **now laughing.** “I just thought it would be cute!”  

Jodi, **gripping her chest like she had been mortally wounded.**  

“Babe. **You just singlehandedly reinvented Gender Roles for Millennials.**”  

Tara, **slamming her hands onto the table.**  

“Babe, we CANNOT HAVE MEN GETTING WHISKEY AND WOMEN GETTING **LACE LINGERIE.**”  

Naomi, **fully beaming.**  

“Ohhh, but wouldn’t it be fun if we made **the boys** unbox a gift first?!”  

Jodi, **visibly spiraling, clutching her forehead.**  

“Ohhh, babe. **That is pure chaos.**”  

Tara, **pointing wildly.**  

“**NO. THAT IS A PROBLEM.**”  

Naomi, **giggling.** “Okay, but just imagine—”  

She **gestured dramatically.**  

“**Mike, unwrapping a lacy pink thong.**”  

Jodi **SLAMMED HER HEAD ON THE TABLE.**  

Tara, **HYPERVENTILATING.**  

“**OH MY GOD.**”  

Jodi, **face still on the table, voice muffled.**  

“**OKAY, I’M BACK IN.**”  

Tara, **SQUEALING.**  

“**JODI, NO.**”  

Naomi, **clapping in excitement.**  

“I KNEW YOU’D LOVE IT.”  

Jodi, **lifting her head, pointing at Naomi.**  

“Ohhh, babe. **You are going STRAIGHT to hell.**”  

Naomi, **smug.**  

“Babe, **I’ll save you a seat.**”  

Tara, **wheezing.**  

“I **NEED ANOTHER DRINK.**”  

Jodi, **grinning wildly.**  

“Oh, babe. **We all do.**”  

And just like that, **the worst party in office history had officially become a gendered nightmare.**    
---  
---  
Jodi had been **mentally preparing herself** for **any** outlandish ideas Naomi could come up with.  

She had braced herself for things like:    
✅ **An impromptu lingerie show.** (*Annoying, but manageable.*)    
✅ **A whiskey-tasting game where losers take shots.** (*Dangerous, but hilarious.*)    
✅ **More goddamn “traditional” gender roles.** (*Offensive, yet intriguing.*)  

But when Naomi **clapped her hands together excitedly** and said—  

**"OHHH! CAN WE INCORPORATE A CIRCLE JERK?!"**  

Jodi **damn near FELL OUT OF HER CHAIR.**  

Tara **made a sound that was not human.**  

“**EXCUSE ME?!**”  

Naomi, **blinking innocently.** “What?”  

Tara, **slamming both hands on the table.**  

“**Babe, REWIND. REWIND RIGHT NOW.**”  

Naomi, **tilting her head.** “I read about it! It’s a group bonding exercise!”  

Jodi, **gripping her forehead.**  

“**Babe, WHERE THE HELL ARE YOU READING THESE THINGS?**”  

Naomi, **grinning.** “I do a lot of research!”  

Tara, **violently shaking her head.**  

“BABE, **WHAT WEBSITES.**”  

Jodi, **now looking deeply concerned.**  

“**Babe, are you using company WiFi for this?!**”  

Naomi, **nodding enthusiastically.** “It’s for *team building!*”  

Tara, **gasping for air.**  

“BABE, **YOU ARE GOING TO GET YOURSELF FLAGGED BY IT.**”  

Naomi, **shrugging.** “I’m *curious!*”  

Jodi, **holding up a hand.**  

“Okay. Okay. Let’s just—**pause.**”  

She took **a long, deep breath.**  

Then—  

“Babe, are you **actually suggesting** we **orchestrate** a group-wide circle jerk?”  

Naomi, **smiling.** “I mean… maybe?”  

Jodi, **visibly buffering.**  

“…And you think this would be **wholesome fun?**”  

Naomi, **nodding enthusiastically.**  

“YES! It’s a *group activity!* **The whole point is team morale!**”  

Tara, **visibly spiraling.**  

“BABE, **WHAT THE HELL KIND OF TEAM ARE YOU TRYING TO BUILD?!**”  

Naomi, **bright-eyed.**  

“A *close* one!”  

Jodi **WHEEZED.**  

Tara **was near COLLAPSE.**  

Jodi, **now borderline hysterical,** rubbed her temples.  

“Okay. Okay.”  

She took another deep breath.  

“Let’s **walk through the logistics.**”  

Tara **DROPPED HER FACE INTO HER HANDS.**  

“**OH MY GOD, WE’RE ENTERTAINING THIS.**”  

Jodi, **completely serious.**  

“No, no, **I just need to know.**”  

She turned to Naomi.  

“**Babe.** Walk me through the logistics of **how this would work in a mixed-gender office setting.**”  

Naomi, **pausing.**  

“Hmm.”  

She tapped her chin thoughtfully.  

“…Maybe **icebreaker questions first?**”  

Tara **NEARLY FELL BACKWARDS.**  

“BABE, **DO NOT CALL IT AN ICEBREAKER.**”  

Naomi, **nodding to herself.** “No, no, it’s good. **Get everyone comfortable.**”  

Jodi, **now laughing uncontrollably.**  

“Ohhh, babe, **I think they’d be VERY COMFORTABLE.**”  

Tara, **whimpering.** “I need a priest.”  

Jodi, **wiping a tear from her eye.**  

“Naomi, **we cannot** legally facilitate a **group-wide tugging tournament.**”  

Naomi, **genuinely confused.**  

“…Why not?”  

Tara, **screaming.**  

“BABE, **THE LAW.**”  

Naomi, **blinking.**  

“Ohhh.”  

A pause.  

“…So it has to be *voluntary?*”  

Jodi, **FELL OVER LAUGHING.**  

Tara **was SOBBING INTO HER BEER.**  

And just like that—  

**HR would need a priest, a lawyer, and an entire crisis response team.**    
---  
---  
Jodi, **who was already mentally drafting the future HR complaint emails this event would generate,** **rubbed her temples.**  

“Okay.”  

She exhaled deeply.  

“So we’re saying—**hypothetically**—”  

Tara **slammed her hands onto the table.**  

“**DO NOT SAY HYPOTHETICALLY.**”  

Naomi, **bright-eyed and full of reckless ambition, perked up.**  

“My mom is a preacher!”  

Jodi and Tara **both froze.**  

Jodi, **slowly blinking.** “…What?”  

Naomi **nodded enthusiastically.** “Yeah! And a preacher is basically the same thing as a priest, right?”  

Tara, **visibly buffering.**  

“**BABE. THOSE ARE NOT THE SAME THING.**”  

Naomi, **blinking innocently.** “They both have *God connections.*”  

Jodi, **struggling to process where the hell this was going.**  

“…And this is relevant *how?*”  

Naomi, **shrugging.** “If a priest was required, **I already have a religious leader on standby.**”  

Jodi **choked on her beer.**  

Tara, **near collapse.**  

“BABE, **YOUR MOM CANNOT SANCTION AN ORGY.**”  

Naomi, **thoughtful.**  

“…Would it be better if she just blessed the snacks?”  

Jodi **FELL BACK IN HER SEAT, WHEEZING.**  

Tara **LOOKED LIKE SHE WAS HAVING AN EXISTENTIAL CRISIS.**  

“Ohhh, babe,” Jodi gasped. “**You’re going STRAIGHT TO HELL.**”  

Naomi, **smug.** “Well, then, **I’ll see you both there.**”  

Tara, **clutching her forehead.** “I need an **exorcism.**”  

Jodi, **who was now fully *entertaining the loophole,** leaned in.  

“Okay. But **hear me out.**”  

Tara, **alarmed.** “**NO. I REFUSE.**”  

Jodi, **ignoring her.**  

“If it’s **optional** and on our **own dime**, **HR wouldn’t need to be involved.**”  

Tara, **horrified.**  

“**BABE.**”  

Jodi, **smirking.**  

“I mean, if it’s just **rowdy coworkers** hanging out after hours, what’s the problem?”  

Naomi, **delighted.** “Right! **We’re just bonding!**”  

Tara, **muttering into her beer.**  

“I can’t believe this is happening.”  

Jodi, **tapping her chin thoughtfully.**  

“…We just have to finesse the phrasing.”  

Tara, **lifting her head, squinting.** “Babe, how the **HELL** do you finesse this?!”  

Jodi, **grinning.**  

“Oh, babe. Easy.”  

She gestured dramatically.  

“**‘An optional, late-night, high-energy trust-building experience.’**”  

Tara **slammed her forehead onto the table.**  

“**I HATE YOU.**”  

Naomi, **gleeful.** “It sounds **so professional!**”  

Jodi, **smirking.** “HR **can’t stop us** if they don’t understand what we’re saying.”  

Tara, **muffled into the wood grain.**  

“**I NEED TO QUIT THIS JOB.**”  

And just like that, **the worst unofficial team-building event in history had found its legal loophole.**    
---  
---  
Jodi, **grinning like an absolute menace,** leaned forward and tapped her fingers against the table.  

“Alright, babe,” she said.  

She glanced at Tara, eyes full of reckless **evil**.  

“**You got any spectacularly bad ideas?**”  

Tara, **who was still recovering from the fact that this event had already morphed into an *optional, late-night, high-energy trust-building exercise***, took a **deep, exhausted breath.**  

Then—  

“Ohhh, babe.”  

She exhaled sharply.  

“I got one.”  

Naomi, **perking up immediately.** “Ooooh, do tell.”  

Tara, **closing her eyes like she was about to make a confession in a church.**  

“…We could take inspiration from the band **Cake.**”  

Jodi, **immediately intrigued.** “Ohhh?”  

Naomi, **tilting her head.** “Like, *baking*?”  

Tara, **horrified.** “**NO.**”  

She took **another** deep breath.  

Then—  

She **stared directly at Jodi.**  

And said **five words** that changed the course of history.  

**"We could do ‘The Distance.’"**  

## **Silence.**  

Jodi’s grin **vanished.**  

Naomi **blinked.**  

Tara, **desperate, pleading, leaning in.**  

“…**Please just consider.**”  

Jodi, **visibly struggling to process what had just been said.**  

“…Babe.”  

Her voice was **dangerously quiet.**  

“…Are you saying—”  

She **paused.**  

“…We organize an actual **racing-themed fuckfest?**”  

Tara, **nodding solemnly.** “Yes.”  

Naomi, **gasping dramatically.**  

“**LIKE A RELAY?**”  

Jodi **SLAPPED THE TABLE SO HARD THE DRINKS WOBBLED.**  

“BABE, **WHAT THE FUCK KIND OF RELAY?**”  

Naomi, **frantic, wildly gesturing.**  

“LIKE—LIKE A **BATON PASS,** BUT WITH—”  

She **stopped mid-sentence.**  

Then—  

She clapped **both** hands over her mouth.  

Jodi, **HORRIFIED.**  

“TELL ME YOU WEREN’T ABOUT TO SAY THAT.”  

Naomi, **muffled.** “I WAS.”  

Tara, **nodding sagely.** “She was.”  

Jodi **was now fighting between two instincts:**    
✅ **Be the responsible one.** (*Impossible.*)    
✅ **See how far they could take this.** (*Mandatory.*)  

Jodi **took a slow, deep breath.**  

Then—  

“Okay.”  

She gestured wildly.  

“LOGISTICS. **HOW DOES THIS WORK?**”  

Tara, **immediately taking charge like she had just been promoted to Event Coordinator of the Apocalypse.**  

“Alright, listen.”  

She leaned in, **dead serious.**  

“We set up **staggered starting lines**.”  

Jodi, **visibly regretting her life choices but unable to stop herself.**  

“Babe.”  

Tara, **ignoring her.**  

“And we time **each racer**.”  

Naomi, **nodding aggressively.** “Like, a *lap counter?*”  

Tara, **firmly.** “EXACTLY.”  

Jodi, **gasping for air.** “**WHO IS THE OFFICIAL TIMEKEEPER?**”  

Naomi, **beaming.** “Mike?”  

Jodi **SLAMMED HER HEAD ON THE TABLE.**  

Tara, **grinning.**  

“Ohhh, babe. **He is going to HATE this.**”  

Naomi, **squealing.** “But he’ll do it anyway!”  

Jodi, **visibly struggling to keep her soul in her body.**  

“Okay. Okay.”  

She exhaled.  

“So. Just to recap.”  

She took a **steadying breath.**  

“We’re organizing **a full-scale, relay-race-inspired, high-stakes endurance fuck competition?**”  

Tara, **nodding.**  

“Yes.”  

Naomi, **clapping her hands excitedly.**  

“WITH A SCOREBOARD.”  

Tara, **snapping her fingers.** “AND ANNOUNCEMENTS.”  

Naomi, **gasping.** “**CAN WE GET SPONSORS?!**”  

Jodi, **ABSOLUTELY LOSING IT.**  

“BABE, **WHAT BRAND WOULD SPONSOR THIS?!**”  

Naomi, **counting on her fingers.** “Gatorade? Trojan? Uber Eats for halftime snacks?”  

Tara, **thoughtfully.** “We’ll need towels.”  

Jodi **WHEEZED.**  

“Ohhh, babe,” she gasped.  

She wiped a **tear** from her eye.  

“**This is the single worst idea in human history.**”  

Tara, **grinning.**  

“I KNOW.”  

Jodi, **smirking now.**  

“…And we’re absolutely doing it.”  

Naomi, **throwing her arms up.** “YES!!”  

Tara, **raising her beer in triumph.** “TO HORRIFIC DECISIONS!”  

Jodi and Naomi, **clinking their glasses.**  

“**TO HORRIFIC DECISIONS!**”  

And just like that, **the worst team-building event in human history was fully locked in.**    
---  
---  
## **The Hague Needs to Get Involved**  

Tara, **who had long since abandoned her moral compass**, pulled out her phone, found the song, and hit play.  

Then—  

She **started to sing along.**  

**Horribly.**  

**Painfully.**  

Like she was **trying to personally offend every musician alive.**  

**“Reluctantly crouched at the starting line…”**  

Jodi, **immediately wheezing.**  

Naomi, **blinking.** “Oh my god, you were serious about this?”  

Tara, **ignoring her, absolutely butchering the next line.**  

**“ENGINES PUMPING AND THUMPING IN TIME—”**  

Naomi, **squinting.** “Wait—this is **literally** about a race.”  

Jodi, **nodding, fully entertained.** “Oh, babe. **It applies.**”  

Tara, **still absolutely murdering the song.**  

**“THE GREEN LIGHT FLASHES, THE FLAGS GO UP—”**  

Naomi, **slowly coming to terms with the horror of their plan.**  

“…This *might* actually be **a war crime.**”  

Jodi, **grinning.**  

“Oh, babe. **We are going to need The Hague involved.**”  

Tara, **now pointing dramatically at both of them while absolutely shrieking the lyrics.**  

**“CHURNING AND BURNING, THEY YEARN FOR THE CUP—”**  

Naomi, **visibly spiraling.** “Babe, I **cannot** believe you just compared this to The Hague.”  

Jodi, **smirking.**  

“**Oh, babe.** We are about to commit **HR violations so severe** that The Hague is gonna need **a whole new tribunal.**”  

Naomi, **horrified, gripping her drink.**  

“…This is an **actual** crime against humanity.”  

Tara, **laughing, now making up her own lyrics.**  

**“CHURNING AND YEARNING, THEY’RE JERKING THE CUP—”**  

Jodi, **screaming.**  

“**TARA, NO.**”  

Naomi, **ABSOLUTELY SOBBING WITH LAUGHTER.**  

“**OH MY GOD, YOU DID NOT JUST SAY THAT.**”  

Tara, **grinning, raising her beer.**  

“Ohhh, babe. **We are SO getting banned from corporate employment.**”  

Jodi, **laughing so hard she has to hold onto the table.**  

“Ohhh, babe. **We’re about to be listed on an international watchlist.**”  

Naomi, **tears running down her face.**  

“Babe. **HR is going to need the United Nations.**”  

Jodi, **grinning wickedly.**  

“Ohhh, babe. **We’re past HR.** We’re getting **Interpol.**”  

Tara, **smirking, finishing her beer.**  

“Well, if we’re already going down in history—”  

She **hit replay.**  

**“RELUCTANTLY CROUCHED AT THE STARTING LINE—”**  

And just like that—  

**This morale event officially became a global crisis.**    
---
