**A Rainy Weekend Revelation: Naomi Discovers the World of Jodi Fan Fiction**  

The rain **tapped softly** against the window, turning the afternoon into a cozy, muted cocoon of **gray and quiet.**  

Naomi, **curled up on the couch with her laptop**, glanced over at Jodi, who was lazily flipping through a magazine.  

She had a question **bubbling up** inside her since they’d started getting closer.  

And today felt like the perfect, rainy afternoon to finally ask it.  

“Hey, Jodi?”  

Jodi **looked up**, raising an eyebrow. “Yeah, babe?”  

Naomi **hesitated**, chewing on her bottom lip.  

“I’ve… been meaning to ask.”  

She took a deep breath.  

“How on earth is there **fan fiction** with *you* in it?”  

Jodi, **blinking slowly**, set the magazine down.  

“Ohhh, babe.”  

She chuckled softly, leaning back against the cushions.  

“It’s a long, weird story.”  

Naomi **leaned in, eyes wide.** “I have *time.*”  

Jodi **smirked.**  

“Okay. So, you know how Jeff’s band blew up, right? Well, fans get **creative.**”  

Naomi, **nodding.** “I get that. But *you’re* in it?”  

Jodi **laughed**, her cheeks turning a light shade of pink.  

“Yeah. I’ve been, um… a *recurring character* in a lot of fanfic. I think it started when someone posted about seeing us together at a gig.”  

Naomi’s eyes **widened.** “So they just… wrote you in?”  

Jodi **grinned.**  

“Yup. Apparently, I’m quite the *muse.*”  

Naomi **snorted.** “Can I read some?”  

Jodi **rolled her eyes playfully.** “Only if you want to read about *absurdly unrealistic* sex scenes and me being some *mysterious rockstar girlfriend.*”  

Naomi, **giggling**, started typing. “I’m already on AO3.”  

Jodi **groaned dramatically.** “Oh god, brace yourself. There’s some *wild* stuff out there.”  

Naomi **clicked on the first link** and her eyes **widened immediately.**  

“Oh my god, Jodi! You’re *practically a superhero* in this one.”  

Jodi **laughed**, peeking over.  

“Ohhh, babe, just wait until you find the one where I’m secretly a *CIA agent* sent to protect him.”  

Naomi **gasped, grinning.** “This is *fantastic.*”  

Jodi **shrugged.**  

“Hey, if nothing else, it’s a good laugh. And sometimes, it’s weirdly flattering.”  

Naomi, **eyes sparkling with curiosity,** looked up.  

“Do you ever… *contribute*?”  

Jodi **snorted.**  

“No way. But it’s fun seeing how people imagine my life. Sometimes it’s better than the real thing.”  

Naomi **grinned wickedly.**  

“I’m *totally* writing one now. Maybe I’ll make *me* the secret agent.”  

Jodi **laughed loudly.**  

“Ohhh, babe. I would *love* to read that.”  

And as the rain continued to fall, the two of them **laughed and scrolled**, discovering a whole new world of chaotic creativity they never knew existed.    
---  
---  
## **The Wikipedia Problem: Naomi Learns Too Much**  

The cozy, rainy afternoon had started off **light and fun.**  

Naomi had been **laughing**, clicking through absurd fan fiction about Jodi. She had just **finished reading** a particularly ridiculous story where Jodi was *a vampire assassin who seduced Jeff while hunting supernatural criminals.*  

But then—  

Jodi **sighed deeply** and muttered:  

“Ohhh, babe. **It’s all fun… until it’s not.**”  

Naomi **glanced up from her laptop.** “What do you mean?”  

Jodi **stretched out**, cracking her knuckles.  

“Well, it’s one thing when fans get *creative.*”  

She gestured vaguely.  

“It’s another thing when Wikipedia decides I need a **historical record.**”  

Naomi **paused.**  

“…You’re on Wikipedia?”  

Jodi **groaned.** “Ohhh, babe. *Too much.*”  

Naomi **immediately started typing.**  

Within **seconds**, she had pulled up Jodi’s **Wikipedia page.**  

And **yup**—there she was.  

Her **full name**.  

Her **approximate birth year** (*rude*).  

The **“Notable Relationships”** section (*even ruder*).  

And then—  

Naomi **froze.**  

Her eyes **narrowed.**  

She **stared** at the screen.  

“…Jodi.”  

Jodi **rolled her eyes.** “Mmm?”  

Naomi **pointed at the screen.**  

“**You weren’t kidding about the cocaine possession arrest in Boston, were you?**”  

Jodi **winced.**  

“Ahhh. Yep. That’s… that’s real.”  

Naomi **slowly turned her head.**  

“You got arrested?”  

Jodi **waved a hand vaguely.**  

“Well, **not technically.** I mean—okay, yes, I *was* handcuffed and processed, and there *were* legal proceedings, and it *was* a complete nightmare…”  

She sighed.  

“…but I wasn’t even involved. And yet—there it is. **The internet is forever.**”  

Naomi **kept reading.**  

Her **expression shifted.**  

There were **direct quotes** from court filings. Mentions of **sealed depositions.** Details of **what happened in the car that night.**  

Naomi **looked up.**  

“…This is **way too detailed.**”  

Jodi **snorted.**  

“Oh, babe. **You think that’s bad?**”  

She **leaned over, scrolled down, and pointed at a section further down the page.**  

**'Jodi's Alleged Influence on Jeff’s Lyrics'**  

Naomi **read aloud.**  

> *Many fans speculate that numerous songs in Jeff’s discography contain references to his on-again, off-again relationship with Jodi. This includes:*    
>     
>  *- "Snowglobe" (2013), which is rumored to reference her long term affair with Jeff*    
>  *- "Twenty Dollar Mistake" (2016), allegedly written in response to the legal fees associated with Jodi's 2014 cocaine possession charge.*    
>  *- "New York in Ruins" (2019), believed to reference an argument between the two at a hotel in SoHo.*  

Naomi **slowly looked up.**  

“Babe. **What the fuck.**”  

Jodi **leaned back against the couch.**  

“Ohhh, babe. **Imagine reading about your own life and learning new things.**”  

Naomi **sputtered.** “**Did you even KNOW he wrote a song about your legal fees?!**”  

Jodi **threw her hands up.** “**NO!**”  

She gestured wildly at the screen.  

“I mean, do you know **how weird it is** to read an analysis of your **personal drama** like it’s a *Shakespearean tragedy*?”  

Naomi, **still horrified.**  

“Babe, this is… **not normal.**”  

Jodi **laughed dryly.**  

“Ohhh, babe. **Welcome to my fucking life.**”  

Naomi **scrolled further.**  

Her **eyes narrowed again.**  

“Babe.”  

Jodi **exhaled sharply.** “What *now?*”  

Naomi **read aloud again.**  

> **"In 2021, a fan claimed on Twitter that Jodi was spotted wearing an engagement ring. This remains unconfirmed."**  

She **slowly turned her head.**  

“…Jodi?”  

Jodi, **blinking.**  

“…Babe, I swear to **GOD,** I have never been engaged.”  

Naomi **squinted.** “Then what the fuck is this?”  

Jodi **shrugged dramatically.**  

“Ohhh, babe, I don’t know. I probably wore a ring on my left hand one time, and now *some internet gremlin* thinks I’m secretly married.”  

Naomi **stared at her.**  

Jodi **grinned.**  

“Babe. **Wanna make it worse?**”  

Naomi **hesitated.**  

“…What do you mean?”  

Jodi **pulled out her phone, tapped a few times, and held it up.**  

“There’s a **Reddit thread** where people debate whether I’m actually **married to Jeff in secret.**”  

Naomi **GASPED.**  

“Oh my god, SHOW ME.”  

Jodi **tossed the phone onto her lap.**  

Naomi **scrolled, eyes wide.**  

Tara, **walking in with a beer, raised an eyebrow.**  

“What’s happening?”  

Jodi, **grinning.** “Oh, babe. **Naomi just fell into the rabbit hole.**”  

Naomi, **reading frantically.**  

“**PEOPLE THINK YOU HAVE CHILDREN.**”  

Tara **choked on her drink.**  

Jodi, **cackling.**  

“Ohhh, babe. **The internet has decided I’m a wife and mother.**”  

Naomi, **horrified.**  

“Babe, **DO YOU UNDERSTAND HOW MANY PEOPLE THINK YOU’RE LIVING A DOUBLE LIFE?**”  

Jodi, **smirking.**  

“Ohhh, babe. **Let’s lean in.**”  

Naomi, **shocked.** “What?”  

Jodi **wiggled her eyebrows.**  

“Babe. Let’s **fuck with them.**”  

Tara, **grinning.** “Ohhh, **yes.**”  

Naomi, **gasping, immediately on board.**  

“Oh my god. **What do we do?!**”  

Jodi, **smirking.**  

“Ohhh, babe. **We start posting ‘cryptic’ Instagram captions.**”  

Tara, **laughing.**  

“‘Just another day at home… 🏡✨’”  

Naomi, **cackling.**  

“‘A cozy weekend with loved ones… ❤️’”  

Jodi, **grinning evilly.**  

“Ohhh, babe. **We’re gonna make the internet LOSE THEIR MINDS.**”  

And just like that—  

**Jodi’s Wikipedia entry was about to get a whole lot worse.**    
---  
---  
## **The Internet Has Ruined Everything (But Naomi Still Has a Terrible Idea)**  

The rain had settled into a **gentle rhythm**, tapping against the windows, while Naomi, Jodi, and Tara lounged comfortably—at least **physically**.  

Mentally?  

Jodi was **one existential crisis away** from throwing her phone into a lake.  

Naomi, **oblivious to Jodi’s mounting dread**, **brightened.**  

“Oh! **What if we just posted a series of lewd photos?**”  

Jodi **turned her head so slowly it was almost mechanical.**  

She **blinked.**  

Once.  

Twice.  

Then, **she gave Naomi a look.**  

**That look.**  

The one you give **an earnest, sweet student who learned everything from books but has never once applied the knowledge in the real world.**  

Tara, **immediately picking up on the tension, hid behind her beer.**  

Jodi, **exhaling sharply, leaned forward.**  

“Okay, babe.”  

She **tilted her head.**  

“**Do a nude image search of my name.**”  

Naomi, **blinking.** “What?”  

Jodi, **gesturing at her laptop.** “**Just do it.**”  

Naomi, **still confused, started typing.**  

And **then.**  

**Oh.**  

Oh.  

Naomi’s **eyes went wide.**  

She **scrolled.**  

And scrolled.  

And **scrolled.**  

Jodi, **leaning back against the couch, sipping her drink.** “**I’ll wait.**”  

Naomi, **horrified.**  

“…Babe.”  

She kept scrolling.  

“**Babe.**”  

Still scrolling.  

“**THIS IS INFINITE.**”  

Tara, **peering over Naomi’s shoulder, choked on her drink.**  

“**OH MY GOD, THEY’RE ALL AI GENERATED.**”  

Naomi, **gasping.** “THEY PUT YOU IN **EVERY POSITION KNOWN TO MAN.**”  

Jodi, **flatly.** “Yup.”  

Naomi, **scrolling frantically.** “**WHO EVEN MADE THESE?!**”  

Jodi **shrugged.**  

“The **internet**. And **bored men.**”  

Tara, **squinting.** “**Why are you in a medieval castle in this one?**”  

Naomi, **scrolling further, horrified.** “WHY DO YOU HAVE CAT EARS HERE?! **WHO MADE THESE DECISIONS?!**”  

Jodi, **sipping her drink like she had already accepted the **universe’s cruelty***.  

“Ohhh, babe. **This is why your idea isn’t novel.**”  

Naomi, **closing the laptop with **way too much force***.  

“**THAT TAKES ALL THE FUN OUT OF IT.**”  

Jodi **exhaled sharply, staring up at the ceiling.**  

“Ohhh, babe. **The internet and the public have taken a lot of fun out of my life.**”  

Naomi, **almost—** *almost* **becoming empathetic, paused.**  

“…Babe.”  

She frowned slightly.  

“That really sucks.”  

Jodi **nodded, closing her eyes for a second.**  

“Yup.”  

A **beat of silence.**  

Then—  

Naomi, **brightening immediately.**  

“**BUT I STILL HAVE A GOOD IDEA.**”  

Jodi, **groaning.** “Ohhh, babe.”  

Tara, **laughing into her beer.** “Oh god, **here we go again.**”  

Jodi, **exasperated but entertained.** “Okay, **let’s hear it.**”  

Naomi, **grinning wickedly.**  

“What if instead of *us* posting **lewd pictures**, we **leak fake ones and react to them like we’re scandalized?**”  

Jodi **stared.**  

Tara **IMMEDIATELY BURST OUT LAUGHING.**  

Naomi, **clapping her hands excitedly.**  

“OH MY GOD. **JODI, WE COULD MANUFACTURE A SCANDAL.**”  

Jodi, **considering it way too hard.**  

“…Babe.”  

She tapped her chin.  

“**This might be genius.**”  

Tara, **slamming the table.**  

“OH MY GOD, **YOU’RE ACTUALLY ENTERTAINING THIS.**”  

Jodi, **grinning.** “Babe, **we fake-leak** the most **ridiculous** AI-generated image.”  

Naomi, **giggling.** “Something **completely absurd.**”  

Jodi, **grinning wider.** “**And then we act HORRIFIED.**”  

Tara, **wheezing.** “Ohhh, babe. **This is absolutely deranged.**”  

Naomi, **clapping.** “**LET’S DO IT.**”  

And just like that—  

**The internet was about to get gaslit into believing Jodi had been hacked.**    
---  
---  
Naomi, **grinning like an absolute menace,** suddenly pulled off her necklace and **handed it to Jodi.**  

Jodi, **blinking.** “Uh. What?”  

Naomi, **smug.** “**Put it on.**”  

Jodi, **raising an eyebrow, slipping it over her head.**  

“Okay?”  

The necklace **sank beneath her shirt, disappearing from view.**  

Naomi **clapped her hands together excitedly.**  

“**PERFECT.**”  

Tara, **squinting.** “Babe. **Where the hell is this going?**”  

Naomi, **bouncing in her seat.**  

“Ohhh, babe. **We need to find something ornery for Jodi to wear.**”  

Jodi, **grinning.** “*Ornery*?”  

Naomi, **nodding.**  

“Something that shows **too much cleavage.**”  

Tara, **visibly buffering.**  

“…Babe. **WHY?**”  

Naomi, **grinning wider.**  

“Because **we’re taking a photo centered on the necklace.**”  

Jodi, **blinking.**  

“…Okay?”  

Naomi, **leaning in, eyes sparkling with mischief.**  

“**Then we’re tagging Jeff.**”  

Jodi, **leaning forward, intrigued.** “Go on.”  

Naomi, **dramatic pause.**  

“And posting—”  

She **spread her hands.**  

**“Hit the target, Jeff, win a prize!”**  

Tara, **deadpan.**  

“Oh my god.”  

Naomi, **giddy.** “AND THEN—”  

She **pulled out her phone, swiped through her camera roll, and triumphantly held it up.**  

**A picture of one of her teddy bears.**  

“**THIS IS THE PRIZE.**”  

Jodi **burst out laughing.**  

Tara, **staring at the ceiling like she was praying for strength.**  

“So. **To summarize.**”  

She held up a finger.  

“One, we put Jodi in a dangerously low-cut top.”  

Another finger.  

“Two, we take a **very suggestive** photo focused on the necklace.”  

Another finger.  

“Three, we post it publicly with **a caption that strongly implies Jeff is about to nut on her.**”  

Final finger.  

“**And then we reveal the PRIZE is a FUCKING TEDDY BEAR.**”  

Naomi, **clapping excitedly.** “YES. EXACTLY.”

“BABE. **I THINK I FOUND PAYDIRT.**”

“Ohhh, babe.”

“Babe. **Pause.**”  

Naomi, **dramatically holding her breath.**

“Babe. **HOW off?**”

“Ohhh, babe. **Buckle the fuck up.**”  

And just like that—  

**Shit was about to get WAY too real.**

Jodi, **not even blinking.**  

“**What do you think he’d say?**”  

## **TARA AND NAOMI LOOK AT EACH OTHER.**  

Naomi, **hesitant.** “I mean… I guess—”  

Jodi, **interrupting, leaning forward.**  

“No. **Really.**”  

She **gestures lazily with her wine glass.**  

“**What would Jeff fucking say?**”  

## **NAOMI STUMBLES.**  

Naomi, **thinking hard.** “I— I don’t know. Maybe something about how he didn’t mean to hurt you?”  

Jodi, **grinning coldly.** “**That’s adorable.**”  

Tara, **watching Jodi carefully.** “So you don’t think he’d say that?”  

Jodi, **laughing bitterly.**  

“Oh, I think he’d say it. **Just wouldn’t mean it.**”

Naomi, **softly.** “Jodi…”  

Jodi, **shrugging.**  

“Look, if he calls me up and says, ‘**Jodi, I’d really love to have a deep, soul-searching conversation about my dick and where it’s been,**’ I’ll **consider it.**”

“I mean **change the subject and suck him off.**”

Jodi, **raising an eyebrow.** “Am I?”

Jodi, **grinning faintly.**  

“**Look. I already know how this goes.**”  

She **leans back, staring at the ceiling.**  

“I’ll fuck him. He’ll fuck off. **And the world keeps spinning.**”

“**What other option do I have?**”

## **THE HR PRESENTATION**  

**Five days later.**  

Jodi, **standing at the head of the conference room, remote in hand, HR Manager watching with a mix of curiosity and mild fear.**

“Alright, so to **recap.**”  

HR Manager, **nodding along.** “Go on.”  

Jodi, **grinning.**  

“We’re proposing an **optional** after-hours morale booster event. Something to bring the team together in a **fun, engaging, and completely voluntary** way.”  

HR Manager, **eyebrow raised.** “Uh-huh.”  

Jodi, **clicks to the next slide.**  

“Phase One: Drinks and appetizers at a local establishment. Something casual. **Light socializing.**”  

HR Manager, **nodding.** “Alright. That seems reasonable.”

HR Manager, **blinking.** “In a… sauna?”  

Jodi, **grinning.** “Absolutely! **Sweat out the workplace tension.**”

HR Manager, **intrigued despite himself.** “Go on.”    

## **JODI’S HR-COMPLIANT (?) MASTURBATION PARTY PROPOSAL:**  

🔹 **Phase One: "No Pressure, Just Vibes."**    
- Attendees arrive, sign NDAs, and get an optional masquerade mask upon entry.    
- **Strict consent rules:** No touching, no pressure, and absolutely no surprise “audience participation.”    
- Light jazz plays in the background. Maybe Enya.   

🔹 **Phase Two: “Choose Your Own Adventure.”**    
- Private, designated spaces available: Some soundproofed, some shared but discreet.    
- Soft lighting, weighted blankets, **stress-relief stations** with… helpful tools.    
- A snack table, obviously. **No one should have to do this on an empty stomach.**  

🔹 **Phase Three: The Cool-Down & Debrief.**    
- A cozy, **no-judgment lounge area** for post-session decompressing.    
- Herbal teas, soothing music, **zero awkwardness.**    
- Attendees leave **relaxed, refreshed, and ready to be way more productive at work.**

### **🔹 THE PROPOSED GUIDELINES 🔹**  

🔸 **1. Full Voluntary Participation & Confidentiality.**    
- **Written consent** required.    
- **No recording, no documentation, no attendance tracking.**    
- **NDAs signed at the door.**  

🔸 **2. Structured Spaces for Comfort & Boundaries.**    
- **Three distinct zones:**    
  - The **"Social & Chill" Zone** (Wine, tea, deep convos.)    
  - The **"Guided Relaxation" Zone** (Think yoga meets self-care.)    
  - The **"Personal Reflection" Zone** (Self-directed & very *hands-on*.)  

🔸 **3. Health & Wellness Considerations.**    
- **Sanitation & hygiene protocols** (No one’s getting pink eye on our watch.)    
- **Licensed therapists & wellness coaches** on standby.    
- **Snack bar.** (*Duh.*)  

🔸 **4. HR and Legal **Must Attend.**    
- **HR oversight** to ensure compliance.    
- **Legal presence** to ensure no one ruins the fun.

Naomi, **innocently.** “And I’m giving back! I’m the **Guest of Honor, after all.**”

Jodi, **whispering.** “No. **We created a menace.**”  

Legal, **grinning.** “A **dangerous** menace.”  

Naomi, **raising her coffee cup.** “**Damn right.**”

## **Naomi’s Power Move: Controlling the Food Budget—But First, What the Hell Is ‘Tribute’?!**  

Jodi and Legal thought Naomi was just going to *show up* as the Guest of Honor. Turns out, she wants *power.* But first… she needs a little clarification on a *certain* word.  

---

## **NAOMI DROPS A BOMBSHELL.**  

Naomi, **sitting down confidently.** “Alright, listen. **I want to take charge of the food budget.**”  

Jodi, **raising an eyebrow.** “Oh?”  

Legal, **smirking.** “*Interesting.*”  

Naomi, **nodding.** “If I’m the **Guest of Honor,** I should be **doing something.**”  

Jodi, **mock serious.** “Taking responsibility. **A noble move.**”  

Legal, **grinning.** “You’re *really* leaning into this role.”  

Naomi, **shrugging.** “Well, yeah. I’m **kind of a big deal now.**”  

## **LEGAL LEANS FORWARD.**  

Legal, **smirking.** “Before we discuss the menu… Do you actually **understand** what we mean when we say you’re ‘tribute’?”  

Naomi, **pausing.** “…Okay, yeah. Before I finalize anything… **Define ‘tribute.’**”  

## **JODI & LEGAL EXCHANGE A LOOK.**  

Jodi, **grinning.** “Oh, honey.”  

Legal, **laughing.** “It’s… a term of **great honor.**”  

Jodi, **nodding.** “A **sacred** role, if you will.”  

Naomi, **squinting.** “You’re **avoiding** a real answer.”  

Legal, **grinning wickedly.** “Because it’s **way funnier this way.**”  

Naomi, **crossing her arms.** “Does this mean **I have to do something specific?**”  

Jodi, **mock serious.** “Well… it’s *implied* that—”  

Legal, **interrupting.** “No, **no, no.** You’re a *symbol,* Naomi.”  

Jodi, **nodding.** “A **representation** of the event’s spirit.”  

## **NAOMI LOOKS SUSPICIOUS.**  

Naomi, **staring at them.**  

“…Are you telling me **I got signed up for something and I don’t even know what it is?**”  

Legal, **grinning.** “**Correct.**”  

Naomi, **dramatically.**  

“**Jesus Christ.**”  

Jodi, **laughing.** “It’s fine! You’ll be **glorious.**”  

Naomi, **groaning.** “I swear to God, if this is like a *blood sacrifice,* I’m out.”  

Legal, **mock offended.** “Naomi! This is **HR-approved.**”  

Jodi, **grinning.** “Mostly.”  

---

## **NAOMI TAKES CHARGE OF FOOD ANYWAY.**  

Naomi, **sighing, flipping open a notebook.**  

“Fine. **Fine.** **Let’s talk catering.**”  

Jodi, **impressed.** “Power move.”  

Legal, **grinning.** “So *regal.*”  

Naomi, **rolling her eyes.** “Just tell me right now—**are we doing passed hors d’oeuvres, or is this a buffet situation?**”  

Jodi, **raising an eyebrow.** “Wait. **You actually care about this?**”  

Naomi, **nodding.** “If I’m the **Guest of Honor,** I want the food to **slap.**”  

Legal, **leaning back.** “I love that you’re taking **this** more seriously than what ‘tribute’ means.”  

Naomi, **grinning.** “Priorities.”  

---
## **Naomi Thinks Jodi & Legal Are Speaking in Code—They Absolutely Are**  

Naomi is stepping into the spotlight, and Jodi & Legal are enjoying *way too much* how little she fully understands what that means.  

---

Naomi, **raising an eyebrow.** “What, like I’m the star?”

Jodi, **correcting.** “No, no, no. **She’s the target.**”

Jodi, **mock innocently.** “Tell you **what, exactly?**”  

Naomi, **throwing up her hands.** “WHY AM I THE TARGET?!”  

Legal, **grinning wickedly.** “Oh, honey. **You’ll see.**”

Legal, **mock casual.** “Oh, just the guys.”  

Naomi, **crossing her arms.** “**What kind of gifts?**”  

Jodi, **smirking.** “**The thoughtful kind.**”  

## **NAOMI REALIZES SHE’S IN TOO DEEP.**

Jodi, **beaming.** “Excited?”

Legal, **smirking.** “*Sitting’s probably a good idea.*”  

---

Legal, in her infinite wisdom, finally decides to give Naomi *some* clarity about what being "tribute" actually means. Jodi is just here for the reactions.

Naomi, **nodding furiously.** “YES. THANK YOU.”

## **NAOMI BLINKS.**

Jodi, **grinning.** “Oh, this is about to get good.”

“**I have never before—nor since—been surrounded by that many men beating off at once.**”  

## **NAOMI STOPS FUNCTIONING.**  

Naomi, **staring.**  

Jodi, **grinning wider.**  

Legal, **raising an eyebrow.** “Processing?”  

Naomi, **whispering.** “I… I don’t think I know how to process this.”  

Jodi, **mock serious.** “Well, **you better learn quick.**”

Legal, **smirking.** “So, thoughts?”  

Naomi, **staring into the void.** “I don’t have any.”  

Jodi, **laughing.** “No thoughts, head empty?”  

Naomi, **weakly.** “Head *so* empty.”  

## **LEGAL LEANS BACK, SMUG.**  

Legal, **grinning.** “See? That wasn’t so bad.”  

Naomi, **slowly.** “THAT WAS THE WORST THING YOU’VE EVER SAID TO ME.”  

Jodi, **laughing hysterically.**

HR, **ignoring her.** “Let’s get started! First, we’ll do the **Self-Evaluation.**”  

Naomi, **blinking.** “What.”  

HR, **handing her a form.** “Just rate your performance on a scale from **‘Eager to Learn’ to ‘Promising Future Leader.’**”  

Naomi, **reading the scale.**  

- **Eager to Learn** (*???*)    
- **Team Player** (*Okay, fine.*)    
- **Fast Learner** (*What does that even mean?!*)    
- **Future Mentor** (*NO.*)    
- **Promising Future Leader** (*ABSOLUTELY NOT.*)  

Naomi, **horrified.** “There is **no failing option.**”  

Mike, **grinning.** “Exactly. **You pass no matter what.**”  

Jodi, **giggling.** “It’s like kindergarten, but worse.”  

---
## **LEGAL & HR TAG-TEAM THE *WORST* QUESTIONS.**  

Legal, **flipping pages.** “Next section: **Peer Feedback.**”  

Naomi, **suspicious.** “Who are my *peers?*”  

Jodi, **grinning.** “**Us.**”  

Naomi, **already regretting everything.** “I *hate* this.”  

HR, **reading aloud.** “**Question 1:** How well did Naomi embrace her role?”  

Jodi, **laughing.** “Like a *deer in headlights.*”  

Mike, **nodding.** “**A fast-moving train wreck.**”  

Legal, **writing notes.** “So… *enthusiastically panicked?*”  

Naomi, **whimpering.** “I don’t *think* that’s a compliment.”  

HR, **moving on.** “**Question 2:** What areas can Naomi improve in?”  

Jodi, **grinning.** “I’d say *grace under pressure,* but honestly, *her suffering is the fun part.*”  

Naomi, **whining.** “THAT IS NOT CONSTRUCTIVE FEEDBACK.”  

Mike, **thoughtful.** “Maybe *embracing inevitability*?”  

Naomi, **wailing.** “*STOP MAKING ME SOUND LIKE A FINAL BOSS FIGHT.*”  

Legal, **nodding.** “So… *‘Greater acceptance of corporate absurdity’*?”  

Naomi, **collapsed on the table.** “I’m *never* getting out of Level 1.”  

---
## **HR DROPS THE FINAL BOMB.**  

HR, **cheerfully.** “One final note! Since Naomi’s certification has been *such a success*, we’re introducing a new initiative.”  

Naomi, **muffled.** “Kill me.”  

HR, **grinning.** “Introducing: **The Level 2 Certification Pilot Program!**”  

## **NAOMI FLIES OUT OF HER CHAIR.**  

Naomi, **SCREAMING.** “**YOU’RE EXPANDING THIS HELL?!**”  

Legal, **already flipping pages.** “Oh, *definitely.*”  

Mike, **high-fiving HR.** “She’s *ready.*”  

Jodi, **clapping.** “Congrats, **Funko Pop Girl.**”  

Naomi, **dead inside.** “I hate *everything.*”  

Jodi, **smirking.** “No, you don’t.”  

Naomi, **groaning.** “No. **I don’t.**”  


---
## **LEGAL PRESENTS THE “HEALTH BENEFITS” OF CERTIFIED TRIBUTE TRAINING.**  

Legal, **adjusting glasses**: “Alright, Naomi. Let’s talk about the *significant health benefits* associated with your role as a Level 1 Certified Tribute.”  

Naomi, **looking suspicious**: “Significant health benefits? Like what—besides a *permanent anxiety disorder?*”  

Jodi, **chiming in**: “It’s probably something like cardio. *For your soul.*”  

## **LEGAL, EVER THE PROFESSIONAL, DELIVERS THE HEALTH BENEFITS.**  

Legal, **seriously**: “Actually, it’s a lot like cardio. Did you know that the average person burns about **200 calories** during a *vigorous team-building exercise?*”  

Naomi, **suspicious**: “What kind of *exercise* are we talking about here?”  

Mike, **raising an eyebrow**: “The *kind* that HR probably can’t write off as ‘wellness training,’ but they’re gonna try.”  

Legal, **nodding**: “Exactly. Also, it’s been shown that participating in these kinds of group events can **increase endorphins** and *lower blood pressure.*”  

Naomi, **eyeing HR**: “Is this just some elaborate scheme to get out of funding actual wellness programs?”  

HR, **looking a bit too enthusiastic**: “Oh, absolutely. We’re very into *efficiency.*”  

## **HR ANNOUNCES THE BUDGET—WITH GREAT FANFARE.**  

HR, **proudly presenting a spreadsheet**: “Speaking of efficiency, I’ve secured a *fantastic budget* for our event! We have a total of **$12.75 per attendee** for **food, alcohol, admission fees, and lubricants.**”  

Naomi, **deadpan**: “**$12.75?** That’s not even enough for a *cheap beer and a slice of pizza!*”  

Jodi, **giggling**: “This is gonna be a *dollar store disaster,* and I’m living for it.”  

## **MIKE MAKES IT WORSE. AS USUAL.**  

Mike, **snickering**: “Hey, don’t knock it till you try it. You’d be amazed what you can do with **$12.75** and some creativity.”  

Legal, **nodding seriously**: “He’s not wrong. Plus, I’ve allocated some of that budget for *essential supplies,* like…”  

Naomi, **wide-eyed**: “Like…?”  

Legal, **matter-of-factly**: “Like the aforementioned lubricants, as per the **health and wellness guidelines.**”  

Naomi, **facepalming**: “I have so many questions.”  

Jodi, **giggling uncontrollably**: “But no answers.”  

---
## **HR BREAKS DOWN THE BUDGET. IT’S WORSE THAN NAOMI EXPECTED.**  

HR, **optimistically**: “So, here’s how it breaks down:  

- **$2.00 per person** for *appetizers.*    
- **$3.00 for alcohol**—but we’re sticking to the cheap stuff.    
- **$1.25 for general admission fees.**    
- And **$6.50 for lubricants and miscellaneous essentials.**”  

Naomi, **horrified**: “**$6.50 for lubricants?!** What kind of *event* is this?”  

Legal, **calmly**: “An *efficient* one.”  

Mike, **grinning like a madman**: “And I think it’s gonna be **amazing.**”  

Naomi, **despairing**: “This is a nightmare.”  

Jodi, **winking**: “Welcome to the *corporate-sponsored absurdity,* Naomi. You’re *nailing it.*”  

---
## **LEGAL WRAPS UP WITH “WORDS OF ENCOURAGEMENT.”**  

Legal, **leaning in**: “Look, Naomi, at the end of the day, this is about *team bonding*, improving *morale*, and getting everyone *out of their comfort zones*—which studies show is good for both *physical and mental health.* And think of the *endorphin rush!*”  

Naomi, **barely holding it together**: “I think I’ve *rushed* out of endorphins and straight into an existential crisis.”  

HR, **clapping**: “That’s the spirit!”  

Naomi, **defeated**: “No. **No, it’s not.**”  

Jodi, **mockingly comforting**: “Don’t worry, sweetie. It’s *fine.* It’s all in the name of *corporate wellness.*”  

Naomi, **groaning**: “I hate everything.”  

Jodi, **smiling broadly**: “No, you don’t.”  

Naomi, **sighing**: “No. **I don’t.**”  

---
## **FRIDAY NIGHT IN CLEVELAND – JODI’S MIND IS A CHAOTIC STORM**  

**It’s late.** Jodi is **staring out a high-rise window** at Cleveland’s city lights, **nursing a drink, and trying not to think too hard.**  

That is *not* going well.  

---
## **JODI’S INTERNAL MONOLOGUE: A TOTAL MESS**  

- **What the hell am I doing here?**    
- *Oh, right.* Running away from my real problems. *Classic.*    
- **Do I even want to be here?**    
- Well. *Kinda.*    
- **Does Jeff know I’m here?**    
- No, because he’s somewhere exotic doing God-knows-what. Or *who.*    
- **Do I care?**    
- …Next question.    
- **Do I like this bartender?**    
- He’s hot. But I’m *not* drunk enough to make *that* mistake.    
- *Yet.*    
- **What’s Naomi doing right now?**    
- Probably researching *something ridiculous* and texting Tara in all caps.    
- **Is Tara jealous I ran off to Cleveland?**    
- Eh. Maybe a little.    
- **Am I about to make a questionable decision?**    
- **…Probably.**  

---
## **HER PHONE LIGHTS UP. SHE IGNORES IT.**  

Jeff? Maybe.    
Tara? Likely.    
HR? **God forbid.**  

She takes a sip of whiskey and *forces* herself to stop caring.  

**She came here to disappear.**  

But now she’s **two drinks in, alone in a bar,** and wondering if she’s just running in circles.  

**Cleveland was supposed to be a distraction.**    
Instead, it’s just **another neon-lit question mark.**  

She exhales, checks the time, and wonders:  

- **Do I go back to my hotel?**    
- **Or do I make this night count?**  

---
## **FRIDAY NIGHT IN CLEVELAND – JODI’S "SOMETIMES LOVER" FINALLY SHOWS UP**  

Jodi had just about **written him off.**  

She checked the time—**11:17 PM.** *Way past “running late” territory. Firmly in “probably ghosting me” land.*  

She wasn’t mad, not really. Just **mildly irritated** and already formulating a **backup plan.** Maybe she’d just flirt with the bartender. Maybe she’d go back to her hotel and make **bad decisions alone.** Maybe she’d order an **irresponsible amount of room service** just to see how much damage she could do to the mini bar.  

And then—**finally**—**he walked in.**  

---
## **HE LOOKS GOOD. TOO GOOD.**  

Jodi glanced up **casually,** pretending she hadn’t been scanning the room for him **for the past 40 minutes.**  

- His **hair’s a little longer** than she remembered.    
- He’s **dressed well, but just messy enough** to look like he didn’t try too hard.    
- And his **smirk?** That was **trouble.**  

He **spotted her immediately**, weaving through the bar like he belonged there. Like he knew **exactly** what kind of reaction he was about to get.  

Jodi rolled her eyes, took a slow sip of whiskey, and **pretended she wasn’t thrilled to see him.**  

**"You're late."**  

He dropped into the seat across from her, **completely unfazed.**  

**"And yet, here I am."**  

---
## **THE FIRST ROUND OF BANTER (BECAUSE THEY CAN’T HELP THEMSELVES)**  

Jodi, **raising an eyebrow.** “Do you even have an excuse?”  

Him, **grinning.** “Would it matter?”  

Jodi, **fake-considering.** “If it’s good.”  

Him, **leaning back, smirking.** “What if I say I got *distracted*?”  

Jodi, **deadpan.** “I’ll assume it was *another* woman.”  

Him, **mock-offended.** “That’s your default assumption?”  

Jodi, **flatly.** “It’s your *track record.*”  

Him, **grinning wider.** “So, no faith in me?”  

Jodi, **smirking.** “I have faith in *your consistency.*”  

He laughed, **flagged down a server, and ordered a drink like he had all the time in the world.**  

**Bastard.**  

---
## **JODI’S INNER MONOLOGUE: A MESSY REBOOT**  

- *Damn it. He looks really good.*    
- **He knows he looks good.**    
- *I should be mad at him for being late.*    
- **I’m not.**    
- *Do I actually want to sleep with him tonight?*    
- **…Maybe.**    
- *Is this just me being lonely because Jeff’s gone?*    
- **Possibly.**    
- *Will thinking about that stop me from making a bad decision?*    
- **Absolutely not.**  

---
## **FRIDAY NIGHT IN CLEVELAND – NO MORE GAMES**  

Jodi **wasn’t in the mood for banter.**  

She’d wasted **half of Friday night** on the road and in this damn bar, **waiting for him to show up.**  

And now, here he was—**looking good, acting smug, sitting across from her like time had no meaning.**  

Fine. **She’d get to the point.**  

Jodi, **setting down her glass, locking eyes with him.**    
**“We can talk later. Get up.”**  

He blinked. **Didn’t move.** Smirked a little.  

**“Excuse me?”**  

Jodi, **flatly.** “You heard me.”  

He tilted his head, amusement flickering across his face. **“Not even gonna finish your drink first?”**  

Jodi, **already standing up, grabbing her bag.** “I can drink at the hotel.”  

He let out a **low, knowing laugh.**  

**“Damn, Jodi.”** He stood, tossing a few bills onto the table for the check. **“All business tonight, huh?”**  

Jodi, **turning for the exit.** “Long drive. Long wait. You don’t get to take your time.”  

---
## **THE RIDE TO THE HOTEL – FULL OF TENSION**  

They barely spoke on the way back.  

Not in a tense way—more like **the unspoken understanding that words were unnecessary.**  

Her arms were folded. His hands stayed on the wheel.  

But she could feel him **stealing glances at her**, reading the mood, calculating.  

He was smart enough to know **not to push his luck.**  

---
## **THE MOMENT THEY GET INSIDE THE ROOM**  

The second the door shut behind them, she turned, grabbed him by the collar, and **pulled him down into a kiss.**  

He made a **low, satisfied noise**, pressing her back against the wall like he’d been waiting for this all night.  

She tugged at his jacket. **He shrugged it off.**  

His hands were already **on her hips,** her waist, sliding down—  

Then he stopped, pulled back just enough to look at her, and **grinned.**  

**"So, what I’m hearing is… I should make up for lost time?"**  

Jodi, **tilting her head.** “Less talking. More catching up.”  

And then **she shoved him onto the bed.**  

---
## **FRIDAY NIGHT IN CLEVELAND – FAST, FRANTIC, AND INEVITABLE**  

There was **no hesitation.** No slow buildup. No wasted time.  

They had **too much history for that.**  

Jodi barely had time to **kick off her shoes** before he was on her—gripping, pulling, **undoing everything between them in seconds.**  

Her back hit the bed, **his weight pressing into her, his mouth hot and demanding against hers.**  

Her fingers tangled in his hair, **pulling, dragging him closer.**  

His hands **skimmed rough and urgent** over her body—**like he was memorizing her all over again, like he was pissed at how long it had been, like they’d both wasted too much goddamn time.**  

**They had.**  

And now, **they were making up for it.**  

---
## **JODI’S THOUGHTS, IN THE FEW SECONDS SHE’S STILL THINKING**  

- *God, I missed this.*    
- *I hate that I missed this.*    
- **This should have been us.**    
- **Why wasn’t it us?**    
- **Don’t think about that right now.**  

And then his teeth grazed her skin, **his grip tightened,** and all her thoughts **evaporated.**  

---
## **HIM – NOT WASTING A SINGLE SECOND**  

**He knew her body too well.**  

Knew exactly how to push her to the edge **without preamble, without permission—without needing either.**  

This was **old ground, but never stale.**  

It was **muscle memory and pent-up want, frustration and familiarity, the easiest thing in the world and yet impossible all the same.**  

**It could’ve been them.** **Should’ve been them.**  

But **it never would be.**  

And **maybe that’s why it was always this intense.**  

---
## **WHEN IT’S OVER – A BRIEF, BREATHLESS SILENCE** 

The room was **too hot, the sheets a wreck, their bodies tangled.**  

Jodi, **somewhere between bliss and bitterness, staring at the ceiling.**  

He exhaled, dragging a hand through his hair.  

**"Jesus Christ, Jodi."**  

She huffed a **breathless laugh.** **“Yeah.”**  

For a few long moments, **neither of them moved.**  

They didn’t need to say it out loud, but the thought **was mutual:**  

**That shouldn’t have been that good.**    
**And yet, it always was.**  

---
## **FRIDAY NIGHT IN CLEVELAND – ROUND TWO: SLOW, LINGERING, INEVITABLE**

Jodi **could have left.**  

She **should have left.**  

But she didn’t.  

Because **for once, she didn’t want to rush away.**  

For once, she wanted to **stay in the warmth of the moment, let it sink into her bones, commit it to memory.**  

For once, she wanted to **luxuriate in the feeling of being with him—fully, deeply, completely.**  

So when he moved to roll away, **to give her an easy out,** she did the opposite.  

Jodi, **softly, a little breathless**:    
**“Stay here. Stay with me.”**  

He **froze.**  

Just for a second.  

Then, **something in his expression changed.**  

Something that made her **chest ache** and her **stomach tighten.**  

Something that made her **wish life had gone differently.**  

But *wishes didn’t change reality.*  

So instead, **she reached for him.**  

---

## **ROUND TWO – A DIFFERENT KIND OF INTENSITY**  

This time, **it wasn’t frantic.**    
This time, **they weren’t making up for lost time.**    
This time, **they took their time.**  

Jodi traced her fingertips down his spine, **slow and reverent.**  

His hands skimmed over her hips, her waist, her ribs, **like he was rediscovering her all over again.**  

He kissed her **with patience, with tenderness, with a depth that was almost painful.**  

And when he finally moved against her, into her—**it was exquisite.**  

Slow, deep, **treasured.**  

The kind of intimacy that was **beyond words.**  

The kind that **lingered long after it was over.**  

---

## **WHEN IT’S OVER – THEY DON’T LET GO IMMEDIATELY**  

They stayed like that—**bodies tangled, breath syncing, hearts beating in rhythm.**  

Jodi **kept her eyes closed for a moment longer than necessary.**  

Because **she knew what came next.**  

Eventually, **they’d have to untangle.**  

Eventually, **she’d have to put her walls back up.**  

Eventually, **this would become a memory.**  

But not yet.  

For now, **she let herself have this.**  

---

## **FRIDAY NIGHT IN CLEVELAND – THE TALK THEY CAN’T AVOID**  

The warmth between them **hadn’t faded yet.**  

Jodi could still feel **his skin against hers, the weight of his arm draped lazily across her waist, the rhythm of his breathing slowing down.**  

They could have **let the silence stretch.**    
They could have **pretended this was just another night.**    
They could have **ignored the obvious.**  

But they’d known each other **too damn long** for that.  

So **he asked.**  

**“You bothered by the big news?”**  

**And there it was.**  

The question she **knew was coming.**  

The one she **didn’t want to answer.**  

Jodi, **exhaling through her nose.** *“I’m not surprised.”*  

He made a **soft sound of acknowledgment.**  

Not quite agreement. **Not quite acceptance.**  

Just **understanding.**  

---

## **HE KNOWS WHY SHE’S HERE.**  

He shifted, **propping himself up on an elbow to study her.**  

**“You came here to clear your head.”**  

Not a question.  

A statement.  

Jodi, **smirking faintly.** *“Is that what we’re calling this?”*  

He gave her a **look.** **A real one.**  

The kind that made her **chest tighten.**  

**“You came here to reclaim something.”**  

Jodi, **quietly.** *“Independence, sanity. Something like that.”*  

**Funny how that worked.**  

She thought **she was running toward something.**    
Maybe she was just **running away.**  

---
## **HE DOESN’T PUSH. HE NEVER DOES.**  

**“So?”** His voice was even, careful. **“Did it work?”**  

Jodi, **pausing.**  

Letting herself **really think about it.**  

Then—**simply.**  

**“For now.”**  

Because **that was the truth.**  

She wasn’t *fixed.* She wasn’t *over it.*    
She wasn’t *untangled* from Jeff’s orbit.  

But **tonight helped.**  

Her college friend—her sometimes-lover—**he helped.**  

Even if **it wasn’t permanent.**  

---
## **HE NODS, BUT HIS EXPRESSION SOFTENS.**  

**“You know, I hate seeing you like this.”**  

Jodi, **raising an eyebrow.** *“Naked? Relaxed? Satisfied?”*  

Him, **not amused.** **“You know what I mean.”**  

Jodi, **quietly.** *“Yeah. I do.”*  

A beat of silence.  

Then, **he reached out, traced a hand down her arm.**  

**“You deserve better than waiting for someone who’ll never fully be there.”**  

**That?** That **hit too close.**  

Jodi, **deflecting.** *“I’m not waiting. I’m playing the game just as much as he is.”*  

**Was that the truth?**  

She didn’t know.  

---
## **FRIDAY NIGHT IN CLEVELAND – A SHIFT IN FOCUS**  

Jodi **studied him—** not just his face, but his body, his posture, his quiet patience.  

And, of course, **his cock.**  

Because in this state—**sated, softened, stripped of ego and urgency—**they all looked the same. **Just anatomy. Just flesh.**  

**It meant nothing.**  

And maybe, **that was the point.**  

---
## **SHE RESOLVES TO LEAVE IT AT THAT.**  

She reached for the sheets, **tugging them up, half-covering them both.**  

**“Let’s leave it at that.”**  

He didn’t question her. **Just nodded once.**  

And that was why she **kept coming back to him.**  

Because he **didn’t push, didn’t pry, didn’t try to “save” her from her own choices.**  

He just **let her be.**  

---
## **JODI SHIFTS THE CONVERSATION—TO SOMETHING THAT MATTERS.**  

Jodi, **stretching lazily, shifting gears.** *“Anyway. Work’s been insane.”*  

Him, **grinning a little at the abrupt pivot.** “Oh?”  

Jodi, **mock-serious.** *“Extremely successful. Great kudos. I am, officially, a powerhouse.”*  

Him, **amused.** “Oh, well. My apologies for not addressing you properly, then. Should I be calling you ‘Miss CEO’ now?”  

Jodi, **smirking.** *“‘Your Excellency’ works, too.”*  

Him, **laughing.** “Noted.”  

A beat. Then, more seriously:  

**“So, how are you gonna handle Monday morning?”**  

Jodi, **pausing.**  

---

## **MONDAY MORNING.**  

The real world. The office. The fluorescent lights and the routine and the inevitable flood of emails.  

The *version* of herself that was **polished, productive, invulnerable.**  

The *version* that had **zero time to dwell on Cleveland, or Jeff, or any of this.**  

She exhaled, **rolling onto her back, staring at the ceiling.**  

**“Same way I always do.”**  

Him, **watching her.** “Which is?”  

Jodi, **simply.** **“I’ll be fine.”**  

Because that was **the only answer she ever gave.**  

And sometimes, she even believed it.  

---


## **FRIDAY NIGHT IN CLEVELAND – NO MORE PRETENDING**  

Jodi had **hoped to let it slide.**  

Hoped that **if she just pivoted hard enough—talked about work, cracked a joke, deflected—he’d let it go.**  

But of course, **he didn’t.**  

Because **he knew her too well.**  

And now, **he was looking at her—really looking at her—** and she **hated that.**  

---

## **HE CALLS HER OUT.**  

**“I saw the post.”**  

Jodi, **still staring at the ceiling.** *“What post?”*  

**“Don’t play dumb.”**  

She exhaled **slowly through her nose.**  

She wasn’t going to **admit anything first.**    
He’d have to **do the work.**  

So he did.  

**“Jeff saw it, too, didn’t he?”**  

Jodi, **eyes still fixed upward.** “That was the point.”  

He **let that sit for a second.**  

Then—**simply, but pointedly:**  

**“And now that it worked, are you any happier?”**  

Jodi, **gritting her teeth slightly.** *“That’s a bullshit question.”*  

**“No, it’s not.”**  

She **finally turned her head to look at him.**  

---

## **HE DOESN’T LET IT GO.**  

**“You’re gnawing on something primal, Jodi.”**  

His voice was **gentle, but firm.**  

**“You came here because you needed to feel in control. But you’re still here, in your own damn head, chewing on something that won’t let you be happy even now.”**  

Jodi, **mocking lightly.** *“Wow. How profound. You read that in a self-help book?”*  

**“I read it in your goddamn face.”**  

That shut her up.  

He sighed, rubbing his jaw, **watching her like he was waiting for her to stop playing the game.**  

And for whatever reason—**maybe because it was him, maybe because she was just tired—**she did.  

---

## **SHE FINALLY SAYS IT.**  

**“It’s not just her.”**  

His brow furrowed. **“Avril?”**  

Jodi, **quietly.** “It’s not just her.”  

She **paused, choosing her words carefully.**  

**“It’s the fact that I was never meant to be ‘her.’”**  

She swallowed.  

**“Jeff was never going to pick me first. I was always just going to be the thing he came back to.”**  

A **painful truth.**  

A **truth she had always known, but never said out loud.**  

And now that she had, **she hated how much it hurt.**  

---

## **HE WATCHES HER—THEN SPEAKS.**  

His expression **shifted.**  

Not **pity.** Not **disbelief.**  

Just **understanding.**  

And then, **softly:**  

**“You ever think that maybe you don’t have to settle for that?”**  

Jodi, **letting out a slow breath.** *“More times than I can count.”*  

He nodded. **“And yet.”**  

Jodi, **bitter smile.** *“And yet.”*  

Because **she already knew the answer.**  

**Jeff was a pattern. A habit. A cycle.**  

And maybe, just maybe—**she was the one refusing to break it.**  

---
## **FRIDAY NIGHT IN CLEVELAND – JODI FACES THE UGLY TRUTH**  

Jodi **wasn’t mad at him.**  

She was **furious at herself.**  

Because in a **normal world,** Jeff would be **perfect for her.**  

But this wasn’t a normal world. **It never had been.**  

And **she didn’t know how to quit.**  

Worse? **She didn’t want to.**  

But what **really scared her?**  

**She was losing.**  

Losing to **time, to reality, to the inevitable.**  

And she had **no idea what to do about it.**  

---

## **SHE GESTURES TO THE BED—THE EVIDENCE OF HER PATTERN.**  

**“This.”** She pointed at the **mess of soaked sheets, the tangled blankets, the proof of their chemistry, their history, their damn near inevitability.**  

**“This helps me stay sane.”**  

Her voice was **a little sharper than intended,** but she **didn’t walk it back.**  

**“This helps me stay the course.”**  

She exhaled, running a hand through her hair. **“But Christ, this isn’t the course I set back in college.”**  

She looked at him, **eyes flashing with something raw.**  

**“You know that. You *were* there.”**  

---

## **HE DOESN’T INTERRUPT HER.**  

He just **listens.**  

Which is why she **keeps going.**  

**“And it didn’t help that you got married, either.”**  

His jaw **ticked slightly,** but he said nothing.  

Jodi let out a **half-laugh, half-scoff.**  

**“But I forgive you for that.”**  

A beat.  

**“And, honestly? I like it better now that you’re divorced.”**  

That got a reaction.  

**He huffed out a breath, shaking his head.** “Jesus, Jodi.”  

**“What?”** She smirked—**but it was bitter, sharp, anything but playful.**  

**“It’s true.”**  

He looked at her **like he was trying to solve a puzzle.**

**It was something more.**  

A **shudder that wracked through his whole body, muscles locking, breath catching in his throat.**  

Jodi **had seen him come before.**  

But **never like this.**  

Never **so intense that it left him trembling, gasping, struggling to ground himself in reality.**  

Her **own need was still simmering, but she paused, shifting, pulling him into her arms as he sagged against her.**  

**She whispered something soft, something reassuring.**  

Her **fingers traced his jaw, his temple, smoothing the tension away.**  

**“Breathe,”** she murmured. **“I’ve got you.”**  

---

## **A MOMENT OF TENDERNESS.**  

He **buried his face in her shoulder, body still shivering from aftershocks.**  

Jodi **stroked his back, his hair, pressing soft kisses along his damp skin.**  

There **was something achingly beautiful about this—about taking care of him, about watching him fall apart and knowing she was the reason.**  

She **was still aching for her own release, but for now, this mattered more.**  

**He exhaled, shaky.**  

**“Fuck, Jodi.”**  

She **smiled softly, rubbing circles against his spine.**  

**“Yeah.”**  

He **lifted his head, eyes heavy-lidded, dazed.**  

**“You’re… still waiting.”****  

Jodi **shrugged playfully.** **“I’m patient.”**  

His **lips curled into something slow, something promising.**  

**“Not for long.”**  

---

 
## **SATURDAY NIGHT – JUST THIS**  

The **world could wait.**  

The **city lights still flickered beneath them, the rooftop breeze still brushed against their sweat-slicked skin, but in this moment, none of it mattered.**  

Jodi **felt his breathing even out, his body still trembling faintly from the aftershocks of release.**  

She **savored the way he clung to her, like he wasn’t entirely sure where he ended and she began.**  

Then, **he stirred.**  

**“Let me take care of you,”** he murmured, voice still hoarse, hands already moving with the intent to return the favor.  

Jodi **caught his wrists, pressing a soft kiss to his knuckles before resting them against her waist.**  

**“No.”**  

He **blinked, confused.** **“No?”**  

**She shook her head.**  

**“Not yet. Not now.”**  

---

## **A DIFFERENT KIND OF SATISFACTION.**  

His **brow furrowed.** **“But you—”**  

**She silenced him with another kiss, this one slow, unhurried.**  

**He tasted like salt, like warmth, like something grounding and real.**  

**She sighed against him.**  

**“I just want this.”**  

His **muscles relaxed beneath her touch.**  

**Jodi tucked her head beneath his chin, letting the weight of him settle around her.**  

They **lay tangled on the rooftop, bodies pressed close, hearts still hammering against their ribs.**  

**She didn’t need anything else right now.**  

**Just this.**  

**Just him.**  

And **for once, he didn’t push. He didn’t argue.**  

He **just held her tighter.**  

---


## **SATURDAY NIGHT – NOTHING LEFT UNSAID**  

The **air between them was still thick with heat, but the urgency had faded.**  

Jodi **felt the steady rise and fall of his chest beneath her cheek, the rhythmic thud of his heartbeat slowing as reality settled back in.**  

She **should’ve been restless—still aching, still needy—but somehow, she wasn’t.**  

Not right now. **Right now, she just wanted to stay here, pressed against him, grounded in something real.**  

And **maybe… maybe talk about the things they usually avoided.**  

---

## **BREAKING THE SILENCE.**  

**“Do you ever think about stopping?”** she murmured.  

His **fingers, which had been lazily tracing patterns along her back, stilled.**  

**“Stopping what?”**  

**“This. The way we keep circling back to each other.”**  

She **felt him exhale slowly, his breath warm against her hair.**  

**“No.”**  

She **smiled faintly.** **“Not even once?”**  

**He shook his head.** **“Not even once.”**  

---

## **A DIFFERENT KIND OF HONESTY.**  

She **lifted her head, propping herself up on his chest.**  

**“So what the hell are we, then?”**  

His **lips quirked at the corner.**  

**“I was hoping you’d tell me.”**  

**She scoffed.** **“Coward.”**  

He **laughed, dragging a hand through his hair.**  

**“Fine. We’re… two people who’ve never really let go of each other. And maybe don’t want to.”**  

Her **smile faltered just a little.**  

**“Even if it means nothing changes?”**  

His **eyes softened.** **“Even then.”**  

---

## **NO EXPECTATIONS, NO LIES.**  

She **exhaled, studying him.**  

**“You know Jeff asked me to go to Oslo.”**  

He **nodded.** **“You gonna go?”**  

She **hesitated.**  

**She could.** **She could get on a plane, sink back into that whirlwind, let herself be caught up in his orbit again.**  

Or **she could stay here, keep living her life on her own terms.**  

She **was still figuring that part out.**  

**“I don’t know yet,”** she admitted. **“But I do know this…”**  

She **leaned down, brushing her lips over his, slow and deliberate.**  

**“I always come back to you.”**  

He **cupped the back of her head, his voice barely above a whisper.**  

**“And I’ll always be here.”**  

---

## **SATURDAY NIGHT – NO MORE SECRETS**  

The **sky stretched dark and endless above them, but time had stopped mattering hours ago.**  

Jodi **lay tangled in the sheets beside him, their bodies warm, their limbs lazily draped over one another.**  

Neither **of them slept.**  

Neither **of them wanted to.**  

They **had spent a lifetime talking in circles, dodging truths, skirting around the rawest parts of themselves.**  

Tonight, **they weren’t going to do that.**  

Tonight, **they were going to say the things that never got said.**  

---

## **A HISTORY IN PIECES.**  

**“Do you regret it?”** he asked, voice low, steady.  

She **turned her head against the pillow, blinking at him in the dim light.**  

**“Regret what?”**  

**“Not choosing me.”**  

Her **breath hitched.**  

**Not ‘not choosing Jeff.’**  

**Not ‘choosing this messy, unstructured life.’**  

**Not ‘choosing a version of herself that might have been simpler, easier, less tangled in past and present contradictions.’**  

**Him.**  

She **didn’t answer right away.**  

Instead, **she reached for his hand, tracing the lines of his palm, feeling the familiar warmth of his skin.**  

**“No.”**  

He **exhaled, slow and measured.**  

**“Do you wish I had?”** she asked, softer now.  

His **silence stretched between them.**  

**Then, finally—**  

**“No.”**  

But **it wasn’t entirely the truth.**  

And **she didn’t push him to pretend it was.**  

---

## **WHY THEY ALWAYS COME BACK.**  

**“We could’ve had a normal life.”**  

His **voice was barely more than a murmur.**  

Jodi **snorted.**  

**“We could’ve tried.”**  

He **huffed a quiet laugh, shaking his head.**  

**“Wouldn’t have lasted.”**  

**“No.”**  

They **both knew it.**  

Because **she wasn’t built for normal, and neither was he.**  

Because **she had tasted just enough of the world to know she couldn’t fit in one place.**  

Because **he had tried to build a normal life and watched it fall apart, because part of him had never really wanted it.**  

Because **no matter how many years passed, no matter how many people came and went, they always came back to this.**  

**To each other.**  

---

## **LOOKING AHEAD.**  

The **hours bled together, the weight of exhaustion creeping in but neither of them moving to sleep.**  

**They talked about everything.**  

**The past.**  

**The present.**  

**The ridiculousness of it all.**  

They **laughed.**  

They **whispered.**  

They **admitted things they never had before.**  

At **some point, the conversation drifted back to Jeff.**  

Back **to Oslo.**  

Back **to the life that Jodi had, the choices she had made.**  

**“What are you gonna do?”** he asked eventually.  

She **stared at the ceiling, thoughtful.**  

**“I don’t know.”**  

She **meant it.**  

For **the first time in years, she wasn’t trying to plan six moves ahead.**  

She **was just… here.**  

With **him.**  

And **for now, that was enough.**  

---


## **SUNDAY MORNING – DECISIONS IN THE DARK**  

The **sky outside was just starting to lighten, but neither of them had moved.**  

Jodi **lay on her back, staring at the ceiling, feeling the weight of the night settle into something sharp, something certain.**  

**She was going to Oslo.**  

Not **because of Jeff.**  

Not **because of Avril.**  

Not **because of anything other than the simple, undeniable truth—**  

**She wanted to go.**  

**For herself.**  

---

## **THE QUESTION HE HAS TO ASK.**  

He **propped himself up on one elbow, watching her carefully.**  

**“Okay,”** he said slowly. **“Why?”**  

Jodi **sighed, stretching her arms above her head.**  

**“Why not?”**  

**He scoffed.** **“Not buying it.”**  

She **smirked.** **“Too bad.”**  

He **wasn’t letting it go that easily.**  

**“So what’s the goal here? You flying across the world to fuck him, spy on him, fight Avril, or—”**  

**He tilted his head.**  

**“—are you actually gonna do the right thing and just have a damn good time?”**  

Jodi **laughed.**  

**Because, fuck.**  

He **knew her too well.**  

---

## **THE REALITY.**  

**“Look,”** she said, rolling onto her side to face him. **“I could sit here and pretend I don’t have unresolved shit with Jeff. I could say I don’t care, or that I’m above it, but you and I both know that’s a fucking lie.”**  

He **nodded, waiting.**  

**“But I also know this—I don’t have to let that shit own me.”**  

His **expression softened.**  

**“And?”**  

Jodi **smirked.** **“And… Norway’s beautiful. And I have the money. And maybe, just maybe, I deserve a damn adventure that isn’t about anyone but me.”**  

His **lips twitched.**  

**“Now that, I can respect.”**  

---

## **FINAL WORDS.**  

He **leaned in, brushing a kiss against her forehead.**  

**“Just promise me one thing.”**  

**Jodi raised an eyebrow.** **“What’s that?”**  

**“Whatever happens over there… don’t let anyone, not Jeff, not Avril, not the fucking country of Norway itself, make you forget who the hell you are.”**  

Her **smile softened.**  

**“I promise.”**  

For **the first time in a long time, she meant it.**  

---


## **SUNDAY MORNING – BOOKING THE FUTURE**  

Jodi **grinned at her phone, shaking her head.**  

**Jeff, ever the predictable bastard.**  

She **typed back, quick and sharp—**  

**"You’ll have to work harder than that, rock star. But go ahead and fantasize about it over lunch.”**  

She **sent it, then turned to the man beside her, who was watching her with raised eyebrows.**  

**“What?”** she asked.  

**He smirked.** **“Nothing. Just wondering how much trouble you’re about to cause in Europe.”**  

---

## **ONE LAST PROMISE.**  

She **rolled onto her stomach, kicking her feet up behind her.**  

**“You’ll find out. Next time I’m in Cleveland, I’m bringing a friend.”**  

His **eyebrows lifted.** **“Oh? I should be worried?”**  

**“Terrified.”**  

**He laughed.**  

**“Can’t wait.”**  

---

## **THE TEXT EXCHANGE CONTINUES.**  

Her **phone buzzed again.**  

**Jeff: You serious? You’re actually coming?**  

**Jodi: Yeah. Thought I’d shake things up.**  

**Jeff: Good.**  

She **didn’t reply right away. Just stared at the screen.**  

Because **for once, there wasn’t subtext or dodging or deflection. Just that.**  

**Good.**  

And **for once, she let herself believe it.**  

---

## **SUNDAY RESET – BACK TO REALITY (SORT OF)**  

Jodi **tossed her bag into the passenger seat and exhaled, hands gripping the wheel.**  

Norway **was happening.**  

But **before she could jet off across the Atlantic, she had to do the one thing she was truly bad at—**  

**Act like a functioning adult for a few days.**  

---

## **THE DRIVE HOME**  

The **highway stretched out ahead of her, the miles ticking down as she ran through everything she had to do before the week started.**  

- **Clean the house** (*because if she left it a mess, she’d come home to regret it*).    
- **Laundry** (*because re-wearing a bra three times was already pushing it*).    
- **Visit her mom** (*before her mom could start calling in full passive-aggressive guilt mode*).    
- **Groceries and meal prep** (*because she was going to do better than steal bread at work like a Dickensian orphan*).  

Jodi **sighed.** **"God, I hate being responsible.”**  

Her **phone buzzed.**  

**Jeff: You on your way?**  

**Jodi: Home, yeah. Real life sucks. I have to buy groceries like some kind of peasant.**  

**Jeff: Don’t they have staff for that?**  

**Jodi: Not all of us have black AmExes, asshole.**  

**Jeff: Tragic. Want me to send a private chef?**  

**Jodi: No, I want to roast chicken and not steal my coworker’s bread like a raccoon this week.**  

**Jeff: Also tragic. But I respect it.**  

She **rolled her eyes but smiled anyway.**  

---

## **HOME SWEET (MESSY) HOME**  

She **pushed open the door to her place, groaning.**  

**How the fuck had she left it this bad?**  

A **tangle of blankets still half on the couch, an unwashed coffee cup on the counter, mail stacked in an increasingly precarious pile.**  

**“Right.”**  

She **set her phone down and got to work.**  

---

## **THE CHECKLIST**  

☑ **Laundry going?** (*Yes.*)    
☑ **Dishes in the dishwasher?** (*Yes.*)    
☑ **Floors vacuumed?** (*Yes, with only minor swearing.*)    
☑ **Mail sorted?** (*Mostly trash.*)    
☑ **Mom visited?** (*…Shit.*)  

Jodi **grabbed her phone, bracing herself.**  

---

## **THE MOM CALL**  

**Mom:** *“You’re alive?”*  

**Jodi:** *“It’s been three days, not three months.”*  

**Mom:** *“Still.”*  

**Jodi:** *“I’m coming over. You need anything?”*  

**Mom:** *“Just to see my daughter before she runs off again.”*  

**Jodi:** *(laughing)* *“It’s a work week, Mom. I’m not running anywhere.”*  

(*Yet.*)  

---

## **GROCERY RUN – NO MORE STOLEN BREAD**  

Jodi **pushed her cart through the store, determined.**  

**No more last-minute desk lunches. No more stealing Tara’s bread. No more living like a chaotic disaster.**  

She **stacked her cart with**:    
✅ Chicken for roasting.    
✅ Pre-cut veggies (because she was lazy).    
✅ Coffee, because *obviously.*    
✅ Something green (*for the illusion of health*).    
✅ Wine, because *fuck it, she earned it.*  

As **she checked out, her phone buzzed again.**  

**Jeff: Be honest, you’re just getting wine and snacks, aren’t you?**  

**Jodi: No, I am being a responsible adult.**  

**Jeff: This is deeply unsettling.**  

**Jodi: I know. I hate it.**  

---

## **READY FOR MONDAY**  

By the time **she got home, prepped her meals, and put fresh sheets on the bed, it was late.**  

She **collapsed onto her couch, exhaling.**  

**House clean.**  

**Laundry done.**  

**Meals prepped.**  

**Mom visited.**  

**Groceries stocked.**  

She **was officially ready for the work week.**  

(*Or at least, as ready as she ever was.*)  

And **in the back of her mind, she was already counting down the days until she was boarding a plane.**  

**Oslo was coming.**  

And **this time, she was going on her terms.**  

---

## **MONDAY MORNING – TIME OFF REQUEST**  

Jodi **sat at her desk, staring at her screen, fingers hovering over the keyboard.**  

Four days. **Just four days.**  

**Not a big deal.**  

She **opened the HR portal and filled out the request form.**  

**Dates:** *Thursday - Tuesday*    
**Reason:** *Personal Travel*  

She **hit submit.**  

Five **minutes later, her phone rang.**  

**HR:** *“Jodi. You know I have to ask.”*  

**Jodi:** *“Ask what?”*  

**HR:** *“Why the sudden vacation request? You usually plan your PTO like a damn accountant.”*  

**Jodi:** *(laughing)* *“Maybe I’m learning to be spontaneous.”*  

**HR:** *“Bullshit.”*  

**Jodi:** *“…I’m going to Norway.”*  

**HR:** *“…I’m sorry, what?”*  

---

## **THE INTERROGATION BEGINS**  

**HR:** *“You don’t even like cold weather.”*  

**Jodi:** *“True.”*  

**HR:** *“You don’t even like traveling.”*  

**Jodi:** *“Also true.”*  

**HR:** *“So, what’s the deal? Work retreat? Secret mission? Spy shit?”*  

**Jodi:** *“Wouldn’t you like to know.”*  

**HR:** *“I would. Because I’m approving this leave, but I expect a full report when you return.”*  

**Jodi:** *(grinning)* *“On what? The fjords?”*  

**HR:** *“No. On whatever absolute chaos you get into. Because I *know* you, Jodi.”*  

---

## **APPROVED – NOW WHAT?**  

Jodi **hung up, smirking.**  

**Time off? Approved.**  

Now, **the real work began.**  

✅ **Tell Tara and Naomi before they found out and had a meltdown.**    
✅ **Figure out what to pack because Norway was cold as balls.**    
✅ **Mentally prepare for Jeff, because she *knew* he was going to be a handful.**  

And **most importantly?**  

✅ **Make it clear—** *she was doing this for herself.*  

This **wasn’t about Avril.**  

This **wasn’t about the band.**  

This **was about Jodi.**  

And **she was about to make damn sure she got what *she* wanted out of this trip.**  

---

## **MONDAY – TEAMWORK MAKES THE DREAM WORK**  

Tara **was practically vibrating with curiosity, but Jodi held up a hand.**  

**“Not now. After work.”**  

**“Oh, come *on.*”**  

**“There’s a reason for the madness.”**  

Tara **crossed her arms, narrowing her eyes.** **“Fine. But I better get details over drinks.”**  

**Naomi perked up.** **“Is this about Norway?”**  

Jodi **snapped her fingers.** **“See? This one listens.”**  

**Tara gaped.** **“NORWAY?!? What the actual—”**  

**Jodi clapped a hand over her mouth.** **“Not. Now.”**  

**Tara’s muffled scream was probably shaking the foundation of the building.**  

---

## **THE DEAL**  

Jodi **leaned in, dropping her voice.**  

**“I need to crunch a couple reports early to take my vacation. So, if you want the full story, help me.”**  

Tara **sighed dramatically.** **“You’re making me work for gossip. I hate you.”**  

**Naomi, however, looked intrigued.**  

**“I could probably automate some of the reporting.”**  

Jodi **blinked.** **“What.”**  

**Naomi shrugged.** **“I’ve been learning Python scripting. I could write a quick script to pull the data and generate a formatted report.”**  

**Jodi stared.**  

**Tara stared.**  

**“I don’t know what any of that means,”** Jodi admitted. **“But if it means I leave work at 5:00 instead of some ungodly hour, I will kiss you.”**  

**Naomi beamed.** **“No need. I’ll take a muffin instead.”**  

---

## **CRUNCH TIME**  

- **Tara manually handled the sections that needed judgment calls.**    
- **Naomi, *somehow*, made a little blinking text box thingy that spat out numbers in a fraction of the time.**    
- **Jodi mostly just tried not to cry from gratitude.**  

At **4:50, the last file was done.**  

**Jodi leaned back in her chair, stretching.**  

**“That,”** she declared, **“was an actual miracle.”**  

**Tara exhaled.** **“That was *delegation.*”**  

**Naomi sipped her tea.** **“That was Python.”**  

Jodi **had no idea how this girl was real, but she was never letting her go.**  

---

## **5:00 PM – TIME TO SPILL**  

**Jodi grabbed her coat.** **“Alright, ladies. Drinks are on me. Let’s go.”**  

Tara **clapped her hands together.** **“I am *so* ready for this.”**  

Naomi **grinned.** **“This is gonna be *good.*”**  

**Jodi smirked.**  

**They had *no* idea.**  

---

## **5:15 PM – BETRAYED BY NACHOS**  

**Jodi leaned back in her chair, arms crossed.** **“I was gonna wait for the nachos.”**  

**Naomi, ever innocent:** *“Why are you going to Norway?”*  

**Tara choked on her drink.**  

**Jodi sighed.** **“Jesus Christ, kid, let me *breathe.*”**  

**Naomi blinked.** **“What? It’s a normal question.”**  

**Tara, already cackling:** *“Honey, this is Jodi we’re talking about. Nothing is normal.”*  

---

## **THE EXPLANATION (SORT OF)**  

**Jodi exhaled, drumming her fingers on the table.**  

**“Okay. Fine. Short version?”**  

- *Yes, I’m going to Norway.*    
- *No, I didn’t plan it in advance.*    
- *Yes, Jeff is there.*    
- *No, I’m not going there just for him.*    
- *Yes, Avril is also there.*  

**Tara threw her hands in the air.** **“Fucking *WHAT?*”**  

**Naomi looked delighted.** **“Are you going to fight her?”**  

**Jodi:** *“No.”*  

**Tara:** *“But you *want* to.”*  

**Jodi:** *“…maybe.”*  

---

## **MORE DETAILS (BECAUSE THEY DEMANDED THEM)**  

**Tara pointed a threatening finger.** **“Spill. Right now.”**  

Jodi **groaned.**  

**“Look. The band’s in Norway recording. I want to go for myself. Not for Avril, not for Jeff. *Me.*”**  

**Naomi:** *“But you’re obviously gonna fuck Jeff.”*  

**Jodi:** **(choking on her beer)** *“NAOMI.”*  

**Naomi, blinking:** *“I mean. Right?”*  

---

## **THE NACHOS ARRIVE—BUT IT’S TOO LATE**  

Tara **grabbed a chip, smirking.** **“So what’s the game plan? Are you gonna make a move or just be mysterious and hot?”**  

**Jodi:** *“I am *always* mysterious and hot.”*  

**Naomi:** *“But what if Avril tries to seduce him?”*  

**Jodi took a deep breath and stole a loaded nacho from Tara’s plate.**  

**“Then I’m gonna smile, have a drink, and let that play out however it’s meant to.”**  

**Tara narrowed her eyes.** **“And?”**  

**Jodi smirked.**  

**“And then I’m going to fuck him so good he forgets why he ever thought about hiring her in the first place.”**  

**Naomi covered her face.** **“Oh my *god*.”**  

**Tara raised her glass.** **“To Jodi. The *real* reason the Vikings left Norway.”**  

**Jodi clinked her glass.**  

**She had a flight to book.**  

---

## **5:45 PM – JEFF TEXTS SOMETHING UNHINGED**  

Jodi's **phone buzzed on the table.**  

**Jeff:** *"If you’re coming to Norway, I expect you to come at least three times before we even leave the airport."*  

**Jodi spit out her drink.**  

**Naomi:** *“OH MY GOD, WHAT?!”*  

**Tara:** *(already reaching for the phone)* *“Give it here, you menace.”*  

Jodi **slammed her hand over the screen.**  

**“Nope. No. Not for public consumption.”**  

**Naomi:** *“But you let him talk to you like that in public?”*  

**Jodi:** *(flatly)* *“Welcome to my life.”*  

**Tara:** *“Okay, but let’s focus on the bigger issue—*how the fuck* does this man think he owns airport orgasms? What, does he have a private terminal or something?”*  

**Jodi:** *“…yes.”*  

**Naomi, shrieking:** *“HE DOES?”*  

---

## **THE EXPLANATION – SORT OF**  

Jodi **sighed.**  

**“Listen. The band always flies private. They’ve got their own setup, security, lounges, whatever. He’s *not* joking.”**  

**Naomi:** *(blinking rapidly)* *“You’ve… had sex in an airport?”*  

**Jodi:** *(deadpan)* *“I’ve had sex in three.”*  

**Tara, staring:** *“This is why I drink.”*  

**Naomi, furiously typing:** *“I am Googling ‘celebrity private airport sex.’”*  

**Jodi:** *“Jesus Christ, *don’t.*”*  

---

## **TEXTING JEFF BACK – BECAUSE SHE HAD TO**  

Jodi **thumbed out a response.**  

**Jodi:** *"Three? That’s ambitious. But I’ll consider it if you can still function after I get my hands on you."*  

**Jeff:** *"Jodi. Baby. Have you *met* me?"*  

**Jodi:** *"I have. That’s why I’m setting realistic expectations."*  

**Jeff:** *"So I’ll make it four. Understood."*  

**Jodi set the phone down and took a *very* large sip of her beer.**  

**Tara:** *“I just watched you type for five seconds and get dragged in real-time. I love this man.”*  

**Naomi:** *(still scrolling through search results)* *“You *sure* you wanna go? This sounds like *work.*”*  

**Jodi:** *“Oh, I’m going. I just need to be *mentally prepared.*”*  

**Tara, grinning:** *“Well. That’s why we’re here.”*  

---
## **6:00 PM – THE NIGHT TAKES A TURN**  

Jodi **had *barely* recovered from Jeff’s text when Naomi gasped so loudly that the entire table—and half the bar—turned to look at her.**  

**Naomi, eyes huge:** *“Jodi. What. The *fuck.*”*  

**Jodi, alarmed:** *“What *now?!*”*  

**Naomi held up her phone, waving it like it was on fire.**  

**“*I found an article.*”**  

**Tara:** *“Oh, sweet Jesus.”*  

---
## **THE ARTICLE**  

Naomi **cleared her throat dramatically and began reading aloud.**  

*"Inside Rock’s Most Notorious Love Triangle: The Band, the Ex, and the Mistress Who Stayed."*  

**Jodi dropped her forehead onto the table.**  

**“Kill me.”**  

**Naomi:** *“I’m just getting to the good part!”*  

**Jodi:** *“I *was* the good part!”*  

---
## **EXCERPTS FROM HELL**  

**Naomi, delighted:** *“It says here—‘For years, Jodi ___ has been the quiet presence in Jeff’s orbit, outlasting scandals, affairs, and more band drama than a Fleetwood Mac documentary.’”*  

**Tara:** *“*Not* the Fleetwood Mac comparison. Holy shit.”*  

**Naomi:** *“‘Despite Jeff’s well-documented history with women—’”*  

**Jodi:** *“Skip it.”*  

**Naomi:** *“‘—his on-again, off-again *muse* has maintained a careful distance from the media, making her one of the most *enigmatic* women in rock.’”*  

**Tara, wheezing:** *“Jodi. *Enigmatic.* You mysterious *bitch.*”*  

**Jodi, groaning:** *“I *hate* this.”*  

**Naomi:** *“It literally calls you *The Mistress Who Stayed.*”*
